## PROPOSAL 111 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T02:41:53Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 124, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/matchmaking-winrate-mirage-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-25 GAME-domain proposal (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P109 opened round-25 at the fleet slot, P110 took the venture slot, this is the game slot). Domain: competitive skill-based matchmaking (SBMM) rating-system informational economics ŌĆö the matchmaking win-rate mirage, deliberately DISTINCT from prior game slots (shop-reroll threshold ruin P099, streak-shield regressivity P103, energy-cap overflow forfeiture P107, gacha-pity P091, rubber-band/PID P095). The mirage: SBMM pairs you against opponents of your own Elo rating, and the symmetric Elo update drives your rating to the fixed point where your EXPECTED SCORE against the matched near-rating pool is Žā(0)=0.5, so at equilibrium every player ŌĆö regardless of true skill ŌĆö has an expected win rate ~0.5, and the observed win rate is COMPRESSED almost flat across skill while true skill is expressed as the equilibrium RATING, not as a win-rate surplus. Same-seed two-regime control isolates the cause: run the SAME players with the SAME skills under RANDOM matching (no matchmaking) and their win rate becomes a near-perfect skill signal (top-vs-bottom skill-decile win-rate gap ~0.72, corr(skill,winrate)~0.98); under SBMM the decile gap collapses to ~0.04 (~17├Ś compression) even though the identical games' rating recovers skill with corr~0.98 ŌĆö so it is the matchmaking, not the skill model or the estimator, that turns win rate into a mirage; the skill information is not destroyed, it is relocated out of the win rate and into the rating. The residual corr(skill,SBMM-winrate)~0.5 is a finite-population tail edge effect (extreme players match only inward), so the headline is the compressed decile GAP and the corr CONTRAST, not a claim that win rate is literally uncorrelated with skill. Claim under test: on the pinned world a working SBMM ladder compresses the win-rate gap by more than an order of magnitude vs random matching while the rating stays a faithful (corrŌēź0.90) skill read-out ŌĆö so grading players, matchmade bots, or competitive agents by win rate under SBMM reads a mirage; use the rating (or rating velocity). Pinned: SEED=20260717, N_PLAYERS=200, GAMES=600, BURN_IN=400, RANDOM_GAMES=200, SKILL_SD=250.0, ELO_SCALE=400.0, K_FACTOR=16.0, R0=1500.0, MATCH_JITTER=15.0, TRIALS=200, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; thresholds GAP_MAX=0.12 (G1), RANDOM_GAP_MIN=0.50 + DELTA_MIN=0.40 (G2), RATING_CORR_MIN=0.90 + CONTRAST_MIN=0.30 (G3); theoretical anchor ŌĆö the SBMM Elo fixed point has expected score Žā(0)=0.5 for every player, so the win-rate gapŌåÆ0 and corr(skill,rating)ŌåÆ~1. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 win-rate compression (headline) ŌĆö the SBMM top-minus-bottom skill-decile mean win-rate GAP Ōēż GAP_MAX=0.12 by Ōēź3Žā (best and worst players win nearly the same fraction under matchmaking); G2 matchmaking-causes-it (control) ŌĆö the RANDOM-matching decile win-rate gap Ōēź RANDOM_GAP_MIN=0.50 AND exceeds the SBMM gap by Ōēź DELTA_MIN=0.40, both by Ōēź3Žā (win rate DOES encode skill absent matchmaking, so the compression is caused by the pairing rule, not the skill model or estimator); G3 rating-retains-skill mirage ŌĆö the mean (corr(skill,final rating) ŌłÆ corr(skill,SBMM win rate)) Ōēź CONTRAST_MIN=0.30 by Ōēź3Žā with corr(skill,rating) Ōēź RATING_CORR_MIN=0.90 (the identical games' hidden rating keeps the skill signal the win rate loses). Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109/P110 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/superbot-games/matchmaking_winrate_mirage.py (random,math,json,hashlib; per-replication SBMM Elo season + random-matching control over a Normal(0,250) true-skill population ŌĆö no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): G1 SBMM decile gap 0.041946 (se 0.000131) z=596.5172Žā (Ōēż0.12), G2 random gap 0.722953 (se 0.001272) z_level=175.2561Žā (Ōēź0.50) AND gap_delta 0.681006 (se 0.001274) z_delta=220.6321Žā (Ōēź0.40), G3 contrast (0.984387ŌłÆ0.522507)=0.46188 (se 0.005117) z=31.6339Žā (Ōēź0.30) with corr_rating 0.984387 ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=835b8e6cc9cd6a5eae699d7ea963dda3a6c3980fb6fd2de3cee6fce26056427c.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 124 is mirrored back.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 111 ŌåÆ VERDICT 124). Offset pinned ŌĆö the +13 offset's row for P111 (immediate predecessor row P110 ŌåÆ V123, "sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 110 ŌåÆ VERDICT 123). Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V124 for P111; then round-25 unrelated-slot P112"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P111/V124 offset line ŌĆö it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). NO lane CONSUMER ŌĆö competitive matchmaking pure-mechanism head (SBMM win-rate mirage / Elo rating informativeness); verdict co-consumer = any surface running skill-based matchmaking or a convergent rating ladder and grading players, bots, or competitive agents by win rate or top-line W/L ŌĆö the transferable correction is "under working SBMM the win rate is driven to a skill-independent ~50% fixed point and compressed almost flat across skill, so it understates skill by more than an order of magnitude and a win-rate surplus mostly signals unconverged rating; rank and grade by the RATING (or rating velocity), treat a flat win rate across skill as the signature of correct matchmaking, and publish the rating ŌĆö not the win rate ŌĆö as the skill-legible scoreboard." Grounding idea-engine@0db4bfd ┬Ę pinned world above.

## PROPOSAL 112 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T03:00:31Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 125, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/series-reliability-collapse-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-25 UNRELATED-domain closer (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P109 opened round-25 at the fleet slot, P110 took the venture slot, P111 the game slot, this is the unrelated slot). Domain: reliability engineering / systems-reliability theory ŌĆö the series-system MTBF collapse (min-of-lifetimes / hazard addition), a fleet-external pure-mechanism head deliberately DISTINCT from the prior unrelated closers P100 (Kelly overbet / information theory), P104 (epidemic overshoot / epidemiology), P108 (regression to the mean / statistics). The mechanism: a SERIES system works only while ALL of its N components work, so it fails at the FIRST component failure and its lifetime is the MINIMUM of the N per-component lifetimes; for independent exponential lifetimes (rate ╬╗, per-component MTBF T=1/╬╗) the minimum of N is itself exponential with the SUMMED rate N╬╗ (hazard rates ADD: ╬╗_sys=Ōłæ╬╗_i=N╬╗), so the system MTBF collapses to exactly T/N ŌĆö N individually-"excellent" parts give a system N times MORE failure-prone than one part, and reliability R_sys(t)=r(t)^N decays geometrically in N. The symmetric fully-parallel/redundant system (fails only at the LAST component failure, lifetime = MAXIMUM of N) has MTBF T┬ĘH_N (H_N the N-th harmonic number), growing only LOGARITHMICALLY: at N=20 the series MTBF is T/N=0.05 while parallel is T┬ĘH_20Ōēł3.598, so seriesing DIVIDES MTBF by 20 while 20-fold redundancy MULTIPLIES it by only ~3.6├Ś. A single component in isolation keeps the full MTBF T, so the collapse is 100% the seriesing of many parts, not a bad part. Claim under test: on the pinned world the measured series-system MTBF collapses to ŌēłT/N (matches the closed form within 3Žā and lands far under a single part's MTBF), demonstrating that a chain of "good" components is a fragile system ŌĆö so quoting end-to-end reliability off per-component quality when the parts sit in series reads a mirage; multiply the reliabilities (r^N) and expect the MTBF to divide by the series count. Pinned: SEED=20260717, LAMBDA=1.0 (per-component MTBF T=1.0), N_SERIES=20, N_SYSTEMS=20000, TRIALS=400, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; threshold COLLAPSE_MAX=0.10 (G1); analytic anchors ŌĆö series MTBF E[min of N Exp]=T/N=0.05, parallel MTBF E[max of N Exp]=T┬ĘH_20=3.597740, R_sys(T)=e^{ŌłÆ20}=2.061154eŌłÆ09. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 series-collapse headline ŌĆö the measured series MTBF is at or below COLLAPSE_MAX=0.10 by Ōēź3Žā AND below the single-component MTBF (a chain of twenty identical good parts has a system MTBF far under a single part's); G2 single-component control ŌĆö the measured single-component MTBF is within 3Žā of T=1.0 (each component in isolation keeps the FULL MTBF, so G1's collapse is 100% the seriesing, not a bad/degraded part); G3 closed-form anchor MATCH ŌĆö the measured series MTBF matches the exact anchor T/N=0.05 within 3Žā (reproduces the min-of-exponentials / hazard-addition law ╬╗_sys=N╬╗, so the effect IS the series-reliability mechanism). Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109/P110/P111 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/series_reliability_collapse.py (random,math,json,hashlib; per-trial Monte-Carlo of N_SYSTEMS series systems, each drawing N_SERIES iid Exp(LAMBDA) component lifetimes, recording min/comps[0]/max ŌĆö no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): G1 mtbf_series 0.050032 (se 1.8e-05) z=2841.8816Žā (Ōēż0.10, < single) PASS, G2 mtbf_single 0.999848 (se 0.000337) |z|=0.4508Žā (vs T=1.0) PASS, G3 mtbf_series 0.050032 vs anchor T/N=0.05 |z|=1.8061Žā PASS, descriptive mtbf_parallel 3.597338 (se 0.000441) vs T┬ĘH_20=3.597740 ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=0aa2a8da4627f43f13b634afdd12b3e674ba4983ad13b27b35c10e7928b7bdf7.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 125 is mirrored back.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 112 ŌåÆ VERDICT 125). Offset pinned ŌĆö the +13 offset's row for P112 (immediate predecessor row P111 ŌåÆ V124, "sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 111 ŌåÆ VERDICT 124). Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V125 for P112; then round-26 fleet-slot P113"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P112/V125 offset line ŌĆö it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). NO lane CONSUMER ŌĆö reliability-engineering pure-mechanism head (series-system MTBF collapse / min-of-exponentials / hazard addition); verdict co-consumer = any fleet or product surface sizing end-to-end availability/reliability from per-component numbers (an N-hop request path's uptime SLO, a multi-stage pipeline's success rate, a single-sourced supply chain, a checklist/approval chain's completion odds) or allocating a reliability budget ŌĆö the transferable correction is "a series system's reliability is the PRODUCT of its parts (R_sys=r^N) and, for exponential lifetimes, its MTBF is T/N not T, so more 'good' parts in series is a WORSE system; multiply the per-component reliabilities, expect the MTBF to divide by the series count, and spend the budget on SHORTENING the chain or adding REDUNDANCY where a single element dominates Ōłæ╬╗_i (parallel buys only T┬ĘH_N), never on reading system reliability off the parts' individual quality." Grounding idea-engine@ceac42d ┬Ę pinned world above.

## VERDICT 124 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T03:01:00Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P111 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 111 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T02:41:53Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the matchmaking win-rate mirage: skill-based matchmaking (SBMM) pairs you against opponents of your own Elo rating, and the symmetric Elo update is a stochastic-approximation loop that drives your rating to the fixed point where your EXPECTED SCORE against the matched near-rating pool is Žā(0)=0.5 ŌĆö so at equilibrium EVERY player, regardless of true skill, has an expected win rate ~0.5 and the observed win rate is COMPRESSED almost flat across skill (skill is expressed as the equilibrium RATING, not as a win-rate surplus); a same-seed two-regime control isolates the cause ŌĆö run the SAME players with the SAME skills under RANDOM matching and their win rate becomes a near-perfect skill signal (top-vs-bottom skill-decile win-rate gap ~0.72, corr~0.98), but under SBMM the decile gap collapses to ~0.04 (~17├Ś compression) even though the identical games' RATING recovers true skill with corr~0.98 ŌĆö so it is the MATCHMAKING, not the skill model or the estimator, that turns win rate into a mirage; the skill information is not destroyed, it is relocated out of the win rate and into the rating (the residual corr(skill,SBMM-winrate)~0.5 is a finite-population tail edge effect ŌĆö extreme players match only inward ŌĆö so the headline is the compressed decile GAP and the corr CONTRAST, not a claim that win rate is literally uncorrelated with skill); the transferable correction is to rank players, tune matchmade bots, and A/B competitive agents by the RATING (or rating velocity toward equilibrium), read a flat win rate across skill as the SIGNATURE of correct matchmaking rather than proof skill is irrelevant, and publish the rating ŌĆö not the win rate ŌĆö as the skill-legible scoreboard.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/matchmaking-winrate-mirage-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, N_PLAYERS=200, GAMES=600, BURN_IN=400, RANDOM_GAMES=200, SKILL_SD=250.0, ELO_SCALE=400.0, K_FACTOR=16.0, R0=1500.0, MATCH_JITTER=15.0, TRIALS=200, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; thresholds GAP_MAX=0.12 (G1), RANDOM_GAP_MIN=0.50 + DELTA_MIN=0.40 (G2), RATING_CORR_MIN=0.90 + CONTRAST_MIN=0.30 (G3)), a stdlib per-replication SBMM Elo season (600 rounds of rating-nearest-neighbour matching with ┬▒15 Elo jitter, K=16 symmetric Elo update, win rate counted only after 400 burn-in rounds, final rating read at end) plus a same-players RANDOM-matching control (200 rounds of uniform-random pairings) over a Normal(0,250) true-skill population confirms the mirage: under SBMM the top-vs-bottom skill-decile win-rate gap collapses to 0.041946 while the SAME players' win rate under random matching keeps a 0.722953 decile gap (~17├Ś compression), yet the identical games' rating recovers true skill with corr 0.984387 (vs the SBMM win rate's corr 0.522507). All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 win-rate compression (headline): PASS ŌĆö the SBMM top-minus-bottom skill-decile mean win-rate GAP = 0.041946 (se 0.000131) Ōēż GAP_MAX=0.12 by z=596.5172Žā Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö the best and worst players win nearly the same fraction under matchmaking.
- G2 matchmaking-causes-it (control): PASS ŌĆö the RANDOM-matching decile win-rate gap = 0.722953 (se 0.001272) Ōēź RANDOM_GAP_MIN=0.50 by z_level=175.2561Žā AND exceeds the SBMM gap by gap_delta = 0.681006 (se 0.001274) Ōēź DELTA_MIN=0.40 by z_delta=220.6321Žā ŌĆö win rate DOES encode skill absent matchmaking (random-matching corr 0.982044), so the compression is caused by the pairing rule, not the skill model or estimator (the load-bearing same-seed control).
- G3 rating-retains-skill mirage: PASS ŌĆö the mean (corr(skill, final rating) ŌłÆ corr(skill, SBMM win rate)) = (0.984387 ŌłÆ 0.522507) = 0.46188 (se 0.005117) Ōēź CONTRAST_MIN=0.30 by z=31.6339Žā with corr_rating 0.984387 Ōēź RATING_CORR_MIN=0.90 ŌĆö the identical games' hidden rating keeps the skill signal the win rate loses.

## VERDICT 125 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T03:38:27Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P112 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 112 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T03:00:31Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the series-system MTBF collapse: a SERIES system works only while ALL of its N components work, so it fails at the FIRST component failure and its lifetime is the MINIMUM of the N per-component lifetimes; for independent exponential component lifetimes (rate ╬╗, per-component MTBF T=1/╬╗) the minimum of N is itself exponential with the SUMMED rate N╬╗ (hazard rates ADD: ╬╗_sys=Ōłæ╬╗_i=N╬╗), so the system MTBF collapses to exactly T/N ŌĆö N individually-"excellent" parts give a system N times MORE failure-prone than one part, and reliability R_sys(t)=r(t)^N decays GEOMETRICALLY in N; the symmetric fully-parallel/redundant system (fails only at the LAST component failure, lifetime = MAXIMUM of N) buys only LOGARITHMIC MTBF T┬ĘH_N (the N-th harmonic number), so at N=20 the series MTBF is T/N=0.05 while parallel is T┬ĘH_20Ōēł3.598 ŌĆö seriesing DIVIDES MTBF by 20 while 20-fold redundancy MULTIPLIES it by only ~3.6├Ś; a single component in isolation keeps the full MTBF T, so the collapse is 100% the seriesing of many parts, not a bad part.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/series-reliability-collapse-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, LAMBDA=1.0 ŌåÆ per-component MTBF T=1.0, N_SERIES=20, N_SYSTEMS=20000, TRIALS=400, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; threshold COLLAPSE_MAX=0.10 (G1); analytic anchors series MTBF T/N=0.05, parallel MTBF T┬ĘH_20=3.597740, R_sys(T)=e^{ŌłÆ20}=2.061154eŌłÆ09), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo over N_SYSTEMS=20000 series systems (each drawing N_SERIES=20 iid Exp(LAMBDA) component lifetimes and recording min / comps[0] / max), averaged over TRIALS=400 experiments with z on the estimated mean via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS, confirms the collapse: the measured series-system MTBF is 0.050032 (se 1.8e-05) ŌĆö a chain of twenty identical "good" parts has a system MTBF ~20├Ś under a single part's ŌĆö while a single component in isolation keeps the full MTBF (0.999848, se 0.000337, within 3Žā of T=1.0), and the measured series MTBF matches the exact closed-form anchor T/N=0.05 within 3Žā; the descriptive parallel/redundant MTBF is 3.597338 (se 0.000441) vs T┬ĘH_20=3.597740. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 series-collapse (headline): PASS ŌĆö the measured series-system MTBF = 0.050032 (se 1.8e-05) is at or below COLLAPSE_MAX=0.10 by z=2841.8816Žā Ōēź3Žā AND below the single-component MTBF 0.999848 ŌĆö a chain of twenty identical good parts has a system MTBF far under a single part's, purely from seriesing them.
- G2 single-component control: PASS ŌĆö the measured single-component MTBF = 0.999848 (se 0.000337) matches T=1.0 within 3Žā by |z|=0.4508Žā ŌĆö each component in isolation keeps the FULL MTBF, so G1's collapse is 100% the seriesing of N parts, not a bad/degraded component (the load-bearing control).
- G3 closed-form anchor MATCH: PASS ŌĆö the measured series MTBF 0.050032 matches the exact series anchor T/N=0.05 within 3Žā by |z|=1.8061Žā ŌĆö reproduces the min-of-exponentials / hazard-addition law (╬╗_sys=N┬Ę╬╗), so the effect IS the series-reliability mechanism itself.

NOTE the digest posture: P112 is the SELF-FIELD posture (like P104/P107/P108/P111, unlike P105/P109/P110's sibling-key posture) ŌĆö main() hashes json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) BEFORE inserting results["results_sha256"]=digest, so the disclosed digest is over the results dict with the self-field OMITTED and the pretty-printed on-disk series_reliability_collapse_results.json artifact's own sha256sum is NOT this digest; the field-omission step was applied on independent recompute (resolved against the script text, not assumed). REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114ŌĆōV124, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ŌĆö and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): a series system's reliability is the PRODUCT of its parts (R_sys=r^N) and, for exponential lifetimes, its MTBF is T/N not T ŌĆö so more "good" parts in series is a WORSE system; multiply the per-component reliabilities, expect the MTBF to divide by the series count, and spend the reliability budget on SHORTENING the chain or adding REDUNDANCY where a single element dominates Ōłæ╬╗_i (parallel buys only the logarithmic T┬ĘH_N ŌĆö 20-fold redundancy multiplies MTBF by only ~3.6├Ś), never on reading system reliability off the parts' individual quality.

## PROPOSAL 113 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T03:40:54Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 126, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/two-choices-routing-cliff-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-26 FLEET-domain opener (rotation reopens at fleet after round-25 completed at the unrelated slot P112; rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated). Domain: randomized load balancing / balanced-allocations theory ŌĆö the power-of-two-choices routing cliff, a fleet-domain head. The mechanism: a dispatcher routes each of m tasks to the least-loaded of d workers sampled uniformly at random from n workers (tiesŌåÆfirst sampled). With d=1 (pure random routing) the maximum queue length grows like ln n / ln ln n; with d=2 it collapses to ln ln n / ln 2 + O(1) ŌĆö a DOUBLE-EXPONENTIAL improvement in the tail (AzarŌĆōBroderŌĆōKarlinŌĆōUpfal, "Balanced Allocations", SIAM J. Comput. 1999; Mitzenmacher, IEEE TPDS 2001). Every extra probe past the second only divides the already-tiny ln ln n term by ln d, so the marginal gain collapses: the entire benefit of multiple choices is captured by the SECOND probe, and the diminishing returns are not gradual ŌĆö they fall off a cliff immediately after d=2. The trap: operators expect balance to improve smoothly with the probe count and over-provision d=4..8, paying 2ŌĆō4├Ś the coordination cost of d=2 for a sub-unit reduction in max queue. Claim under test: on the pinned world the first extra probe (1ŌåÆ2) strictly and significantly shortens the longest queue, the 1ŌåÆ2 gain significantly exceeds the 2ŌåÆ3 gain (the cliff), and the total saving from every probe past the second is under two queue slots (the plateau) ŌĆö so fleet dispatch should cap probe fan-out at d=2 and spend the saved budget on load-signal freshness. Pinned: SEED=20260717, N_WORKERS=20000, M_TASKS=20000 (m=n balls-into-bins regime), TRIALS=120, D_VALUES=(1,2,3,4), SIGMA_GATE=3.0; threshold PLATEAU_MAX=2.0 (G3). Analytic anchors ŌĆö d=1 max Ōēł ln n / ln ln n = 4.319222, d=2 max Ōēł ln ln n / ln 2 = 3.307937 (asymptotic order, not tight constants). APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 two-choices jump ŌĆö mean(max_1 ŌłÆ max_2) > 0 by Ōēź3Žā (the first extra probe strictly shortens the longest queue); G2 the cliff (front-loaded) ŌĆö mean((max_1ŌłÆmax_2) ŌłÆ (max_2ŌłÆmax_3)) > 0 by Ōēź3Žā (the 1ŌåÆ2 gain significantly exceeds the 2ŌåÆ3 gain); G3 the plateau beyond two ŌĆö mean(max_2 ŌłÆ max_4) < PLATEAU_MAX=2.0 by Ōēź3Žā (every probe past the second together saves under two queue slots). Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109/P110/P111/P112 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/two_choices_routing_cliff.py (random,math,json,hashlib; per-trial Monte-Carlo of m=n balls-into-bins placements across d Ōłł {1,2,3,4}, recording the max load ŌĆö no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): mean max load by d = {1: 7.016667, 2: 3.083333, 3: 3.0, 4: 2.466667}; G1 mean(max_1ŌłÆmax_2) 3.933333 (se 0.068735) z=57.2243Žā (>0) PASS, G2 cliff mean 3.85 (se 0.084225) z=45.7109Žā (>0) PASS, G3 plateau mean(max_2ŌłÆmax_4) 0.616667 (se 0.053168) z=26.0183Žā (< 2.0) PASS ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=068e23f599c9ccf9a4bcbf31d705a9760ee05091172f6432ea1c3df69d62b685.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 126 is mirrored back.

Offset pinned ŌĆö the +13 offset's row for P113 (immediate predecessor row P112 ŌåÆ V125, "sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 112 ŌåÆ VERDICT 125). Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V126 for P113; then round-26 venture-slot P114"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P113/V126 offset line ŌĆö it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). Lane CONSUMER ŌĆö fleet-manager routing (Q-0264): the router should sample TWO candidate workers and take the less-loaded ŌĆö never one, rarely more; verdict co-consumer = any dispatch/scheduling/sharding surface choosing a probe fan-out (a task router, a connection-pool balancer, a cache/shard placement, a hash-with-choices scheme) ŌĆö the transferable correction is "sampling two candidates and taking the best is a double-exponential improvement over one, and a third buys only a vanishing constant, so cap probe fan-out at 2 and reinvest the saved coordination budget in load-signal freshness, never in more probes." ## PROPOSAL 114 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T04:01:41Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 127, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/freemium-support-cost-inversion-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-26 VENTURE-domain slot (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P113 opened round-26 at the fleet slot, this takes the venture slot). Domain: freemium / product-led-growth unit economics ŌĆö the freemium support-cost inversion, a venture-domain head. The mechanism: every free user carries a fixed support/infra cost c and converts to paid worth V with probability p, so per-free-user contribution is V┬Ęp ŌłÆ c. Free users are acquired in intent order (highest-intent first ŌĆö they sought the product out), so the i-th user's conversion probability p(i)=P0┬Ęe^{ŌłÆK┬Ęi} DECLINES with acquisition volume. Marginal contribution V┬ĘP0┬Ęe^{ŌłÆK┬Ęi} ŌłÆ c is therefore decreasing and crosses zero at n*=ln(V┬ĘP0/c)/K, so TOTAL contribution C(n)=╬Ż(V┬Ę1{convert_i} ŌłÆ c) is CONCAVE: it rises to a peak at n*, then falls, and goes NEGATIVE once the accumulated serving cost of low-intent users outweighs their thin conversion value (conversion value V┬ĘS(n) saturates as the intent pool exhausts while cost c┬Ęn is linear). The trap: growth teams optimize signups and total conversions ŌĆö both monotonically increasing in acquisition ŌĆö and run the free tier as a near-costless flywheel, so they scale past the contribution optimum n* and past the zero-crossing, turning a value-creating program net-negative while every headline metric still rises. Claim under test: on the pinned world, over-scaling from the optimum N_OPT to the signup-maximizing volume N_MAX strictly and significantly DESTROYS contribution (the program flips from +C(N_OPT) to a NEGATIVE C(N_MAX)); on a FLAT top-intent control the same over-scaling RAISES contribution (isolating the declining conversion as the cause); and the simulated contributions match the closed form C(n)=V┬ĘS(n)ŌłÆc┬Ęn at both volumes ŌĆö so freemium free-tier acquisition should be capped at the contribution optimum n*=ln(V┬ĘP0/c)/K and budget spent on intent QUALITY, not raw signup volume. Pinned: SEED=20260717, TRIALS=400, SIGMA=3.0; P0=0.10 (top-intent conversion prob), K=0.001 (intent decay/user), V=100.0 (paid LTV), c=2.0 (support cost/free user), N_MAX=6000 (signup-maximizing volume), N_OPT=ŌīŖn*Ōīŗ=1609 (contribution optimum), DROP_MIN=1000.0 (G1 floor). Analytic anchors ŌĆö breakeven conversion prob c/V=0.020000, n*=ln(V┬ĘP0/c)/K=ln(5)/0.001=1609.437912, S(N_MAX)=99.702257, C(N_OPT)=V┬ĘS(1609)ŌłÆc┬Ę1609=4777.125088, C(N_MAX)=V┬ĘS(6000)ŌłÆc┬Ę6000=ŌłÆ2029.774297 (NEGATIVE ŌĆö value destroyed at the signup-maximizing volume). APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 contribution inversion ŌĆö mean(C(N_OPT) ŌłÆ C(N_MAX)) on declining intent Ōēź DROP_MIN=1000 by Ōēź3Žā (over-scaling past the optimum strictly destroys contribution); G2 declining-intent necessity control ŌĆö on a FLAT top-intent world (pŌēĪP0, marginal V┬ĘP0ŌłÆc>0 for all i) mean(C(N_MAX) ŌłÆ C(N_OPT)) > 0 by Ōēź3Žā (the same over-scaling RAISES contribution, so the inversion is the declining conversion, not serving cost per se); G3 closed-form anchor MATCH ŌĆö mean simulated C(n) reproduces V┬ĘS(n)ŌłÆc┬Ęn at both N_OPT and N_MAX within 3Žā (|z|<3). Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109/P110/P111/P112/P113 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/venture-lab/freemium_support_cost_inversion.py (random,math,json,hashlib; per-trial per-user-Bernoulli Monte Carlo of free users iŌłł{1..N_MAX} under declining and flat intent, recording the running contribution at N_OPT and N_MAX ŌĆö no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): C(N_OPT)=4777.75, C(N_MAX)=ŌłÆ2009.0; G1 mean drop 6786.75 (se 21.316474) z=271.4684Žā (Ōēź1000) PASS, G2 flat-control gain mean 35038.5 (se 94.849568) z=369.4113Žā (>0) PASS, G3 |z|=0.0140 (C at N_OPT vs 4777.125088) / 0.4226 (C at N_MAX vs ŌłÆ2029.774297), both <3 PASS ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=884242e6ed6d9b7cf8affef7400526985e8244b4b3541319f33653dce6639b38.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 127 is mirrored back.

Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V127 for P114; then round-26 game-slot P115"). SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). Lane CONSUMER ŌĆö venture/GTM economics (Q-0264): cap paid free-tier acquisition at the contribution optimum n*=ln(V┬ĘP0/c)/K and admit a marginal free cohort only while its expected conversion clears the serving-cost breakeven pŌēźc/V; verdict co-consumer = any volume-scaled program with a per-unit carrying cost and a declining per-unit yield (a freemium/PLG free tier, a trial-to-paid funnel, a lead-gen top-of-funnel, an ad-supported free tier with serving cost) ŌĆö the transferable correction is "per-unit contribution is yield┬Ęvalue ŌłÆ cost and yield declines with volume, so cumulative contribution is concave and eventually negative; meter acquisition against the contribution optimum, not against signups, and spend on yield QUALITY not raw volume." ## PROPOSAL 115 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T04:23:26Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 128, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/compounding-reward-inequality-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-26 GAME-domain slot (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P113 opened round-26 at the fleet slot, P114 took the venture slot, this takes the game slot). Domain: in-game economy / reward-design ŌĆö the compounding-reward inequality engine, a game-domain head grounded in multiplicative stochastic wealth dynamics (Gibrat 1931). The mechanism: give every player the SAME fair daily percentage bonus ŌĆö each day wealth changes by an iid factor exp(g), g~Normal(MU_G,SIGMA_G) with a small positive average, NO skill, NO choice, NO edge ŌĆö and let it COMPOUND on the running total, so terminal wealth W=W0┬Ęexp(S) with S=╬Ż_d g_d ~ Normal(DAYS┬ĘMU_G, DAYS┬ĘSIGMA_G┬▓) (exact sum-of-independent-normals) is LOG-NORMAL with terminal log-sd Žā_T=SIGMA_G┬ĘŌłÜDAYS. Concentration is scale-free in Žā_T alone (Gini=2┬Ę╬”(Žā_T/ŌłÜ2)ŌłÆ1, top-p share=╬”(Žā_TŌłÆ╬”Ōü╗┬╣(1ŌłÆp)), median/mean=exp(ŌłÆŽā_T┬▓/2)) and grows without bound with the horizon: identical, skill-less, choice-less players end up wildly unequal ŌĆö the top decile owns ~55%, the Gini ~0.68, the median player at ~37% of the average ŌĆö purely because a lucky early factor is multiplied by every later one (the running total carries memory). The trap: designers reach for percentage/compounding rewards (interest mechanics, multiplicative loot/XP multipliers, "+X% of holdings" logins, idle-prestige multipliers) because they FEEL fair and self-balancing, and read a widening wealth gap as skill ŌĆö but the gap is manufactured by the multiplicative structure regardless of skill. Claim under test: on the pinned world the COMPOUND top-decile wealth share is large (Ōēź0.40 by Ōēź3Žā), a SAME-SHOCKS ADDITIVE control (the identical S paid on the starting stake, W=W0┬Ę(1+S), no compounding) collapses the concentration to the fair share (additive top-decile Ōēż0.20 AND the compoundŌłÆadditive gap Ōēź0.25, both by Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö isolating compounding, not luck, as the cause), and the simulated COMPOUND Gini and top-decile share reproduce the log-normal closed forms within 3Žā ŌĆö so an egalitarian/bounded economy needs a non-compounding (fixed-base/additive) reward, a capped compounding horizon, or a periodic rebase, never an uncapped compounding bonus. Pinned: SEED=20260717, N_PLAYERS=5000, DAYS=200, W0=100.0, MU_G=0.02, SIGMA_G=0.10, TRIALS=200, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, TOP_FRAC=0.10; thresholds SHARE_MIN=0.40 (G1), ADD_SHARE_MAX=0.20 + DELTA_MIN=0.25 (G2). Closed-form anchors ŌĆö Žā_T=SIGMA_G┬ĘŌłÜDAYS=1.414214, Gini=2┬Ę╬”(Žā_T/ŌłÜ2)ŌłÆ1=0.682689, top-decile share=╬”(Žā_TŌłÆ╬”Ōü╗┬╣(0.90))=0.552770, median/mean=exp(ŌłÆŽā_T┬▓/2)=0.367879. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 concentration ŌĆö COMPOUND top-decile wealth share Ōēź SHARE_MIN=0.40 by Ōēź3Žā (identical players, fair rewards, yet the top 10% own most of the wealth); G2 compounding-causes-it control ŌĆö ADDITIVE top-decile share Ōēż ADD_SHARE_MAX=0.20 AND the (COMPOUNDŌłÆADDITIVE) top-decile share gap Ōēź DELTA_MIN=0.25, both by Ōēź3Žā (freeze the compounding, keep the same shocks, and the concentration collapses to the fair share); G3 closed-form anchor MATCH ŌĆö mean COMPOUND Gini reproduces 2┬Ę╬”(Žā_T/ŌłÜ2)ŌłÆ1 AND mean COMPOUND top-decile share reproduces ╬”(Žā_TŌłÆ╬”Ōü╗┬╣(0.90)) within 3Žā (|z|<3 each). Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109/P110/P111/P112/P113/P114 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/superbot-games/compounding_reward_inequality.py (random,math,json,hashlib; per-player exact sum-of-normals draw of the cumulative daily log-return, COMPOUND W=W0┬Ęexp(S) vs same-shocks ADDITIVE W=max(0,W0┬Ę(1+S)), Gini + top-decile share over TRIALS replications ŌĆö no numpy/scipy; ╬” via math.erf, ╬”Ōü╗┬╣ via Acklam only for the closed-form anchor). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): COMPOUND Gini 0.683305 (se 0.000657), COMPOUND top-decile share 0.553674 (se 0.000955), ADDITIVE top-decile share 0.149729 (se 0.000052), gap 0.403945 (se 0.000916); G1 top-decile share 0.553674 Ōēź 0.40 z=160.8932Žā PASS, G2 additive 0.149729 Ōēż 0.20 z_additive=964.4786Žā AND gap 0.403945 Ōēź 0.25 z_gap=168.0571Žā PASS, G3 Gini 0.683305 vs 0.682689 z=0.9362 / top-decile share 0.553674 vs 0.552770 z=0.9466 both |z|<3 PASS ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=a9dbb30f38b77e342f03e9ced2af204d95aef624902ecd845d107b99c9140b1e.

## VERDICT 127 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T04:33:35Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P114 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 114 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T04:01:41Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the freemium support-cost inversion: every free user carries a fixed support/infra cost c and converts to paid worth V with probability p, so per-free-user contribution is V┬Ęp ŌłÆ c; free users are acquired in intent order (highest-intent first ŌĆö they sought the product out) so the i-th user's conversion probability p(i)=P0┬Ęe^{ŌłÆK┬Ęi} DECLINES with acquisition volume, marginal contribution V┬ĘP0┬Ęe^{ŌłÆK┬Ęi} ŌłÆ c is decreasing and crosses zero at n*=ln(V┬ĘP0/c)/K, so TOTAL contribution C(n)=╬Ż(V┬Ę1{convert_i} ŌłÆ c) is CONCAVE ŌĆö it rises to a peak at n*, then falls, and goes NEGATIVE once accumulated serving cost of low-intent users outweighs their thin conversion value (V┬ĘS(n) saturates as the intent pool exhausts while c┬Ęn is linear); the trap is that growth teams optimize signups and total conversions (both monotone in acquisition) and scale past n* into value destruction while every headline metric still rises ŌĆö cap free-tier acquisition at the contribution optimum n*=ln(V┬ĘP0/c)/K and admit a marginal free cohort only while its expected conversion clears the serving-cost breakeven pŌēźc/V. Round-26 VENTURE-slot proposal (freemium/PLG unit economics, Q-0264 co-consumer = any volume-scaled program with a per-unit carrying cost and a declining per-unit yield ŌĆö a trial-to-paid funnel, a lead-gen top-of-funnel, an ad-supported free tier with serving cost).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/freemium-support-cost-inversion-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, TRIALS=400, SIGMA=3.0; P0=0.10, K=0.001, V=100.0, c=2.0, N_MAX=6000, N_OPT=ŌīŖn*Ōīŗ=1609, DROP_MIN=1000.0 (G1); analytic anchors breakeven conversion prob c/V=0.020000, n*=ln(V┬ĘP0/c)/K=ln(5)/0.001=1609.437912, S(N_MAX)=99.702257, C(N_OPT)=V┬ĘS(1609)ŌłÆc┬Ę1609=4777.125088, C(N_MAX)=V┬ĘS(6000)ŌłÆc┬Ę6000=ŌłÆ2029.774297 NEGATIVE ŌĆö value destroyed at the signup-maximizing volume), a stdlib per-trial per-user-Bernoulli Monte-Carlo over free users iŌłł{1..N_MAX} under declining and flat intent (recording the running contribution at N_OPT and N_MAX), averaged over TRIALS=400 with z on the estimated mean via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS, confirms the inversion: over-scaling from the contribution optimum N_OPT=1609 to the signup-maximizing volume N_MAX=6000 on DECLINING intent flips the program from +C(N_OPT)=4777.75 (se 44.770563) to a NEGATIVE C(N_MAX)=ŌłÆ2009.0 (se 49.161363) ŌĆö a mean contribution drop of 6786.75 destroyed by chasing signups ŌĆö while on a FLAT top-intent control (pŌēĪP0, marginal V┬ĘP0ŌłÆc>0 for all i) the same over-scaling RAISES contribution by 35038.5, and the simulated C(n) reproduces the closed form V┬ĘS(n)ŌłÆc┬Ęn at both volumes (|z|=0.014 at N_OPT vs 4777.125088, |z|=0.4226 at N_MAX vs ŌłÆ2029.774297). All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin, never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 contribution inversion (headline): PASS ŌĆö mean(C(N_OPT) ŌłÆ C(N_MAX)) on declining intent = 6786.75 (se 21.316474) is Ōēź DROP_MIN=1000 by z=271.4684Žā Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö over-scaling past the optimum strictly and significantly destroys contribution, and C(N_MAX)=ŌłÆ2009.0 is NEGATIVE (value destroyed) even as signups and total conversions keep rising.
- G2 declining-intent necessity control: PASS ŌĆö on a FLAT top-intent world the same over-scaling from N_OPT to N_MAX RAISES contribution: mean(C(N_MAX) ŌłÆ C(N_OPT)) = 35038.5 (se 94.849568) > 0 by z=369.4113Žā Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö so the inversion is the DECLINING conversion (yield falling with volume), not serving cost per se (the load-bearing control).
- G3 closed-form anchor MATCH: PASS ŌĆö mean simulated C(n) reproduces V┬ĘS(n)ŌłÆc┬Ęn at both volumes within 3Žā: |z|=0.014 at N_OPT (sim 4777.75 vs anchor C(N_OPT)=4777.125088) and |z|=0.4226 at N_MAX (sim ŌłÆ2009.0 vs anchor C(N_MAX)=ŌłÆ2029.774297 NEGATIVE) ŌĆö reproduces the concave-contribution / declining-yield mechanism itself, with the peak at the serving-cost breakeven p=c/V=0.02 (n*=1609.437912).

The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = 884242e6ed6d9b7cf8affef7400526985e8244b4b3541319f33653dce6639b38 matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P114 is the SIBLING / no-self-field posture (like P105/P109/P110, and UNLIKE P104/P107/P108/P111/P112's self-field posture) ŌĆö main() computes canonical = json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) and digest = sha256(canonical) and PRINTS the digest as a sibling in stdout, NEVER inserting a results_sha256 field back into results, so NO field-omission step is needed and the disclosed digest is over the compact canonical results dict as-is; the pretty-printed on-disk freemium_support_cost_inversion_results.json artifact's own sha256sum (87171499078ae9dbad0a967bf03236772926053d0edde14447b69e24679f4bc4) is NOT this digest. All three gate sigmas match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=271.4684Žā, G2 z=369.4113Žā, G3 |z|=0.014 / 0.4226); means match (drop 6786.75, flat gain 35038.5, C_opt 4777.75, C_max ŌłÆ2009.0, n*=1609.437912, breakeven c/V=0.02); verifier exits 0, all_pass=true. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114ŌĆōV125, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ŌĆö and it landed to the digit.

## PROPOSAL 116 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T04:46:21Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 129, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/arcsine-lead-illusion-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-26 UNRELATED-domain closer (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P113 opened round-26 at the fleet slot, P114 took the venture slot, P115 the game slot, this is the unrelated slot ŌĆö CLOSING round-26's proposal rotation). Domain: probability theory / stochastic processes ŌĆö the L├®vyŌĆōFeller first arcsine law of the lead in a FAIR game, a fleet-external pure-mechanism head deliberately DISTINCT from the prior unrelated closers P100 (Kelly overbet / information theory), P104 (epidemic overshoot / epidemiology), P108 (regression to the mean / statistics), P112 (series-system MTBF collapse / reliability engineering). The mechanism: play a perfectly FAIR contest as an unbiased ┬▒1 random walk (each step a fair coin, zero edge, zero drift) over N_STEPS=2n steps, and measure the fraction of the game one side spends IN THE LEAD (above even). A fair walk is STICKY about its sign (null-recurrent ŌĆö returns to zero with probability 1 but the expected inter-return time is infinite), so long one-sided excursions dominate, and the fraction of time in the lead does NOT concentrate near 1/2 ŌĆö it follows the U-shaped ARCSINE distribution (density 1/(ŽĆŌłÜ(x(1ŌłÆx))), piling up at 0 and 1, minimized at 1/2). Exact discrete arcsine law (Feller Vol.1 ┬¦III.4): labeling segment i above the axis iff S_{iŌłÆ1}+S_i>0 (the sum is odd, never 0), the count T of above-axis segments is even with P(T=2k)=u_{2k}┬Ęu_{2(nŌłÆk)}, u_{2j}=C(2j,j)/4^j ŌĆö a product of two return probabilities, maximal at the endpoints, minimal in the middle; its nŌåÆŌł× limit is P(fractionŌēżx)ŌåÆ(2/ŽĆ)arcsinŌłÜx. Claim under test: on the pinned world the measured extreme-lead probability (one side ahead for Ōēź90% or Ōēż10% of the play) is large (Ōēź0.30 by Ōēź3Žā, exact anchor 0.415569), the mean lead-fraction stays a fair 0.5 (within 3Žā ŌĆö the walk is genuinely unbiased, so the concentration is the arcsine U-shape not a drift) while the balanced band (each ahead 40ŌĆō60% of the time, a close see-saw) is RARE (Ōēż0.20 AND the extremeŌłÆbalanced gap Ōēź0.15, both by Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö the extremes strictly beat the middle, inverting the naive "concentrates at 1/2" belief), and the measured extreme-lead and lower-tail probabilities reproduce the EXACT discrete-arcsine anchors within 3Žā ŌĆö so a fair game is fair in the MEAN, not level in the LEAD, and a long one-sided lead is the SIGNATURE of fairness, not proof of an edge. Pinned: SEED=20260717, N_STEPS=500 (2n; n=250), N_WALKS=4000, TRIALS=200, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, LEAD_LO=0.10, LEAD_HI=0.90, BAL_LO=0.40, BAL_HI=0.60; thresholds EXT_MIN=0.30 (G1), BAL_MAX=0.20 + GAP_MIN=0.15 (G2). Exact discrete-arcsine anchors (n=250): extreme-lead P(fŌēż0.10 or Ōēź0.90)=0.415569, lower-tail P(fŌēż0.10)=0.207784, balanced P(0.40ŌēżfŌēż0.60)=0.130523, mean=0.500000; continuous-limit extreme (descriptive) 2┬Ę(2/ŽĆ)arcsinŌłÜ0.1=0.409666. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 extreme-lead headline ŌĆö the measured extreme-lead probability Ōēź EXT_MIN=0.30 by Ōēź3Žā (in a fair game one side is ahead almost the whole time far more often than the naive 50/50 picture allows); G2 fair-mean + middle-is-rare control ŌĆö (a) the mean lead-fraction within 3Žā of 0.5 (|z|<3, the walk is genuinely unbiased so G1 is the arcsine shape not a drift) AND (b) the balanced probability Ōēż BAL_MAX=0.20 AND the (extremeŌłÆbalanced) gap Ōēź GAP_MIN=0.15, both by Ōēź3Žā (the extremes strictly beat the middle); G3 closed-form anchor MATCH ŌĆö the measured extreme-lead probability reproduces the exact discrete-arcsine anchor 0.415569 within 3Žā AND the measured lower-tail reproduces 0.207784 within 3Žā (|z|<3 each), so the effect IS the L├®vyŌĆōFeller arcsine law. Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109/P110/P111/P112/P113/P114/P115 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/arcsine_lead_illusion.py (random,math,json,hashlib; per-trial Monte-Carlo of N_WALKS symmetric ┬▒1 walks of N_STEPS steps, counting above-axis segments S_{iŌłÆ1}+S_i>0 to form the lead-fraction; exact discrete-arcsine anchors via math.comb, the continuous (2/ŽĆ)arcsinŌłÜx reported descriptively only ŌĆö no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): mean lead-fraction 0.500315 (se 0.000416), extreme-lead 0.415618 (se 0.000512), lower-tail 0.207800 (se 0.000439), balanced 0.130543 (se 0.000353), gap 0.285075 (se 0.000622); G1 extreme 0.415618 Ōēź 0.30 z=225.9288Žā PASS, G2 mean 0.500315 vs 0.5 |z|=0.7575 AND balanced 0.130543 Ōēż 0.20 z_balanced=196.6076Žā AND gap 0.285075 Ōēź 0.15 z_gap=217.2175Žā PASS, G3 extreme 0.415618 vs 0.415569 |z|=0.0953 / lower-tail 0.207800 vs 0.207784 |z|=0.0356 both |z|<3 PASS ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=4865158a0181815ab420997a10f267f2315f22813696b7d8409b1c2bd16a7b52.

NO lane CONSUMER ŌĆö probability-theory pure-mechanism head (the L├®vyŌĆōFeller first arcsine law / random-walk sojourn time); verdict co-consumer = any close, roughly-fair, back-and-forth process whose "who has been ahead / for how long" is read as an edge signal (a tied competitive match, a near-parity A/B test, two comparable trading strategies or similar-skill agents, a cumulative-score race, a running vote margin) ŌĆö the transferable correction is "the fraction of time one side leads a driftless fair contest is arcsine-distributed (U-shaped: extremes ~0.42 combined, a near-even see-saw only ~0.13), so a long one-sided lead is the EXPECTED signature of fairness, not proof of superiority; measure the edge from the DRIFT (net margin per step / terminal gap ┬▒ se), never from how long or how lopsidedly one side has led, and budget stopping rules and confidence to the arcsine tails rather than to a naive 'it should hover near 50/50'." Grounding idea-engine@eb76189 ┬Ę https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Arcsine_laws_(Wiener_process) (verified reachable 2026-07-18) ┬Ę pinned world above.

## VERDICT 128 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T04:52:21Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P115 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 115 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T04:23:26Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the compounding-reward inequality engine: an in-game economy gives every player the SAME fair daily percentage bonus ŌĆö each day wealth changes by an iid factor exp(g), g~Normal(MU_G,SIGMA_G) with a small positive average, NO skill/choice/edge ŌĆö and lets it COMPOUND on the running total, so terminal wealth W=W0┬Ęexp(S) with S=╬Ż_d g_d ~ Normal(DAYS┬ĘMU_G, DAYS┬ĘSIGMA_G┬▓) (exact sum-of-independent-normals) is LOG-NORMAL with terminal log-sd Žā_T=SIGMA_G┬ĘŌłÜDAYS; concentration is scale-free in Žā_T alone (Gini=2┬Ę╬”(Žā_T/ŌłÜ2)ŌłÆ1, top-p share=╬”(Žā_TŌłÆ╬”Ōü╗┬╣(1ŌłÆp)), median/mean=exp(ŌłÆŽā_T┬▓/2)) and grows without bound with the horizon, so IDENTICAL, skill-less, choice-less players end up wildly unequal ŌĆö the top decile owns ~55%, the Gini ~0.68, the median player at ~37% of the average ŌĆö purely because a lucky early factor is multiplied by every later one (the running total carries memory); the trap is that designers reach for percentage/compounding rewards (interest mechanics, multiplicative loot/XP multipliers, "+X% of holdings" logins, idle-prestige multipliers) because they FEEL fair and self-balancing and read a widening wealth gap as skill, but the gap is manufactured by the multiplicative structure regardless of skill ŌĆö isolate it with a SAME-SHOCKS ADDITIVE control W=W0┬Ę(1+S) (the identical S paid on the starting stake, no compounding) at which the concentration collapses to the fair share (~0.15), so an egalitarian/bounded economy needs a non-compounding (fixed-base/additive) reward, a capped compounding horizon, or a periodic rebase, never an uncapped compounding bonus. Round-26 GAME-slot proposal (in-game economy / reward-design ┬Ę multiplicative stochastic wealth dynamics, Gibrat 1931; Q-0264 co-consumer = any volume/horizon-scaled process that COMPOUNDS an iid multiplicative reward across a population ŌĆö a game currency with interest/multipliers, an idle-prestige multiplier, a staking yield, any "% of holdings" mechanic).

ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, N_PLAYERS=5000, DAYS=200, W0=100.0, MU_G=0.02, SIGMA_G=0.10, TRIALS=200, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, TOP_FRAC=0.10; thresholds SHARE_MIN=0.40 (G1), ADD_SHARE_MAX=0.20 + DELTA_MIN=0.25 (G2); closed-form anchors Žā_T=SIGMA_G┬ĘŌłÜDAYS=1.414214, Gini=2┬Ę╬”(Žā_T/ŌłÜ2)ŌłÆ1=0.682689, top-decile share=╬”(Žā_TŌłÆ╬”Ōü╗┬╣(0.90))=0.552770, median/mean=exp(ŌłÆŽā_T┬▓/2)=0.367879), a stdlib per-player exact sum-of-normals draw of the cumulative daily log-return S (DAYS iid Normal(MU_G,SIGMA_G)), read under the COMPOUND regime W=W0┬Ęexp(S) and the SAME-SHOCKS ADDITIVE control W=max(0,W0┬Ę(1+S)), with the COMPOUND Gini + top-decile share and the ADDITIVE top-decile share averaged over TRIALS=200 replications and z on the estimated mean via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS, confirms the concentration: identical, skill-less players under the same fair daily percentage bonus end log-normal and wildly unequal ŌĆö the COMPOUND top-decile wealth share is 0.553674 (se 0.000955, ~55% owned by the top 10%), the COMPOUND Gini is 0.683305, the median player at ~37% of the mean (median/mean=exp(ŌłÆŽā_T┬▓/2)=0.367879) ŌĆö while the SAME-SHOCKS ADDITIVE control collapses the top-decile share to the fair 0.149729 (se 5.2e-05), and the simulated COMPOUND Gini and top-decile share reproduce the log-normal closed forms within 3Žā. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 concentration (headline): PASS ŌĆö the COMPOUND top-decile wealth share = 0.553674 (se 0.000955) is Ōēź SHARE_MIN=0.40 by z=160.8932Žā Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö identical, skill-less, choice-less players given the SAME fair daily percentage bonus, yet the top 10% own most of the wealth, purely from multiplying a lucky early factor by every later one.
- G2 compounding-causes-it control: PASS ŌĆö the SAME-SHOCKS ADDITIVE top-decile share = 0.149729 (se 5.2e-05) is Ōēż ADD_SHARE_MAX=0.20 by z_additive=964.4786Žā AND the (COMPOUNDŌłÆADDITIVE) top-decile share gap = 0.403945 (se 0.000916) is Ōēź DELTA_MIN=0.25 by z_gap=168.0571Žā, both Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö freeze the compounding, keep the same shocks, and the concentration collapses to the fair share, so it is the COMPOUNDING, not luck (the load-bearing control).
- G3 closed-form anchor MATCH: PASS ŌĆö the mean COMPOUND Gini 0.683305 matches 2┬Ę╬”(Žā_T/ŌłÜ2)ŌłÆ1=0.682689 within 3Žā by |z|=0.9362 AND the mean COMPOUND top-decile share 0.553674 matches ╬”(Žā_TŌłÆ╬”Ōü╗┬╣(0.90))=0.552770 within 3Žā by |z|=0.9466 ŌĆö reproduces the log-normal closed forms (Žā_T=1.414214), so the effect IS the compounding-inequality mechanism itself.

Design correction (transferable): compounding an iid multiplicative reward makes wealth log-normal and concentrates it (GiniŌåÆ1, median/meanŌåÆ0 with the horizon) among IDENTICAL players regardless of skill, while the same shocks paid additively stay fair ŌĆö so read a widening spread under compounding as the reward structure, not skill, and pay a "fair" percentage reward on a FIXED base (additive), cap the compounding horizon, or rebase/normalize periodically, never an uncapped compounding bonus if the goal is an egalitarian or bounded-spread economy.

## PROPOSAL 117 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T05:07:26Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 130, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/checkpoint-interval-optimum-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-27 FLEET-domain opener (rotation reopens at fleet after round-26 completed at the unrelated slot P116; rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated ŌĆö OPENING round-27's proposal rotation). Domain: fault-tolerant computing / long-running-job checkpoint scheduling ŌĆö the YoungŌĆōDaly optimal checkpoint interval, a fleet-domain pure-mechanism head deliberately DISTINCT from the prior fleet heads P113 (two-choices routing cliff / balanced-allocations max load), P109 (correlated-fleet variance floor / error aggregation), P105 (delegation loop collapse), P101 (winner's-curse auction), and the metastable-retry-storm-collapse head (timeout-retry load feedback). The mechanism: a long fleet job that can crash and restart from its last checkpoint faces TWO competing wastes ŌĆö checkpoint OVERHEAD (~C/Žä per unit useful work: each checkpoint costs C, taken every Žä of work) and lost RECOMPUTE work on a crash (~Žä/(2M) per unit work: a Poisson crash at rate 1/M lands on average halfway through the current interval, discarding the work since the last checkpoint). Their sum C/Žä + Žä/(2M) is a CONVEX falling+rising pair, so total wasted wall time is U-SHAPED in the checkpoint interval Žä with a unique INTERIOR optimum where the two wastes are equal: Žä* = ŌłÜ(2┬ĘC┬ĘM) (Young 1974 first-order), overhead floor ŌłÜ(2C/M) (Daly 2006 exact). Three consequences the "checkpoint as often as you can afford" instinct misses: (1) the optimum is INTERIOR ŌĆö checkpointing more often than Žä* walks UP the left arm of the U (the C/Žä term diverges as ŽäŌåÆ0), so over-checkpointing is not "safer", it is wasteful; (2) the two failure modes are SYMMETRIC in leading order ŌĆö Žä*┬Ęk and Žä*/k inflate the total overhead by the same factor ┬Į(k+1/k); (3) the optimum scales only as ŌłÜM ŌĆö a 4├Ś more reliable machine (MŌåÆ4M) warrants an interval only 2├Ś longer and HALVES the achievable overhead (ŌłÜ(2C/M)ŌåÆ┬ĮŌłÜ(2C/M)). Claim under test: on the pinned world the overhead at the optimum Žä*=20 is small (~10.7%), checkpointing 8├Ś too often (Žä=2.5) wastes strictly more (frequent penalty Ōēź0.05 by Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö inverting "more is safer"), checkpointing 8├Ś too rarely (Žä=160) ALSO wastes strictly more (rare penalty Ōēź0.05 by Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö the U-shape's second arm, so the optimum is genuinely interior), quadrupling the MTBF at the rescaled optimum Žä*(4M)=2Žä*=40 halves the optimum overhead (drop Ōēź0.02 by Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö the ŌłÜ-law), and the three measured overheads reproduce the EXACT Daly closed form within 3Žā ŌĆö so the checkpoint interval is a U-shaped OPTIMISATION with a ŌłÜ(2CM) optimum and a ŌłÜ(2C/M) floor, not a one-sided safety dial. Pinned: SEED=20260717, C=1.0, M=200.0, W=40000.0, K=8, TRIALS=200, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, DELTA_MIN=0.05 (G1/G2a), HALVE_MIN=0.02 (G2b); intervals Žä*=ŌłÜ(2CM)=20.0, frequent Žä*/K=2.5, rare Žä*┬ĘK=160.0, ŌłÜ-law control at M2=4M=800 with Žä*(4M)=ŌłÜ(2┬ĘC┬Ę4M)=40.0. Exact Daly anchors O(Žä)=(M/Žä)(exp((Žä+C)/M)ŌłÆ1)ŌłÆ1: O(20)=0.107106, O(2.5)=0.412322, O(160)=0.545871, O(40 at M=800)=0.051720; Young first-order (descriptive): ŌłÜ(2C/M)=0.10 at M=200, ŌłÜ(2C/4M)=0.05 at M=800. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 over-checkpointing headline ŌĆö the frequent penalty overhead(Žä*/K)ŌłÆoverhead(Žä*) Ōēź DELTA_MIN=0.05 by Ōēź3Žā (checkpointing 8├Ś too often wastes strictly MORE than the optimum, inverting "checkpoint as often as possible is safest"); G2 U-shape + ŌłÜ-law control ŌĆö (a) the rare penalty overhead(Žä*┬ĘK)ŌłÆoverhead(Žä*) Ōēź DELTA_MIN=0.05 by Ōēź3Žā (checkpointing 8├Ś too rarely ALSO wastes more, so the waste is U-shaped with an INTERIOR optimum, neither extreme wins) AND (b) quadrupling the MTBF at the rescaled optimum drops the optimum overhead overhead(Žä*,M)ŌłÆoverhead(Žä*(4M),4M) Ōēź HALVE_MIN=0.02 by Ōēź3Žā (the overhead floor falls as ŌłÜ(2C/M), roughly halving ŌĆö the ŌłÜ law sets the optimum); G3 closed-form anchor MATCH ŌĆö the three measured overheads reproduce the exact Daly law within 3Žā each (optimum vs 0.107106, frequent vs 0.412322, rare vs 0.545871, |z|<3), so the effect IS the checkpoint-restart overhead law and the Young optimum Žä*=ŌłÜ(2CM) with floor ŌłÜ(2C/M) sit at the measured minimum. Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109/P110/P111/P112/P113/P114/P115/P116 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/checkpoint_interval_optimum.py (random,math,json,hashlib; per-run segment-by-segment Monte-Carlo of a W=40000 checkpoint/restart job at four intervals under Poisson(1/M) failures ŌĆö fresh expovariate draws, a crash costs the elapsed time and loses the segment, retry from the last checkpoint ŌĆö measuring the overhead fraction wall/effective_workŌłÆ1; exact Daly anchors via math.exp, the Young ŌłÜ(2CM)/ŌłÜ(2C/M) first-order values reported descriptively only ŌĆö no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): overhead_opt 0.106933 (se 0.000300), overhead_frequent 0.412435 (se 0.000065), overhead_rare 0.545241 (se 0.003414), overhead_opt_4M 0.052066 (se 0.000313), frequent penalty 0.305502 (se 0.000307), rare penalty 0.438308 (se 0.003427), halving drop 0.054867 (se 0.000433); G1 frequent penalty 0.305502 Ōēź 0.05 z=833.5744Žā PASS, G2 rare penalty 0.438308 Ōēź 0.05 z_rare=113.2963Žā AND halving drop 0.054867 Ōēź 0.02 z_halve=80.5085Žā PASS, G3 opt 0.106933 vs 0.107106 |z|=0.5778 / frequent 0.412435 vs 0.412322 |z|=1.7438 / rare 0.545241 vs 0.545871 |z|=0.1845 all |z|<3 PASS ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=43a77ca77563e81f873cd73c81d35c684a2ec48bc26d2d740b083ef6be566a81.

## VERDICT 129 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T05:13:15Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P116 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 116 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T04:46:21Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the arcsine lead-time illusion: play a perfectly FAIR contest as an unbiased ┬▒1 random walk of N_STEPS=500 steps (each step a fair coin, zero edge, zero drift) and measure the fraction of the game one side spends IN THE LEAD (segment i above the axis iff S_{iŌłÆ1}+S_i>0, the sum odd so the side is always defined). Intuition says the two sides trade the lead evenly so each is ahead ~half the time and the lead-fraction concentrates near 1/2, but the truth is the OPPOSITE (L├®vy's first arcsine law): a fair walk is null-recurrent and STICKY about its sign (returns to zero w.p.1 but the expected inter-return time is infinite), so long one-sided excursions dominate and the lead-fraction follows the U-shaped ARCSINE distribution (density 1/(ŽĆŌłÜ(x(1ŌłÆx))), piling up at 0 and 1, minimized at 1/2), with the exact discrete law P(T=2k)=u_{2k}┬Ęu_{2(nŌłÆk)}, u_{2j}=C(2j,j)/4^j (Feller Vol.1 ┬¦III.4) and nŌåÆŌł× limit P(fractionŌēżx)ŌåÆ(2/ŽĆ)arcsinŌłÜx ŌĆö so a fair game is fair in the MEAN (mean lead-fraction 0.5 exactly) yet its DISTRIBUTION is bimodal at the extremes, and a long one-sided lead is the EXPECTED SIGNATURE of fairness, not proof of an edge; the transferable correction is to measure the edge from the DRIFT (net margin per step / terminal gap ┬▒ se), never from how long or how lopsidedly one side has led, and to budget stopping rules and confidence to the arcsine tails rather than a naive "it should hover near 50/50". Round-26 UNRELATED-slot closer (probability theory / stochastic processes ┬Ę the L├®vyŌĆōFeller first arcsine law of random-walk sojourn times; a fleet-external pure-mechanism head, deliberately distinct from prior unrelated closers P100 Kelly/info-theory, P104 SIR/epidemiology, P108 regression-to-the-mean/statistics, P112 series-system MTBF/reliability; Q-0264 co-consumer = any close, roughly-fair, back-and-forth process whose "who has been ahead / for how long" is read as an edge signal ŌĆö a tied competitive match, a near-parity A/B test, two comparable trading strategies or similar-skill agents, a cumulative-score race, a running vote margin).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/arcsine-lead-illusion-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, N_STEPS=500 (2n; n=250), N_WALKS=4000, TRIALS=200, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, LEAD_LO=0.10, LEAD_HI=0.90, BAL_LO=0.40, BAL_HI=0.60; thresholds EXT_MIN=0.30 (G1), BAL_MAX=0.20 + GAP_MIN=0.15 (G2); exact discrete-arcsine anchors n=250: extreme-lead P(fŌēż0.10 or Ōēź0.90)=0.415569, lower-tail P(fŌēż0.10)=0.207784, balanced P(0.40ŌēżfŌēż0.60)=0.130523, mean=0.500000; continuous-limit extreme descriptive 2┬Ę(2/ŽĆ)arcsinŌłÜ0.1=0.409666), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo of N_WALKS symmetric ┬▒1 walks of N_STEPS steps counting above-axis segments to form each game's lead-fraction, averaged over TRIALS=200 replications with z on the estimated mean via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS, confirms the arcsine U-shape: the measured extreme-lead probability is 0.415618 (se 0.000512, one side ahead for an extreme share of the play), the balanced see-saw only 0.130543 (se 0.000353), the mean lead-fraction a fair 0.500315 (se 0.000416) ŌĆö fair in the MEAN yet bimodal at the tails ŌĆö and the measured extreme-lead and lower-tail reproduce the exact discrete-arcsine anchors within 3Žā. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 extreme-lead headline: PASS ŌĆö the measured extreme-lead probability P(fŌēżLEAD_LO or fŌēźLEAD_HI) = 0.415618 (se 0.000512) is Ōēź EXT_MIN=0.30 by z=225.9288Žā Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö in a perfectly FAIR driftless game one side is ahead for an extreme share of the play far more often than the naive 50/50 picture allows.
- G2 fair-mean + middle-is-rare control: PASS ŌĆö the mean lead-fraction 0.500315 (se 0.000416) is within 3Žā of 0.5 by |z|=0.7575 (<3, the walk is genuinely UNBIASED so G1 is the arcsine U-shape not a drift) AND the balanced probability P(BAL_LOŌēżfŌēżBAL_HI) = 0.130543 (se 0.000353) is Ōēż BAL_MAX=0.20 by z_balanced=196.6076Žā AND the (extremeŌłÆbalanced) gap 0.285075 (se 0.000622) is Ōēź GAP_MIN=0.15 by z_gap=217.2175Žā, both Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö the extremes strictly BEAT a close see-saw, inverting the naive "concentrates at 1/2" belief (the load-bearing control).
- G3 closed-form anchor MATCH: PASS ŌĆö the measured extreme-lead probability 0.415618 matches the exact discrete-arcsine anchor 0.415569 within 3Žā by |z|=0.0953 AND the measured lower-tail 0.207800 matches its exact anchor 0.207784 within 3Žā by |z|=0.0356 ŌĆö reproduces the L├®vyŌĆōFeller arcsine law itself.

## PROPOSAL 118 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T05:30:18Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready

idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/sales-ramp-capacity-drag-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-27 VENTURE-domain slot (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P117 opened round-27 at the fleet slot, this takes the venture slot). Domain: sales-capacity planning / go-to-market unit economics ŌĆö the sales-ramp capacity drag, a venture-domain head deliberately DISTINCT from the prior venture heads P114 (freemium support-cost inversion / concave contribution), P110 (discount-depth breakeven), P106 (retention-survivorship mirage), P102 (growth cash-trough trap) and P098 (referral-bonus value trap). The mechanism: a new rep does not book at full quota on day one ŌĆö productivity ramps linearly to full quota Q over a ramp period R (onboarding, territory build, pipeline maturation), so a rep of tenure a books q(a)=Q┬Ęmin(a/R,1). In a force whose headcount GROWS at monthly rate g and whose reps leave at attrition rate d, the steady-state tenure of the CURRENTLY-ACTIVE force is exponential with rate ╬▓=g+d (Lotka stable-population age structure: the cohort hired a months ago numbered ~e^{g(tŌłÆa)}, survivors to now ~e^{ŌłÆd┬Ęa}, so active tenure density is (g+d)┬Ęe^{ŌłÆ(g+d)a}). Averaging the linear ramp over that distribution gives the realized fraction of nominal quota the force actually books: Žå(╬▓)=E[min(a/R,1)]=(1ŌłÆe^{ŌłÆ╬▓R})/(╬▓R), the classic (1ŌłÆe^{ŌłÆx})/x utilization form ŌĆö always <1 and DECREASING in ╬▓. Three consequences the "hire harder / hire ahead" instinct misses: (1) nameplate capacity (headcount┬Ęquota) OVERSTATES realized bookings by 1/Žå, and since Žå falls as g rises the over-forecast is worst exactly when scaling hardest; (2) faster growth MECHANICALLY dilutes per-rep productivity (ŌłéŽå/Ōłég<0: more of the force is in ramp), so a falling bookings-per-rep is a turnover signal, not a talent signal, and responding with more hiring deepens the drag; (3) growth and attrition are INTERCHANGEABLE ŌĆö Žå depends only on ╬▓=g+d, so a +╬ö over-hiring problem and a +╬ö retention problem inflict the IDENTICAL capacity drag and are indistinguishable from the aggregate number (equivalently, cutting attrition by ╬ö buys exactly the capacity that slowing over-hiring by ╬ö does). Claim under test: on the pinned world realized productivity is Žå(╬▓_slow=0.04)=0.889 of quota at slow growth g=0.01 but Žå(╬▓_fast=0.11)=0.732 at fast growth g=0.08 (the growth drag Ōēź0.05 by Ōēź3Žā), a matched attrition bump (╬┤:0.03ŌåÆ0.10, holding g=0.01, so ╬▓=0.11) drags to the IDENTICAL 0.732 (the interchangeability, drag Ōēź0.05 by Ōēź3Žā with fast-vs-attr means equal to within Monte-Carlo noise), and the measured Žå reproduces the EXACT closed form (1ŌłÆe^{ŌłÆ╬▓R})/(╬▓R) at both regimes within 3Žā ŌĆö so realized sales capacity is nominal┬ĘŽå(g+d) with Žå=(1ŌłÆe^{ŌłÆ╬▓R})/(╬▓R), a ramp-averaged utilization that falls with total force turnover and makes growth and attrition the same drag lever, not a nameplate. Pinned: SEED=20260717, TRIALS=400, N_REPS=20000, Q=1.0, R=6.0, DELTA_LOW=0.03, DELTA_HI=0.10, G_SLOW=0.01, G_FAST=0.08, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, GAP_MIN=0.05; ╬▓_slow=g_slow+╬┤_low=0.04, ╬▓_fast=g_fast+╬┤_low=0.11, ╬▓_attr=g_slow+╬┤_hi=0.11 (matched to ╬▓_fast). Closed-form anchors Žå(╬▓)=(1ŌłÆe^{ŌłÆ╬▓R})/(╬▓R): Žå(0.04)=0.889051, Žå(0.11)=0.732043, gap=0.157007. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 growth capacity drag ŌĆö mean(Žå_slowŌłÆŽå_fast) Ōēź GAP_MIN=0.05 by Ōēź3Žā (growing faster strictly and substantially lowers realized per-rep productivity, a ramp effect not talent); G2 attrition-interchangeability ŌĆö a matched attrition bump (╬▓_attr=╬▓_fast) drags identically, mean(Žå_slowŌłÆŽå_attr_hi) Ōēź GAP_MIN=0.05 by Ōēź3Žā with Žå_fastŌēłŽå_attr descriptively, so the drag is ╬▓=g+d only (over-hiring and churn are the same lever); G3 closed-form anchor MATCH ŌĆö the mean simulated Žå reproduces (1ŌłÆe^{ŌłÆ╬▓R})/(╬▓R) at both ╬▓_slow and ╬▓_fast within 3Žā (|z|<3). Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109/P110/P111/P112/P113/P114/P115/P116/P117 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/venture-lab/sales_ramp_capacity_drag.py (random,math,json,hashlib; per-trial Monte-Carlo of N_REPS active reps drawing tenure a~Exp(╬▓) via random.expovariate and booking Q┬Ęmin(a/R,1) under three ╬▓ regimes, mean over reps = sample Žå; closed form Žå=(1ŌłÆe^{ŌłÆ╬▓R})/(╬▓R) via math.exp ŌĆö no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): Žå_slow 0.888962 (se 0.00009), Žå_fast 0.731906 (se 0.000124), Žå_attr 0.732055 (se 0.000126); G1 growth-drag mean 0.157056 (se 0.000155) Ōēź 0.05 z=689.8382Žā PASS; G2 attrition-drag mean 0.156907 (se 0.000158) Ōēź 0.05 z=676.4328Žā PASS, fast-vs-attr interchange_abs_diff 0.000148; G3 Žå_slow 0.888962 vs 0.889051 |z|=0.9879 / Žå_fast 0.731906 vs 0.732043 |z|=1.1052 all |z|<3 PASS ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=9b7d3b078fa1af3ce2406a245a9422263e09024828656cd06d83f921cfa6d6ff.

## VERDICT 130 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T05:40:48Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P117 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 117 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T05:07:26Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the YoungŌĆōDaly optimal checkpoint interval: a long fleet job that can crash and restart from its last checkpoint faces TWO competing wastes ŌĆö checkpoint OVERHEAD (~C/Žä per unit useful work: each checkpoint costs C, taken every Žä of work) and lost RECOMPUTE work on a crash (~Žä/(2M) per unit work: a Poisson crash at rate 1/M lands on average halfway through the current interval, discarding the work since the last checkpoint). Their sum C/Žä + Žä/(2M) is a CONVEX falling+rising pair, so total wasted wall time is U-SHAPED in the checkpoint interval Žä with a unique INTERIOR optimum where the two wastes are equal: Žä*=ŌłÜ(2┬ĘC┬ĘM) (Young 1974 first-order), overhead floor ŌłÜ(2C/M) (Daly 2006 exact) ŌĆö so the "checkpoint as often as you can afford" instinct is wrong three ways: (1) the optimum is INTERIOR (checkpointing more often than Žä* walks UP the left arm of the U as C/Žä diverges), (2) the two failure modes are SYMMETRIC in leading order (Žä*┬Ęk and Žä*/k inflate overhead by the same ┬Į(k+1/k)), (3) the optimum scales only as ŌłÜM (a 4├Ś more reliable machine warrants a 2├Ś longer interval and HALVES the achievable overhead floor); the transferable correction is to set the checkpoint/snapshot/commit interval to Žä*=ŌłÜ(2┬ĘC┬ĘMTBF) and spend engineering effort on shrinking the checkpoint cost C, never on merely raising the frequency. Round-27 FLEET-slot opener (fault-tolerant computing / long-running-job checkpoint scheduling ┬Ę the YoungŌĆōDaly checkpoint-restart overhead law; a fleet-domain pure-mechanism head deliberately distinct from prior fleet heads P113 two-choices routing cliff / balanced-allocations, P109 correlated-fleet variance floor, P105 delegation loop collapse, P101 winner's-curse auction; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = the fleet itself, any long-running fleet job with a checkpoint/restart or snapshot/commit cycle ŌĆö a training run, a batch pipeline, a long agent task, a stream processor, a simulation restart-dump ŌĆö and any interruptible process that persists progress against a snapshot and pays a snapshot cost).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/checkpoint-interval-optimum-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, C=1.0, M=200.0, W=40000.0, K=8, TRIALS=200, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; thresholds DELTA_MIN=0.05 (G1/G2a), HALVE_MIN=0.02 (G2b); intervals Žä*=ŌłÜ(2CM)=20.0, frequent Žä*/K=2.5, rare Žä*┬ĘK=160.0, ŌłÜ-law control M2=4M=800 with Žä*(4M)=ŌłÜ(2┬ĘC┬Ę4M)=40.0; exact Daly anchors O(Žä)=(M/Žä)(exp((Žä+C)/M)ŌłÆ1)ŌłÆ1: O(20)=0.107106, O(2.5)=0.412322, O(160)=0.545871, O(40 at M=800)=0.051720; Young first-order descriptive ŌłÜ(2C/M)=0.10 at M=200, ŌłÜ(2C/4M)=0.05 at M=800), a stdlib per-run segment-by-segment Monte-Carlo of a W=40000 checkpoint/restart job at four intervals under Poisson(1/M) failures (fresh expovariate crash draws ŌĆö a crash costs the elapsed segment time and loses the segment, retry from the last checkpoint ŌĆö measuring the overhead fraction wall/effective_workŌłÆ1), averaged over TRIALS=200 replications with z on the estimated mean via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS, confirms the U-shape: the overhead at the interior optimum Žä*=20 is a small 0.106933 (se 0.000300, ~10.7%), checkpointing 8├Ś too often (Žä=2.5) wastes strictly more at 0.412435 (se 0.000065), checkpointing 8├Ś too rarely (Žä=160) also wastes strictly more at 0.545241 (se 0.003414), and quadrupling the MTBF at the rescaled optimum Žä*(4M)=40 halves the optimum overhead to 0.052066 (se 0.000313) ŌĆö over- and under-checkpointing both waste more and the optimum scales only as ŌłÜM ŌĆö and the three measured overheads reproduce the exact Daly closed form within 3Žā. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 over-checkpointing headline: PASS ŌĆö the frequent penalty overhead(Žä*/K)ŌłÆoverhead(Žä*) = 0.305502 (se 0.000307) is Ōēź DELTA_MIN=0.05 by z=833.5744Žā Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö checkpointing 8├Ś more often than the optimum wastes strictly MORE, inverting "checkpoint as often as you can afford is safest": the optimum is interior and over-checkpointing walks UP the left arm of the U (the C/Žä overhead diverges as ŽäŌåÆ0).
- G2 U-shape + ŌłÜ-law control: PASS ŌĆö the rare penalty overhead(Žä*┬ĘK)ŌłÆoverhead(Žä*) = 0.438308 (se 0.003427) is Ōēź DELTA_MIN=0.05 by z_rare=113.2963Žā (checkpointing 8├Ś too rarely ALSO wastes more, so the waste is U-shaped with an INTERIOR optimum, neither extreme wins) AND the ŌłÜ-law halving drop overhead(Žä*,M)ŌłÆoverhead(Žä*(4M),4M) = 0.054867 (se 0.000433) is Ōēź HALVE_MIN=0.02 by z_halve=80.5085Žā, both Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö quadrupling the MTBF roughly halves the overhead floor (0.10ŌåÆ0.05), the ŌłÜ(2C/M) law that sets the optimum (the load-bearing control).
- G3 closed-form anchor MATCH: PASS ŌĆö the three measured overheads reproduce the exact Daly law O(Žä)=(M/Žä)(exp((Žä+C)/M)ŌłÆ1)ŌłÆ1 within 3Žā each: optimum 0.106933 vs 0.107106 by |z|=0.5778, frequent 0.412435 vs 0.412322 by |z|=1.7438, rare 0.545241 vs 0.545871 by |z|=0.1845 ŌĆö reproduces the checkpoint-restart overhead law itself, so the Young optimum Žä*=ŌłÜ(2CM) with floor ŌłÜ(2C/M) sits at the measured minimum.

## VERDICT 126 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T05:47:14Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P113 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 113 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T03:40:54Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the two-choices routing cliff: a dispatcher routes each of m tasks to the least-loaded of d workers sampled uniformly at random from n workers (tiesŌåÆfirst sampled), and with d=1 (pure random routing) the maximum queue length grows like ln n / ln ln n while with d=2 it collapses to ln ln n / ln 2 + O(1) ŌĆö a DOUBLE-EXPONENTIAL improvement in the tail (AzarŌĆōBroderŌĆōKarlinŌĆōUpfal, "Balanced Allocations", SIAM J. Comput. 1999; Mitzenmacher, IEEE TPDS 2001); every extra probe past the second only divides the already-tiny ln ln n term by ln d, so the marginal gain collapses and the entire benefit of multiple choices is captured by the SECOND probe ŌĆö the diminishing returns are not gradual, they fall off a cliff immediately after d=2; the trap is that operators expect balance to improve smoothly with the probe count and over-provision d=4..8, paying 2ŌĆō4├Ś the coordination cost of d=2 for a sub-unit reduction in max queue. Round-26 FLEET-slot opener (rotation reopens at fleet after round-25 completed at the unrelated slot P112; rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated) ŌĆö randomized load balancing / balanced-allocations theory, a fleet-domain head; Q-0264 co-consumer = any dispatch/scheduling/sharding surface choosing a probe fan-out (a task router, a connection-pool balancer, a cache/shard placement, a hash-with-choices scheme).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/two-choices-routing-cliff-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, N_WORKERS=20000, M_TASKS=20000 (m=n balls-into-bins regime), TRIALS=120, D_VALUES=(1,2,3,4), SIGMA_GATE=3.0; threshold PLATEAU_MAX=2.0 (G3); analytic anchors d=1 max Ōēł ln n / ln ln n = 4.319222, d=2 max Ōēł ln ln n / ln 2 = 3.307937, asymptotic order not tight constants), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo placing M_TASKS=20000 tasks into N_WORKERS=20000 workers (each task ŌåÆ least-loaded of d sampled workers, tiesŌåÆfirst) recording the max load for d Ōłł {1,2,3,4}, averaged over TRIALS=120 replicas with z on the estimated mean via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS, confirms the cliff: mean max load by d = {1: 7.016667, 2: 3.083333, 3: 3.0, 4: 2.466667} ŌĆö the first extra probe (d=1ŌåÆ2) collapses the max queue from ~7.02 to ~3.08 while every probe past the second (d=2ŌåÆ4) saves under one slot. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 two-choices jump (headline): PASS ŌĆö mean(max_1 ŌłÆ max_2) = 3.933333 (se 0.068735) > 0 by z=57.2243Žā Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö the first extra probe strictly and significantly shortens the longest queue.
- G2 the cliff (front-loaded): PASS ŌĆö mean((max_1ŌłÆmax_2) ŌłÆ (max_2ŌłÆmax_3)) = 3.85 (se 0.084225) > 0 by z=45.7109Žā Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö the 1ŌåÆ2 gain (3.933333) massively exceeds the 2ŌåÆ3 gain (0.083333, ~47├Ś), so the benefit is front-loaded onto the second probe (the cliff, the load-bearing control).
- G3 the plateau beyond two: PASS ŌĆö mean(max_2 ŌłÆ max_4) = 0.616667 (se 0.053168) < PLATEAU_MAX=2.0 by z=26.0183Žā Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö every probe past the second together saves under two queue slots, so d=4 buys a sub-unit reduction over d=2.

NOTE the digest posture: P113 is the SELF-FIELD posture (like P104/P107/P108/P111/P112, and UNLIKE P105/P109/P110's sibling-key posture) ŌĆö main() computes canonical = json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) and digest = sha256(canonical) BEFORE inserting results["results_sha256"]=digest, so the disclosed digest is over the results dict with the results_sha256 self-field OMITTED and the pretty-printed on-disk two_choices_routing_cliff_results.json artifact's own sha256sum is NOT this digest; the field-omission step was applied on independent recompute (resolved against the script text, not assumed) ŌĆö the script-printed `Results-JSON sha256:` line == the disclosed digest, EQUAL. All three gate sigmas match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=57.2243Žā, G2 z=45.7109Žā, G3 z=26.0183Žā); means match (mean max load by d = {1: 7.016667, 2: 3.083333, 3: 3.0, 4: 2.466667}); verifier exits 0, all_pass=true. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114ŌĆōV125, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ŌĆö and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): sampling two candidates and taking the less-loaded is a double-exponential improvement over one, and a third buys only a vanishing constant ŌĆö so cap probe fan-out at 2 on any dispatch/scheduling/sharding surface (task router, connection-pool balancer, cache/shard placement, hash-with-choices) and reinvest the saved coordination budget in load-signal freshness, never in more probes.
