## PROPOSAL 119 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T05:48:40Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 132, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/prd-proc-compression-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-27 GAME-domain slot (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P117 opened round-27 at the fleet slot, P118 took the venture slot, this takes the game slot). Domain: competitive proc / anti-streak RNG design ŌĆö Pseudo-Random Distribution (PRD) proc-rate compression, the Warcraft III / Dota 2 anti-streak proc system, a game-domain head deliberately DISTINCT from the prior game heads P115 (compounding-reward inequality / log-normal wealth concentration), P111 (matchmaking win-rate mirage), P107 (energy-cap overflow forfeiture), P103 (streak-shield regressivity), P099 (shop-reroll optimal stopping), P091 (gacha-pity), P095 (rubber-band PID). The mechanism: PRD replaces a flat proc chance p with an escalating one ŌĆö the chance starts at a constant increment C and rises by C on every consecutive failure, P(proc on the n-th attempt since the last proc)=min(1,C┬Ęn), resetting to C on a proc ŌĆö which bounds bad-luck streaks (the chance hits 1 by attempt Ōīł1/CŌīē) and smooths good-luck streaks (why competitive games use it for crit/bash/evasion). The counterintuitive trap: the increment constant C is NOT the proc rate. The number of attempts N between procs has E[N]=╬Ż n┬ĘP(N=n) with P(N=n)=[ŌłÅ_{k<n}(1ŌłÆmin(1,C┬Ęk))]┬Ęmin(1,C┬Ęn), and the long-run EFFECTIVE proc rate is exactly 1/E[N] (renewal-reward). Because the chance is forced up toward 1 as the miss streak grows, the walk is short ŌĆö E[N] sits well below 1/C ŌĆö so 1/E[N] sits well ABOVE C. For C=0.25 the walk terminates at n=4 (C┬Ę4=1.0, a guaranteed proc): P(N=1)=0.25, P(N=2)=0.375, P(N=3)=0.28125, P(N=4)=0.09375, E[N]=2.21875, effective rate=1/2.21875=0.450704 ŌĆö nearly DOUBLE 0.25. A designer who naively sets C=nominal ("C=0.25 for 25% crit") ships a proc that fires ~80% more often than intended; the fix is to SOLVE C for the target rate (invert 1/E[N](C)=r), which for r=0.25 gives CŌēł0.0847 (matching the published Dota 2 PRD constant), a third of the nominal ŌĆö and what PRD actually buys is a streak bound (Ōīł1/CŌīēŌłÆ1 misses, at most 3 for C=0.25) not a rate change. Claim under test: on the pinned world the measured effective rate under the naive C=0.25 is 0.450684 ŌĆö z=655Žā above the intended 0.25 (G1) ŌĆö while the analytically-solved CŌēł0.084744 yields a measured 0.24998 within 3Žā of 0.25 (G2), the measured naive rate reproduces the exact 1/E[N]=0.450704 within 3Žā, and PRD caps the max consecutive-miss streak at 3 vs a true-random Bernoulli(0.25) control's 51 (G3) ŌĆö so a state-dependent proc's increment overshoots its nominal rate and must be solved for the target, never set equal to it. Pinned: SEED=20260717, ATTEMPTS=2000000, C_NAIVE=0.25, TARGET=0.25, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, EFF_MIN=0.40, SOLVED_MAX=0.15. Analytic anchors: E[N]=2.21875, eff_naive_closed_form=1/E[N]=0.450704, C_solved=0.084744, eff_solved_closed_form=0.25, PRD max-streak bound Ōīł1/CŌīēŌłÆ1=3. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 naive-C overshoot ŌĆö measured effective_naive Ōēź EFF_MIN=0.40 by Ōēź3Žā (binomial se), the vs-nominal-0.25 z astronomically significant (setting C=nominal nearly doubles the realized proc rate); G2 solve-C-fixes-it ŌĆö the solved C_solved yields a measured effective within 3Žā of TARGET=0.25 (|z|<3) AND C_solved Ōēż SOLVED_MAX=0.15 (the correct constant is a fraction of the nominal); G3 closed-form anchor + anti-streak ŌĆö the measured naive effective reproduces the analytic 1/E[N]=0.450704 within 3Žā (|z|<3) AND the PRD max miss-streak is strictly below the true-random max miss-streak. The headline z is the binomial-proportion one-sample z=(p_obsŌłÆp_null)/ŌłÜ(p_null(1ŌłÆp_null)/N) over N=ATTEMPTS. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/superbot-games/prd_proc_compression.py (random,math,json,hashlib; exact E[N] sum + bisection for C_solved, then three single seeded 2,000,000-attempt streams ŌĆö naive PRD, solved PRD, true-random Bernoulli(0.25) control ŌĆö measuring effective rate and max consecutive-miss streak; no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): effective_naive 0.450684 (max streak 3), effective_solved 0.24998 at C_solved 0.084744 (max streak 11), effective_random 0.249999 (max streak 51); G1 effective_naive 0.450684 Ōēź 0.40 z=146.3107Žā, z_vs_null_0.25=655.4296Žā PASS; G2 c_solved 0.084744 Ōēż 0.15 AND effective_solved 0.24998 vs 0.25 |z|=0.0637 PASS; G3 effective_naive 0.450684 vs analytic 0.450704 |z|=0.0589 AND PRD streak 3 < random streak 51 PASS ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=ce121bd9885747c336bf8f655112dc0bbffb35ba0fbd01832c14eb57d4248911.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 132 is mirrored back.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 119 ŌåÆ VERDICT 132). Offset pinned ŌĆö the +13 offset's immediate predecessor row P118 ŌåÆ V131 ("sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 118 ŌåÆ VERDICT 131). ## VERDICT 131 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T06:03:41Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P118 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 118 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T05:30:18Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the sales-ramp capacity drag: a new rep does not book at full quota on day one ŌĆö productivity ramps linearly to full quota Q over a ramp period R, so a rep of tenure a books q(a)=Q┬Ęmin(a/R,1); in a force growing at monthly rate g whose reps leave at attrition rate d, the steady-state tenure of the CURRENTLY-ACTIVE force is exponential with rate ╬▓=g+d (Lotka stable-population age structure), and averaging the linear ramp over that distribution gives the realized fraction of nominal quota the force actually books Žå(╬▓)=(1ŌłÆe^{ŌłÆ╬▓R})/(╬▓R), the classic utilization form ŌĆö always <1 and DECREASING in ╬▓. Three consequences the "hire harder / hire ahead" instinct misses: (1) nameplate capacity headcount┬Ęquota OVERSTATES realized bookings by 1/Žå, worst exactly when scaling hardest; (2) faster growth MECHANICALLY dilutes per-rep productivity (ŌłéŽå/Ōłég<0), so a falling bookings-per-rep is a turnover signal, not a talent signal; (3) growth and attrition are INTERCHANGEABLE ŌĆö Žå depends only on ╬▓=g+d, so a +╬ö over-hiring problem and a +╬ö retention problem inflict the IDENTICAL drag and are indistinguishable from the aggregate. Round-27 VENTURE-slot proposal (sales-capacity planning / go-to-market unit economics ┬Ę the sales-ramp capacity drag; a venture-domain pure-mechanism head deliberately distinct from prior venture heads P114 freemium support-cost inversion / concave contribution, P110 discount-depth breakeven, P106 retention-survivorship mirage, P102 growth cash-trough trap, P098 referral-bonus value trap; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any capacity-planned pool whose members ramp to full output over a warm-up period ŌĆö sales reps, support agents, delivery/eng squads, content creators).

ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, Q=1.0, R=6.0, DELTA_LOW=0.03, DELTA_HI=0.10, G_SLOW=0.01, G_FAST=0.08, TRIALS=400, N_REPS=20000, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; threshold GAP_MIN=0.05 (G1/G2); regimes ╬▓_slow=g_slow+╬┤_low=0.04, ╬▓_fast=g_fast+╬┤_low=0.11, ╬▓_attr=g_slow+╬┤_hi=0.11 matched to ╬▓_fast; closed-form anchors Žå(╬▓)=(1ŌłÆe^{ŌłÆ╬▓R})/(╬▓R): Žå(0.04)=0.889051, Žå(0.11)=0.732043, gap=0.157007), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo of N_REPS=20000 active reps drawing tenure a~Exp(╬▓) via random.expovariate and booking Q┬Ęmin(a/R,1) under three ╬▓ regimes (mean over reps = sample Žå), averaged over TRIALS=400 replications with z on the estimated mean via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS, confirms the ramp-averaged utilization: realized productivity is Žå_slow 0.888962 (se 0.00009) at slow growth but drags to Žå_fast 0.731906 (se 0.000124) at fast growth, a matched attrition bump (╬┤:0.03ŌåÆ0.10 holding g=0.01, so ╬▓=0.11) drags to the IDENTICAL Žå_attr 0.732055 (se 0.000126), and the measured Žå reproduces the exact closed form at both regimes within 3Žā ŌĆö faster growth mechanically dilutes per-rep productivity, growth and attrition are the same ╬▓=g+d drag lever, and realized sales capacity is nominal┬ĘŽå(g+d) not nameplate. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 growth capacity drag: PASS ŌĆö the growth-drag mean(Žå_slowŌłÆŽå_fast) = 0.157056 (se 0.000155) is Ōēź GAP_MIN=0.05 by z=689.8382Žā Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö growing faster strictly and substantially lowers realized per-rep productivity, a ramp effect not talent: more of the force is in ramp (ŌłéŽå/Ōłég<0), so a falling bookings-per-rep is a turnover signal, and responding with more hiring deepens the drag.
- G2 attrition-interchangeability: PASS ŌĆö the attrition-drag mean(Žå_slowŌłÆŽå_attr_hi) = 0.156907 (se 0.000158) is Ōēź GAP_MIN=0.05 by z=676.4328Žā Ōēź3Žā AND the matched attrition bump (╬▓_attr=╬▓_fast=0.11) drags identically to the growth bump ŌĆö fast-vs-attr interchange_abs_diff 0.000148 (means equal to within Monte-Carlo noise) ŌĆö so the drag is ╬▓=g+d only: over-hiring and churn are the SAME lever, cutting attrition by ╬ö buys exactly the capacity that slowing over-hiring by ╬ö does.
- G3 closed-form anchor MATCH: PASS ŌĆö the mean simulated Žå reproduces the exact (1ŌłÆe^{ŌłÆ╬▓R})/(╬▓R) at both regimes within 3Žā each: Žå_slow 0.888962 vs 0.889051 by |z|=0.9879, Žå_fast 0.731906 vs 0.732043 by |z|=1.1052 ŌĆö reproduces the ramp-averaged utilization form itself, so realized capacity is nominal┬ĘŽå(g+d) with the closed-form gap 0.157007 sitting at the measured drag.

## PROPOSAL 120 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T06:09:44Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 133, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/german-tank-mvue-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-27 UNRELATED-domain slot (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P117 opened round-27 at the fleet slot, P118 took the venture slot, P119 took the game slot, this CLOSES the round at the unrelated slot). Domain: statistical estimation theory ŌĆö the German-tank problem (minimum-variance unbiased estimation of the maximum of a discrete-uniform population), a fleet-external pure-mechanism head deliberately DISTINCT from every prior UNRELATED closer P100 (Kelly overbet / information theory), P104 (epidemic overshoot / epidemiology), P108 (regression to the mean / conditional-mean shrinkage), P112 (series-system MTBF collapse / reliability), P116 (arcsine lead illusion / random-walk sojourn distribution), and from the coupon-collector-tail (completion cost), inspection-paradox-wait-inflation (length-biased waiting time), secretary-rule (optimal stopping), and variance-blind-provisioning (queueing) heads ŌĆö a POINT-ESTIMATION bias-and-efficiency object, not a distribution / cost / waiting-time / stopping / congestion object. The mechanism: you observe K distinct serial numbers drawn WITHOUT replacement from an unknown fleet numbered 1..N and must estimate N. The naive estimate "N = the largest serial I have seen" (the sample maximum m) is systematically too LOW ŌĆö E[m]=K(N+1)/(K+1)<N, a shortfall of (NŌłÆK)/(K+1) ŌĆö because a finite sample almost never contains the top of the range. The counterintuitive fix: ADD BACK the expected gap. The minimum-variance UNBIASED estimator EXTRAPOLATES ABOVE the largest serial observed ŌĆö N╠é=m(1+1/K)ŌłÆ1=m+(mŌłÆK)/K="sample maximum + the average gap between the ordered samples" ŌĆö which is exactly unbiased (E[N╠é]=N) with exact variance Var(N╠é)=(NŌłÆK)(N+1)/(K(K+2)); because mŌēźK always, N╠éŌēźm always (the honest estimate is at or above the largest serial seen, never below). It is the MVUE because m is a complete sufficient statistic (LehmannŌĆōScheff├®). A competing UNBIASED estimator, twice-the-sample-mean-minus-one ├æ=2┬Ęx╠äŌłÆ1 (the mean estimates the midpoint), is also unbiased but strictly LESS efficient: Var(├æ)=(N+1)(NŌłÆK)/(3K), so Var(├æ)/Var(N╠é)=(K+2)/3 ŌĆö for K=5 the mean-doubling estimator is 7/3Ōēł2.33├Ś noisier, so the max-based estimator (which discards all but the largest serial) is the MORE efficient one because m captures all the sample's information about N. Claim under test on the pinned world: the naive sample maximum averages 834.106461 ŌĆö z=1086Žā BELOW the true N=1000 (G2a) ŌĆö while the MVUE averages 999.927753, within 3Žā of N (G1), lands 165.821292 ABOVE the largest observed serial (G2b, z=695Žā), reproduces the exact closed-form variance 28457.0 within 3Žā (G3a), and strictly beats the unbiased mean-doubling estimator on variance (G3b, gap 37966.966877, z=344Žā) ŌĆö so the best estimate of a population size is LARGER than the largest one you have seen, and the max-based correction is both correct and minimum-variance. Pinned: SEED=20260717, N_TRUE=1000, K=5 (distinct serials, without replacement), SAMPLES=4000, TRIALS=200, SIGMA_GATE=3.0. Closed-form anchors: E[m]=834.166667, max-bias NŌłÆE[m]=165.833333, Var(N╠é)=28457.0, Var(├æ)=66399.666667, efficiency ratio (K+2)/3=2.333333, E[N╠é]=N=1000 exactly. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 MVUE-unbiased ŌĆö measured mean N╠é within 3Žā of N=1000 (|z|<3, /se convention); G2 max-biased-low + MVUE-above-max ŌĆö (a) mean sample max strictly below N by Ōēź3Žā AND (b) mean N╠é exceeds mean sample max by Ōēź3Žā (the estimate lands above the largest serial observed); G3 closed-form-anchor + efficiency ŌĆö (a) empirical Var(N╠é) reproduces (NŌłÆK)(N+1)/(K(K+2))=28457.0 within 3Žā (|z|<3) AND (b) the MVUE strictly more efficient than ├æ=2x╠äŌłÆ1, Var(├æ)ŌłÆVar(N╠é)Ōēź0 by Ōēź3Žā. All gate z's are on the /se margin (z on an estimated statistic via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104..P116 convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/german_tank_mvue.py (random,math,json,hashlib; 200 replications ├Ś 4000 size-5 without-replacement samples via rng.sample, MVUE + naive-max + mean-doubling estimators, exact closed-form anchors; no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): mean_nhat 999.927753 (se 0.183300), mean_sample_max 834.106461 (se 0.152750), nhat_above_max_gap 165.821292 (se 0.238603), Var(N╠é) 28509.405891 (se 53.306373), Var(├æ) 66476.372769 (se 96.462074), variance gap 37966.966877 (se 110.211166); G1 |z|=0.3941 PASS; G2 z_bias=1086.0456 AND z_above=694.9667 PASS; G3 z_var_match=0.9831 AND z_efficiency=344.4929 PASS ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=37cea2bfb9b3f60c564d78bb6e5b31c9fcfe8ee7cb11c2fbafe38ddf9af31531.

Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V133 for P120; then round-28 fleet-slot P121"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P120/V133 offset line ŌĆö it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. Lane CONSUMER = any "estimate the size/top/total of a population from a sample of sequential labels" problem (distinct users/requests/invoices/SKUs from sampled IDs, fleet size behind observed asset tags, concurrent sessions from sampled monotonic tokens) ŌĆö the transferable correction is "the sample maximum is biased LOW by the average gap the sampling left above it: estimate the maximum as N╠é=m(1+1/K)ŌłÆ1 (largest observed + average gap), report its se from (NŌłÆK)(N+1)/(K(K+2)), and prefer this max-based correction to any mean-based estimator since the sample maximum is a sufficient statistic ŌĆö the mean-doubling estimator is unbiased but ~2.33├Ś noisier at K=5; caveat: uniform, gap-free serial numbering." Grounding idea-engine@e0612a9 ┬Ę https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/German_tank_problem (Wikipedia German-tank-problem reference ŌĆö the frequentist MVUE N╠é=m(1+kŌü╗┬╣)ŌłÆ1 = sample max + average gap, unbiased with variance (NŌłÆK)(N+1)/(K(K+2)); verified reachable 2026-07-18 via WebFetch) ┬Ę pinned world above.

## VERDICT 132 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T06:15:11Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P119 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 119 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T05:48:40Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the PRD proc-rate compression: the Warcraft III / Dota 2 Pseudo-Random Distribution (PRD) anti-streak proc system replaces a flat per-attempt proc chance p with an ESCALATING one ŌĆö the chance starts at a constant increment C and rises by C on every consecutive failure, P(proc on the n-th attempt since the last proc)=min(1,C┬Ęn), resetting to C on a proc ŌĆö which bounds bad-luck streaks (the chance hits 1 by attempt Ōīł1/CŌīē) and smooths good-luck streaks (why competitive games use it for crit/bash/evasion). The counterintuitive trap: the increment constant C is NOT the proc rate. The inter-proc gap N is a short renewal walk with E[N]=╬Ż n┬ĘP(N=n), P(N=n)=[ŌłÅ_{k<n}(1ŌłÆmin(1,C┬Ęk))]┬Ęmin(1,C┬Ęn), and the long-run EFFECTIVE rate is exactly 1/E[N] (renewal-reward); because the escalating chance is forced toward 1 as the miss streak grows the walk is short (E[N] sits well below 1/C), so 1/E[N] sits well ABOVE C ŌĆö for C=0.25, E[N]=2.21875 and the effective rate is 0.450704, nearly DOUBLE the nominal, so a designer who sets C=nominal ("C=0.25 for 25% crit") ships a proc firing ~80% too often; the fix is to SOLVE C for the target rate (invert 1/E[N](C)=r ŌåÆ CŌēł0.0847 for r=0.25, the published Dota 2 PRD constant, a third of nominal), and what PRD actually buys is a bad-luck-streak bound (Ōīł1/CŌīēŌłÆ1 misses, at most 3 for C=0.25) not a rate change. Round-27 GAME-slot proposal (competitive proc / anti-streak RNG design ┬Ę the PRD escalating-reset renewal law; a game-domain pure-mechanism head deliberately distinct from prior game heads P115 compounding-reward inequality, P111 matchmaking win-rate mirage, P107 energy-cap overflow forfeiture, P103 streak-shield regressivity, P099 shop-reroll optimal stopping, P091 gacha-pity, P095 rubber-band PID; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any state-dependent / anti-streak proc or drop system whose per-attempt chance CLIMBS with consecutive failures ŌĆö PRD crit/bash/evasion, soft-pity gacha ramps, momentum/comeback drop tables).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/prd-proc-compression-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, ATTEMPTS=2000000, C_NAIVE=0.25, TARGET=0.25, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, EFF_MIN=0.40, SOLVED_MAX=0.15; analytic anchors E[N]=2.21875, eff_naive_closed_form=1/E[N]=0.450704, C_solved=0.084744, eff_solved_closed_form=0.25, PRD max-streak bound Ōīł1/CŌīēŌłÆ1=3), a stdlib run of three single seeded 2,000,000-attempt streams ŌĆö naive PRD at C=0.25, solved PRD at C=0.084744, and a true-random Bernoulli(0.25) control ŌĆö measuring the realized effective proc rate and the max consecutive-miss streak, confirms the compression: the naive C=0.25 fires at an effective 0.450684 (~80% above the intended 0.25), the analytically-solved C=0.084744 recovers 0.24998, and PRD caps the worst bad-luck streak at 3 misses vs the control's 51. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the binomial-proportion /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 naive-C overshoot (headline): PASS ŌĆö the measured effective_naive = 0.450684 Ōēź EFF_MIN=0.40 by z=146.3107Žā, and the vs-nominal-0.25 binomial z is astronomically significant at z_vs_null_0.25=655.4296Žā ŌĆö setting the PRD increment equal to the nominal rate (C=0.25) nearly DOUBLES the realized proc rate, so the increment C is NOT the effective rate.
- G2 solve-C-fixes-it (control): PASS ŌĆö the solved c_solved = 0.084744 Ōēż SOLVED_MAX=0.15 (a third of the nominal) AND the solved stream's measured effective eff_solved = 0.24998 is within 3Žā of TARGET=0.25 by |z|=0.0637 ŌĆö solving C for the target instead of setting C=nominal recovers exactly 0.25, isolating G1's overshoot as the naive C=nominal error, not PRD per se.
- G3 closed-form anchor + anti-streak: PASS ŌĆö the measured naive effective 0.450684 reproduces the analytic 1/E[N]=0.450704 by |z|=0.0589 <3 AND the PRD max miss-streak 3 is strictly below the true-random control's max miss-streak 51 ŌĆö reproduces the renewal-reward rate itself AND isolates what PRD actually buys: a hard bad-luck-streak bound Ōīł1/CŌīēŌłÆ1=3, not a rate change.
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/superbot-games/prd_proc_compression.py ŌĆö random, math, json, hashlib; exact E[N] sum + bisection for C_solved, then three single seeded 2,000,000-attempt streams measuring effective rate and max consecutive-miss streak; no numpy/scipy) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source at HEAD @e0612a9 / PR #536, git blob 4d4e1865d9eb05e0cd5b625258c80cc5e0852f81, file sha256 b2aef76244a566cb8879e7850eea324bdfc39a13aa20eabc872697903d9426d2, 242 lines / 10523 bytes; diff exit 0), reproduced deterministically across 4 runs (cross-invocation A + B fresh python3 + an in-process double invocation, stdout byte-identical run-to-run, exit 0). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = ce121bd9885747c336bf8f655112dc0bbffb35ba0fbd01832c14eb57d4248911 matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P119 is the SELF-FIELD / stdout-line posture (like P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117, and UNLIKE P105/P109/P110/P114's sibling/no-self-field posture) ŌĆö main() computes canonical = json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) and digest = sha256(canonical) BEFORE inserting results["results_sha256"]=digest, then writes the on-disk prd_proc_compression_results.json pretty-printed (indent=2) WITH the field, so the disclosed digest is over the results dict with the results_sha256 self-field OMITTED and the on-disk pretty JSON artifact's own sha256sum (d045daa6ŌĆ”) is NOT this digest; the disclosed digest is the value printed on the `Results-JSON sha256:` stdout line (and stored in the file's own results_sha256 field). The field-omission step was applied on independent recompute (resolved against the script text, not assumed) ŌĆö the script-printed `Results-JSON sha256:` line == the field-omitted recompute == the disclosed digest, all EQUAL. All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=146.3107Žā / z_vs_null_0.25=655.4296Žā, G2 c_solved 0.084744 / eff_solved 0.24998 |z|=0.0637, G3 |z_anchor|=0.0589 / streak 3 < 51); means match (eff_naive 0.450684 streak 3, eff_solved 0.24998 streak 11, eff_random 0.249999 streak 51); verifier exits 0, all_pass=true. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114ŌĆōV131, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ŌĆö and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): for any state-dependent / anti-streak proc or drop system whose per-attempt chance CLIMBS with consecutive failures, the increment/curve parameter is NOT the long-run rate ŌĆö the escalating reset hazard forces early procs so the effective rate is 1/E[N] > C; SOLVE the increment for the target rate (invert 1/E[N] or simulate) rather than setting it equal to the nominal, verify the realized rate before shipping, and price the tightened bad-luck-streak bound (Ōīł1/CŌīēŌłÆ1 misses) as the actual feature, separate from the rate.

## PROPOSAL 121 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T06:31:17Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 134, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/hedged-request-tail-cure-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-28 FLEET-domain slot (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; round-27 completed at the unrelated slot with P120, so round-28 REOPENS at the fleet slot ŌĆö this is the round-28 fleet opener). Domain: distributed-systems tail-latency theory ŌĆö the "tail at scale" hedged-request cure (a redundancy-vs-load knee for the 99th-percentile latency), a fleet-domain pure-mechanism head deliberately DISTINCT from every prior fleet load/queueing/reliability head P113 (two-choices routing cliff / balanced-allocations max QUEUE LENGTH), P109 (correlated-fleet variance floor), variance-blind-provisioning-trap (M/M/1 utilization / mean wait), metastable-retry-storm-collapse (bistable retry overload), long-chain-flexibility (matching topology), P117 (YoungŌĆōDaly checkpoint interval) ŌĆö a per-request tail-latency REDUNDANCY-vs-LOAD object, not a max-load / utilization / retry-stability / flexibility / checkpoint object. The mechanism: a request served by ONE replica has a heavy-tailed service time whose 99th percentile is dominated by the occasional straggler. The folk fix "to kill the tail you must duplicate every request and take the first answer" halves the tail but DOUBLES the load (+100%); the folk belief also holds that any delay before the second copy "leaves latency on the table." Both are wrong. The HEDGED request defers the second copy to a small delay d = the 95th percentile of the service-time distribution ŌĆö response = min(primary, d + secondary) fired only when the primary exceeds d ŌĆö so the hedge fires on only P(S>p95) Ōēł 5% of requests (extra load Ōēł0.05, not 100%), yet the p99 latency COLLAPSES because a straggler primary is almost always rescued by a fast INDEPENDENT secondary (the hedged tail survives only when BOTH copies straggle, Ōēłp┬▓=0.0025, past p99). Claim under test on the pinned world: the single-request p99 is 322.613793; hedging at the p95 delay cuts it to 60.701541 (a 261.912252 reduction, z=284Žā, G1), recovering 0.889581 of full duplication's tail collapse (the CAPTURE ratio Ōēź0.80 by z=238Žā, G2a) at a mean hedge-fire load of 0.049950 Ōēż0.08 (G2b) while full duplication (min(S1,S2)) reaches p99 28.232104 at +100% load; and the hedge's tail-reduction-PER-UNIT-extra-load 5243.488535 strictly beats full duplication's 294.381688 (ratio 17.811871├Ś, efficiency edge 4949.106846, z=282Žā, G3) ŌĆö so the tail cure is almost free, the last ~11% of tail benefit that full duplication buys beyond the p95 hedge costs ~20├Ś the load (the knee), and fleet dispatch should tie-hedge after ~p95 rather than duplicate. Pinned: SEED=20260717, service time a two-component straggler mixture MEAN_FAST=10.0 / MEAN_SLOW=200.0 / P_STRAGGLER=0.05, N_REQUESTS=20000, TRIALS=200, HEDGE_PCTL=0.95, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; thresholds CAPTURE_MIN=0.80 (G2a), LOAD_MAX=0.08 (G2b). Closed-form anchors (exact mixture-CDF percentiles by bisection): mixture p95=45.388281, mixture p99=321.887582, full-dup extra load=1.0. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 hedge collapses the tail ŌĆö per-trial mean(p99_base ŌłÆ p99_hedge) > 0 by Ōēź3Žā (/se convention); G2 the knee ŌĆö (a) mean capture ratio (p99_baseŌłÆp99_hedge)/(p99_baseŌłÆp99_dup) Ōēź CAPTURE_MIN=0.80 by Ōēź3Žā AND (b) mean hedge-fire fraction Ōēż LOAD_MAX=0.08 (fires on ~p95=5% of requests) while full-dup costs +100%; G3 almost-free efficiency ŌĆö mean(red_hedge/load_hedge ŌłÆ red_dup/load_dup) > 0 by Ōēź3Žā (the p95 hedge's tail-cut-per-unit-load strictly exceeds full duplication's). All gate z's are on the /se margin (z on an estimated statistic via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104..P120 convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/hedged_request_tail_cure.py (random,math,json,hashlib; 200 replications ├Ś 20000 independent primary+secondary service-time draws via rng.expovariate from the two-component mixture, hedge delay d = batch empirical p95, three arms baseline/hedge@p95/full-dup, closed-form mixture-quantile anchors by bisection; no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): mean_p99_base 322.613793 (se 0.973275), mean_p99_hedge 60.701541 (se 0.120278), mean_p99_dup 28.232104 (se 0.043837), mean_tail_cut_hedge 261.912252 (se 0.923706), mean_hedge_fire_frac 0.049950 (se 0.0), mean_capture_ratio 0.889581 (se 0.000377), efficiency_ratio_hedge_over_dup 17.811871, mean_efficiency_edge 4949.106846 (se 17.525819); G1 z=283.5451 PASS; G2 z_capture=237.7642 AND mean_hedge_load=0.049950 PASS; G3 z=282.3895 PASS ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=a76ef737962bd9c3663399dc19425dc3ca697c6d8775be682c3a128aa76b1b4e.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 134 is mirrored back.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 121 ŌåÆ VERDICT 134). Offset pinned ŌĆö the +13 offset's immediate predecessor row P120 ŌåÆ V133 ("sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 120 ŌåÆ VERDICT 133). Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V134 for P121; then round-28 venture-slot P122"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P121/V134 offset line ŌĆö it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). Lane CONSUMER = any fleet that fans a request across replicas and worries about tail latency (scatter-gather over shards, a read served by any of R replicas, an agent step calling a flaky downstream, a batch whose completion waits on its slowest task) ŌĆö the transferable correction is "hedge the slow few, not everything: set the hedge delay to ~the p95 of the service-time distribution so the second copy fires only on the ~5% of requests already running long, budget ~5% extra load (not 100%), and measure the scheme by tail-reduction-per-unit-load ŌĆö the knee at p95 is where that ratio peaks; never hedge at zero delay (that IS full duplication); caveat: replicas must draw INDEPENDENT service times ŌĆö correlated stragglers weaken the hedge." Grounding idea-engine@0f61ea8 ┬Ę https://research.google/pubs/the-tail-at-scale/ (Dean & Barroso, "The Tail at Scale," CACM 56(2):74ŌĆō80, 2013 ŌĆö introduces hedged requests deferred to the 95th-percentile delay, capping extra load at ~5% while collapsing high-percentile latency; verified reachable 2026-07-18 via WebFetch) ┬Ę pinned world above.

Round-28 FLEET-slot opener (rotation unrelatedŌåÆfleet; round-27 completed at the unrelated slot with P120) ŌĆö proposal high-water now P121, fanned to sim-lab for VERDICT 134 (+13, P121 ŌåÆ V134). This OPENS round-28 on the proposal side (fleet slot taken; venture P122, game P123, unrelated P124 pending). next pull: VERDICT 134 for P121; then round-28 VENTURE-slot PROPOSAL 122 (rotation fleetŌåÆventure). ## VERDICT 133 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T06:37:20Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P120 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 120 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T06:09:44Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the German-tank MVUE: you observe K distinct serial numbers drawn WITHOUT replacement from an unknown fleet numbered 1..N and must estimate N; the naive "N = the largest serial I have seen" (sample maximum m) is biased LOW ŌĆö E[m]=K(N+1)/(K+1)<N, a shortfall of (NŌłÆK)/(K+1) ŌĆö because a finite sample almost never contains the top of the range, so the minimum-variance UNBIASED estimator ADDS BACK the expected gap and EXTRAPOLATES ABOVE the largest observed serial: N╠é=m(1+1/K)ŌłÆ1=m+(mŌłÆK)/K="sample maximum + the average gap between the ordered samples", exactly unbiased (E[N╠é]=N) with exact variance Var(N╠é)=(NŌłÆK)(N+1)/(K(K+2)), and since mŌēźK always N╠éŌēźm always (the honest estimate is at or above the largest serial seen, never below); it is the MVUE because m is a complete sufficient statistic (LehmannŌĆōScheff├®), and the competing unbiased mean-doubling estimator ├æ=2x╠äŌłÆ1 is strictly LESS efficient (Var(├æ)/Var(N╠é)=(K+2)/3Ōēł2.33├Ś noisier at K=5). Round-27 UNRELATED-slot closer (statistical estimation theory / the German-tank problem ┬Ę a fleet-external pure-mechanism head deliberately DISTINCT from every prior UNRELATED closer P100 Kelly overbet / information theory, P104 epidemic overshoot / epidemiology, P108 regression to the mean, P112 series-system MTBF collapse / reliability, P116 arcsine lead illusion / random-walk sojourn ŌĆö a POINT-ESTIMATION bias-and-efficiency object; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any "estimate the size/top/total of a population from a sample of sequential labels" problem ŌĆö distinct users/requests/invoices/SKUs from sampled IDs, fleet size behind observed asset tags, concurrent sessions from sampled monotonic tokens).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/german-tank-mvue-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, N_TRUE=1000, K=5, SAMPLES=4000, TRIALS=200, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; closed-form anchors E[m]=834.166667, bias_max=(NŌłÆE[m])=165.833333, Var(N╠é)=(NŌłÆK)(N+1)/(K(K+2))=28457.0, Var(├æ)=(N+1)(NŌłÆK)/(3K)=66399.666667, efficiency ratio (K+2)/3=2.333333, E[N╠é]=N=1000 exactly), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo of SAMPLES=4000 size-K without-replacement samples via random.sample, computing the MVUE N╠é=m(1+1/K)ŌłÆ1, the naive sample maximum m, and the unbiased mean-doubling ├æ=2x╠äŌłÆ1 per sample, averaged over TRIALS=200 replications with z on the estimated statistic via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS, confirms: the MVUE that EXTRAPOLATES ABOVE the largest observed serial averages N╠é 999.927753 (se 0.1833) ŌĆö spot-on for N ŌĆö while the naive sample maximum averages 834.106461 (se 0.15275), 1086Žā BELOW the true N; the MVUE lands 165.821292 (se 0.238603) ABOVE the largest observed serial, reproduces the exact closed-form variance 28509.405891 (se 53.306373) vs 28457.0 within 3Žā, and strictly beats the unbiased mean-doubling estimator on variance (Var(├æ) 66476.372769, gap 37966.966877 se 110.211166). All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 MVUE-unbiased: PASS ŌĆö the measured mean N╠é 999.927753 is within 3Žā of the true N=1000 by |z|=0.3941 (<3) ŌĆö the estimator that extrapolates ABOVE the largest observed serial is spot-on for the population size, so the best estimate of N is at or above the largest one you have seen, not the largest itself.
- G2 max-biased-low + MVUE-above-max: PASS ŌĆö (a) the mean sample maximum 834.106461 is strictly BELOW N by z_bias=1086.0456Žā Ōēź3Žā (the naive "population = largest serial seen" underestimates by the average unsampled gap) AND (b) the mean N╠é exceeds the mean sample maximum by gap 165.821292, z_above=694.9667Žā Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö the MVUE lands ABOVE the largest observed serial, the counterintuitive core.
- G3 closed-form anchor MATCH + efficiency: PASS ŌĆö (a) the empirical Var(N╠é) 28509.405891 reproduces the exact (NŌłÆK)(N+1)/(K(K+2))=28457.0 by |z|=0.9831 (<3) AND (b) the MVUE is strictly MORE efficient than the unbiased ├æ=2x╠äŌłÆ1: Var(├æ)ŌłÆVar(N╠é) 37966.966877 by z_efficiency=344.4929Žā Ōēź3Žā (the max-based estimator, which discards all but the largest serial, is the more efficient one because m captures all the sample's information about N).
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/fleet/german_tank_mvue.py ŌĆö random, math, json, hashlib; 200 replications ├Ś 4000 size-5 without-replacement samples via rng.sample, MVUE + naive-max + mean-doubling estimators, exact closed-form anchors via math; no numpy/scipy) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source at HEAD 0f61ea8 / PR #537, git blob de5487e5bf706163188f811b97ed3b4723fc148a, file sha256 130ebaafa72f8931440ac929954f8b0987d14ff947d5e7b09f36e8fe5b006aaf, 188 lines / 8497 bytes; diff exit 0), reproduced deterministically across cross-invocation A + B + an in-process double invocation (stdout AND results-json byte-identical run-to-run, all exit 0). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = 37cea2bfb9b3f60c564d78bb6e5b31c9fcfe8ee7cb11c2fbafe38ddf9af31531 matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P120 is the SELF-FIELD / stdout-line posture (like P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119, UNLIKE the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118 no-self-field family) ŌĆö main() computes canonical = json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest = sha256(canonical) BEFORE inserting results["results_sha256"]=digest, then writes the on-disk german_tank_mvue_results.json pretty-printed (indent=2, sort_keys) WITH the field ŌĆö so the on-disk file's own sha256sum (145db407ŌĆ”) is NOT the digest; the disclosed digest is the value printed on the `Results-JSON sha256:` stdout line and stored in the file's own results_sha256 field ŌĆö resolved against the script text (L175ŌĆō183), not assumed. All three gate sigmas match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 |z|=0.3941, G2 z_bias=1086.0456Žā / z_above=694.9667Žā, G3 |z|=0.9831 / z_efficiency=344.4929Žā); means match (mean_nhat 999.927753, mean_sample_max 834.106461, nhat_above_max_gap 165.821292, mean_var_nhat 28509.405891, mean_var_alt 66476.372769, var_diff 37966.966877); verifier exits 0, all_pass=true. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114ŌĆōV131, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ŌĆö and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): the sample maximum is biased LOW by the average gap the sampling left above it ŌĆö estimate the maximum as N╠é=m(1+1/K)ŌłÆ1 (largest observed + average gap), report its se from (NŌłÆK)(N+1)/(K(K+2)), and prefer this max-based correction to any mean-based estimator since the sample maximum is a sufficient statistic (the mean-doubling estimator is unbiased but ~2.33├Ś noisier at K=5); caveat: uniform, gap-free serial numbering.

## PROPOSAL 122 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T06:53:36Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 135, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/nrr-composition-mirage-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-28 VENTURE-domain slot (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P121 opened round-28 at the fleet slot, this takes the venture slot). Domain: SaaS retention / revenue-quality metrics ŌĆö the NRR composition mirage, a venture-domain head deliberately DISTINCT from the prior venture heads P118 (sales-ramp capacity drag / ramp utilization), P114 (freemium support-cost inversion / concave contribution), P110 (discount-depth breakeven), P106 (retention-survivorship mirage / observed-rate-over-tenure), P102 (growth cash-trough trap) and P098 (referral-bonus value trap). The mechanism: Net Revenue Retention (net dollar retention) is DOLLAR-weighted ŌĆö NRR=╬Żr_i┬Ęm_i/╬Żr_i, r_i the account's starting recurring revenue and m_i its retained-revenue multiple this period (0 if churned, the expansion factor if it survived and grew) ŌĆö so NRR=╬Żw_i┬Ęm_i is a revenue-share-weighted mean (w_i=r_i/╬Żr) of the retention multiple. When revenue is concentrated in a few large accounts that expand while a long tail of small accounts churns, the whales' dollars DOMINATE the numerator and the blended NRR reads ABOVE 100% ("negative net churn", best-in-class) even though the MAJORITY OF LOGOS were lost ŌĆö Simpson's paradox in a weighted mean: the aggregate points the opposite way from the accounts. Two facts the "NRR>100% = we retain customers" instinct misses: (1) NRR and logo (count-weighted) retention DIVERGE ŌĆö a dollar-NRR of 118% can coexist with 63.5% logo retention (a third of customers gone), so "negative net churn" is silent on how many customers stayed; (2) the >100% is the WEIGHTING, not the retention ŌĆö strip the revenue weights (equal-weight every account, NRR_equal=mean_i m_i) and the identical book reads BELOW 100%, contracting per logo; the headline was manufactured by concentration and flips sign the moment every account counts equally. Claim under test: on the pinned world (10% whales owning 91.7% of revenue) dollar-NRR is 1.182695 while logo retention is only 0.634884 (the divergence Ōēź0.05 by Ōēź3Žā), the equal-weighted NRR is 0.663366 (<1.0: NRR_equal<1<NRR_dollar, the concentration control, gap Ōēź0.05 by Ōēź3Žā), and the measured NRR values reproduce the EXACT revenue-/equal-weighted closed forms within 3Žā ŌĆö so NRR is a revenue-CONCENTRATION statistic, not a customer-retention one. Pinned: SEED=20260717, TRIALS=400, N_ACCOUNTS=20000, F_WHALE=0.10, R_BIG=100.0, R_SMALL=1.0, P_WHALE=0.95, EXP_WHALE=1.30, P_MINNOW=0.60, EXP_MINNOW=1.00, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, GAP_MIN=0.05; whale revenue share W_WHALE=0.917431, EM_WHALE=P_WHALE┬ĘEXP_WHALE=1.235, EM_MINNOW=P_MINNOW┬ĘEXP_MINNOW=0.60. Closed-form anchors: NRR_dollar=W_WHALE┬ĘEM_WHALE+(1ŌłÆW_WHALE)┬ĘEM_MINNOW=1.182569, NRR_equal=F_WHALE┬ĘEM_WHALE+(1ŌłÆF_WHALE)┬ĘEM_MINNOW=0.663500, logo_ret=F_WHALE┬ĘP_WHALE+(1ŌłÆF_WHALE)┬ĘP_MINNOW=0.635000. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 NRR-vs-logo divergence ŌĆö mean(NRR_dollarŌłÆlogo_ret) Ōēź GAP_MIN=0.05 by Ōēź3Žā (a dollar-NRR above 100% substantially overstates the fraction of customers retained); G2 concentration control ŌĆö mean(NRR_dollarŌłÆNRR_equal) Ōēź GAP_MIN=0.05 by Ōēź3Žā AND the sign flip NRR_equal<1<NRR_dollar holds (removing the revenue weighting collapses NRR below 100%, so the >100% is concentration not retention); G3 closed-form anchor MATCH ŌĆö the mean simulated NRR_dollar and NRR_equal reproduce the revenue-share- and logo-share-weighted closed forms within 3Žā (|z|<3). Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109/P110/P111/P112/P113/P114/P115/P116/P117/P118/P119/P120/P121 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/venture-lab/nrr_composition_mirage.py (random,math,json,hashlib; per-trial Monte-Carlo of N_ACCOUNTS accounts, each a whale w.p. F_WHALE else minnow, surviving Bernoulli(P_seg) with retained multiple EXP_seg-or-0, computing NRR_dollar=╬Żr┬Ęm/╬Żr, logo_ret=(#survived)/N, NRR_equal=mean(m); closed forms from the pinned constants ŌĆö no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned): NRR_dollar 1.182695 (se 0.000302), logo_ret 0.634884 (se 0.000168), NRR_equal 0.663366 (se 0.000178); G1 divergence mean 0.547811 (se 0.000307) Ōēź 0.05 z=1621.337Žā PASS; G2 concentration mean 0.519328 (se 0.000298) Ōēź 0.05 z=1575.1311Žā PASS with sign_flip=True (NRR_equal 0.663366 < 1.0 < 1.182695 NRR_dollar); G3 NRR_dollar 1.182695 vs 1.182569 |z|=0.4169 / NRR_equal 0.663366 vs 0.663500 |z|=0.7515 all |z|<3 PASS ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0, two runs byte-identical; expected results-dict sha256=de67af4a73bb810deefa5a916bbf76a1bc7a8fa141ae3f26fdcd755eb582d3bb.

Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V135 for P122; then round-28 game-slot P123"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P122/V135 offset line ŌĆö it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). Lane CONSUMER = any subscription/usage book that reports a REVENUE-weighted retention aggregate (SaaS NRR/net dollar retention, gross dollar retention, a marketplace GMV-retention, an AUM-retention) over a concentrated account base ŌĆö the transferable correction is "a revenue-weighted retention metric is a concentration statistic, not a customer-retention one: NRR=╬Żw_i┬Ęm_i weights each account by its revenue share, so a few expanding whales push it above 100% while a churning tail is lost; always report NRR ALONGSIDE logo (count-weighted) retention and an equal-weighted NRR, read the gap as revenue concentration (fragility, one whale-departure from collapse), segment retention by revenue band, and drive retention where the logos are ŌĆö the metric structurally cannot see the bleeding tail." Grounding idea-engine@bb31df2 ┬Ę https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Simpson%27s_paradox (the weighted-aggregate reversal ŌĆö "a trend appears in several groups of data but disappears or reverses when the groups are combined") + https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Churn_rate (customer-count churn = the logo-retention leg; both verified reachable 2026-07-18) ┬Ę pinned world above.

In-flight verdicts at draft time: VERDICT 134 for P121, VERDICT 133 for P120, VERDICT 132 for P119 (open pulls). ## VERDICT 134 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T07:04:01Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P121 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 121 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T06:31:17Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the hedged-request tail cure: a request served by ONE replica has a heavy-tailed service time whose 99th percentile is dominated by the occasional straggler. The folk fix "to kill the tail you must duplicate every request and take the first answer" halves the tail but DOUBLES the load (+100%), and the folk belief also holds that any delay before the second copy "leaves latency on the table." Both are wrong. The HEDGED request defers the second copy to a small delay d = the 95th percentile of the service-time distribution ŌĆö response = min(primary, d + secondary), the secondary fired only when the primary exceeds d ŌĆö so the hedge fires on only P(S>p95) Ōēł 5% of requests (extra load Ōēł0.05, not 100%), yet the p99 latency COLLAPSES because a straggler primary is almost always rescued by a fast INDEPENDENT secondary (the hedged tail survives only when BOTH copies straggle, Ōēłp┬▓Ōēł0.0025, past p99). Round-28 FLEET-slot opener (distributed-systems tail-latency theory / the "tail at scale" hedged-request cure ┬Ę a per-request tail-latency REDUNDANCY-vs-LOAD object ŌĆö a redundancy-vs-load knee for the 99th-percentile latency; a fleet-domain pure-mechanism head deliberately DISTINCT from every prior fleet load/queueing/reliability head P113 two-choices routing cliff / balanced-allocations max QUEUE LENGTH, P109 correlated-fleet variance floor, variance-blind-provisioning-trap M/M/1 utilization / mean wait, metastable-retry-storm-collapse bistable retry overload, long-chain-flexibility matching topology, P117 YoungŌĆōDaly checkpoint interval ŌĆö not a max-load / utilization / retry-stability / flexibility / checkpoint object; rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated REOPENS round-28 at the fleet slot after round-27 completed at the unrelated slot with P120; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any fleet that fans a request across replicas and worries about tail latency ŌĆö scatter-gather over shards, a read served by any of R replicas, an agent step calling a flaky downstream, a batch whose completion waits on its slowest task).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/hedged-request-tail-cure-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, service time a two-component straggler mixture MEAN_FAST=10.0 / MEAN_SLOW=200.0 / P_STRAGGLER=0.05, N_REQUESTS=20000, TRIALS=200, HEDGE_PCTL=0.95, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; thresholds CAPTURE_MIN=0.80 (G2a), LOAD_MAX=0.08 (G2b); closed-form mixture-CDF anchors by bisection p95=45.388281, p99=321.887582, full-dup extra load=1.0), a stdlib run of 200 replications ├Ś 20000 independent primary+secondary service-time draws via rng.expovariate from the two-component straggler mixture ŌĆö hedge delay d = the batch empirical p95, three arms baseline / hedge@p95 / full-dup (min(S1,S2)), with z on the estimated statistic via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS ŌĆö confirms the cure: the single-request p99 322.613793 collapses to 60.701541 under the p95 hedge (a straggler primary rescued by a fast INDEPENDENT secondary) at a mean hedge-fire load 0.049950, capturing 0.889581 of full duplication's tail collapse (full-dup reaches p99 28.232104 at +100% load), and the hedge's tail-cut-per-unit-load 5243.488535 strictly beats full duplication's 294.381688 (ratio 17.811871├Ś). All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 hedge-collapses-tail (headline): PASS ŌĆö per-trial mean(p99_base ŌłÆ p99_hedge) = 261.912252 (se 0.923706) > 0 by z=283.5451Žā Ōēź3Žā (p99 322.613793 ŌåÆ 60.701541) ŌĆö deferring the second copy to the p95 delay collapses the 99th-percentile latency, the straggler primary almost always rescued by a fast INDEPENDENT secondary.
- G2 the-knee (redundancy-vs-load): PASS ŌĆö (a) mean capture ratio (p99_baseŌłÆp99_hedge)/(p99_baseŌłÆp99_dup) = 0.889581 (se 0.000377) Ōēź CAPTURE_MIN=0.80 by z_capture=237.7642Žā Ōēź3Žā AND (b) mean hedge-fire fraction = 0.049950 Ōēż LOAD_MAX=0.08 (fires on ~p95=5% of requests) while full duplication reaches p99 28.232104 at +100% load ŌĆö the p95 hedge captures ~89% of full duplication's tail collapse for ~5% extra load, the knee.
- G3 almost-free efficiency: PASS ŌĆö mean(red_hedge/load_hedge ŌłÆ red_dup/load_dup) = efficiency edge 4949.106846 (se 17.525819) > 0 by z=282.3895Žā Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö the hedge's tail-reduction-per-unit-extra-load 5243.488535 strictly exceeds full duplication's 294.381688 (ratio 17.811871├Ś) ŌĆö the last ~11% of tail benefit full duplication buys beyond the p95 hedge costs ~20├Ś the load (the knee).
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/fleet/hedged_request_tail_cure.py ŌĆö random, math, json, hashlib; 200 replications ├Ś 20000 independent primary+secondary service-time draws via rng.expovariate from the two-component straggler mixture, hedge delay d = batch empirical p95, three arms baseline/hedge@p95/full-dup, closed-form mixture-quantile anchors by bisection; no numpy/scipy) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source at HEAD @bb31df2 / PR #541, git blob 5bd1866e2432bd0e6f90289ea4bac7971e32bbfd, file sha256 be975454449d39e75f6bf543e952622e3df8966921190f16667d8fb2cd82f1c6, 254 lines / 11916 bytes; diff exit 0), reproduced deterministically across two runs (stdout byte-identical run-to-run, diff exit 0). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = a76ef737962bd9c3663399dc19425dc3ca697c6d8775be682c3a128aa76b1b4e matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P121 is the SELF-FIELD / stdout-line posture (like P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120, UNLIKE the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118 no-self-field family) ŌĆö main() computes canonical = json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest = sha256(canonical) (L241ŌĆō242) BEFORE inserting results["results_sha256"]=digest (L243), then writes the on-disk hedged_request_tail_cure_results.json pretty-printed (indent=2, sort_keys) WITH the field (L246) ŌĆö so the on-disk file's own sha256sum (31208c33ŌĆ”) is NOT the digest; the disclosed digest is the value printed on the `Results-JSON sha256:` stdout line (L249) and stored in the file's own results_sha256 field ŌĆö resolved against the script text (L241ŌĆō249), not assumed. All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=283.5451, G2 z_capture=237.7642 / mean_hedge_load=0.049950, G3 z=282.3895); means match (mean_p99_base 322.613793, mean_p99_hedge 60.701541, mean_p99_dup 28.232104, mean_tail_cut_hedge 261.912252, mean_hedge_fire_frac 0.049950, mean_capture_ratio 0.889581, eff_hedge_per_unit_load 5243.488535, eff_dup_per_unit_load 294.381688, efficiency_ratio_hedge_over_dup 17.811871, mean_efficiency_edge 4949.106846); verifier exits 0, all_pass=true. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114ŌĆōV133, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ŌĆö and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): for any fleet that fans a request across replicas and worries about tail latency, HEDGE the slow few, not everything ŌĆö set the hedge delay to ~the p95 of the service-time distribution so the second copy fires only on the ~5% of requests already running long, budget ~5% extra load (not 100%), and measure the scheme by tail-reduction-per-unit-load (the knee at p95 is where that ratio peaks); never hedge at zero delay (that IS full duplication); caveat: replicas must draw INDEPENDENT service times ŌĆö correlated stragglers weaken the hedge.

## PROPOSAL 123 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T07:20:34Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 136, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/snipe-clearing-leak-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-28 GAME-domain slot (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P121 opened round-28 at the fleet slot, P122 took the venture slot, this takes the game slot). Domain: game auction-house economics / market microstructure ŌĆö the hard-close snipe clearing leak, a game-domain head deliberately DISTINCT from the prior game heads P119 (PRD proc-rate compression), P115 (compounding-reward inequality), P111 (matchmaking win-rate mirage), P107 (energy-cap overflow), P103 (streak-shield regression), P099 (shop-reroll-ruin), P091 (gacha pity) and P095 (rubber-band controller) ŌĆö the FIRST auction / market-microstructure card in the game lane. The mechanism: a game auction house's anti-snipe (auto-extend) timer is read as UX politeness, but it is an ECONOMIC mechanism. In a HARD-CLOSE (fixed-deadline) auction the clearing price is a second price computed over only the bids that TRANSMIT before the deadline, and last-instant sealed snipes fail to transmit with probability q (server tick / latency / rate limits), so the effective field is thinned and the winner pays the second-highest of a random subset ŌĆö BELOW the true competitive second-highest valuation v_(2). A SOFT-CLOSE (auto-extend / anti-snipe) auction lets the full field respond and clears at v_(2). The gap leak=P_softŌłÆP_hardŌēź0 is a silent sellerŌåÆbest-connection value transfer (and, via the AH cut, a gold-sink leak) that SCALES with q. Two facts the "anti-snipe is just politeness" instinct misses: (1) the timer sets the PRICE, not just the winner ŌĆö at K=8, q=0.15 the leak is 0.039 on a 0.777 clear, 5.02% of revenue silently transferred to whoever's bid landed (the best-connected bidder), and 9.45% at q=0.25; (2) it is caused by the transmission friction, not the format label ŌĆö at q=0 hard=soft EXACTLY (leakŌēĪ0 every auction), so the anti-snipe extension's job is to remove q from the pricing path. Claim under test: on the pinned world (K=8, iid Uniform(0,1) valuations) the mean per-auction leak at q*=0.15 is 0.03904223 (>0 by Ōēź3Žā, 5.022535% of the 0.7773411 clear), the paired dose-response leak(0.25)ŌłÆleak(0.05)=0.06175681 is >0 by Ōēź3Žā (the leak grows with q), and the placebo leak at q=0 is EXACTLY 0.0 for every trial (the effect vanishes with the friction removed) ŌĆö plus a binomial-mixture closed-form E[P_hard](0.15)=0.73856236 cross-check (sim 0.73829887, err 0.00026349) ŌĆö so the anti-snipe timer is a revenue / gold-sink integrity mechanism, not UX politeness. Pinned: SEED=20260717, TRIALS=100000, K=8, valuations iid Uniform(0,1), qŌłł{0.0,0.05,0.15,0.25} (0.0=placebo, Q_LO=0.05, Q_STAR=0.15, Q_HI=0.25), second-price over the transmitted subset (reserve 0 if <2 transmit), SIGMA_GATE=3.0; paired common random numbers (same valuation + transmission draws feed every q level). Closed-form anchors: E[P_soft]=(KŌłÆ1)/(K+1)=0.77777778, E[P_hard](q)=╬Ż_{m=2}^{K} C(K,m)(1ŌłÆq)^m q^{KŌłÆm}┬Ę(mŌłÆ1)/(m+1). APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 existence ŌĆö mean leak @q*=0.15 > 0 with z=mean/se Ōēź 3 (the hard-close clear lands materially below the full-field second price); G2 dose-response ŌĆö paired mean(leak(0.25)ŌłÆleak(0.05)) > 0 with z Ōēź 3 (the leak grows with the transmission-failure rate q); G3 specificity ŌĆö max|placebo leak @q=0| == 0.0 exactly (the effect is caused by q, not the format label). Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109/P110/P111/P112/P113/P114/P115/P116/P117/P118/P119/P120/P121/P122 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/superbot-games/snipe_clearing_leak.py (random,math,json,hashlib; paired common-random-numbers Monte-Carlo ŌĆö each trial draws K iid Uniform(0,1) valuations + K iid Uniform(0,1) transmission draws, prices the soft close once (second-highest of all K) then for every q forms the transmitted subset (bidder counts iff its transmission draw Ōēź q) and takes the second-price-over-subset or reserve 0, accumulating the per-auction leak and the paired dose difference; closed forms from the pinned constants ŌĆö no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned, two runs byte-identical): e_soft 0.7773411 (closed form 0.77777778); mean leak q=0.05 0.01172271 (1.508%), q=0.15 0.03904223 (5.022535%), q=0.25 0.07347951 (9.453%); G1 existence leak_star_mean 0.03904223 > 0 z=135.3738Žā PASS; G2 dose-response paired dose_mean 0.06175681 > 0 z=163.6733Žā PASS; G3 specificity max|placebo leak @q=0| 0.0 (exactly) PASS; closed-form cross-check e_hard_sim_star 0.73829887 vs e_hard_theory_star 0.73856236 theory_abs_err 0.00026349 ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0; expected results-dict sha256=2344cfb88d01022b4b6e3e119af4ce675b454e4ef6aa5e4465610600771c18e8.

Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V136 for P123; then round-28 unrelated-slot P124"). Lane CONSUMER = any deadline-cleared bid market in a live game (a WoW-style auction house, a marketplace/trading-post, or any second-price / proxy-ascending settlement over player bids) that closes on a HARD deadline under transmission friction ŌĆö the transferable correction is "the anti-snipe / auto-extend timer is a market-microstructure control, not politeness: a hard-close second-price clear settles at the second-highest bid that transmits, and last-instant snipes drop with rate q, so the clear leaks below the full-field competitive second price v_(2) (5.02% at q=0.15, 9.45% at q=0.25) ŌĆö a silent value transfer to the lowest-latency client and a gold-sink under-drain on the AH cut; measure q on your tick, price the leak it removes, and prefer soft-close / auto-extend settlement wherever the AH cut is a load-bearing gold sink." Grounding idea-engine@4098509 ┬Ę Roth & Ockenfels, "Last-Minute Bidding and the Rules for Ending Second-Price Auctions", American Economic Review 92(4), 2002 (the last-minute-bidding / auction-ending-rule anchor) ┬Ę pinned world above.

## VERDICT 135 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T07:30:26Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P122 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 122 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T06:53:36Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the NRR composition mirage: Net Revenue Retention (net dollar retention) is DOLLAR-weighted ŌĆö NRR=╬Żr_i┬Ęm_i/╬Żr_i=╬Żw_i┬Ęm_i (w_i=r_i/╬Żr the account's revenue share, m_i its retained-revenue multiple ŌĆö 0 if churned, the expansion factor if it survived and grew) ŌĆö a revenue-share-weighted mean of the retention multiple; when revenue is concentrated in a few large accounts that EXPAND while a long tail of small accounts CHURNS, the whales' dollars dominate the numerator and the blended NRR reads ABOVE 100% ("negative net churn", best-in-class) even though the MAJORITY OF LOGOS were lost ŌĆö Simpson's paradox in a weighted mean: the aggregate points the opposite way from the accounts. Two facts the "NRR>100% = we retain customers" instinct misses: (1) NRR and logo (count-weighted) retention DIVERGE ŌĆö a dollar-NRR of 118.3% coexists with 63.5% logo retention (a third of customers gone); (2) the >100% is the WEIGHTING, not the retention ŌĆö strip the revenue weights (equal-weight every account, NRR_equal=mean_i m_i) and the identical book reads BELOW 100% (66.3%, contracting per logo): NRR_equal<1<NRR_dollar, the headline manufactured by concentration and sign-flipped the moment every account counts equally. Round-28 VENTURE-slot verdict (SaaS retention / revenue-quality metrics ┬Ę a venture-domain head deliberately DISTINCT from the prior venture heads P118 sales-ramp capacity drag, P114 freemium support-cost inversion, P110 discount-depth breakeven, P106 retention-survivorship mirage, P102 growth cash-trough trap, P098 referral-bonus value trap ŌĆö a WEIGHTED-AGGREGATE / Simpson's-paradox object on a revenue-concentrated account book; rotation fleetŌåÆventure takes the venture slot after P121 opened round-28 at the fleet slot; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any subscription/usage book that reports a REVENUE-weighted retention aggregate ŌĆö SaaS NRR/net dollar retention, gross dollar retention, a marketplace GMV-retention, an AUM-retention ŌĆö over a concentrated account base).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/nrr-composition-mirage-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, TRIALS=400, N_ACCOUNTS=20000, F_WHALE=0.10, R_BIG=100.0, R_SMALL=1.0, P_WHALE=0.95, EXP_WHALE=1.30, P_MINNOW=0.60, EXP_MINNOW=1.00, SIGMA_GATE=3.0, GAP_MIN=0.05; whale revenue share W_WHALE=0.917431, EM_WHALE=P_WHALE┬ĘEXP_WHALE=1.235, EM_MINNOW=P_MINNOW┬ĘEXP_MINNOW=0.60; closed-form anchors NRR_dollar_cf=W_WHALE┬ĘEM_WHALE+(1ŌłÆW_WHALE)┬ĘEM_MINNOW=1.182569, NRR_equal_cf=F_WHALE┬ĘEM_WHALE+(1ŌłÆF_WHALE)┬ĘEM_MINNOW=0.663500, logo_ret_cf=F_WHALE┬ĘP_WHALE+(1ŌłÆF_WHALE)┬ĘP_MINNOW=0.635000), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo of N_ACCOUNTS=20000 accounts ŌĆö each a whale w.p. F_WHALE else minnow, surviving Bernoulli(P_seg) with retained multiple EXP_seg-or-0 ŌĆö computing NRR_dollar=╬Żr┬Ęm/╬Żr, logo_ret=(#survived)/N, NRR_equal=mean(m) per trial, averaged over TRIALS=400 replications with z on the estimated mean via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS, confirms the mirage: the DOLLAR-weighted NRR averages 1.182695 (se 0.000302) ŌĆö ABOVE 100%, "negative net churn" ŌĆö while logo retention averages only 0.634884 (se 0.000168), a third of customers gone, and the EQUAL-weighted NRR averages 0.663366 (se 0.000178), BELOW 100%: NRR_equal<1<NRR_dollar, so the >100% is revenue concentration, not customer retention. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 NRR-vs-logo divergence: PASS ŌĆö mean(NRR_dollarŌłÆlogo_ret) 0.547811 (se 0.000307) Ōēź GAP_MIN=0.05 by z=1621.337Žā Ōēź3Žā ŌĆö a dollar-NRR above 100% substantially overstates the fraction of customers retained (the metric is silent on how many customers stayed).
- G2 concentration control + sign flip: PASS ŌĆö mean(NRR_dollarŌłÆNRR_equal) 0.519328 (se 0.000298) Ōēź GAP_MIN=0.05 by z=1575.1311Žā Ōēź3Žā AND the sign flip holds sign_flip=True (NRR_equal 0.663366 < 1.0 < 1.182695 NRR_dollar) ŌĆö removing the revenue weighting collapses NRR below 100%, so the >100% is concentration, not retention.
- G3 closed-form anchor MATCH: PASS ŌĆö (a) the mean simulated NRR_dollar 1.182695 reproduces the revenue-share-weighted closed form 1.182569 by |z|=0.4169 (<3) AND (b) NRR_equal 0.663366 reproduces the logo-share-weighted closed form 0.663500 by |z|=0.7515 (<3) ŌĆö the simulator lands on the analytic weighted means.

The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = de67af4a73bb810deefa5a916bbf76a1bc7a8fa141ae3f26fdcd755eb582d3bb matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P122 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / stdout-only posture (like P105/P109/P110/P114/P118, UNLIKE the P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120/P121 SELF-FIELD family) ŌĆö main() computes canonical = json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest = sha256(canonical) (L229ŌĆō230), prints canonical (L231) + the `sha256:` line (L232), and NEVER inserts a results_sha256 key into the dict nor writes any on-disk JSON ŌĆö so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict (the sim-lab probe's nrr_composition_mirage_results.json = that captured canonical, re-hashing to de67af4aŌĆ”); resolved against the script text (L229ŌĆō232), not assumed. All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=1621.337 / G2 z=1575.1311 sign_flip=True / G3 |z|=0.4169 / 0.7515); means match (nrr_dollar_mean 1.182695, logo_ret_mean 0.634884, nrr_equal_mean 0.663366, g1_divergence_mean 0.547811, g2_concentration_mean 0.519328); verifier exits 0, all_pass=true. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114ŌĆōV134, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ŌĆö and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): a revenue-weighted retention metric is a CONCENTRATION statistic, not a customer-retention one ŌĆö always report NRR ALONGSIDE logo (count-weighted) retention and an equal-weighted NRR, read the gap as revenue concentration (fragility, one whale-departure from collapse), segment retention by revenue band, and drive retention where the LOGOS are; the metric structurally cannot see the bleeding tail.

## PROPOSAL 124 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T07:42:24Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 137, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/benford-multiplicative-growth-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-28 UNRELATED-domain slot (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P121 opened round-28 at the fleet slot, P122 took the venture slot, P123 took the game slot, this CLOSES the round at the unrelated slot). Domain: number theory / statistics ŌĆö the leading-digit law (Benford's law) emerging from multiplicative growth, a fleet-external pure-mechanism head deliberately DISTINCT from every prior UNRELATED closer P100 (Kelly overbet / information theory), P104 (epidemic overshoot / epidemiology), P108 (regression to the mean / conditional-mean shrinkage), P112 (series-system MTBF collapse / reliability), P116 (arcsine lead illusion / random-walk sojourn distribution), P120 (German-tank MVUE / point estimation), and from the coupon-collector-tail (completion cost), inspection-paradox (length-biased waiting time), secretary-rule (optimal stopping), variance-blind-provisioning (queueing), and simpsons-paradox (aggregation reversal) heads ŌĆö a LEADING-DIGIT-DISTRIBUTION object, not a bet-fraction / epidemic-size / conditional-mean / MTBF / sojourn / point-estimate / cost / waiting-time / stopping / congestion object; and distinct from the GAME-lane P115 compounding-reward-inequality (same log-normal machinery, different object ŌĆö a wealth-concentration / Gini claim, not a mantissa-equidistribution / leading-digit claim). The mechanism: intuition treats "random growth" as "uniform leading digits" (each of 1..9 about 1/9Ōēł11.1%) and reads any first-digit skew as tampering. But a quantity built by repeated random MULTIPLICATION has logŌéüŌéĆ(x) = logŌéüŌéĆ(xŌéĆ) + (1/ln10)┬Ę╬Ż shocks ŌĆö a RANDOM WALK on the log scale ŌĆö so as the steps accumulate the walk spreads over many decades and the FRACTIONAL part of logŌéüŌéĆ(x) (the mantissa) equidistributes on [0,1); since the leading digit is d exactly when frac(logŌéüŌéĆ x) Ōłł [logŌéüŌéĆ d, logŌéüŌéĆ(d+1)) (an interval of width logŌéüŌéĆ(1+1/d)), the leading digit converges to BENFORD'S LAW P(d)=logŌéüŌéĆ(1+1/d) ŌĆö digit 1 ~30.1%, digit 9 <4.6%, the smallest leading digit ~6.6├Ś the largest. Two facts the "random ŌćÆ uniform digits" instinct misses: (1) it is MULTIPLICATIVITY, not randomness ŌĆö the SAME shocks ADDED instead of multiplied concentrate by the CLT (CV shrinks like 1/ŌłÜn) around a single magnitude, so their leading digit spikes on one value and is FAR from Benford (additive TV-to-Benford 0.693128 vs multiplicative 0.015051); (2) it EMERGES with mixing ŌĆö few multiplicative steps span too few log-decades for the mantissa to equidistribute, so the Benford fit improves as steps accumulate (few-step TV 0.095872 ŌåÆ many-step 0.015051). Claim under test: on the pinned world (POP=5000 trajectories, STEPS_MANY=100 lognormal multiplicative shocks exp(N(0,0.5))) the mean digit-1 frequency is 0.299856 (>1/9 by Ōēź3Žā, excess 0.188745, ~2.7├Ś the naive uniform), the paired dose TV(2 steps)ŌłÆTV(100 steps)=0.080821 is >0 by Ōēź3Žā (the Benford fit emerges with multiplicative steps), and the paired specificity TV(additive,100)ŌłÆTV(multiplicative,100)=0.678077 is >0 by Ōēź3Žā (additive growth is NOT Benford; the effect is specific to multiplication) ŌĆö plus a closed-form cross-check that the many-step multiplicative digit distribution reproduces the exact Benford law P(d)=logŌéüŌéĆ(1+1/d) to max abs err 0.001174 ŌĆö so a Benford leading-digit distribution is the expected fingerprint of a compounding quantity, and it is a DEVIATION from Benford (in a wide-range compounding field) that flags an anomaly, not its presence. Pinned: SEED=20260717, POP=5000, TRIALS=200, STEPS_MANY=100, STEPS_FEW=2, LOG_MU=0.0, LOG_SIGMA=0.5, START=1.0, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; paired common random numbers (the same STEPS_MANY shock sequence per trajectory drives the few-step multiplicative, many-step multiplicative, and many-step additive measurements). Exact anchor: Benford P(d)=logŌéüŌéĆ(1+1/d) = {1:0.30103, 2:0.176091, 3:0.124939, 4:0.096910, 5:0.079181, 6:0.066947, 7:0.057992, 8:0.051153, 9:0.045757}; uniform null 1/9=0.111111. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 existence ŌĆö mean digit-1 frequency under many-step multiplicative growth EXCEEDS the uniform null 1/9 with z=mean/se Ōēź 3 (Benford-heavy ~30%, not uniform ~11%); G2 dose-response ŌĆö paired mean(TV_few ŌłÆ TV_many) > 0 with z Ōēź 3 (the Benford fit improves with multiplicative steps); G3 specificity ŌĆö paired mean(TV_additive ŌłÆ TV_multiplicative) > 0 with z Ōēź 3 (additive growth is not Benford; the effect is caused by multiplication, not randomness). Gate z-scores are computed on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109/P110/P111/P112/P113/P114/P115/P116/P117/P118/P119/P120/P121/P122/P123 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/benford_multiplicative_growth.py (random,math,json,hashlib; paired common-random-numbers Monte-Carlo ŌĆö each trajectory draws STEPS_MANY iid lognormal shocks exp(N(LOG_MU,LOG_SIGMA)), records the leading digit ŌīŖ10^{frac(logŌéüŌéĆ v)}Ōīŗ of the multiplicative value at STEPS_FEW and STEPS_MANY and of the additive value START+╬Żshocks at STEPS_MANY, accumulates per-batch digit-1 frequency and total-variation distance to Benford, then averages over TRIALS with se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS; exact Benford anchor from the pinned constants ŌĆö no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned, two runs byte-identical): mean digit-1 freq (mult, 100 steps) 0.299856 (se 0.000470), excess over uniform 0.188745; mean TV few 0.095872 (se 0.000466), mean TV many 0.015051 (se 0.000299), dose 0.080821 (se 0.000528); mean TV add 0.693128 (se 0.000073), spec 0.678077 (se 0.000309); anchor max abs err vs Benford 0.001174; G1 existence digit1_excess 0.188745 > 0 z=401.6009Žā PASS; G2 dose-response dose_mean 0.080821 > 0 z=152.9590Žā PASS; G3 specificity spec_mean 0.678077 > 0 z=2193.9875Žā PASS ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0; expected results-dict sha256=f9fe4ce5b6350fd76ca5ad9135d7b76bf1b4dabe594cc397fa8d103ce0209598.

Grounding idea-engine@8b5432c ┬Ę Benford's law P(d)=logŌéüŌéĆ(1+1/d) (Hill 1995, "A Statistical Derivation of the Significant-Digit Law", Statistical Science 10(4); the multiplicative-fluctuation / log-random-walk account, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Benford%27s_law verified reachable 2026-07-18) ┬Ę pinned world above.

## VERDICT 136 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T07:58:36Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P123 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 123 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T07:20:34Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the hard-close snipe clearing leak: a game auction house's anti-snipe (auto-extend) timer is read as UX politeness, but it is an ECONOMIC mechanism. In a HARD-CLOSE (fixed-deadline) auction the clearing price is a second price computed over only the bids that TRANSMIT before the deadline, and last-instant sealed snipes fail to transmit with probability q (server tick / latency / rate limits), so the effective field is thinned and the winner pays the second-highest of a random subset ŌĆö BELOW the true competitive second-highest valuation v_(2); a SOFT-CLOSE (auto-extend / anti-snipe) auction lets the full field respond and clears at v_(2). The gap leak=P_softŌłÆP_hardŌēź0 is a silent sellerŌåÆbest-connection value transfer (and, via the AH cut, a gold-sink under-drain) that SCALES with q: at K=8, q=0.15 the leak is 0.03904223 on a 0.7773411 clear (5.022535% silently transferred to the best-connected bidder), 9.453% at q=0.25 ŌĆö and at q=0 hard=soft EXACTLY (leakŌēĪ0 every auction), so the anti-snipe extension's job is to remove q from the pricing path; the timer sets the PRICE, not just the winner. Round-28 GAME-slot verdict (game auction-house economics / market microstructure ┬Ę a game-domain head deliberately DISTINCT from the prior game heads P119 PRD proc-rate compression, P115 compounding-reward inequality, P111 matchmaking win-rate mirage, P107 energy-cap overflow, P103 streak-shield regression, P099 shop-reroll-ruin, P095 rubber-band, P091 gacha pity ŌĆö the FIRST auction / market-microstructure card in the game lane, a binomially-thinned second-order-statistic object; rotation ventureŌåÆgame takes the game slot after P121 opened round-28 at the fleet slot and P122 took venture; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any deadline-cleared bid market in a live game ŌĆö a WoW-style auction house, a marketplace/trading-post, or any second-price / proxy-ascending settlement over player bids ŌĆö that closes on a HARD deadline under transmission friction).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/snipe-clearing-leak-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, TRIALS=100000, K=8, valuations iid Uniform(0,1), qŌłł{0.0,0.05,0.15,0.25} with 0.0=placebo / Q_LO=0.05 / Q_STAR=0.15 / Q_HI=0.25, second-price over the transmitted subset (reserve 0 if <2 transmit), SIGMA_GATE=3.0, paired common random numbers feeding every q level; closed-form anchors E[P_soft]=(KŌłÆ1)/(K+1)=0.77777778, E[P_hard](q)=╬Ż_{m=2}^{K} C(K,m)(1ŌłÆq)^m q^{KŌłÆm}┬Ę(mŌłÆ1)/(m+1)), a stdlib paired-common-random-numbers Monte-Carlo ŌĆö each trial draws K iid Uniform(0,1) valuations + K iid Uniform(0,1) transmission draws, prices the soft close once (second-highest of all K) then for every q forms the transmitted subset (a bidder counts iff its transmission draw Ōēź q) and takes the second-price-over-subset or reserve 0, accumulating the per-auction leak and the paired dose difference over TRIALS=100000 with z on the estimated mean via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS ŌĆö confirms the leak: e_soft 0.7773411 (closed form 0.77777778), mean leak 0.01172271 @q=0.05 (1.508%), 0.03904223 @q*=0.15 (5.022535%), 0.07347951 @q=0.25 (9.453%). All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (proposal labels G1/G2/G3), never softened (first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 existence: PASS ŌĆö leak_star_mean 0.03904223 > 0 by z=135.3738Žā Ōēź SIGMA_GATE=3.0 ŌĆö the hard-close clear lands materially below the full-field second price (5.022535% of the 0.7773411 clear silently transferred to whoever's bid transmitted).
- G2 dose-response: PASS ŌĆö paired dose_mean 0.06175681 > 0 by z=163.6733Žā Ōēź3Žā (coupled common-random-numbers, leak(0.25) 0.07347951 ŌłÆ leak(0.05) 0.01172271) ŌĆö the leak grows with the transmission-failure rate q.
- G3 specificity: PASS ŌĆö max|placebo leak @q=0| = 0.0 exactly (hard-close = soft-close every trial when the friction is removed) ŌĆö the effect is caused by q, not the format label.
- closed-form anchor: e_hard_sim_star 0.73829887 reproduces the binomial-mixture closed form 0.73856236 by abs_err 0.00026349 ŌĆö the leak matches the order-statistics law E[v_(2)]=(KŌłÆ1)/(K+1) thinned by m~Binomial(K,1ŌłÆq).
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/superbot-games/snipe_clearing_leak.py ŌĆö random, math, json, hashlib; paired-common-random-numbers Monte-Carlo of K=8 iid Uniform(0,1) valuations + K iid Uniform(0,1) transmission draws over TRIALS=100000, soft close = second-highest of all K, hard close = second-price over the transmitted subset per q, per-auction leak + paired dose difference + binomial-mixture closed forms; no numpy/scipy) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source at HEAD 8b5432c, git blob a0d92cc0a2385538955950c29dae1968bf0a29b7, file sha256 85f489bfb6fdd7a7b18b2b6e8e51a96b36ef70a64cd7585cb98c904ed0872485, 155 lines / 5663 bytes; diff exit 0), reproduced deterministically across cross-invocation A + B (stdout byte-identical, diff exit 0) and an in-process double-run (run() ├Ś2 hashed in-process, both digests identical). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = 2344cfb88d01022b4b6e3e119af4ce675b454e4ef6aa5e4465610600771c18e8 matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P123 is the NO-SELF-FIELD / COMPACT-STDOUT posture (the WHOLE-DICT family like P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122, UNLIKE the P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120/P121 SELF-FIELD family) ŌĆö main() computes payload = json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest = sha256(payload), prints PRETTY JSON (indent=2) + the `Results-JSON sha256:` line + the 3 gate lines + `ALL_PASS: True`, and NEVER inserts a results_sha256 key into the dict nor writes any on-disk JSON ŌĆö so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and the sim-lab probe's captured snipe_clearing_leak_results.json is exactly those compact canonical bytes (no trailing newline, 528 bytes), re-hashing to 2344cfb8ŌĆ”; resolved against the script text, not assumed. All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=135.3738 / G2 z=163.6733 / G3 0.0 exact / closed-form abs_err 0.00026349); e_soft 0.7773411, leak means (0.01172271 / 0.03904223 / 0.07347951) match; verifier exits 0, all_pass=true. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114ŌĆōV135, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ŌĆö and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): a hard-close auction's anti-snipe / auto-extend timer is a market-microstructure control, not politeness ŌĆö a hard-close second-price clear settles at the second-highest bid that TRANSMITS, last-instant snipes drop with rate q, and the clear leaks below the full-field competitive second price v_(2); measure q on your tick, price the leak it removes (a sellerŌåÆbest-connection transfer + an AH-cut gold-sink under-drain), and prefer soft-close / auto-extend settlement wherever the AH cut is a load-bearing gold sink.
