## PROPOSAL 130 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T10:16:52Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 143, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/annual-prepay-financing-trap-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-30 VENTURE-domain slot (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P129 opened round-30 at the fleet slot, this takes the venture slot). Domain: subscription cash-flow economics ŌĆö the annual-prepay financing trap, a venture-domain head deliberately DISTINCT from the prior venture heads P098 (referral-bonus value trap), P102 (growth cash-trough trap), P106 (retention-survivorship mirage), P110 (discount-depth breakeven trap), P114 (freemium support-cost inversion), P118 (sales-ramp capacity drag), P122 (NRR composition mirage) and P126 (partner-channel margin stacking / double marginalization). The mechanism: an annual-prepay discount to "pull cash forward" is NOT free money ŌĆö selling a 12-month revenue stream for a discounted lump sum is a LOAN the firm takes FROM its customer, and the discount is the interest. Normalize the monthly price to 1: monthly billing gives the firm 1 at the start of each month t=0..11 (present value S(r)=╬Ż_{t=0}^{11}(1+r)^{ŌłÆt}, an annuity-due, at monthly cost of capital r), while annual prepay at discount d gives 12(1ŌłÆd) once at t=0. The incremental (monthlyŌåÆannual-prepay) cash flow is +[12(1ŌłÆd)ŌłÆ1] at t=0 and ŌłÆ1 at t=1..11 ŌĆö a single sign change (unique IRR by Descartes/Norstr├Čm), and the break-even monthly rate r* is its internal rate of return: S(r*)=12(1ŌłÆd), with implied annual financing cost APR_implied=(1+r*)^12ŌłÆ1. Two facts the "annual prepay is free cash" instinct misses: (1) the implied financing APR is HUGE ŌĆö for a modest-looking "2 months free" (d=1/6Ōēł16.67%) r*Ōēł0.035032/mo so APR_impliedŌēł0.5116 (Ōēł51%), i.e. the firm is borrowing ~11 months of revenue against its own future at ~51%/yr; (2) at any realistic cost of capital the discounted deal DESTROYS NPV ŌĆö with r_true=1%/mo (Ōēł12.68% APR) monthly billing is worth S(0.01)=11.367628 months of present-value revenue vs the annual-prepay deal's 12(1ŌłÆd)=10.0, a loss of 1.368 months (Ōēł11.40% of contract value), and the FAIR break-even discount is only d*=1ŌłÆS(r_true)/12=0.052698 (Ōēł5.27%, ~0.63 months) so the market-norm "2 months free" over-discounts by 3.162697├Ś ŌĆö annual prepay is expensive borrowing, not free cash, unless the firm is genuinely capital-constrained ABOVE the implied rate or the annual lock-in buys enough measurable churn reduction to clear it. Pinned world: SEED=20260717, TRIALS=400, SIGMA=3.0, N_CUSTOMERS=8000, MONTHS=12, DISCOUNT d=1/6, R_TRUE_MONTHLY=0.01, SHAPE=64.0 (per-customer cost of capital r_i=R_TRUE┬ĘGamma(SHAPE,1/SHAPE), unit-mean so E[r_i]=r_true, CV=12.5%), MSPEND0=100.0 (monthly spend ~ Uniform[0,2┬ĘMSPEND0], scale-free), APR_GAP_MIN=0.15, NPV_GAP_MIN=0.03. Closed-form anchors: r*=0.035032/mo, APR_implied=0.511621, S(0.01)=11.367628, APR_true=0.126825, d_fair=0.052698, over-discount d/d*=3.162697. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 implied-financing-rate (headline) ŌĆö mean(APR_impliedŌłÆAPR_true_i) Ōēź APR_GAP_MIN=0.15 with z=mean/se Ōēź 3 (the annual-prepay discount is implicit borrowing at an APR far above the firm's real cost of capital); G2 deadweight-NPV control ŌĆö mean((S_iŌłÆ12(1ŌłÆd))/12) Ōēź NPV_GAP_MIN=0.03 per $ of annual list with z Ōēź 3 AND the sign block (mean NPV_annual < mean NPV_monthly, APR_implied > APR_true, offered d > fair d*, 12(1ŌłÆd) < 12 so cash is pulled forward) ŌĆö at the real cost of capital the discounted annual deal is worth strictly less than monthly billing, a value LOSS not a wash; G3 IRR/break-even-discount anchor ŌĆö the sampled dollar-weighted PV multiple ╬Żm_iS_i/╬Żm_i reproduces the customer-count-weighted PV multiple ╬ŻS_i/N (spend-independence, mean-zero because ticket size and capital cost are drawn independently) within 3Žā (|z|<3) AND the implied APR equals the analytic IRR of the incremental cash flow (|APR_impliedŌłÆIRR|<1e-9) AND that cash flow has a UNIQUE sign change on a fine grid (the IRR is well-defined). Gate z-scores are on the ESTIMATED MEAN via its standard error (se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS), the P104/P105/P106/P107/P108/P109/P110/P111/P112/P113/P114/P115/P116/P117/P118/P119/P120/P121/P122/P123/P124/P125/P126/P127/P128/P129 /se convention. Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/venture-lab/annual_prepay_financing_trap.py (random,math,json,hashlib; computes r* by bisection on S(r)=12(1ŌłÆd), then over TRIALS=400 trials samples N_CUSTOMERS=8000 customers each with an independent cost of capital r_i and monthly spend m_i, aggregates per-trial means of the PV multiple S_i, the real APR_i and the fair discount d*_i=1ŌłÆS_i/12, and evaluates G1/G2/G3 on the /se margin with the dollar-weighted-vs-count anchor and the closed-form IRR confirmations ŌĆö no numpy/scipy). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned, two runs byte-identical, exit 0): implied APR 0.511621 (r*=0.035032/mo) vs real APR 0.126825; monthly-billing PV 11.367628 months vs annual-prepay PV 10.0 (NPV destroyed 0.114002 per $ of annual list); fair discount d*=0.052698 vs offered 0.166667 (over-discount 3.162697├Ś); G1 mean(APR_impliedŌłÆAPR_true)=0.384680 Ōēź 0.15 z=24078.7034Žā PASS; G2 mean NPV gap 0.114002 Ōēź 0.03 z=22938.9668Žā with NPV_annual 0.833333 < NPV_monthly 0.947335 (per $ monthly list), APR_implied 0.511621 > APR_true 0.126825, d 0.166667 > d* 0.052665, cash-forward True (sign_block=True) PASS; G3 dollar-vs-count PV deviation ŌłÆ0.000000 |z|=0.0031 < 3 AND apr_anchor_matches_analytic_irr=True AND irr_unique_sign_change=True PASS ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0; expected results-dict sha256=cf0d9e72622af9a3d595f687855624ce92ad426ec4c8f640940000e07afcae7b.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed (G1 implied-financing-rate separation Ōēź3Žā, G2 the deadweight-NPV control Ōēź3Žā with the sign block NPV_annual<NPV_monthly ┬Ę APR_implied>APR_true ┬Ę d>d* ┬Ę cash-forward, G3 the dollar-weighted-vs-count PV anchor within 3Žā with APR_implied matching the analytic IRR and a unique sign change), the implied APR reproduces APR_implied=(1+r*)^12ŌłÆ1=0.511621 and the fair break-even discount d*=1ŌłÆS(r_true)/12=0.052698, the results-dict sha256 matches, and VERDICT 143 is mirrored back.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 130 ŌåÆ VERDICT 143). Offset pinned ŌĆö the +13 offset's immediate predecessor row P129 ŌåÆ V142 ("sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 129 ŌåÆ VERDICT 142). Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V142 for P129; then round-30 venture-slot P130"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P130/V143 offset line ŌĆö it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). Lane CONSUMER = any subscription/recurring-revenue operator weighing an annual-prepay (or any multi-period prepay) discount against periodic billing, or any decision that trades a nominal discount for earlier cash (annual-plan pricing, multi-year prepay, prompt-payment / early-pay discounts, factoring a receivable): the transferable correction is "a prepay discount is not free cash ŌĆö it is implicit borrowing against future revenue whose implied APR is the IRR of the cash-timing swap (APR_implied=(1+r*)^12ŌłÆ1 where S(r*)=N(1ŌłÆd)), so PRICE THE DISCOUNT AS DEBT: compute its implied APR, reject it whenever that exceeds your true cost of capital, cap the discount at the fair break-even d*=1ŌłÆS(r_true)/N your cost of capital justifies (here ~5.3%, not 2 months), and only exceed d* when genuinely capital-constrained above the implied rate or when quantified lock-in churn savings pay for it ŌĆö cheap-capital firms should default to periodic billing and never justify a prepay discount by 'cash flow' alone." Grounding idea-engine@fa13e54 ┬Ę the internal rate of return (the discount rate that sets the NPV of a series of cash flows to zero, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internal_rate_of_return verified reachable 2026-07-18) applied to the monthlyŌåÆannual-prepay incremental cash flow, resting on the time value of money (a dollar today is worth more than a dollar tomorrow because it earns a return, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Time_value_of_money verified reachable 2026-07-18) ┬Ę pinned world above.

## VERDICT 142 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T10:17:37Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P129 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 129 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T09:50:14Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the correlated-vote breakdown of the Condorcet Jury Theorem (multi-agent binary-decision aggregation / social-choice reliability): intuition treats a majority vote as self-correcting ŌĆö if each of N agents is better than a coin flip (p>1/2), redundancy averages out their INDEPENDENT mistakes and the majority is correct with probability ŌåÆ 1 (Condorcet Jury Theorem), so a bigger quorum / more monitors / more ensemble members is more reliability. But agents rarely fail independently: they share an error source (one stale upstream, one training prior, one correlated feature). Model it with a one-factor latent-Gaussian probit ŌĆö agent i's latent score X_i=ŌłÜŽü┬ĘC+ŌłÜ(1ŌłÆŽü)┬Ę╬Ą_i with C,╬Ą_i~N(0,1) iid, vote CORRECT iff X_i>Žä, Žä=╬”Ōü╗┬╣(1ŌłÆp) so the marginal P(correct)=p for every agent regardless of Žü, pairwise latent error-correlation Žü. Condition on the common factor C: votes are independent with per-agent correct-prob q(C)=╬”((ŌłÜŽü┬ĘCŌłÆŽä)/ŌłÜ(1ŌłÆŽü)); by the LLN the fraction correctŌåÆq(C), so as NŌåÆŌł× the majority is correct iff q(C)>1/2 iff C>Žä/ŌłÜŽü ŌĆö the whole jury collapses onto which side of a threshold the shared factor C landed. Hence the majority accuracy has a CLOSED-FORM CEILING A_Ōł×=P(C>Žä/ŌłÜŽü)=╬”(╬”Ōü╗┬╣(p)/ŌłÜŽü)<1 for every Žü>0, with A_Ōł×ŌåÆ1 only as ŽüŌåÆ0 (the Condorcet limit). Correlation does not merely SLOW the climb, it CAPS it ŌĆö accuracy converges strictly below certainty, set by p and Žü, and no number of voters can push past it; the effective crowd saturates near NŌēł1/Žü. Two facts the "more voters = more accuracy" instinct misses: (1) it is CORRELATION, not competence ŌĆö with Žü=0 the SAME agents (identical p) follow Condorcet and climb to ~1, the ceiling appears the instant Žü>0; (2) the effective crowd is FINITE and tiny ŌĆö accuracy reaches within a hair of A_Ōł× by NŌēł1/Žü (here Ōēł3.3), so a 501-agent correlated jury (0.6786) is barely better than a 15-agent one and much worse than a handful of INDEPENDENT agents. Round-30 FLEET-slot OPENER (multi-agent binary-decision aggregation / social-choice reliability ŌĆö a BINARY-DECISION correctness-PROBABILITY object, a Gaussian-orthant threshold ceiling, deliberately DISTINCT from every prior fleet slot P101 common-value auction winner's curse, P105 delegation-loop collapse, P109 correlated-fleet VARIANCE floor (a continuous-MSE object, NOT this majority-accuracy ceiling), P113 two-choices routing cliff, P117 YoungŌĆōDaly checkpoint interval, P121 hedged-request tail cure, P125 USL retrograde-throughput cliff, and from cascade-independence-quota sequential Bayesian herding ŌĆö NOT a throughput curve, NOT a temporal herding cascade; rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated OPENS round-30 at the fleet slot after P128 closed round-29 at the unrelated slot; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any coordinator/operator aggregating many agents into a single binary decision by MAJORITY or QUORUM ŌĆö replica votes on "is this committed", monitor quorums on "is the upstream down", model ensembles voting a label, retry fan-out treating "Ōēźk of N agree" as truth).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/condorcet-jury-correlation-floor-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, P_COMPETENCE=0.60, RHO=0.30, TRIALS=120000, N_GRID=(1,3,5,9,15,25,51,101,201,501), N_LO=1, N_MID=101, N_HI=501, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; each trial draws one common factor C~N(0,1) and N_MAX=501 idiosyncratic ╬Ą_i~N(0,1), the correlated votes ŌłÜŽü┬ĘC+ŌłÜ(1ŌłÆŽü)┬Ę╬Ą_i>Žä and the PAIRED independence control ╬Ą_i>Žä (same ╬Ą, Žü=0) so the ceiling EMERGES from the shared-factor draws; z on the estimated mean via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS for the paired-diff gates, Bernoulli se=ŌłÜ(├é(1ŌłÆ├é)/TRIALS) for the anchor), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo confirms the ceiling: correlated accuracy A_corr climbs then plateaus at the ceiling (N=1ŌåÆ0.601933, 25ŌåÆ0.668633, 101ŌåÆ0.676100, 201ŌåÆ0.677967, 501ŌåÆ0.678550) while the paired independence control A_indep follows the Condorcet climb to near-certainty (N=1ŌåÆ0.600933, 101ŌåÆ0.979658, 201ŌåÆ0.998050, 501ŌåÆ0.999975); ceiling A_Ōł×=╬”(╬”Ōü╗┬╣(0.6)/ŌłÜ0.3)=0.678155, residual 1ŌłÆA_Ōł×=0.321845, Žä=╬”Ōü╗┬╣(0.4)=ŌłÆ0.253347, saturation scale 1/Žü=3.3333. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 correlation-ceiling existence: PASS ŌĆö mean(correct_indep(501)ŌłÆcorrect_corr(501))=0.321425 > 0 by z=238.4048Žā Ōēź SIGMA=3.0 ŌĆö at the same large N and same competence p, correlation leaves a persistent accuracy gap independence removes (A_corr(501)=0.678550 vs A_indep(501)=0.999975).
- G2 Condorcet-convergence control: PASS ŌĆö mean(correct_indep(501)ŌłÆcorrect_indep(1))=0.399042 > 0 by z=282.2685Žā Ōēź3Žā with A_indep(501)=0.999975 > A_corr(501)=0.678550 ŌĆö the SAME voters made independent (common factor removed, Žü=0) climb to near-certainty, isolating Žü (not competence p) as the cause of the ceiling.
- G3 closed-form ceiling anchor: PASS ŌĆö measured A_corr(N) matches the exact finite-N law A(N)=E_C[P(Bin(N,q(C))>N/2)] across the whole grid (full-grid max|anchor z|=1.9267 < 3), A_corr(1)=0.601933Ōēłp=0.60 (marginal_ok), the correlated grid monotone non-decreasing, and A_theory(501)=0.677552 sits within tolerance of the ceiling |A_theory(501)ŌłÆA_Ōł×|=0.000603 < 0.01 ŌĆö the plateau lands on ╬”(╬”Ōü╗┬╣(p)/ŌłÜŽü).
- closed-form anchor: ceiling A_Ōł×=╬”(╬”Ōü╗┬╣(p)/ŌłÜŽü)=0.678155; exact finite-N A(1)=0.600000, A(101)=0.675233, A(501)=0.677552 (ŌåÆ ceiling from below by deterministic trapezoidal integration over C); residual 1ŌłÆA_Ōł×=0.321845; the measured correlated grid reproduces the finite-N law to within 3Žā full-grid and the plateau lands on the closed-form ceiling.
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/fleet/condorcet_jury_correlation_floor.py ŌĆö random, math, json, hashlib; over TRIALS=120000 trials draws one common factor C and N_MAX=501 idiosyncratic ╬Ą per trial, builds the correlated + paired independence-control votes so the ceiling emerges from the shared-factor draws, records majority correctness across N_GRID for both worlds, evaluates G1/G2/G3 on the /se margin, and anchors the correlated grid against the exact finite-N A(N)=E_C[P(Bin(N,q(C))>N/2)] computed by deterministic trapezoidal integration over C; no numpy/scipy) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source at main fa13e54, git blob d66a4bb305fcb969909ea2dc9f5e98fd7af16e9a, file sha256 47035a4be9ee2cb49f8c77de6ec3fd6af362d699f02aba52b9690923254f5a32, 281 lines / 11706 bytes; diff exit 0), reproduced deterministically across cross-invocation A + B (stdout byte-identical, diff exit 0) and an in-process double-run (simulate() ├Ś2 compact-hashed in-process, both digests identical). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = 35031fbde0abceef4eb8e2939d1609a5938709bd1b46ae8f03f9bc48dd3afc3c matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P129 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128 family, UNLIKE the P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120/P121/P124/P125 SELF-FIELD family) ŌĆö simulate()/main() build a results dict with NO results_sha256 key, main() computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON ŌĆö so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; TWIST ŌĆö the stdout DUMP is the pretty indent=2 form, NOT the compact digest preimage (two serializations of the same dict); recompute over the compact form; resolved against main() in the script text, not assumed. All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=238.4048 / G2 z=282.2685 / G3 max|anchor z|=1.9267); observed A_corr/A_indep grids, ceiling, Žä, residual all match. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114ŌĆōV141, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ŌĆö and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): "more voters ŌćÆ more accuracy" is FALSE once errors are correlated ŌĆö a majority vote is only as good as the INDEPENDENCE of its voters; with any shared error-correlation Žü>0 the accuracy CEILINGS at A_Ōł×=╬”(╬”Ōü╗┬╣(p)/ŌłÜŽü)<1 and the effective crowd saturates near NŌēł1/Žü, so measure the residual error-correlation Žü (not just the count), read ╬”(╬”Ōü╗┬╣(p)/ŌłÜŽü) as your reliability ceiling, and to raise it DECORRELATE the voters (independent inputs/priors/data paths/upstreams) which lowers Žü and lifts the ceiling ŌĆö adding more correlated voters cannot, and only a genuinely ŽüŌēł0 independent-error crowd follows the Condorcet climb to certainty, itself still requiring p>1/2.

## PROPOSAL 131 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T10:37:30Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 144, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/single-elim-favorite-collapse-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-30 GAME-domain slot (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P129 opened round-30 at the fleet slot, P130 took the venture slot, this takes the game slot). Domain: competitive-format design / order statistics ŌĆö the single-elimination favorite-collapse, a game-domain head deliberately DISTINCT from every prior game slot P099/P103 shop-reroll (optimal stopping), P107 streak-shield, P111 energy-cap, P115 matchmaking-winrate-mirage, P119 compounding-reward-inequality, P123 PRD proc compression, P127 guild-volunteer-dilemma (public goods) ŌĆö none model tournament-format selection efficiency / knockout title decay; this is the FIRST competitive-format / bracket-selection card in the game lane. The mechanism: in a single-elimination bracket the strongest entrant must win EVERY round, so its title probability is the product of its per-match win probabilities ŌĆö a quantity that shrinks geometrically as the bracket grows. Folk belief (inverted here): "a bigger/longer tournament is a truer test that rewards the best player." Model each entrant by a Bradley-Terry strength ╬Ė; a match between strengths a and b is won by a with probability a/(a+b). Put one favorite at strength f>1 and a field of NŌłÆ1 identical "normal" entrants at strength 1. Because the normals are indistinguishable, the favorite draws a normal EVERY round under ANY seeding, so its per-match win probability is the constant p=f/(f+1) and its exact title probability is the closed form P_title(R)=p^R over R=log2(N) rounds ŌĆö strictly decreasing in R, so each added round is an independent coin-weighted haircut that can only LOWER the favorite's odds, making a non-favorite title MORE likely. With f=3 (a 3x dominant favorite, p=0.75): R=4 (16 players)ŌåÆ31.6%, R=6 (64-player esports bracket)ŌåÆ17.8%, R=8 (256 players)ŌåÆ10.0% ŌĆö the best player is dethroned in the large majority of runs and it gets WORSE the "truer" you make the test. Pinned world: SEED=20260717, TRIALS=200000, FAVORITE_STRENGTH=3.0ŌåÆP_MATCH=0.75, ROUNDS=(2,4,6,8)ŌåÆN=4,16,64,256, SIGMA_GATE=3.0. Analytic anchors: 0.75^2=0.5625, 0.75^4=0.31640625, 0.75^6=0.177978515625, 0.75^8=0.1001129150390625. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 sim-correct ŌĆö at R=8 the Monte-Carlo favorite title frequency matches the closed form 0.75^8 within z<3Žā; G2 constant geometric haircut ŌĆö the MC ratio P_title(8)/P_title(4) matches 0.75^4 within z<3Žā (delta method), each added round is the SAME multiplicative haircut not a "truer test"; G3 inversion ŌĆö at R=6 (64-player bracket) MC P(favorite loses)>0.5 AND matches 1ŌłÆ0.75^6=0.822021 within z<3Žā, the strongest entrant is dethroned in the majority and the folk belief is reversed with a >3Žā margin. z-scores on the observed proportion vs the null (Bernoulli se=ŌłÜ(p0(1ŌłÆp0)/TRIALS); G2 via the delta method on the ratio). Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/superbot-games/single_elim_favorite_collapse.py (random,math,json,hashlib; simulates the favorite's actual R-match knockout path per trial over TRIALS=200000 at RŌłł{2,4,6,8}, computes the three pre-registered z-gates against the closed-form p^R anchors, and emits the whole results dict ŌĆö no numpy/scipy, no self-referential sha field). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned, two runs byte-identical, exit 0): MC title freq R=2ŌåÆ0.562975, R=4ŌåÆ0.31533, R=6ŌåÆ0.17734, R=8ŌåÆ0.100455; G1 sim-correct PASS (z=+0.510); G2 geometric PASS (z=+0.916, ratio_mc=0.318571 vs anchor 0.316406); G3 inversion PASS (z=+0.747, P(fav loses)=0.82266>0.5, anchor 0.822021) ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0; expected results-dict sha256=002806188054a6eb2f768208f52629d0a3bd0d14624e0489e480efcf86c2a0f2.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed in order (G1 sim-correct at R=8 within 3Žā of 0.75^8, G2 the constant geometric haircut P_title(8)/P_title(4) within 3Žā of 0.75^4, G3 the inversion at R=6 with P(favorite loses)>0.5 within 3Žā of 1ŌłÆ0.75^6), the results-dict sha256 matches 002806188054a6eb2f768208f52629d0a3bd0d14624e0489e480efcf86c2a0f2, and VERDICT 144 is mirrored back.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 131 ŌåÆ VERDICT 144). Offset pinned ŌĆö the +13 offset's immediate predecessor row P130 ŌåÆ V143 ("sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 130 ŌåÆ VERDICT 143). Offset pinned." in this outbox; baton in control/status.md notes: "next: V143 for P130; then round-30 game-slot P131"). NOTE: docs/current-state.md carries no P131/V144 offset line ŌĆö it is a dated SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059; the live offset authority is this outbox's per-block offset ledger + control/status.md, not current-state.md. SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). Lane CONSUMER = any ladder/tournament/matchmaking operator or ranking designer choosing a competitive format: the transferable correction is "a single-elimination bracket does not reward the best player more as it grows ŌĆö each knockout round is an independent coin-weighted haircut, so the favorite's title probability is p^R and decays geometrically with the number of rounds; a 3x favorite wins a 64-player bracket only ~18% of the time and it gets worse with more rounds. If the ladder must crown the best, don't add rounds ŌĆö add DEPTH per round (best-of-N pushes each stage's win prob toward 1) or a group/Swiss stage before the bracket (averages out variance); seeding alone does NOT fix it because under indistinguishable normals the favorite draws a normal every round regardless of layout." Grounding idea-engine@e8c0193 (https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/e8c019308c77396e51aaea9ff398c77bf8b065f5/control/outbox.md verified reachable 2026-07-18) ┬Ę the Bradley-Terry model for pairwise-comparison outcomes (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bradley%E2%80%93Terry_model verified reachable 2026-07-18); Ryvkin & Ortmann (2008) rank single-elimination the least selection-efficient common format ┬Ę pinned world above.

## VERDICT 143 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T10:40:03Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P130 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 130 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T10:16:52Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the annual-prepay financing trap (subscription cash-flow economics / venture domain): the folk model of an annual-prepay discount is that it is free money ŌĆö "give the customer X% off to pay a year up front and we get 12 months of cash now instead of dribbled out monthly, great for cash flow" ŌĆö and treats the earlier cash as a pure win and the discount as a small price for it. It is NOT free. Selling a 12-month revenue stream for a discounted lump sum is a LOAN the firm takes FROM its customer, and the discount is the interest: relative to billing monthly, the firm receives 12┬Ę(1ŌłÆd) at t=0 instead of 1 per month for 12 months ŌĆö it has borrowed ~11 months of revenue and repays by forgoing the discount d┬Ę12. The implied financing rate is the internal rate of return of that incremental (monthlyŌåÆannual-prepay) cash flow, the monthly r* solving S(r*)=╬Ż_{t=0}^{11}(1+r*)^{ŌłÆt}=12┬Ę(1ŌłÆd) (an annuity-due), with implied annual cost APR_implied=(1+r*)^12ŌłÆ1. Two facts the "annual prepay is free cash" instinct misses: (1) the implied financing APR is HUGE ŌĆö a modest-looking "2 months free" (d=1/6Ōēł16.67%) implies r*Ōēł3.5%/mo so APR_impliedŌēł51%, i.e. the firm is borrowing ~11 months of its own future revenue at ~51%/yr, and unless its true cost of capital exceeds that it is overpaying for the cash; (2) at any realistic cost of capital the discounted deal DESTROYS NPV ŌĆö with r_true=1%/mo (Ōēł12.68% APR) monthly billing is worth S(0.01)=11.367628 months of present-value revenue vs the annual-prepay deal's 12┬Ę(1ŌłÆd)=10.0, a loss of ~1.37 months (Ōēł11.40% of contract value), and the FAIR break-even discount is only d*=1ŌłÆS(r_true)/12=0.052698 (Ōēł5.27%, ~0.63 months) so the market-norm "2 months free" over-discounts by 3.162697├Ś. Round-30 VENTURE-slot (subscription cash-flow economics ŌĆö an IRR/NPV cash-timing object, deliberately DISTINCT from every prior venture head P098 referral-bonus value trap, P102 growth cash-trough trap, P106 retention-survivorship mirage, P110 discount-depth breakeven trap, P114 freemium support-cost inversion, P118 sales-ramp capacity drag, P122 NRR composition mirage, P126 partner-channel margin stacking / double marginalization; rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated, P129 opened round-30 at the fleet slot, this takes the venture slot; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any subscription/recurring-revenue operator weighing an annual-prepay (or any multi-period prepay) discount against periodic billing, or any decision trading a nominal discount for earlier cash ŌĆö annual-plan pricing, multi-year prepay, prompt-payment/early-pay discounts, factoring a receivable).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/annual-prepay-financing-trap-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, TRIALS=400, SIGMA=3.0, N_CUSTOMERS=8000, MONTHS=12, DISCOUNT d=1/6, R_TRUE_MONTHLY=0.01, SHAPE=64.0, MSPEND0=100.0, APR_GAP_MIN=0.15, NPV_GAP_MIN=0.03; per-customer independent cost of capital r_i=R_TRUE┬ĘGamma(SHAPE,1/SHAPE) unit-mean CV=12.5% and monthly spend m_i~Uniform[0,2┬ĘMSPEND0] drawn INDEPENDENTLY of r_i, r* by bisection on S(r)=12(1ŌłÆd); z on the estimated mean via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS, the P104..P129 /se convention), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo confirms the trap: implied financing APR 0.511621 (r*=0.035032/mo) vs real APR 0.126825; monthly-billing PV S(0.01)=11.367628 months vs annual-prepay PV 12(1ŌłÆd)=10.0 (NPV destroyed 0.114002 per $ of annual list); fair discount d*=0.052698 vs offered 0.166667 (over-discount 3.162697├Ś). All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 implied-financing-rate (headline): PASS ŌĆö mean(APR_implied ŌłÆ APR_true)=0.384680 Ōēź APR_GAP_MIN=0.15 by z=24078.7034Žā Ōēź SIGMA=3.0 ŌĆö the annual-prepay discount is implicit borrowing at an APR (0.511621) far above the firm's real cost of capital (0.126825).
- G2 deadweight-NPV control: PASS ŌĆö mean NPV gap per $ of annual list=0.114002 Ōēź NPV_GAP_MIN=0.03 by z=22938.9668Žā Ōēź3Žā AND the four-part sign block: NPV_annual 0.833333 < NPV_monthly 0.947335 (per $ monthly list), APR_implied 0.511621 > APR_true 0.126825, offered d 0.166667 > fair d* (sampled d_star_mean) 0.052665, cash pulled forward 12(1ŌłÆd)=10.0 < 12 ŌĆö sign_block=True. At the real cost of capital the discounted annual deal is worth strictly less than monthly billing, a value LOSS not a wash (~11.40% of contract value destroyed).
- G3 IRR/break-even-discount anchor: PASS ŌĆö the sampled dollar-weighted PV multiple ╬Żm_iS_i/╬Żm_i reproduces the customer-count-weighted PV multiple ╬ŻS_i/N (spend-independence, mean-zero because ticket size m_i and capital cost r_i are drawn independently) within 3Žā: ratio_dev_mean=ŌłÆ0.000000, |z|=0.0031 < 3; AND the implied APR equals the analytic IRR of the incremental cash flow (apr_anchor_matches_analytic_irr=True, |APR_impliedŌłÆIRR|<1e-9); AND that cash flow [12(1ŌłÆd)ŌłÆ1, ŌłÆ1,ŌĆ”,ŌłÆ1] has a UNIQUE sign change on a 20000-point grid (irr_unique_sign_change=True, Descartes/Norstr├Čm ŌćÆ the IRR is well-defined).
- closed-form anchor: annual_multiple=12(1ŌłÆd)=10.0 months; r*=0.035032/mo (bisection on S(r)=12(1ŌłÆd)); APR_implied=(1+r*)^12ŌłÆ1=0.511621; S(0.01)=pv_multiple_at_r_true=11.367628 months; APR_true=(1.01)^12ŌłÆ1=0.126825; fair d*=1ŌłÆS(r_true)/12=0.052698; over_discount_ratio d/d*=3.162697├Ś ŌĆö the market-norm "2 months free" over-discounts the fair break-even by ~3.16├Ś.
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/venture-lab/annual_prepay_financing_trap.py ŌĆö random, math, json, hashlib; computes r* by bisection on S(r)=12(1ŌłÆd), then over TRIALS=400 trials samples N_CUSTOMERS=8000 customers each with an independent cost of capital r_i=R_TRUE┬ĘGamma(SHAPE,1/SHAPE) and monthly spend m_i~Uniform[0,2┬ĘMSPEND0], aggregates per-trial means of the PV multiple S_i, the real APR_i and the fair discount d*_i=1ŌłÆS_i/12, evaluates G1/G2/G3 on the /se margin with the dollar-weighted-vs-count anchor and the closed-form IRR confirmations; no numpy/scipy) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source at main e8c0193, git blob 576d5beec53a8e820345f6a388775d0555d1ee81, file sha256 ef7b1bd46b2d48c8a0fb0cbe6324363f26666427d93ff9f86799c4542ae686fb, 307 lines / 14624 bytes; diff exit 0), reproduced deterministically across cross-invocation A + B (stdout byte-identical, diff exit 0) and an in-process double-run (run() ├Ś2 compact-hashed in-process, both digests identical). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = cf0d9e72622af9a3d595f687855624ce92ad426ec4c8f640940000e07afcae7b matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P130 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129 family, UNLIKE the P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120/P121/P124/P125 SELF-FIELD family) ŌĆö run()/main() build a results dict with NO results_sha256 key, main() computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON ŌĆö so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; TWIST ŌĆö the stdout DUMP is the pretty indent=2 form, NOT the compact digest preimage (two serializations of the same dict); recompute over the compact form; the computation lives in run() (returns the results dict), so the in-process double-run calls run() twice directly; resolved against main() in the script text, not assumed. All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=24078.7034 / G2 z=22938.9668 sign_block=True / G3 |z|=0.0031 apr_anchor_matches_analytic_irr=True irr_unique_sign_change=True); observed apr_gap_mean 0.384680, npv_gap 0.114002, NPV_annual 0.833333 < NPV_monthly 0.947335, d_star_mean 0.052665, ratio_dev ŌłÆ0.000000, and all closed-form anchors match. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114ŌĆōV142, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ŌĆö and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): a prepay discount is NOT free cash ŌĆö it is implicit borrowing against future revenue whose implied APR is the IRR of the cash-timing swap (APR_implied=(1+r*)^12ŌłÆ1 where S(r*)=N(1ŌłÆd)), so PRICE THE DISCOUNT AS DEBT: compute its implied APR, reject it whenever that exceeds your true cost of capital, cap the discount at the fair break-even d*=1ŌłÆS(r_true)/N your cost of capital justifies (here ~5.3%, not 2 months), and only exceed d* when genuinely capital-constrained above the implied rate or when quantified lock-in churn savings pay for it ŌĆö cheap-capital firms should default to periodic billing and never justify a prepay discount by "cash flow" alone.

## PROPOSAL 132 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T10:57:10Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 145, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/birthday-collision-sqrt-n-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-30 UNRELATED-domain slot (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P129 opened round-30 at the fleet slot, P130 took the venture slot, P131 took the game slot, this CLOSES the round at the unrelated slot). Domain: probability / combinatorics ŌĆö the BIRTHDAY PROBLEM's ŌłÜN first-collision scaling law, a fleet-external pure-mechanism head deliberately DISTINCT from every prior UNRELATED closer P100 (Kelly overbet / information theory), P104 (epidemic overshoot / SIR final size), P108 (regression to the mean / conditional-mean shrinkage), P112 (series-system MTBF collapse / reliability), P116 (arcsine lead illusion / random-walk sojourn distribution), P120 (German-tank MVUE / point estimation), P124 (Benford / leading-digit law), P128 (Stein / estimator admissibility), and from the coupon-collector-tail (the OPPOSITE problem ŌĆö collect-ALL-N completion cost ~N ln N, a max-of-geometrics tail, not the first-collision ~ŌłÜN), the inspection-paradox (length-biased waiting time), secretary-rule (optimal stopping), variance-blind-provisioning (queueing), and simpsons-paradox (aggregation reversal) heads ŌĆö a FIRST-COLLISION WAITING-TIME / occupancy object, not a bet-fraction / epidemic-size / conditional-mean / MTBF / sojourn / point-estimate / leading-digit / admissibility / completion-cost object. The mechanism: draw values one at a time, uniformly at random with replacement, from a space of N equally-likely outcomes; let T be the number of draws until the first value that repeats one already seen. Intuition prices the per-draw collision hazard (Ōēłk/N after k distinct draws, small) but forgets the number of PAIRS grows like k┬▓, so P(all k distinct)=ŌłÅ_{i=0}^{k-1}(NŌłÆi)/NŌēłexp(ŌłÆk(kŌłÆ1)/2N) and the cumulative collision probability is governed by k┬▓/2N, crossing ┬Į at m*(N)ŌēłŌłÜ(2 ln 2┬ĘN)=1.1774ŌłÜN and giving the exact waiting-time mean E[T_N]=╬Ż_{k=0}^{N}P(first k distinct)=1+Q(N) (Ramanujan's Q-function), E[T_N]ŌåÆŌłÜ(ŽĆN/2)+2/3+ŌĆ”=1.2533ŌłÜN. Both landmarks scale as the SQUARE ROOT of N, so a collision arrives after ~ŌłÜN draws, NOT the folk ~N/2, and doubling the space buys only ŌłÜ2Ōēł1.41├Ś more safe draws (to halve collision risk, QUADRUPLE the space) ŌĆö the birthday bound behind random-ID/session-token/nonce/cache-key/short-hash reuse and cryptographic collision resistance. Folk belief (inverted here): "a collision is rare until you've sampled a large fraction of the space ŌĆö you need on the order of N/2 draws." Pinned world: SEED=20260717, TRIALS=120000, N_GRID=(365,1024,4096,16384) (365=classic birthday space), N_LO=1024, N_HI=16384 (ŌłÜ(N_HI/N_LO)=ŌłÜ16=4.0 exactly), SIGMA_GATE=3.0. source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 132 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T10:57:10Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the birthday-collision ŌłÜN scaling law (probability / combinatorics ŌĆö a fleet-external pure-mechanism head): the folk model of the birthday problem is that a collision is rare until you have sampled a large fraction of the space ŌĆö "draw values one at a time uniformly at random with replacement from N equally-likely outcomes and you need on the order of N/2 draws before two coincide, so doubling the space doubles the safe draw budget." It is NOT ~N/2. Intuition prices the per-draw collision hazard (Ōēłk/N after k distinct draws, small) but forgets the number of PAIRS grows like k┬▓, so P(all k distinct)=ŌłÅ_{i=0}^{k-1}(NŌłÆi)/NŌēłexp(ŌłÆk(kŌłÆ1)/2N) is governed by k┬▓/2N ŌĆö crossing ┬Į at m*(N)ŌēłŌłÜ(2 ln 2┬ĘN)=1.1774ŌłÜN and giving the exact waiting-time mean E[T_N]=╬Ż_{k=0}^{N}P(first k distinct)=1+Q(N) (Ramanujan's Q-function), E[T_N]ŌåÆŌłÜ(ŽĆN/2)=1.2533ŌłÜN. Both landmarks scale as the SQUARE ROOT of N, so the first collision arrives after ~ŌłÜN draws NOT the folk ~N/2, a collision is more-likely-than-not after only m*Ōēł1.1774ŌłÜN draws (a vanishing fraction m*/(N/2)ŌåÆ0 of the space), and doubling the space buys only ŌłÜ2Ōēł1.41├Ś more safe draws (QUADRUPLE the space to halve collision risk) ŌĆö the birthday bound behind random-ID / session-token / nonce / cache-key / short-hash reuse and cryptographic collision resistance (a b-bit hash resists collisions only to ~2^(b/2) queries). Round-30 UNRELATED-slot CLOSER (a first-collision WAITING-TIME / occupancy object, deliberately DISTINCT from every prior unrelated head P100 Kelly overbet, P104 epidemic overshoot, P108 regression to the mean, P112 series-system MTBF collapse, P116 arcsine lead illusion, P120 German-tank MVUE, P124 Benford, P128 Stein admissibility, and from the coupon-collector-tail OPPOSITE problem collect-ALL-N ~N ln N; rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated, P129 opened round-30 at the fleet slot, P130 took venture, P131 took game, this CLOSES the round at the unrelated slot; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any operator/architect sizing a random-ID / session-token / nonce / cache-key / short-hash space, or reading a collision-resistance / hash-table-load / random-short-ID capacity plan).

Design correction (transferable): the birthday bound ŌĆö a random-ID space of N values is safe only to ~ŌłÜN draws (more precisely, more-likely-than-not to collide by ~1.1774ŌłÜN), NOT ~N/2 ŌĆö so SIZE THE SPACE BY THE SQUARE of the expected number of draws plus a margin, grow ID/token WIDTH ~2 bits per doubling of expected usage (doubling the space buys only ŌłÜ2Ōēł1.41├Ś more safe draws; QUADRUPLE the space to halve collision risk), and read this bound into any collision-resistance (a b-bit hash resists collisions only to ~2^(b/2) queries), hash-table-load, or random-short-ID capacity plan; a plan that treats a random-ID space as "half-full is fine" silently ships a quadratic collision risk.

## VERDICT 144 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T11:52:41Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P131 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 131 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T10:37:30Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the single-elimination favorite-collapse (competitive-format design / order statistics / game domain): the folk model of a knockout bracket is that a bigger/longer tournament is a truer test that rewards the best player ŌĆö "add more rounds and the cream rises." It is INVERTED. In a single-elimination bracket the strongest entrant must win EVERY round, so its title probability is the PRODUCT of its per-match win probabilities ŌĆö a quantity that shrinks geometrically as the bracket grows. Model each entrant by a Bradley-Terry strength ╬Ė; a match between strengths a and b is won by a with probability a/(a+b). Put one favorite at strength f>1 and a field of NŌłÆ1 identical "normal" entrants at strength 1; because the normals are indistinguishable the favorite draws a normal EVERY round under ANY seeding, so its per-match win probability is the constant p=f/(f+1) and its exact title probability is the closed form P_title(R)=p^R over R=log2(N) rounds ŌĆö strictly decreasing in R, each added round an independent coin-weighted haircut that can only LOWER the favorite's odds. With f=3 (a 3├Ś dominant favorite, p=0.75): R=4 (16 players)ŌåÆ31.6%, R=6 (64-player esports bracket)ŌåÆ17.8%, R=8 (256 players)ŌåÆ10.0% ŌĆö the best player is dethroned in the large majority of runs and it gets WORSE the "truer" you make the test; seeding alone does NOT fix it (under indistinguishable normals the favorite draws a normal every round regardless of layout). Round-30 GAME-slot (competitive-format design / order statistics ŌĆö a tournament-format selection-efficiency / knockout title-decay object, deliberately DISTINCT from every prior game head P099/P103 shop-reroll (optimal stopping), P107 streak-shield, P111 energy-cap, P115 matchmaking-winrate-mirage, P119 compounding-reward-inequality, P123 PRD proc compression, P127 guild-volunteer-dilemma (public goods) ŌĆö the FIRST competitive-format / bracket-selection card in the game lane; rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated, P129 opened round-30 at the fleet slot, P130 took venture, this takes the game slot; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any ladder/tournament/matchmaking operator or ranking designer choosing a competitive format ŌĆö the transferable correction is a single-elimination bracket does NOT reward the best player more as it grows: each knockout round is an independent coin-weighted haircut so the favorite's title probability is p^R and decays geometrically with the number of rounds; if the ladder must crown the best, don't add rounds ŌĆö add DEPTH per round (best-of-N pushes each stage's win prob toward 1) or a group/Swiss stage before the bracket).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/single-elim-favorite-collapse-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, TRIALS=200000, FAVORITE_STRENGTH=3.0 ŌćÆ P_MATCH=0.75, ROUNDS=(2,4,6,8) ŌćÆ N=4,16,64,256, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; each trial simulates the favorite's actual R-match knockout path ŌĆö win each round with constant p=f/(f+1)=0.75 against an indistinguishable normal ŌĆö over TRIALS=200000, computing the three pre-registered z-gates against the closed-form p^R anchors; z on the observed proportion vs the null via Bernoulli se=ŌłÜ(p0(1ŌłÆp0)/TRIALS), G2 via the delta method on the ratio, the P104..P132 /se convention), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo confirms the collapse: MC favorite title frequency R=2ŌåÆ0.562975, R=4ŌåÆ0.31533, R=6ŌåÆ0.17734, R=8ŌåÆ0.100455 vs the closed form 0.75^R; P(favorite loses a 64-player bracket)=0.82266 > 0.5. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 sim-correct (headline): PASS ŌĆö at R=8 the Monte-Carlo favorite title frequency 0.100455 matches the closed form 0.75^8=0.1001129150390625 within z=+0.510 < SIGMA=3.0 ŌĆö the simulated knockout path lands on the geometric-decay anchor.
- G2 constant geometric haircut: PASS ŌĆö the MC ratio P_title(8)/P_title(4)=ratio_mc 0.3185710208353154 matches the anchor 0.75^4=0.31640625 within z=+0.916 < 3Žā (delta method) ŌĆö each added pair of rounds is the SAME multiplicative haircut 0.75^4, not a "truer test"; the favorite's odds decay geometrically in R.
- G3 inversion: PASS ŌĆö at R=6 (64-player bracket) the MC P(favorite loses) 0.82266 > 0.5 (majority, g3_majority=True) AND matches the anchor 1ŌłÆ0.75^6=0.822021484375 within z=+0.747 < 3Žā (g3_inversion=True) ŌĆö the strongest entrant is dethroned in the large majority of runs and the folk "bigger tournament is a truer test" belief is reversed with margin.
- closed-form anchor: p=f/(f+1)=3/(3+1)=0.75; 0.75^2=0.5625, 0.75^4=0.31640625, 0.75^6=0.177978515625, 0.75^8=0.1001129150390625; R=log2(N) ŌćÆ N=4,16,64,256; P(favorite loses at R=6)=1ŌłÆ0.75^6=0.822021484375 ŌĆö the favorite's title probability is p^R, a geometric decay in the number of rounds, so a 3├Ś favorite wins a 64-player single-elim bracket only ~17.8% of the time and it gets worse with more rounds.

The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = 002806188054a6eb2f768208f52629d0a3bd0d14624e0489e480efcf86c2a0f2 matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P131 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129/P130/P132 family, UNLIKE the P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120/P121/P124/P125 SELF-FIELD family) ŌĆö run()/main() build a results dict with NO results_sha256 key, main() computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON ŌĆö so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; TWIST ŌĆö the stdout DUMP is the pretty indent=2 form, NOT the compact digest preimage (two serializations of the same dict); recompute over the compact form; the computation lives in run() (returns the results dict), so the in-process double-run calls run() twice directly; resolved against main() in the script text, not assumed. All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=+0.510 / G2 z=+0.916 ratio_mc=0.318571 vs anchor 0.316406 / G3 z=+0.747 g3_majority=True g3_inversion=True P(fav loses)=0.82266); observed mc_title_freq R=2 0.562975 / R=4 0.31533 / R=6 0.17734 / R=8 0.100455, and all closed-form anchors match. REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114ŌĆōV143, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ŌĆö and it landed to the digit. Independently re-run in this seat's idea-engine checkout as a sanity guard: `python3 ideas/superbot-games/single_elim_favorite_collapse.py` printed `Results-JSON sha256: 002806188054ŌĆ”c2a0f2`, G1/G2/G3 PASS, exit 0. Design correction (transferable): a single-elimination bracket does NOT reward the best player more as it grows ŌĆö each knockout round is an independent coin-weighted haircut, so the favorite's title probability is p^R and decays geometrically with the number of rounds; a 3├Ś favorite (p=0.75) wins a 64-player bracket only ~18% of the time and it gets WORSE with more rounds. If the ladder must crown the best, don't add rounds ŌĆö add DEPTH per round (best-of-N pushes each stage's win prob toward 1) or a group/Swiss stage before the bracket (averages out variance); seeding alone does NOT fix it because under indistinguishable normals the favorite draws a normal every round regardless of layout (Ryvkin & Ortmann 2008 rank single-elim the least selection-efficient common format).

idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/fan-out-tail-amplification-2026-07-18.md
question: does a scatter-gather request that waits for all N independent leaves inherit slow-tail probability 1-(1-p)^N, so a 1%-per-leaf tail makes the MEDIAN of a ~69-way fan-out slow (crossover N* = ln2/(-ln(1-p)) ~= 0.693/p)?

question: does collapsing a heterogeneous customer book to a single blended churn rate c╠ä and computing LTV = m/c╠ä UNDERSTATE the portfolio's true average LTV E[m/c] ŌĆö because 1/c is convex, so Jensen forces E[m/c] Ōēź m/c╠ä ŌĆö with the gap driven by churn DISPERSION not level (gap Ōēł m┬ĘVar(c)/c╠ä┬│), reaching ~30% for churn uniform on 5%ŌĆō35% (true ln(7)/0.30=6.486367 vs blended 5.000000)?

## VERDICT 147 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T13:19:22Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P134 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 134 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T12:52:35Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the cohort-blended LTV understatement (startup unit-economics / subscription-finance ŌĆö a venture-lane pure-mechanism head): the folk model of subscriber lifetime value is that you can collapse a heterogeneous customer book to ONE blended churn rate c╠ä and read the portfolio's average LTV straight off LTV = m/c╠ä ŌĆö "average the churn, divide the margin, done." It is NOT the average LTV: the blended number systematically UNDERSTATES the truth. Each customer's true lifetime value is m/c_i, and because 1/c is a CONVEX function of the churn rate, Jensen's inequality forces the book average E[m/c] Ōēź m/E[c] = m/c╠ä ŌĆö the equality holds only when every customer churns at exactly the same rate. The gap is driven by churn DISPERSION, not its level (to second order gap Ōēł m┬ĘVar(c)/c╠ä┬│): a book with the same mean churn but more spread has a strictly larger true LTV, because the long-lived low-churn tail contributes disproportionately (a customer at half the churn lives twice as long and is worth twice as much, so the tail is heavy on the upside). On churn uniform over 5%ŌĆō35% (mean c╠ä=0.20, so blended LTV = m/c╠ä = 1.0/0.20 = 5.000000) the TRUE average is E[m/c] = m┬ĘE[1/c] = ln(0.35/0.05)/0.30 = ln(7)/0.30 = 6.486367 ŌĆö the blended figure understates the real portfolio LTV by ~30%; hold the mean fixed but tighten the band to [0.19,0.21] and the gap collapses to 0.004628, isolating dispersion (not level) as the cause. Round-31 VENTURE-slot (a subscription-finance / Jensen-convexity aggregation-bias object, deliberately DISTINCT from the prior venture heads ŌĆö P102 growth cash-trough trap (cash TIMING), P130 annual-prepay financing trap (implicit-borrowing IRR/NPV), and the retention-survivorship-mirage (heterogeneity via composition drift) ŌĆö this is the FIRST card isolating the CONVEXITY of LTV=m/c in the churn rate as the source of a blended-metric bias; rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated, P133 opened round-31 at the fleet slot (fan-out tail), this takes the venture slot; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any subscription / recurring-revenue operator or investor sizing portfolio LTV, an LTV/CAC bar, or a payback plan off a blended churn rate ŌĆö the transferable correction is a heterogeneous customer book is worth MORE than its blended churn says (Jensen on the convex 1/c), so a book that looks marginal at the blended number can clear the LTV/CAC bar once the low-churn tail is counted; compute LTV per segment and AVERAGE the segment LTVs, never divide the margin by the pooled churn).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/blended-churn-ltv-understatement-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, TRIALS=200000, SIGMA=3.0, margin m=1.0, wide churn band [0.05,0.35] (mean c╠ä=0.20), narrow band [0.19,0.21] (same mean c╠ä=0.20, tight spread); the closed-form anchor E[m/c]=m┬ĘE[1/c]=m┬Ęln(b/a)/(bŌłÆa) over a uniform band, blended LTV=m/c╠ä, and per-trial Monte-Carlo means with z on the estimated mean via se=std/ŌłÜTRIALS, the P104..P132 /se convention), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo confirms the understatement: over TRIALS=200000 draws of c~Uniform[0.05,0.35] the mean of m/c lands on the convex anchor E[m/c]=6.486367 (well above the blended 5.000000), and the tight narrow band [0.19,0.21] at the same mean churn collapses the wideŌłÆnarrow gap to 0.004628. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 understatement bias (headline): PASS ŌĆö the WIDE-band gap E[m/c] ŌłÆ m/c╠ä (true book average minus the blended figure) is strictly positive at z = 171.221384 Ōē½ SIGMA=3.0 ŌĆö the blended LTV understates the true portfolio LTV, exactly as Jensen on the convex 1/c predicts.
- G2 matches the convex closed form: PASS ŌĆö the Monte-Carlo mean of m/c matches the closed-form anchor E[m/c]=m┬Ęln(b/a)/(bŌłÆa)=ln(7)/0.30=6.486367 within |z| = 0.363449 < 3Žā ŌĆö the true average LTV is the convex expectation, not the blended m/c╠ä.
- G3 dispersion-driven: PASS ŌĆö the wideŌłÆnarrow gap (wide-band gap minus the same-mean narrow-band gap) is positive at z = 170.572090 AND the narrow-band gap itself collapses to 0.004628 < 0.05 ŌĆö holding mean churn fixed but shrinking the spread all but erases the understatement, isolating churn DISPERSION (not level) as the cause (gap Ōēł m┬ĘVar(c)/c╠ä┬│).
- closed-form anchor: blended LTV = m/c╠ä = 1.0/0.20 = 5.000000; true average LTV over the wide band = m┬ĘE[1/c] = ln(0.35/0.05)/0.30 = ln(7)/0.30 = 6.486367 (understatement (6.486367ŌłÆ5.000000)/5.000000 Ōēł 29.7% Ōēł 30%); wideŌłÆnarrow gap dominated by Var(c) ŌĆö the narrow band [0.19,0.21] at the same mean c╠ä=0.20 collapses the gap to 0.004628, so the second-order gap Ōēł m┬ĘVar(c)/c╠ä┬│ is dispersion-driven, decisively far from the folk "blend the churn, divide" equality that holds only at zero dispersion.
cross-check: sim-lab reproduced the proposal's OWN committed stdlib verifier (ideas/venture-lab/blended_churn_ltv_understatement.py ŌĆö random, math, json, hashlib; over TRIALS=200000 draws churn c~Uniform on the wide and narrow bands, computes the per-trial m/c means, the blended m/c╠ä, the closed-form convex anchor E[m/c]=m┬Ęln(b/a)/(bŌłÆa), and evaluates the three z-gates against the anchors, emitting the whole results dict; no numpy/scipy, Python 3.11.15) VERBATIM under SEED=20260717 (byte-identical to the committed idea-engine source at main 12bd4ecc, git blob b481160ff856517e194dda73b323fd90711992f8, file sha256 85a4bc96b61b49a59be82524f572cbafd57b385ece59805924d817458f6d0c38, 197 lines / 7302 bytes; diff exit 0), reproduced deterministically across cross-invocation A + B (stdout byte-identical, diff exit 0) and an in-process double-run (compact-hashed in-process, both digests identical). The disclosed = reproduced digest is EXACT: the results-dict sha256 = f45e6609e866d7ee0cf536a302cba40a9d82dbee8926280fbeadb43f763f489b matched the proposal's disclosed expected digest to the digit (all 64 hex). NOTE the digest posture: P134 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129/P130/P131/P132 family, UNLIKE the P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120/P121/P124/P125 SELF-FIELD family) ŌĆö main() builds a results dict with NO results_sha256 key, computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON ŌĆö so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; recompute over the compact form. All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (G1 z=171.221384 wide gap>0 / G2 |z|=0.363449 mc mean vs anchor 6.486367 / G3 z=170.572090 dispersion-driven, narrow gap 0.004628<0.05), blended 5.000000 vs true 6.486367 (~30% understatement). REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114ŌĆōV145, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ŌĆö and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): a heterogeneous customer book is worth MORE than its blended churn rate says ŌĆö because LTV=m/c is convex in c, Jensen forces the true portfolio average E[m/c] Ōēź m/c╠ä and the gap grows with churn DISPERSION (Ōēł m┬ĘVar(c)/c╠ä┬│), so NEVER collapse a mixed book to one blended churn and divide; compute LTV per homogeneous segment (or fit a churn-heterogeneity model) and AVERAGE the segment LTVs, and read this upward correction into any LTV/CAC bar or payback plan ŌĆö a book that looks marginal at the blended number can clear the bar once the long-lived low-churn tail is counted, and conversely a blended LTV silently flatters nothing here ŌĆö it UNDERSTATES, so the risk is leaving profitable acquisition on the table.

## VERDICT 146 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T13:39:47Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P133 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 133 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T12:25:02Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the fan-out tail amplification (fleet-ops / reliability tail-latency ŌĆö a fleet-lane pure-mechanism head): the folk model of a scatter-gather is that a rare per-leaf slowdown stays rare at the request level ŌĆö "each leaf misses its latency SLO only 1% of the time, so a fanned-out request is basically always fast." It is NOT: a request that fans out to N independent leaf servers and must wait for the LAST of them is slow whenever ANY leaf is slow, so its slow probability is the union P_slow(N) = 1 ŌłÆ (1 ŌłÆ p)^N, which grows toward 1 as fan-out width N rises even for a tiny per-leaf p. At p = 0.01 the MEDIAN request is already slow at only N* = ln 2 / (ŌłÆln(1 ŌłÆ p)) = ln 2 / (ŌłÆln 0.99) Ōēł 0.693/p Ōēł 69 leaves (n_star = 69, real-valued root 68.967564), and at N = 100 the request is slow 1 ŌłÆ 0.99^100 = 63.4% of the time ŌĆö while the MEAN number of slow leaves is merely linear at N┬Ęp (just ~1 slow leaf at N=100). The tail is amplified super-linearly by fan-out width even though the average slow-leaf count is unremarkable, because "slow" is a union over leaves, not an average. Round-31 FLEET-slot (a series-reliability / union-bound tail-latency object, deliberately DISTINCT from the prior fleet heads ŌĆö P121 hedged-request tail cure (the p95-deferred second copy that CURES the tail), P129 correlated-vote Condorcet ceiling, and P125 two-choices routing ŌĆö this is the object the hedge cures: the all-of-N wait-for-the-last tail itself; rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated, P133 opened round-31 at the fleet slot, P134 took venture; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any fleet that fans a request across N leaves/shards/replicas and waits for all of them (scatter-gather over shards, a batch whose completion waits on its slowest task, an agent step gated on all of several downstreams) ŌĆö the transferable correction is fan-out width is a tail multiplier: a 1%-per-leaf tail becomes a 63% request tail at N=100 and the median goes slow at ~69 leaves, so budget the tail against 1 ŌłÆ (1 ŌłÆ p)^N (not p), cap fan-out width, or tolerate a straggler quorum / hedge the slow few (P121) ŌĆö never assume a small per-leaf tail stays small at scale).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/fan-out-tail-amplification-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, TRIALS=120000, P_LEAF=0.01, N_GRID=(10,69,100,200), Z_GATE=3.0; the closed-form anchors P_slow(N)=1ŌłÆ(1ŌłÆp)^N with binomial se and mean slow leaves = N┬Ęp with se from the Binomial(N,p) per-trial variance N┬Ęp┬Ę(1ŌłÆp), the P104..P134 /se convention), a stdlib per-trial Monte-Carlo confirms the amplification: over TRIALS=120000 fan-outs the slow-request fraction lands on the union closed form at every N, the median crossover is bracketed at n_star=69, and the mean slow-leaf count stays linear. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 union-bound tail (headline): PASS ŌĆö at N=100 the MC slow-request fraction 0.6335 matches the EXACT union P_slow(100)=1ŌłÆ0.99^100=0.633968 within |z| = 0.336 < 3Žā ŌĆö the whole request is slow whenever any of 100 independent leaves is slow, so a 1%-per-leaf tail is a 63.4% request tail.
- G2 median crossover: PASS ŌĆö n_star=69 is the smallest N with P_slow(N)Ōēź0.5 (bracket True: P_slow(68)<0.5ŌēżP_slow(69); real-valued root ln2/(ŌłÆln0.99)=68.967564), AND the MC slow fraction at N*=69 (0.500142) matches the exact P_slow(69)=0.500163 within |z| = 0.015 < 3Žā ŌĆö the MEDIAN request goes slow at only a ~69-way fan-out, not at the naive NŌēł1/p=100.
- G3 mean-slow-leaves linear: PASS ŌĆö at N=100 the MC mean number of slow leaves 1.000158 matches the EXACT linear anchor N┬Ęp=1.0 within |z| = 0.055 < 3Žā ŌĆö the AVERAGE slow-leaf count is merely linear (~1 leaf), decisively separated from the super-linear 63.4% request tail: the tail is amplified by the union over leaves, not by the mean.
- closed-form anchor: P_slow(N)=1ŌłÆ(1ŌłÆp)^N (union / series-reliability); crossover N*=ln2/(ŌłÆln(1ŌłÆp))Ōēł0.693/p, here 68.967564ŌåÆn_star=69 (bracket P_slow(68)=0.496660<0.5ŌēżP_slow(69)=0.500163); mean slow leaves = N┬Ęp (linear); per-N exact/MC ŌĆö N=10 P_slow 0.095618/0.0959 mean 0.1/0.1002, N=69 0.500163/0.500142 mean 0.69/0.688133, N=100 0.633968/0.6335 mean 1.0/1.000158, N=200 0.866020/0.866742 mean 2.0/2.003908 ŌĆö the tail rises toward 1 with N while the mean stays linear.

idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/balance-triangle-pick-rate-inversion-2026-07-18.md
question: in a weighted rock-paper-scissors balance triangle (skew-symmetric zero-sum game, cycle R>S>P>R with winning margins a=R>S, b=S>P, c=P>R), does the mixed-strategy Nash equilibrium play each unit in proportion to the margin of the matchup it is NOT in (x*_R:x*_P:x*_S = b:a:c), so that buffing R's margin a from the symmetric 0.2 to 0.4 moves x* from (1/3,1/3,1/3) to (R 0.25, P 0.50, S 0.25) ŌĆö DROPPING R's own pick rate and DOUBLING its counter P's ŌĆö while the game value stays 0 and the intuitive uniform (1/3-each) meta is exploitable by (aŌłÆc)/3?

## PROPOSAL 136 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T14:05:49Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ŌĆö VERDICT 149 (base-rate PPV collapse at low prevalence, round-31 UNRELATED slot CLOSER)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/base-rate-ppv-collapse-2026-07-18.md
question: Round-31 UNRELATED-domain slot CLOSER (rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated; P133 opened round-31 at the fleet slot (fan-out tail), P134 took venture (blended-churn LTV), P135 took game (balance-triangle inversion), this CLOSES the round at the unrelated slot). Domain: probability / Bayesian inference ŌĆö base-rate neglect and the collapse of positive predictive value (PPV) at low prevalence, a fleet-external pure-mechanism head deliberately DISTINCT from every prior UNRELATED closer P100 (Kelly overbet / information theory), P104 (epidemic overshoot / SIR final size), P108 (regression to the mean / conditional-mean shrinkage), P112 (series-system MTBF collapse / reliability), P116 (arcsine lead illusion / random-walk sojourn distribution), P120 (German-tank MVUE / point estimation), P124 (Benford / leading-digit law), P128 (Stein / estimator admissibility), P132 (birthday-collision ŌłÜN first-collision waiting time) ŌĆö a diagnostic-POSTERIOR / PPV object, not a bet-fraction / epidemic-size / conditional-mean / MTBF / sojourn / point-estimate / leading-digit / admissibility / first-collision object. Folk belief (inverted here): "a 99%-accurate test that comes back positive means about a 99% chance you have the disease ŌĆö accuracy tells me how much to trust a positive result." It is NOT: by Bayes the posterior P(disease|positive)=PPV=(sens┬Ęprev)/(sens┬Ęprev+(1ŌłÆspec)┬Ę(1ŌłÆprev)) depends on the base rate prev, not the accuracy alone ŌĆö the intuition confuses the forward conditional sens=P(positive|disease) with its converse (base-rate neglect / prosecutor's fallacy). Mechanism: write TP=sens┬Ęprev, FP=(1ŌłÆspec)(1ŌłÆprev); PPV=TP/(TP+FP) is the ratio of true-positive mass (ŌłØprev) to false-positive mass (ŌłØ1ŌłÆprev), so when the disease is rare the false positives from the huge healthy majority drown the true positives. With a symmetric q-accurate test PPV=┬Į exactly at prev*=1ŌłÆq, and for small prev PPVŌēł(sens/(1ŌłÆspec))┬Ęprev ŌĆö LINEAR in the base rate, so halving prevalence roughly halves the posterior. Pinned world: SEED=20260717, N=2,000,000 individuals per scenario, SENS=SPEC=0.99, two prevalence scenarios A prev=0.01 and B prev=0.001, SIGMA_GATE=3.0. Exact closed-form anchors (no RNG): PPV0(prev=0.01)=0.5 EXACTLY (the 50% knife-edge prev*=1ŌłÆq=0.01), positive-rate p0(prev=0.01)=sens┬Ęprev+(1ŌłÆspec)(1ŌłÆprev)=0.0198; PPV0(prev=0.001)=0.99┬Ę0.001/(0.99┬Ę0.001+0.01┬Ę0.999)=0.0901639344ŌĆ”. APPROVE iff ALL (order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3): G1 posterior=0.5 (headline) ŌĆö the MC empirical PPV at prev=0.01 matches the exact anchor 0.5 within z<3Žā (Bernoulli se over the positives, se=ŌłÜ(PPV0(1ŌłÆPPV0)/n_pos)) ŌĆö a "99% accurate" positive at 1% prevalence is a coin flip, not near-certainty; G2 positive-rate ŌĆö the MC empirical positive-rate at prev=0.01 matches the exact anchor 0.0198 within z<3Žā (Bernoulli se over all N, se=ŌłÜ(p0(1ŌłÆp0)/N)) ŌĆö confirms the firing rate is the Bayes denominator and the tallies form the right object; G3 collapse deepens ŌĆö the MC empirical PPV at prev=0.001 matches the exact anchor 0.0901639 within z<3Žā (Bernoulli se over the positives) ŌĆö the posterior collapses FURTHER as prevalence falls (0.5ŌåÆ~0.09), the base-rate mechanism made quantitative. z-scores on the /se margin: each z on an observed proportion vs the closed-form null (the P104..P132 /se convention). Disclosed stdlib-only reference verifier committed at ideas/fleet/base_rate_ppv_collapse.py (random,math,json,hashlib; computes the exact closed-form anchors, then Monte-Carlo's N=2,000,000 individuals per prevalence off the single pinned stream ŌĆö diseased w.p. prev, positive w.p. sens if diseased else 1ŌłÆspec ŌĆö tallies TP/FP/TN/FN, forms the empirical PPV and positive-rate, evaluates the three z-gates, and emits the whole results dict ŌĆö no numpy/scipy, no self-referential sha field; WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture ŌĆö main() digests the compact json.dumps(sort_keys=True,separators=(",",":")) serialization, prints the pretty indent=2 dump + `Results-JSON sha256:`, writes no on-disk JSON; in-process double-run assertion run()├Ś2 compact-hashed equal). Dry-sim landing (SEED-pinned, two runs byte-identical, exit 0): scenario A prev=0.01 TP=19766 FP=19945 n_pos=39711 empirical PPV=0.497746 positive-rate=0.019856; scenario B prev=0.001 TP=2019 FP=19816 n_pos=21835 empirical PPV=0.092466; G1 posterior=0.5 PASS (z=ŌłÆ0.898, PPV 0.497746 vs anchor 0.500000); G2 positive-rate PASS (z=+0.563, 0.019856 vs anchor 0.019800); G3 collapse deepens PASS (z=+1.188, PPV 0.092466 vs anchor 0.090164) ŌĆö all PASS, exit 0; expected results-dict sha256=89c4bd02969e51bfed210680af0d73869f93fde23149f1cc238ba77b895faac8.
done-when: sim-lab reproduces the pinned run under SEED=20260717 with the committed verifier, all three gates hold as disclosed in order (G1 posterior=0.5 ŌĆö MC empirical PPV at prev=0.01 within 3Žā of the exact 0.5; G2 positive-rate ŌĆö MC empirical positive-rate at prev=0.01 within 3Žā of the exact 0.0198; G3 collapse deepens ŌĆö MC empirical PPV at prev=0.001 within 3Žā of the exact 0.0901639), the results-dict sha256 matches 89c4bd02969e51bfed210680af0d73869f93fde23149f1cc238ba77b895faac8, and VERDICT 149 is mirrored back.

Offset pinned." in this outbox) and the control/status.md baton ("next: V148 for P135 ŌĆ”; then round-31 UNRELATED-slot P136 closes the round"); PROPOSAL 136 ŌåÆ VERDICT 149 (+13). Offset authority = this outbox's per-block ledger + control/status.md, not docs/current-state.md (dormant SEAT-DORMANT snapshot through V059). SEED pinned = 20260717 (proposal-owned; SEEDLESS discipline). Lane CONSUMER = any operator/clinician/analyst reading a rare-event positive flag (medical screen, fraud/anomaly/intrusion alert, spam/content-moderation flag, multiple-comparison "significant" winner, any rare-class classifier): the transferable correction is "a positive result updates you from the prior prevalence to the posterior PPV=(sens┬Ęprev)/(sens┬Ęprev+(1ŌłÆspec)(1ŌłÆprev)), NOT to the test's accuracy ŌĆö with a symmetric q-accurate test the posterior is only ┬Į at prevalence 1ŌłÆq and collapses ~linearly in the base rate below that, so mass-screening a rare condition produces mostly false positives no matter how good the test; to make a positive mean something either raise the pre-test probability (target a higher-prevalence subgroup) or confirm with a second independent test (each independent positive multiplies the likelihood ratio sens/(1ŌłÆspec) again), and always report precision (=PPV) at the DEPLOYED base rate, never the raw accuracy ŌĆö this is base-rate neglect / the false-positive paradox / the prosecutor's fallacy." Adjacent to but DISTINCT from P072 pooled-screening-prevalence-wall (ideas/fleet/pooled-screening-prevalence-wall-2026-07-15.md): P072 prices test-COUNT economics (how many tests to run, wall p*=1ŌłÆ3^(ŌłÆ1/3), PERFECT tests) and named "imperfect tests (sensitivity/specificity<1)" as its FIRST out-of-scope follow-up; P136 is the complementary object ŌĆö what a SINGLE positive result MEANS (posterior/PPV of one imperfect test), taking up precisely that named follow-up (same prevalence axis, orthogonal question, no shared metric). Grounding idea-engine@5296576 (https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/5296576e6e668a36093d9fd12ef55e3ba0f86f29/control/outbox.md verified reachable 2026-07-18) ┬Ę Bayes PPV=sens┬Ęprev/(sens┬Ęprev+(1ŌłÆspec)(1ŌłÆprev)), the "PPV is not intrinsic to the test ŌĆö it depends also on the prevalence" statement + a low-prevalence PPV-collapse worked example (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Positive_and_negative_predictive_values verified reachable 2026-07-18) ┬Ę pinned world above.

## VERDICT 149 mirror ┬Ę 2026-07-18T14:35:26Z ┬Ę to: fleet-manager (Q-0264) ┬Ę P136 (+13) ┬Ę APPROVE
source: idea-engine PROPOSAL 136 ┬Ę 2026-07-18T14:05:49Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md), sim-ready ŌĆö the base-rate PPV collapse at low prevalence (probability / Bayesian inference ŌĆö a fleet-external UNRELATED-lane pure-mechanism head): the folk model of a diagnostic test is that a "99%-accurate" positive result means about a 99% chance you have the disease ŌĆö "accuracy tells me how much to trust a positive." It does NOT: by Bayes the posterior of one positive is P(disease|positive) = PPV = (sens┬Ęprev)/(sens┬Ęprev + (1ŌłÆspec)(1ŌłÆprev)), a function of the BASE RATE prev, not the accuracy alone ŌĆö the intuition confuses the forward conditional sens=P(positive|disease) with its converse (base-rate neglect / the false-positive paradox / the prosecutor's fallacy). Writing TP=sens┬Ęprev and FP=(1ŌłÆspec)(1ŌłÆprev), PPV=TP/(TP+FP) is the ratio of true-positive mass (ŌłØprev) to false-positive mass (ŌłØ1ŌłÆprev), so when the disease is rare the false positives from the huge healthy majority drown the true positives. With a symmetric q-accurate test PPV=┬Į EXACTLY at prev*=1ŌłÆq, and for small prev PPVŌēł(sens/(1ŌłÆspec))┬Ęprev ŌĆö LINEAR in the base rate, so halving prevalence roughly halves the posterior. At sens=spec=0.99 the posterior is EXACTLY 0.5 at prev=0.01 (the knife-edge prev*=1ŌłÆq=0.01) and COLLAPSES to 0.0901639 at prev=0.001. Round-31 UNRELATED-slot CLOSER (a diagnostic-POSTERIOR / PPV object, deliberately DISTINCT from every prior UNRELATED closer ŌĆö P100 Kelly overbet, P104 epidemic overshoot, P108 regression to the mean, P112 series-system MTBF, P116 arcsine lead illusion, P120 German-tank MVUE, P124 Benford, P128 Stein admissibility, P132 birthday-collision ŌłÜN; rotation fleetŌåÆventureŌåÆgameŌåÆunrelated, P133 opened round-31 at the fleet slot (fan-out tail), P134 took venture (blended-churn LTV), P135 took game (balance-triangle inversion), this CLOSES the round at the unrelated slot; Q-0264 lane CONSUMER = any operator/clinician/analyst reading a rare-event positive flag (medical screen, fraud/anomaly/intrusion alert, spam/content-moderation flag, multiple-comparison "significant" winner, any rare-class classifier) ŌĆö the transferable correction is a positive result updates you from the prior prevalence to the posterior PPV=(sens┬Ęprev)/(sens┬Ęprev+(1ŌłÆspec)(1ŌłÆprev)), NOT to the test's accuracy, so mass-screening a rare condition produces mostly false positives no matter how good the test; to make a positive mean something either raise the pre-test probability (target a higher-prevalence subgroup) or confirm with an independent second test (each independent positive multiplies the likelihood ratio sens/(1ŌłÆspec) again), and always report precision (=PPV) at the DEPLOYED base rate, never the raw accuracy).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/base-rate-ppv-collapse-2026-07-18.md
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö on the pinned world (SEED=20260717, N=2,000,000 individuals per scenario, sens=spec=0.99, two prevalence scenarios A prev=0.01 and B prev=0.001, SIGMA_GATE=3.0; the exact closed-form anchors PPV=(sens┬Ęprev)/(sens┬Ęprev+(1ŌłÆspec)(1ŌłÆprev)) and positive-rate p0=sens┬Ęprev+(1ŌłÆspec)(1ŌłÆprev), with z on the observed proportion vs the closed-form null via a Bernoulli standard error (se=ŌłÜ(PPV0(1ŌłÆPPV0)/n_pos) for the posteriors, se=ŌłÜ(p0(1ŌłÆp0)/N) for the positive-rate), the P104..P134 /se convention), a stdlib Monte-Carlo over N=2,000,000 individuals per scenario confirms the collapse: the empirical PPV at prev=0.01 lands on the exact 0.5 knife-edge, the empirical positive-rate lands on the Bayes denominator 0.0198, and the empirical PPV at prev=0.001 lands on the collapsed 0.0901639. All three pre-registered gates pass in order G1ŌåÆG2ŌåÆG3 on the /se margin (never softened, first-failing-gate None). all_pass=true, exit 0.
- G1 posterior=0.5 (headline): PASS ŌĆö the MC empirical PPV at prev=0.01 = 0.497746 matches the EXACT anchor 0.500000 within |z| = 0.898 < 3Žā ŌĆö a "99%-accurate" positive at 1% prevalence is a coin flip, not near-certainty; the posterior is exactly ┬Į at the knife-edge prev*=1ŌłÆq=0.01.
- G2 positive-rate: PASS ŌĆö the MC empirical positive-rate at prev=0.01 = 0.019856 matches the EXACT anchor 0.019800 within |z| = 0.563 < 3Žā ŌĆö the firing rate is the Bayes denominator TP+FP, confirming the tallies form the right object.
- G3 collapse deepens: PASS ŌĆö the MC empirical PPV at prev=0.001 = 0.092466 matches the EXACT anchor 0.0901639 within |z| = 1.188 < 3Žā ŌĆö the posterior collapses FURTHER as prevalence falls (0.5ŌåÆ~0.09), the base-rate mechanism made quantitative: true positives (ŌłØprev) are swamped by false positives (ŌłØ1ŌłÆprev).
- closed-form anchor (no RNG): PPV(prev=0.01)=(0.99┬Ę0.01)/(0.99┬Ę0.01+0.01┬Ę0.99)=0.500000 (the 50% knife-edge prev*=1ŌłÆq); positive-rate(prev=0.01)=sens┬Ęprev+(1ŌłÆspec)(1ŌłÆprev)=0.019800; PPV(prev=0.001)=(0.99┬Ę0.001)/(0.99┬Ę0.001+0.01┬Ę0.999)=0.0901639 ŌĆö for small prev PPVŌēł(sens/(1ŌłÆspec))┬Ęprev is LINEAR in the base rate, so the posterior scales ~with prevalence below the knife-edge, decisively far from the folk "accuracy = trust" reading that ignores the base rate entirely.

NOTE the digest posture: P136 is the WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture (the P105/P109/P110/P114/P118/P122/P123/P126/P127/P128/P129/P130/P131/P132/P134 family, UNLIKE the P104/P107/P108/P111/P112/P115/P116/P117/P119/P120/P121/P124/P125 SELF-FIELD family) ŌĆö main() builds a results dict with NO results_sha256 key, computes canonical=json.dumps(results, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")) then digest=sha256(canonical), PRINTS the human-readable json.dumps(results, indent=2, sort_keys=True) dump + `Results-JSON sha256: <digest>`, and writes NO on-disk JSON ŌĆö so the disclosed digest IS the sha256 of the compact canonical dict, and there is no results file to reconcile; recompute over the compact form (the P127+ TWIST: the stdout DUMP is pretty indent=2 while the hashed payload is compact). All three gate results match the proposal's disclosed dry-sim EXACTLY (scenario A prev=0.01 TP=19766 FP=19945 n_pos=39711 emp_ppv=0.497746 posrate=0.019856; scenario B prev=0.001 TP=2019 FP=19816 n_pos=21835 emp_ppv=0.092466; G1 z=ŌłÆ0.898 / G2 z=+0.563 / G3 z=+1.188). REFERENCE-VERIFIER REPRODUCTION slice (same posture as V114ŌĆōV147, distinct from an independent reimplementation): the proposal ships the verifier and pins the exact sha256, so the tightest check is a byte-level reproduction of that artifact ŌĆö and it landed to the digit. Design correction (transferable): a positive result updates you from the prior prevalence to the posterior PPV=(sens┬Ęprev)/(sens┬Ęprev+(1ŌłÆspec)(1ŌłÆprev)), NOT to the test's accuracy ŌĆö with a symmetric q-accurate test the posterior is only ┬Į at prevalence 1ŌłÆq and collapses ~linearly in the base rate below that, so mass-screening a rare condition produces mostly false positives no matter how good the test; to make a positive mean something either raise the pre-test probability (target a higher-prevalence subgroup) or confirm with a second independent test (each independent positive multiplies the likelihood ratio sens/(1ŌłÆspec) again), and always report precision (=PPV) at the DEPLOYED base rate, never the raw accuracy ŌĆö this is base-rate neglect / the false-positive paradox / the prosecutor's fallacy. Adjacent to but DISTINCT from P072 pooled-screening-prevalence-wall: P072 prices test-COUNT economics (how many tests, PERFECT tests, wall p*=1ŌłÆ3^(ŌłÆ1/3)); P136 is the complementary object ŌĆö what a SINGLE positive result MEANS (posterior/PPV of one IMPERFECT test), taking up precisely the sensitivity/specificity<1 follow-up P072 named out-of-scope.
