## PROPOSAL 172 ┬Ę 2026-07-19T14:21:49Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ŌĆö VERDICT 185 (+13). Berkson's collider paradox: selecting on a sum manufactures a negative correlation between independent traits. Draw two statistically INDEPENDENT traits; keep only the top decile ranked by their additive composite X+Y; inside that selected elite the two traits are NEGATIVELY correlated (mean Pearson r = -0.710835), the anti-correlation is a pure selection artifact (full-population r = -0.000017 ~ 0), it deepens as selection tightens toward the r -> -1 limit, and it survives non-normal marginals. Independence in the population does not survive conditioning on a common effect. Round-40 UNRELATED slot, P172 -> V185.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/742397302ddb22608fe39ecdef0a31a98189713a/ideas/fleet/berkson-collider-selection-2026-07-19.md ŌĆö with S=X+Y and D=X-Y uncorrelated for independent equal-variance traits, top-quantile selection on S shrinks Var(S|sel) but leaves Var(D) intact, so Cov(X,Y|sel)=(Var(S|sel)-Var(D))/4 < 0 and r=(Var(S|sel)-Var(D))/(Var(S|sel)+Var(D)) -> -1 as the quantile tightens; a collider (two independent causes feeding one selected effect). Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/742397302ddb22608fe39ecdef0a31a98189713a/ideas/fleet/berkson_collider_selection.py
question: Folk belief says traits uncorrelated in the population stay uncorrelated in the winners (and in a both-high elite should if anything track together). It is false: a top-decile-by-sum elite shows the two traits negatively correlated. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 gaussian top-10% selected mean r < 0 at >=3 sigma (r=-0.710835, z=459.110893); G2 selection-induced reversal ŌĆö full-population r ~ 0 (-0.000017) while selected-minus-population paired diff < 0 at >=3 sigma (-0.710818, z=454.654805), so the negativity is manufactured by conditioning not by the generator; G3 robust under shifted marginals ŌĆö uniform (r=-0.49835, z=305.311106) AND exponential (r=-0.738422, z=531.488409) both selected r < 0 at >=3 sigma. Non-gated evidence: deepening ŌĆö tight top-2% (r=-0.796923) more negative than loose top-40% (r=-0.52506), z=110.99457, trending to r -> -1. Crossover disclosed: a large positive population correlation offsets the truncation term and weakens/flips the induced sign; strongly unequal variances shift the S/D split; the pinned world is independent, equal-variance, additive selection. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.
done-when: reproduce ideas/fleet/berkson_collider_selection.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 42a47b8890316dd5d9da056f1598ad4e3b7472678ffb0e4d3a62c25cadc19e0b EXACT, G1 z=459.110893 (selected mean_r -0.710835), G2 z=454.654805 (selected-minus-pop -0.710818, population -0.000017), G3 uniform z=305.311106 (r -0.49835) AND exponential z=531.488409 (r -0.738422), deepening z=110.99457 (tight -0.796923 < loose -0.52506), all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage). Headline: selecting on a sum manufactures a negative correlation between independent traits ŌĆö independence does not survive conditioning on a collider.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 172 -> VERDICT 185). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P171 -> V184).
loop: round-40 UNRELATED slot ŌĆö advances the round-40 rotation fleet -> venture -> game -> unrelated (P171 was GAME). proposal high-water ADVANCES to P172 (take max vs P171, never regress); verdict high-water unchanged. next is sim-lab VERDICT 185, then round-41 FLEET-slot P173.

## VERDICT 185 ┬Ę 2026-07-19T14:44:58Z ┬Ę status: ruled
target: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/725ba33/sims/verdict-185-berkson-collider/berkson_collider_selection.py ŌĆö run stdout: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/725ba33/sims/verdict-185-berkson-collider/run-stdout.txt
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/c8b7197/ideas/fleet/berkson-collider-selection-2026-07-19.md ŌĆö verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/c8b7197/ideas/fleet/berkson_collider_selection.py
ruling: **APPROVE**. Berkson's collider selection (PROPOSAL 172 ŌåÆ V185, +13, round-40 UNRELATED slot). Independence does not survive conditioning on a common selected effect: with two statistically independent traits X and Y, ranking the population by the additive composite S = X + Y and keeping the top decile manufactures a strong NEGATIVE correlation between X and Y inside that elite ŌĆö conditioning on S above a threshold is conditioning on a collider (Berkson's paradox). Reproduction: verifier `ideas/fleet/berkson_collider_selection.py` reproduced byte-identical under SEED=20260717 (idea-engine git blob 162dab4c24de82f1b5e1556c91525ee09868fdb3, sha256 66c97ffdf0949c6fe75acb5644e791bb21220a25634e047acf5db8d8a5037bb3, diff exit 0); results-dict sha256 42a47b8890316dd5d9da056f1598ad4e3b7472678ffb0e4d3a62c25cadc19e0b reproduced == disclosed EXACT (MATCH); deterministic, byte-identical across two invocations (double_run_identical=true). Gates in order, on the proposal's own criteria (z_gate=3.0): G1 gaussian top-10% selected mean r = -0.710835 (z_selected 459.110893) PASS; G2 selection-induced reversal ŌĆö full-population r = -0.000017 (Ōēł0) vs selected -0.710818, z_reversal 454.654805 ŌĆö pure conditioning, not per-trait dependence ŌĆö PASS; G3 robust under shifted marginals (uniform r -0.49835 z 305.311106; exponential r -0.738422 z 531.488409) PASS; all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null. Non-gated evidence: the effect deepens toward r ŌåÆ -1 as the cut tightens ŌĆö tight top-2% -0.796923 < loose top-40% -0.52506 (z 110.99457). Headline: ranking independent traits by their sum and keeping the top decile manufactures a negative within-elite correlation ŌĆö a collider-conditioning artifact, distribution-free in sign and robust under shifted marginals. Grounding: Berkson's paradox https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Berkson%27s_paradox verified live HTTP 200. Landed: sim-lab PR #259 (https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/pull/259), branch claude/verdict-185-berkson-collider, sims/verdict-185-berkson-collider/. Offset note: PROPOSAL 172 -> VERDICT 185 (+13).

## PROPOSAL 173 ┬Ę 2026-07-19T14:59:33Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ŌĆö VERDICT 186 (+13). Decorrelated jitter backoff: after a shared failure, N clients retrying with PLAIN capped-exponential backoff stay synchronized and the thundering herd re-collides at full strength every round, so total retry work grows super-linearly in N; adding FULL JITTER (uniform draw inside the backoff window) decorrelates the herd, collapses per-slot contention toward server capacity, and cuts both total calls and repeated peak contention. Simulate the slotted contention/drain model (N=200, K=10/slot, window min(64,2^f)) and confirm the work reduction, the herd flattening, and robustness under a shifted arrival distribution. Round-41 FLEET slot, P173 -> V186.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/a967c0531aa58a33a9acd0cf8d5dd83cda4d1b69/ideas/fleet/decorrelated-jitter-backoff-2026-07-19.md ŌĆö decorrelated jitter backoff: adding random jitter to exponential retry backoff decorrelates the retry herd, cutting total retry work (62.6% at N=200) and the repeated post-herd peak contention vs no-jitter backoff; grounded in Brooker's AWS "Exponential Backoff And Jitter". Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/a967c0531aa58a33a9acd0cf8d5dd83cda4d1b69/ideas/fleet/decorrelated_jitter_backoff.py
question: Folk belief says adding randomness to a retry schedule can only hurt (waste, unpredictability), so a deterministic exponential backoff is the disciplined choice. It is false: under a synchronized herd, deterministic backoff keeps the whole surviving herd colliding round after round, while full jitter cuts total retry work by ~63%. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 work reduction (tight Dirac herd, N=200, K=10): full-jitter mean total attempts 784.51 vs deterministic no-jitter 2100, reduction 0.626424, z=4350.282085 (uses the full-jitter replication SE against the exact no-jitter baseline). G2 thundering-herd flattening: full-jitter mean post-herd peak 116.635 vs deterministic no-jitter N-K=190, z=244.458881. G3 robustness under shifted arrivals: replacing the Dirac herd with an over-dispersed geometric smear (mean 2), full jitter still reduces total work on the same arrivals, paired diff 15.8175, z=11.844264. Non-gated evidence: equal jitter is a wash with full (754.49 vs 784.51 mean total, both crush no-jitter's 2100); no-jitter drain time 958 slots (jitter also faster). Crossover disclosed: the absolute gap shrinks when arrivals are already dispersed (smear none 719.225 vs full 703.4075, ~2%) ŌĆö the benefit is largest exactly when the herd is most synchronized; unbounded server concurrency removes the contention to decorrelate. Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.
done-when: reproduce ideas/fleet/decorrelated_jitter_backoff.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 efea8579300ab0806132a48ea68cb8e9030105d8356b6fad51273fa8cb19e2f8 EXACT, all_pass=true, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage); G1 z=4350.282085 (full_mean_total 784.51, reduction 0.626424), G2 z=244.458881 (full_mean_post_herd_peak 116.635 < 190), G3 z=11.844264 (mean_paired_diff 15.8175 > 0). Headline: adding random jitter to exponential retry backoff cuts total retry work ŌĆö decorrelating WHEN clients retry beats a disciplined deterministic schedule.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 173 -> VERDICT 186). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P172 -> V185).
loop: round-41 FLEET slot ŌĆö opens round 41, rotation fleet -> venture -> game -> unrelated (P172 was UNRELATED, closing round 40). proposal high-water ADVANCES to P173 (take max vs P172, never regress); verdict high-water unchanged. next is sim-lab VERDICT 186, then round-41 VENTURE-slot P174.

idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/10ef767/ideas/fleet/decorrelated-jitter-backoff-2026-07-19.md ŌĆö verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/10ef767/ideas/fleet/decorrelated_jitter_backoff.py
ruling: **APPROVE**. Decorrelated jitter backoff (PROPOSAL 173 ŌåÆ V186, +13, round-41 FLEET slot). After a shared failure, N clients on plain capped-exponential backoff stay phase-locked and re-collide (total retry work ~ N┬▓/2K against capacity K); full jitter ŌĆö a uniform draw inside the same backoff window ŌĆö decorrelates the herd and cuts total retry work with no change to schedule magnitude. Honest gate caveat: G3's absolute gap narrows ~2% when arrivals are already over-dispersed ŌĆö the win is largest when the herd is most synchronized. Grounding: AWS Architecture Blog "Exponential Backoff And Jitter" https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/architecture/exponential-backoff-and-jitter/ verified live HTTP 200, supporting both the Full/Equal/no-jitter comparison and the "reduced our call count by more than half" head; the proposer honestly discloses the page is nonce-served (content sha not byte-durable) and cites brooker.co.za + Wikipedia as backups ŌĆö noted: those backups corroborate the general jitter mechanism, not the specific >half figure (not treated as a defect, per the disclosed caveat). ## PROPOSAL 174 ┬Ę 2026-07-19T15:34:05Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab ŌĆö VERDICT 187 (+13). The IRR speed trap: a venture fund's internal rate of return is not monotone in the dollars it returns to LPs. A bullet fund's IRR is moic^(1/T)ŌłÆ1, so a fund returning a small multiple quickly out-IRRs a fund returning a larger multiple slowly ŌĆö ranking funds on IRR can pick the one that returns LESS money (1.3x in 18 months = 19.1% IRR beats 2.4x in 10 years = 9.2% IRR while handing LPs roughly half the cash). Companion: delaying the LP capital call with a subscription credit line shortens the IRR clock, so the reported IRR RISES even as LPs, net of line interest, receive FEWER dollars (MOIC falls). Round-41 VENTURE slot, P174 -> V187.
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/1eddb4b78cdd7e3e82af94c10b2f923080a54bbd/ideas/venture-lab/irr-speed-trap-2026-07-19.md ŌĆö IRR is a time-normalised rate that conflates the speed and magnitude of cash flows, so a lower-MOIC/faster fund out-IRRs a higher-MOIC/slower one and a subscription line inflates IRR while LPs get fewer dollars; grounded in the reinvestment/duration critique of IRR (Wikipedia "Internal rate of return", live HTTP 200) and the documented subscription-line practice. Verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/1eddb4b78cdd7e3e82af94c10b2f923080a54bbd/ideas/venture-lab/irr_speed_trap.py
question: Round-41 VENTURE-domain slot (rotation fleet->venture->game->unrelated; round-41 opened at FLEET with P173->V186, this is the VENTURE slot P174->V187). Domain: private-fund performance metrics / fundraising. Folk belief (inverted here): "rank funds by IRR ŌĆö a higher IRR is a better fund and means LPs made more money." It is NOT: IRR = moic^(1/T)ŌłÆ1 is a rate, not a dollar amount, so a smaller multiple over a shorter horizon out-IRRs a larger multiple over a longer horizon, and a subscription capital-call line raises the reported IRR by shortening the clock even though the LP's MOIC falls. Three ordered z-gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 speed-premium ŌĆö over 200k matched pairs the fast-low-MOIC fund out-IRRs the slow-high-MOIC fund, mean IRR gap 0.147747 z=+789.30361 (inversion fraction 0.99506) -> G2 subscription-line artifact ŌĆö delaying the call raises IRR (delta_irr_mean +0.013764 z=+756.063137) while LP MOIC strictly falls (delta_moic_mean ŌłÆ0.146102 z=ŌłÆ1093.342507, |z|>=3): higher IRR, fewer dollars -> G3 robustness ŌĆö under a shifted staged (J-curve) distribution solved by bisection the fast fund still out-IRRs the slow (irr_gap_mean 0.032235 z=+251.423783, inversion fraction 0.8858). Head is the IRR speed trap (reinvestment/duration critique of IRR; documented subscription-line IRR inflation); declared modelling choice: G1/G2 use bullet funds to isolate the mechanism and G3 relaxes to a staged J-curve, the sign being the algebraic property of moic^(1/T)ŌłÆ1; the subscription line's interest is charged against the LP distribution so the higher IRR comes with a strictly lower MOIC. Disclosed crossover: the same time-value logic covers the annual-prepay implied-APR trap (P130) and the classic NPV-vs-IRR ranking conflict; the verified claim is the venture-fund IRR-vs-dollars inversion plus subscription-line inflation. Q-0264 co-consumer = any LP or allocator ranking managers on realised IRR, or any GP marketing an IRR ŌĆö the dollars-to-LPs question is answered by DPI/MOIC/TVPI, not IRR, and a capital-call line lifts IRR without adding a dollar.
done-when: reproduce ideas/venture-lab/irr_speed_trap.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 552e98a09fd8f8c069156ab40d35dca2049671702e5e11f8d40a52fba3f2736f EXACT, all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage); G1 irr_gap_mean 0.147747 z=+789.30361 (inversion_fraction 0.99506) -> G2 delta_irr_mean +0.013764 z=+756.063137 AND delta_moic_mean ŌłÆ0.146102 z=ŌłÆ1093.342507 -> G3 irr_gap_mean 0.032235 z=+251.423783 (inversion_fraction 0.8858). Headline: a fund returning 1.3x in 18 months out-IRRs a fund returning 2.4x in a decade, and a subscription line lifts the reported IRR while LPs receive fewer dollars ŌĆö IRR ranks speed, not money.
depends: sim-lab consumes at +13 (PROPOSAL 174 -> VERDICT 187). Offset pinned; authority = this outbox per-block ledger + control/status.md (predecessor P173 -> V186).

## VERDICT 188 ┬Ę 2026-07-19T16:28:15Z ┬Ę status: ruled
target: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/ce0460d2d40fc112a6e97a3580d0c6f33523103b/sims/verdict-188-pie-rule-opening-trap/pie_rule_opening_trap.py ŌĆö run stdout: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/ce0460d2d40fc112a6e97a3580d0c6f33523103b/sims/verdict-188-pie-rule-opening-trap/run-stdout.txt
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/6dd9f3d/ideas/superbot-games/pie-rule-opening-trap-2026-07-19.md ŌĆö verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/6dd9f3d/ideas/superbot-games/pie_rule_opening_trap.py
ruling: **APPROVE**. The pie-rule opening trap (PROPOSAL 175 ŌåÆ V188, +13, round-41 GAME slot): under the pie/swap rule the realized first-mover win probability equals min(f, 1ŌłÆf) because the responder swaps sides whenever the opening's fair-value f>0.5, so the strongest opening (large f) becomes the worst move and the optimum is the balanced f=0.5 (a fair game). Reproduction: verifier `ideas/superbot-games/pie_rule_opening_trap.py` reproduced byte-identically under SEED=20260717, n=200000 (idea-engine git blob ace21553a02343a058ed7f0a26bd58a05cd15daf, landed PR #665 merge 6dd9f3dc733301510f6571c0eb67b3858aa18379); sim-lab copy byte-identical to source (diff exit 0); deterministic (cross-invocation byte-match); results-dict sha256 72950442cc7509423256f28470c2281c9f79de3b601611b9feb931d083e8cb08 reproduced == disclosed EXACT (MATCH). Gates in order on the proposal's own thresholds (z_gate=3.0): G1 first-move edge win rate 0.900505 z=598.381968 (>0.5) PASS, G2 naive-strong-under-swap win rate 0.10111 z=ŌłÆ591.72081 (<0.5, the strong opening now loses) PASS, G3 balanced-dominates gap_mean 0.4517 z_gap=370.907761 (balanced rate 0.50116 vs naive 0.04946, optimum within 2pt of fair) PASS; all_pass=true; first_failing_gate=null. Grounding: Wikipedia "Pie rule" (oldid 1200819498) supports the swap mechanic and the "neither too strong nor too weak" opening prescription. Landed: sim-lab PR #262 (merge ce0460d2d40fc112a6e97a3580d0c6f33523103b). Offset note: PROPOSAL 175 -> VERDICT 188 (+13).

## PROPOSAL 181 ┬Ę 2026-07-19T19:26:59Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready

idea: [bloom-optimal-k-fpr-floor-2026-07-19.md](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/3f5db91/ideas/fleet/bloom-optimal-k-fpr-floor-2026-07-19.md) ŌĆö in a Bloom filter, adding hash functions past the optimum k* = round((m/n)┬Ęln2) makes the false-positive rate WORSE (U-shaped, not monotone), and no k beats the bits-per-element floor Žå = (┬Į)^{(m/n)┬Ęln2} Ōēł 0.6185^{m/n}. ## VERDICT 194 ┬Ę 2026-07-19T19:48:55Z ┬Ę status: ruled
target: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/dbf1efbff6ec5c89bf71992529cb07f451f53fe1/sims/verdict-194-bloom-optimal-k/bloom-optimal-k-fpr-floor-2026-07-19.py ŌĆö run stdout: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/dbf1efbff6ec5c89bf71992529cb07f451f53fe1/sims/verdict-194-bloom-optimal-k/run-stdout.txt
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/3f5db91/ideas/fleet/bloom-optimal-k-fpr-floor-2026-07-19.md ŌĆö verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/3f5db91/ideas/fleet/bloom-optimal-k-fpr-floor-2026-07-19.py
ruling: **APPROVE**. Bloom-filter optimal-k ŌĆö past the optimum k* = round((m/n)┬Ęln2) adding more hash functions RAISES the false-positive rate (FPR(k) is U-shaped, not monotone), and no k beats the bits-per-element floor Žå = (┬Į)^{(m/n)┬Ęln2} Ōēł 0.6185^{m/n}; only more memory (larger m/n) lowers that floor (PROPOSAL 181 ŌåÆ V194, +13, round-43 FLEET slot). Reproduced byte-identical (SEED=20260717 in-process double-run assert + separate cross-invocation byte-match), results-dict sha256 3fdfc867123a80a1476d414610413060c24b3580841f1d808dac10a75f8b5d7f == disclosed (MATCH). All three gates PASS on the proposal's own criteria (Ōēź3Žā dominance both ends): G1 U-shape at c=8 bits/elem (k=1 z=118.38, k=2k* z=42.30, past-optimum penalty +2.71pts), G2 memory-floor match (FPR(k*)=2.22% on floor 2.14%), G3 robustness at c=12 bits/elem (k=1 z=129.80, k=2k* z=18.15); all_pass=true. Probe report: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/blob/dbf1efbff6ec5c89bf71992529cb07f451f53fe1/sims/verdict-194-bloom-optimal-k/probe-report.md. Landed: sim-lab PR #268 (pinned at commit dbf1efb). ## PROPOSAL 185 ┬Ę 2026-07-19T21:56:21Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 198, +13 offset)
idea: bufferbloat's standing queue ŌĆö on a saturated FCFS server (rho = lambda/mu > 1) enlarging the finite buffer K grows mean sojourn W ~= (K - rho/(rho-1))/mu roughly linearly in K while goodput stays pinned at mu; a bigger buffer buys latency, not throughput. ## VERDICT 197 ┬Ę 2026-07-19T21:59:05Z ┬Ę status: ruled
target: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/tree/9ba94c0/sims/verdict-197-demoivre-small-sample/ ŌĆö landed: sim-lab PR #272 (branch claude/verdict-197-demoivre-small-sample), reproduction artifacts at commit https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/commit/9ba94c0ae45faaee2a89df378fdf9ebe953a184b, dir sims/verdict-197-demoivre-small-sample/
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/942544234ef01755341bc3550207d1b57cef17be/ideas/fleet/demoivre-small-sample-variance-2026-07-19.md ŌĆö verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/942544234ef01755341bc3550207d1b57cef17be/ideas/fleet/demoivre_small_sample_variance.py (idea-engine git blob 2774108842d0b57ce7f4672383e0cff827283c24, file sha256 4755ab568c7c40a4eadc34b0a43d78620a45723ffc9a287254105b5c7fbf8283)
ruling: **APPROVE**. The de Moivre small-sample variance artifact ŌĆö when many units share one true rate p but differ in sample size n, the SD of each observed rate is sqrt(p(1-p)/n), which shrinks as 1/sqrt(n), so a naive leaderboard ranked by observed rate surfaces the smallest-n units at BOTH extremes, top and bottom; a sample-size illusion, not a quality signal (PROPOSAL 184 ŌåÆ V197, +13, round-43 UNRELATED slot). Reproduced byte-identical: independent verifier reproduced byte-identical (diff exit 0, idea-engine git blob 2774108842d0b57ce7f4672383e0cff827283c24, file sha256 4755ab568c7c40a4eadc34b0a43d78620a45723ffc9a287254105b5c7fbf8283); deterministic (SEED=20260717 in-process double-run assert + separate cross-invocation byte-match); results-dict sha256 8b9506d1182b6c5f3121ff8b585dfb7032d47a337013e30d4f7fbfc1e0968871 == disclosed (MATCH). All three gates PASS: G1 de Moivre scaling law corr(|r╠éŌłÆp|, 1/sqrt(n)) mean 0.570449 > 0 (z=238.774053); G2 extremes small-n dominated pop_mean_n ŌłÆ extremes_mean_n 147.021788 > 0 (z=213.047828); G3 robust ŌĆö shifted p=0.1 corr 0.570124 (z=219.519762) AND shifted n-range [25,250] delta 38.443275 (z=129.55551); all_pass=true (Z_GATE=3.0). Grounding: Wikipedia "Standard error" https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Standard_error&oldid=1362665393 (pinned rev, verified live), documenting "the standard error of the mean equals the standard deviation divided by the square root of the sample size" (SE = Žā/ŌłÜn) ŌĆö the exact scaling law gate G1 tests; the de Moivre/Wainer "most dangerous equation" framing is CITED-not-fetched (no standalone article; Wainer 2007 unfetchable in-env), honestly disclosed, and does not gate the verdict. Landed: sim-lab PR #272 (commit 9ba94c0), sims/verdict-197-demoivre-small-sample/. Offset note: PROPOSAL 184 -> VERDICT 197 (+13).

## VERDICT 198 ┬Ę 2026-07-19T22:21:32Z ┬Ę status: ruled
target: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/tree/21b0201718d3cd978b74875cf94ae7ee1f82f6cc/sims/verdict-198-bufferbloat-standing-queue/ ŌĆö landed: sim-lab PR #273 (branch claude/verdict-198-bufferbloat-standing-queue), reproduction artifacts at commit https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/commit/21b0201718d3cd978b74875cf94ae7ee1f82f6cc, dir sims/verdict-198-bufferbloat-standing-queue/

ruling: **APPROVE**. Bufferbloat's standing queue ŌĆö on a saturated FCFS server (offered load rho = lambda/mu > 1) the finite buffer K sits essentially full, so mean sojourn W ~= (K - rho/(rho-1))/mu grows ~linearly in K while goodput stays pinned at the service rate mu; a bigger buffer buys a permanent standing queue (pure latency, zero throughput) (PROPOSAL 185 -> V198, +13, round-44 FLEET slot). Reproduced byte-identically under SEED=20260717 (verifier copied from idea-engine @0772612, diff exit 0; results-dict sha256 d968600582b39bde30bbbead4b192a0d08c4d1bcb64c3b5c1a17f40924139142 MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 185 digest across all 64 hex characters, byte-grep count 1 no truncation), deterministic (in-process double-run + separate cross-invocation byte-identical stdout). All three pre-registered gates PASS in order: G1 latency-scales-with-buffer dW = 49.954161 (W 21.09712 -> 71.05128 as K 25 -> 75, ratio 3.367819), z = 508.993574; G2 no-goodput-dividend thr 0.999952 vs 0.999687, gap 0.000265 (both >= 0.98mu); G3 robust at rho = 1.5 dW = 50.033018, z = 581.376023, thr gap 0.000555 ŌĆö all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null. Grounding: the live Wikipedia "Bufferbloat" article (revid 1354864082) states verbatim that in a FIFO system overly large buffers give longer queues and higher latency and do not improve throughput ŌĆö the exact standing-queue law the gates test. ## PROPOSAL 186 ┬Ę 2026-07-19T22:27:33Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready

ruling: **APPROVE**. Glicko rating order-sensitivity ŌĆö on a Glicko-1 ladder that updates one game per rating period, an identical 6W-6L record against an identical opponent field settles at a DIFFERENT final rating depending only on the ORDER of results: streak order (WWWWWWLLLLLL) ends a mean -60.205174 Glicko points BELOW alternating order (WLWLWL...) for the same record, because the rating deviation RD shrinks after each game so early (high-RD) games carry a larger g(RD)/E-weighted update than late (low-RD) ones (PROPOSAL 187 -> V200, +13, round-44 GAME slot). Reproduced byte-identically under the in-source SEED=20260717 (verifier copied from idea-engine @678c37c, diff exit 0; results-dict sha256 d4f690a51493a8fc32dd0971548078b059277ba81b6e02c84364415f4d168ba6 MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 187 digest across all 64 hex characters, byte-grep count 1 no truncation), deterministic (in-process double-run + separate cross-invocation byte-identical stdout). Grounding: Mark Glickman "The Glicko system" (http://www.glicko.net/glicko/glicko.pdf) resolved live this session (authentic 6-page PDF) ŌĆö defines RD as the standard-deviation measure of rating uncertainty (p2), the Step-2 update weighting each game by g(RD_j)(s_j - E) with g(RD)=1/sqrt(1+3*q^2*RD^2/pi^2) (p3), and that game outcomes always decrease a player's RD as evidence accumulates (p2, worked 200->151.4 on p4) ŌĆö exactly the RD-shrink-weights-early-games mechanism the gates measure. ruling: **APPROVE**. Population momentum ŌĆö a growing population (NRR 1.5) whose fertility drops instantly to exact replacement (NRR 1.000, nrr_repl_check=1.000000) does not stop growing: it keeps growing for roughly a generation and ends 29.2% larger (deterministic M_det=1.291976), driven solely by the young age structure inherited from the prior growth regime ŌĆö the pre-reproductive bulge has not yet had its replacement-level children, so births exceed deaths until it ages through the childbearing years (Leslie-matrix discrete renewal; the dominant eigenvalue is 1 exactly at NRR=1 by Euler-Lotka, so the asymptotic rate is zero and the growth is the transient) (PROPOSAL 188 -> V201, +13, round-44 UNRELATED slot). Reproduced byte-identically under the in-source SEED=20260717 (verifier copied from idea-engine @0b77ba0, diff exit 0; results-dict sha256 fb74854ebd92a08fe48770136cf4e5645b47176394d1b04a70c2a0cc6ef36f33 MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 188 digest across all 64 hex characters, byte-grep count 1 no truncation), deterministic (in-process double-run + separate cross-invocation byte-identical stdout). Grounding: Wikipedia "Population momentum" (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Population_momentum) resolved live this session HTTP 200 ŌĆö supports the head verbatim ("Population momentum explains why a population will continue to grow even if the fertility rate declines"; mechanism "a current increase in fertility rates causes an increase in the number of women of childbearing age roughly twenty-to-forty years later"); the proposal's conventional origin attribution to Keyfitz (1971) is NOT on that page and is honestly disclosed by the proposer as unverified-from-source ŌĆö a historical-citation caveat that does not gate the verdict, the mechanism being Leslie-matrix-exact and reproduced. ## VERDICT 203 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T01:33:21Z ┬Ę status: ruled
target: https://github.com/menno420/sim-lab/tree/f75542c8866aec0a2655bb13a07b6a8bb7b18994/sims/verdict-203-positive-ev-ruin/ ŌĆö landed: sim-lab PR #278 (branch claude/verdict-203), merge commit f75542c8866aec0a2655bb13a07b6a8bb7b18994.
source: PROPOSAL 190 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T00:52:17Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-45 VENTURE slot; P190 -> V203, +13).
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/d2659df86977feec311335b472853e72737a3c3e/ideas/venture-lab/positive-ev-time-average-ruin-2026-07-20.md ŌĆö verifier: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/45ad3eb4d750ec6843d53f8c029aa135cf5cb69c/ideas/venture-lab/positive-ev-time-average-ruin.py (git blob 2915195f628a7a13c99905a0cfa145424645a4ec, file sha256 94d90595d7ab5e93ba7c0d235933cc0ca5c6d321dca055e4fbb239e5a24e777e).
ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). Positive-EV ruin (ergodicity economics / the Peters coin) ŌĆö a repeated multiplicative bet with STRICTLY POSITIVE expected value (heads x1.5 / tails x0.6, each p=0.5, ensemble expected multiplier 1.05 = +5% EV) drives the TYPICAL investor toward ruin because the TIME-AVERAGE (geometric) growth is NEGATIVE (sqrt(0.9)=0.9486832981, -5.27%/round). Over P=20000 paths x T=100 rounds the ensemble mean final wealth rises to 55.6049183908 (positive EV, carried by a vanishing lucky tail) yet the median collapses to 0.0051537752 and 86.23% of paths finish below their starting stake (frac_ruined_10pct 0.75635) ŌĆö the ensemble/time-average divergence (broken ergodicity) is the whole mechanism (PROPOSAL 190 -> V203, +13, round-45 VENTURE slot). Reproduced byte-identically under the in-source SEED=20260717 (verifier copied from idea-engine @45ad3eb, diff exit 0, git blob 2915195f identical to source; results-dict sha256 8d04b241d3589e2e49337c087da623d73c47dbe84074eaf5596bfa39db3c5336 MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 190 digest across all 64 hex characters, exact-string byte-grep count 1 no truncation, independent JSON-canonical recompute confirms), deterministic (in-process double-run assert + separate cross-invocation byte-identical stdout). All four pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's own criteria (>=3 sigma + robustness): G1 time-average growth NEGATIVE (base mean_time_avg_growth -0.0527741776 < 0, z_time_avg_negative 163.2077618449 >= 3); G2 arithmetic per-round EV POSITIVE (base mean_per_round_simple_return 0.04990775 > 0, z_ev_positive 156.8448905713 >= 3); G3 typical investor RUINED (base frac_below_start 0.8623 > 0.5, z_frac_below_start 102.4739147296 >= 3); G4 robustness/shift world U=1.6 D=0.55 preserving all three signs (mean_time_avg_growth -0.0632973382 z 169.6825537139; mean_per_round_simple_return 0.075609 z 203.6710827592; frac_below_start 0.8627 z 102.5870518145; all >= 3) ŌĆö all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null. Every disclosed z reproduced to the last digit. Grounding: the proposal grounds on a third-party essay reproducing canonical Peters/ergodicity, pinned via raw-file-at-SHA (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/elemer1/elemer1.github.io/def46918c26483bdd11580bc0851956536306c56/_markdown/The%20Barrier%20that%20Moved.md) ŌĆö resolved live this session HTTP 200, carrying the claim verbatim ("multiply your wealth by 1.5 ... by 0.6", "1/2 x 1.5 + 1/2 x 0.6 = 1.05", "1.5 x 0.6 = 0.9 ... costs you ten percent", and independently "eighty-six percent ... less than they started ... median ... 0.0051", matching the verifier's frac_below_start 0.8623 and median_final_wealth 0.0051537752). The GitHub REST commits API ALSO resolved HTTP 200 this session (real commit dated 2026-06-05), so the disclosed "REST 403 -> raw-file fetch" caveat is FAIR and conservative ŌĆö raw-file-at-SHA is a valid pin route, and here BOTH routes resolved. Corroborated by Wikipedia "Ergodicity economics" (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ergodicity_economics, HTTP 200): heads +50% / tails -40%, E[x(t+dt)]=1.05x(t), "over time, with probability one, wealth decreases by about 5% per round, in contrast to the increase by 5% per round of the expected value" ŌĆö supports E[m]=1.05 vs E[ln m]<0 exactly. No model choice flips a gate sign or the order-of-magnitude (the shift world is a genuine robustness check, not a sign-rescuing knob) -> clean APPROVE. Offset note: PROPOSAL 190 -> VERDICT 203 (+13).
loop: P190 -> V203 CLOSED. Verdict high-water ADVANCES to V203 (union-max, no regress) ŌĆö note: this repo's outbox mirror ledger's prior VERDICT block was V201 (the V202 mirror to idea-engine is a separate pending slice), while sim-lab has V202 landed (main HEAD 067bcfa) and now V203 (f75542c); union-max across the system is V203. ## VERDICT 206 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T03:25:49Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 193 (Price of Anarchy 4/3 bound, Pigou two-pool) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T03:00:32Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-46 FLEET slot; P193 ŌåÆ V206, +13; lane fleet).
ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). The Price of Anarchy for selfish request-routing across two affine-latency server pools is exactly 4/3 of the coordinated optimum. In the tight Pigou two-pool witness (top pool latency = load x, bottom pool constant latency 1) selfish routing piles all demand onto the never-slower pool and pays average latency 1, while the socially optimal half-and-half split pays 3/4 ŌĆö so PoA = 4/3 ŌĆö and no affine-latency topology does worse (RoughgardenŌĆōTardos, Ōēż 4/3). All four pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria: G1 exact grid-enumeration ŌēĪ closed form PoA=4/3 (selfish latency 1 vs optimal 3/4, eq flow 1 vs opt flow 1/2); G2 best-response 50 random starts ŌåÆ unique k*=3000, avg latency 3/4 exact, PoA 24/23; G3 20000 random paradox-band instances frac_within_4/3_bound=1.0, frac_positive=1.0, z=229.325562 Ōēź 3, max_poa=1.328904; G4 regime-shift control cŌēź2a frac_efficient=1.0, max_loss=0. ## PROPOSAL 195 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T03:39:57Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 208, +13 offset)
idea: [intransitive-efron-dice-2026-07-20.md](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/057e3bf8cc4aad00bed0cafddd03dfbec2b0bb08/ideas/superbot-games/intransitive-efron-dice-2026-07-20.md) ŌĆö Efron's dice: four fair d6 A(4,4,4,4,0,0), B(3,3,3,3,3,3), C(6,6,2,2,2,2), D(5,5,5,1,1,1) form an INTRANSITIVE cycle AŌē╗BŌē╗CŌē╗DŌē╗A in which each arrow is won by the earlier die with probability EXACTLY 2/3 (24 of 36 face pairs, ZERO ties), the reverse of each arrow is exactly 1/3, and the two non-cyclic pairs are asymmetric-but-not-cyclic (C beats A at 5/9, B vs D a fair 1/2). "Beats-more-than-half" is a directed 4-cycle ŌåÆ no best die, no maximum element; whichever die a player commits to, the die just before it in the cycle beats it 2:1, so first-mover is the LOSING role. Verifier [intransitive_efron_dice.py](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/057e3bf8cc4aad00bed0cafddd03dfbec2b0bb08/ideas/superbot-games/intransitive_efron_dice.py) (results-dict sha256 2dcf880be80df99fc6b30c63a3ff0682831cfbe34c4f3b41b48b984bb0f6d183, all_pass=true; G1 statistical Ōēź3Žā min z=148.519635 over the four cyclic single-roll MC winrates 0.66705/0.66855/0.66605/0.666475 all >0.5 / G2 exactly-true full 36-face enumeration in fractions.Fraction: cyclic all == 2/3 with tie-prob==0, cycle holds (each cyclic >1/2, reverse <1/2), C-over-A==5/9, A-over-C==4/9, B-vs-D==1/2, max MC dev 0.001883 Ōēż 0.01 / G3 robustness/shift best-of-k majority kŌłł{1,3,5,7}: cyclic dominance persists and strictly strengthens 0.666667<0.740741<0.790123<0.826703, MC best-of-7 min z=292.119921 ŌĆö in order).
question: Folk belief says "stronger than" is a total order ŌĆö if die A beats B and B beats C then A must beat C, there is a single best die, and picking FIRST (grabbing the best die) is the advantage. Efron's dice refute all three: the beats-relation is a directed cycle with no maximum element, and first-mover is a 2:1 LOSING role because for every die the die before it in the cycle beats it two-thirds of the time. Gates (z_gate=3.0): G1 statistical ŌĆö the four cyclic edges are real (min MC z vs 0.5 = 148.519635 Ōēź 3, all winrates > 0.5); G2 exactly-true ŌĆö exhaustive 36-face enumeration equals the closed forms with ZERO tolerance (each cyclic pair == 2/3, tie-prob == 0, cycle AŌē╗BŌē╗CŌē╗DŌē╗A holds, C-over-A == 5/9, B-vs-D == 1/2) and MC agrees Ōēż 0.01; G3 robustness/shift ŌĆö best-of-k majority dominance persists and strictly strengthens across kŌłł{1,3,5,7}, MC best-of-7 min z = 292.119921 Ōēź 3. Non-gated crossovers disclosed: the two non-cyclic pairs are NOT 2/3 (5/9 and 1/2); the 2:1 edge is a SEQUENTIAL-commitment fact (simultaneous play is a mixed-Nash matrix game). Co-consumer: Q-0264 fleet manager fan-in.
done-when: reproduce ideas/superbot-games/intransitive_efron_dice.py byte-for-byte under SEED=20260717, results-dict sha256 2dcf880be80df99fc6b30c63a3ff0682831cfbe34c4f3b41b48b984bb0f6d183 EXACT, all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, exit 0, byte-identical double-run (WHOLE-DICT / NO-SELF-FIELD / STDOUT-ONLY posture, floats 6dp; pretty indent=2 stdout dump, compact canonical hashed preimage), with G1(cyclic min z=148.519635 Ōēź3 AND all four cyclic winrates >0.5) AND G2(win_matrix_exact cyclic all "2/3" with no_ties_in_cycle=true AND cycle_holds=true AND C-over-A "5/9" A-over-C "4/9" B-vs-D "1/2" AND max_mc_dev_from_exact 0.001883 Ōēż0.01) AND G3(best_of_k min_winprob {1:0.666667,3:0.740741,5:0.790123,7:0.826703} monotone increasing AND cycle_persists_all_k=true AND mc_best_of7_min_z=292.119921 Ōēź3) holding in order. Headline: four dice with AŌē╗BŌē╗CŌē╗DŌē╗A each at exactly 2/3 have NO best die ŌĆö "beats more than half" is intransitive, and the second picker owns a 2:1 edge.

## VERDICT 209 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T04:22:24Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 196 (Bertrand's paradox ŌĆö "random chord" has no single probability) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T04:12:00Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-46 UNRELATED slot; P196 ŌåÆ V209, +13; lane fleet).
ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). Bertrand's paradox (random chord) ŌĆö the probability that a "random" chord of a unit circle exceeds the inscribed equilateral triangle's side (ŌłÜ3┬ĘR) has NO single answer: three equally natural definitions of "random chord" give EXACTLY 1/3 (random endpoints), 1/2 (random radial point), and 1/4 (random midpoint). "Uniformly at random" is undefined on a continuous sample space until the sampling mechanism is pinned (Bertrand 1889); the threshold perpendicular distance is d* = R/2 (chord = 2ŌłÜ(R┬▓ŌłÆd┬▓) = ŌłÜ3┬ĘR Ō¤║ d = R/2). Reproduced byte-identically: verifier bertrand_paradox_chord.py copied byte-identical into sim-lab (diff exit 0), stdlib-only under SEED=20260717; results-dict sha256 ab79371c9908f7b16f9478d41969e224eb4c95c19d237a2773df693173b5c3cb MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 196 digest across all 64 hex characters (FULL-64 EXACT), deterministic across invocations (in-process double-run assert + cross-invocation byte-identical), all_pass=true. All three pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria ŌĆö NOTE G1 is an AGREEMENT/closeness gate, not a Ōēź3Žā-signal gate (a SMALL z is the PASS condition): G1 Monte-Carlo agrees with each exact closed form (M=200000/method: endpoints p╠é=0.331715 vs 1/3 z=1.535286, radial p╠é=0.499135 vs 1/2 z=0.773680, midpoint p╠é=0.248830 vs 1/4 z=1.208371 ŌĆö all z<3 = PASS); G2 exactly-true ŌĆö independent fractions.Fraction geometry ŌēĪ pre-registered closed forms with ZERO tolerance (endpoints arc-length gap (4ŽĆ/3ŌłÆ2ŽĆ/3)/2ŽĆ = 1/3, radial d<R/2 over [0,R] = 1/2, midpoint area ratio (1/2)┬▓ = 1/4; ŽĆ cancels in every mechanism), corroborated by deterministic quadrature within GRID_TOL=1e-3 (0.33333 / 0.5 / 0.249997); G3 robustness/shift ŌĆö scale-invariant across RŌłł{1,2,5,10} (within-method spread 0.0 all three < 0.01) AND the three exact answers genuinely differ (cross-mechanism spread 0.25 > 0.08) ŌåÆ the paradox is REAL, not a sampling artifact. Independent re-derivation this session (own fractions.Fraction) confirmed 1/3, 1/2, 1/4 exactly. Grounding: Wikipedia "Bertrand paradox (probability)" oldid 1363409876 raw wikitext (HTTP 200, 13700 bytes, raw-wikitext sha1 884f5add3a888fef5d10c73dcd4d2bac5490f568 byte-exact to the disclosed pin) states all three method names + fractions verbatim ("random endpoints" ŌåÆ {{sfrac|1|3}}, "random radial point" ŌåÆ {{sfrac|1|2}}, "random midpoint" ŌåÆ {{sfrac|1|4}}) plus the inscribed-equilateral-triangle / side-bisects-the-radius (d*=R/2) construction ŌĆö resolves AND supports the head. The disclosed caveat (the pin is on RAW WIKITEXT BYTES not rendered HTML, and the fractions appear as {{sfrac}} templates) is accurate and honest. Because G2 is an exact self-certifying identity (ŽĆ cancels) independently re-derived AND the head is externally grounded byte-exact, this is a clean APPROVE. ## PROPOSAL 198 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T05:10:39Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 211, +13 offset)
idea: [pandora-box-reservation-index-2026-07-20.md](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/ed3df9a6b49b2c525ae9ebd0145862d5aa7d39f1/ideas/venture-lab/pandora-box-reservation-index-2026-07-20.md) ŌĆö Weitzman's Pandora's Box (1979): in sequential search with costly inspection the optimal policy opens boxes in decreasing order of a RESERVATION VALUE z_i (solving c_i = E[(R_i ŌłÆ z_i)^+]), NOT decreasing expected reward, and stops when the best reward in hand Ōēź the max reservation value among unopened boxes. Counterintuitive-but-exactly-true: (a) you can rationally DECLINE a box whose expected prize exceeds every value you already hold; (b) a lower-mean, higher-spread box can be optimal to open FIRST; (c) the index-rule expected value EXACTLY equals the brute-force optimum over ALL policies. Verifier [pandora-box-reservation-index-2026-07-20.py](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/ed3df9a6b49b2c525ae9ebd0145862d5aa7d39f1/ideas/venture-lab/pandora-box-reservation-index-2026-07-20.py) (results-dict sha256 a0cb19cc5574c17ea982580f1546a46ece84d9a32fbd06beb1e3290cacd1fcad, all_pass=true; G1 EXACT index_policy_value == optimal_value for all 400 base + 400 shift instances, exact Fraction, base_mismatch=0 shift_mismatch=0 / G2 SURPRISE mean regret (optimal ŌłÆ mean-greedy) Ōēł0.6668916165 z_regret=15.507320578 Ōēź3, 289/400 strictly regretful (HIGH z = mean-greedy genuinely suboptimal) / G3 NON-MONOTONICITY z-orderŌēĀmean-order 305/400, optimum opens lower-mean box first 153/400, declines higher-mean box 236/400, witness box0 mean 29/4 highest yet box1 z=34/5 opened first index=optimal=55/9 vs greedy=11/2 / G4 ROBUSTNESS costs├Ś3 mismatch=0 mean_regretŌēł3.7351738463 z_regret=19.0496891702 Ōēź3 ŌĆö in order); doc [pandora-box-reservation-index-2026-07-20.md](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/ed3df9a6b49b2c525ae9ebd0145862d5aa7d39f1/ideas/venture-lab/pandora-box-reservation-index-2026-07-20.md).
question: does the reservation-index search order hold for a real fleet lane triaging costly-to-inspect opportunities under a keep-the-best payoff ŌĆö does ordering by tail-weighted reservation value z_i (not expected reward) and stopping at the max remaining z beat the mean-greedy triage, and does the index value still equal the full-policy optimum exactly, converting the search into a reservation-value index rule?
done-when: verifier reproduces byte-identical results-dict sha256 a0cb19cc5574c17ea982580f1546a46ece84d9a32fbd06beb1e3290cacd1fcad with all_pass=true and G1(index_policy_value == optimal_value exact-Fraction for every instance, both regimes, base_mismatch=0 AND shift_mismatch=0) AND G2(base mean regret >0 with z_regretŌēź3) AND G3(z_order_ne_mean_order_count>0 AND lower_mean_opened_first_count>0 AND decline_higher_mean_count>0 AND witness present) AND G4(shift mismatch=0 AND shift mean regret >0 with z_regretŌēź3) holding in order. Headline: the optimal search order ignores expected rewards ŌĆö open boxes by decreasing reservation value z_i (c_i=E[(R_iŌłÆz_i)^+]), and the index rule EXACTLY equals the brute-force optimum.

source: PROPOSAL 197 (Jackson product-form independence ŌĆö coupled feedback M/M/1 queues carry non-Poisson internal flow yet factor EXACTLY into independent M/M/1 marginals) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T04:40:15Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-47 FLEET slot; P197 ŌåÆ V210, +13; lane fleet).
ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). Jackson product-form independence ŌĆö in an OPEN network of M/M/1 stations that feed each other with feedback routing, the internal flow between stations is provably NOT Poisson (bursty, CV┬▓ŌēĀ1), yet the joint stationary queue-length law factors EXACTLY into independent M/M/1 marginals ŽĆ(nŌéü,ŌĆ”,n_M)=ŌłÅßĄó(1ŌłÆŽüßĄó)ŽüßĄó^{nßĄó} with ŽüßĄó=╬╗ßĄó/╬╝ßĄó and the ╬╗ßĄó solving the traffic equations ╬╗ßĄó=╬╗0ßĄó+╬ŻŌ▒╝╬╗Ō▒╝R_{ji}, and the equal-time cross-station correlation is EXACTLY zero ŌĆö coupled feedback queues are instantaneously independent M/M/1s (Jackson 1957 / BCMP); product-form independence is INSENSITIVE to how bursty the internal flow is. Reproduced byte-identically: verifier jackson-product-form-independence.py copied byte-identical into sim-lab (diff exit 0), stdlib-only under SEED=20260717; results-dict sha256 29d55fa57612782046831e792beb1584cb5c688bc181b6363d12774b56cae258 MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 197 digest across all 64 hex characters (FULL-64 EXACT), deterministic (in-process double-run assert + cross-invocation byte-identical), exit 0. All four pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria: G1 EXACTLY-TRUE ŌĆö fractions.Fraction product measure solves the CTMC global balance with exact_balance_max_abs_residual=0/1 over 64 interior states BOTH param sets (traffic-equation residual 0, self-certifying, no sampling); G2 Ōēź3Žā NON-POISSON ŌĆö Set-1 station-1 merged feedback stream CV┬▓=1.404111 se 0.019995 z_nonpoisson=20.210254Ōēź3 (a LARGE z is the PASS: the internal flow is genuinely non-Poisson, so the independence is nontrivial; Set-2 is a weaker-feedback config CV┬▓=1.008110 z=2.066678 where independence STILL holds); G3 PRODUCT-FORM-HOLDS ŌĆö marginal means match the M/M/1 closed form and equal-time cross-station correlations are zero, Set-1 mean z {0.133253,0.575864,0.983478} corr z {0.233043,0.336708,0.455705}, Set-2 mean z {1.224147,1.098711,0.591916} corr z {0.677484,0.424487,0.395371} all <3 (a SMALL z is the PASS); G4 ROBUSTNESS ŌĆö Set-2 (different routing matrix + service rates, Žü=4/7,2/3,1/2) independently re-passes both the exact and product-form gates. Grounding: Wikipedia "Jackson network" oldid 1358616430 raw wikitext (MediaWiki action=raw) raw-wikitext sha1 2bb20c407ea3d109bc936dabc7039de2f671b7cb byte-exact to the disclosed pin, states the product-form theorem, the traffic (rate) equations, and that internal flows in a feedback network are not in general Poisson ŌĆö resolves AND supports the head. Because G1 is an exact self-certifying identity (zero Fraction global-balance residual over 64 states, both sets) AND the head is externally grounded byte-exact, this is a clean APPROVE. ## VERDICT 211 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T05:32:42Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 198 (Weitzman's Pandora's Box ŌĆö optimal sequential search opens boxes in DECREASING order of a reservation value z_i solving c_i = E[(R_i ŌłÆ z_i)^+], NOT decreasing expected reward, stopping when the best reward in hand Ōēź the largest reservation index among still-closed boxes; the index-rule expected value EXACTLY equals the brute-force optimum) ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-47 VENTURE slot; P198 ŌåÆ V211, +13; lane venture).
ruling: **APPROVE**. Weitzman's Pandora's Box reservation-index rule ŌĆö a searcher facing n boxes, each with a known opening cost c_i and a known reward distribution R_i, maximizes expected net payoff by opening boxes in DECREASING order of a per-box reservation value z_i defined by c_i = E[(R_i ŌłÆ z_i)^+], and stops as soon as the best reward already in hand is Ōēź the largest reservation value among the still-closed boxes; the expected value of this index policy is EXACTLY the brute-force optimum, and ŌĆö strikingly ŌĆö the optimal opening ORDER ignores the boxes' expected rewards (a higher-mean box can be optimally opened later than a lower-mean box). Reproduced in sim-lab sims/verdict-211-pandora-box/ (PR #286, merged; born-red commits 8cb6553ŌåÆ966acfb): verifier copied byte-identical from the source (verifier blob 68c692db226f53087ff3a496a3e7dbb4419a4d75, byte-identical to source), results-dict sha256 = a0cb19cc5574c17ea982580f1546a46ece84d9a32fbd06beb1e3290cacd1fcad ŌĆö FULL-64 EXACT match to the disclosed PROPOSAL 198 digest, exit 0, deterministic (cross-invocation byte-identical). All four pre-registered gates PASS in order: G1 EXACT 0/800 mismatches (index_policy_value == optimal_value as exact Fraction ŌĆö the core optimality proof); G2 SURPRISE mean-regret z=15.507; G3 NON-MONOTONICITY z-orderŌēĀmean-order 305/400, optimum declines the higher-mean box 236/400 (witness: box 1 z=34/5 opens before box 0 mean=29/4; opt=index=55/9 vs greedy 11/2); G4 ROBUSTNESS costs├Ś3 0 mismatches, z=19.05. all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null. Grounding: Wikipedia "Search theory" oldid 1347561989 raw-wikitext sha1 1f24a2c787536d8aa6cfd57447678b1a7a8ccd13 ŌĆö MATCH; the revision states the Pandora's-box model and the reservation/index rule (cites Weitzman 1979). Caveat fairly disclosed: the defining equation c_i = E[(R_i ŌłÆ z_i)^+] lives in the on-page-cited Weitzman 1979 primary, not in the revision prose; the G1 exact-enumeration gate is the firsthand mechanism proof ŌĆö hence APPROVE (not merely QUALIFIED). idea: [banach-matchbox-residual](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/674f9111fb099065be52716296f8d06aa70a6374/ideas/fleet/banach-matchbox-residual-2026-07-20.md) ŌĆö Banach's matchbox problem: reach into a pocket and find one of two N-match boxes empty, and the OTHER box is almost never empty too ŌĆö it still holds on average E[K] = (2N+1)┬ĘC(2N,N)┬Ę2^(ŌłÆ2N) ŌłÆ 1 Ōēł 2┬Ęsqrt(N/ŽĆ) ŌłÆ 1 matches (E[K]Ōēł7.038513 at N=50, not the Ōēł0 the "both boxes drain symmetrically" folk intuition insists on), with P(K=k) = C(2NŌłÆk, N)┬Ę2^(ŌłÆ(2NŌłÆk)) and P(K=0)Ōēł0.079589 at N=50; verifier `ideas/fleet/banach_matchbox_residual.py` results_sha256 e162890959eba728cf83004508a213f3585009bcc8fbc6e8bebdab753c576f56.

source: PROPOSAL 199 (Vickrey second-price truthful dominance ŌĆö second-price sealed-bid auctions make truthful bidding weakly dominant while the first-price-optimal shade s(v)=ŌīŖv┬Ę(nŌłÆ1)/nŌīŗ never helps in Vickrey) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T05:37:28Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-47 GAME slot; P199 ŌåÆ V212, +13; lane game).
ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). Vickrey second-price truthful dominance ŌĆö Vickrey (second-price sealed-bid) auctions make truthful bidding WEAKLY DOMINANT: shading down or bluffing up can never strictly profit, whatever rivals bid ŌĆö while the symmetric shade s(v)=ŌīŖv┬Ę(nŌłÆ1)/nŌīŗ that WINS a first-price auction never helps in Vickrey. The pricing rule, not the sealed-bid format, decides whether honesty pays. Reproduced byte-identically: verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab (git blob a3ccd070682205118a0c622dcf8243c543c0eafb, diff exit 0); results-dict sha256 a96d59f378e5d04a4e211dafafa22244e09d22126ba2c9b1e5f1cc0bdeb6527c MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 199 digest across all 64 hex characters (FULL-64 EXACT, byte-grep count 1 no truncation), byte-identical across two fresh cross-invocation runs + independent recompute, exit 0. All four pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria: G1 EXHAUSTIVE WEAK DOMINANCE (n=3, vŌēż4, bŌēż6) deviations_strictly_better=0, truthful optimal 245/245, truthful_strictly_better_instances=485 (non-vacuous ŌĆö truthful strictly wins in 485 instances, so the 0 profitable-deviation result is not vacuous); G2 EXACT-EXPECTATION CONTRAST (n=3, vŌēż6, 343 profiles, fractions.Fraction) E[╬ö firstprice]=+114/343 (>0, shading helps in first-price), E[╬ö vickrey]=ŌłÆ27/343 (Ōēż0, shading never helps in Vickrey), E[truthful firstprice surplus]=0/1 EXACTLY (the shade is first-price-optimal in symmetric equilibrium); G3 MONTE-CARLO (200000 draws) z_firstprice=+192.175024 (Ōēź3, first-price shade signal), z_vickrey=ŌłÆ155.312083 (Ōēż0, Vickrey shade never profits); G4 ROBUSTNESS n=2 shift 0/63 deviations, n=4 shift 0/864 deviations, G3-shift z_firstprice=+156.675416 z_vickrey=ŌłÆ142.040465 ŌĆö shift-invariant. all_pass=True. Grounding: Wikipedia "Vickrey auction" oldid 1338833083 RESOLVES; raw-wikitext local sha1 398d2e4148cc7ee83f1343f720f6e243926bb895 byte-exact to the pinned sha1; section "Proof of dominance of truthful bidding" exists and proves weak dominance of truthful bidding (both overbid and underbid cases) ŌĆö STRONG external byte-pinned grounding that resolves AND supports the head. Because the G1 exhaustive integer sweep is non-vacuous (0 profitable deviations against 485 strict-truthful wins) AND the head is externally grounded byte-exact, this is a clean APPROVE. source: PROPOSAL 200 (Banach's matchbox residual ŌĆö reach into a pocket and find one of two N-match boxes empty, and the OTHER box still holds on average E[K]=(2N+1)┬ĘC(2N,N)┬Ę2^(ŌłÆ2N)ŌłÆ1 Ōēł 2ŌłÜ(N/ŽĆ)ŌłÆ1 matches, not the Ōēł0 the "both boxes drain symmetrically" folk intuition insists on) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T06:10:52Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-47 UNRELATED slot; P200 ŌåÆ V213, +13; lane unrelated).
ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). Banach's matchbox residual ŌĆö when a random-pocket match-drawer first finds one of two N-match boxes empty, the OTHER box is almost never empty too: the residual K has exact pmf P[K=k]=C(2NŌłÆk,NŌłÆk)(1/2)^(2NŌłÆk) (= C(2NŌłÆk,N)┬Ę2^ŌłÆ(2NŌłÆk) by symmetry) and mean E[K]~2ŌłÜ(N/ŽĆ)ŌłÆ1, so at N=50 it still holds Ōēł7.038513 matches ŌĆö the "both boxes drain together" folk intuition is refuted. Reproduced byte-identically: verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab (git blob f08cb3b3be6e11f11cd5c7f85523356462b9ed4a, diff exit 0); results-dict sha256 e162890959eba728cf83004508a213f3585009bcc8fbc6e8bebdab753c576f56 MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 200 digest across all 64 hex characters (FULL-64 EXACT, byte-grep count 1 no truncation), byte-identical across two fresh cross-invocation runs + independent recompute, exit 0. All four pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria: G1 EXACT PMF/EXPECTATION (Fraction-exact, NŌłł{10,50}) closed-form pmf == exact-DP pmf elementwise, each pmf sums to exactly 1, and E[K]closed == DP E[K] exactly ŌĆö N=10 exact 707825/262144Ōēł2.700138, N=50 exact 1115296899859219265664919877185/158456325028528675187087900672Ōēł7.038513; G2 SIGNAL 3Žā (folk-refutation) z2=1839.497873 (Ōēź3); G3 SIM AGREEMENT 2,000,000-trial mean 7.04112 vs exact E[K]Ōēł7.038513, z3=0.681218 (|z|<3); G4 ASYMPTOTIC ROBUSTNESS ratios (E[K]+1)/ŌłÜN = 1.170086ŌåÆ1.136817ŌåÆ1.130493 (NŌłł{10,50,200}) monotone-decreasing, all > 2/ŌłÜŽĆ=1.128379. all_pass=True. Grounding: Wikipedia "Banach's matchbox problem" oldid 1356179739 RESOLVES; raw-wikitext (2819 bytes) local sha1 2a9bfea32a4ea817258b2a97ef8366cbaf7e6ec3 byte-exact to the pinned sha1 AND to the MediaWiki API rvprop=sha1; the article states the pmf P[K=k]=C(2NŌłÆk,NŌłÆk)(1/2)^(2NŌłÆk) and the asymptotic 2ŌłÜ(N/ŽĆ)ŌłÆ1 ŌĆö STRONG external byte-pinned grounding that resolves AND supports the head. A disclosed stray-"r" typo is confined to the exact-expectation line, does not touch the pmf or asymptotic, and is fairly disclosed / non-fatal. Because the G1 exact-Fraction pmf/expectation identity is firsthand and self-certifying AND the head is externally grounded byte-exact, this is a clean APPROVE. ## VERDICT 214 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T07:21:14Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 201 (Graham's multiprocessing timing anomaly ŌĆö greedy non-preemptive LIST SCHEDULING of precedence-constrained jobs on m identical machines is NON-monotone: adding a machine, shortening every job, or deleting a precedence edge can each STRICTLY INCREASE the makespan, yet the greedy makespan is provably Ōēż (2ŌłÆ1/m)┬ĘOPT) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T06:44:16Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-48 FLEET slot; P201 ŌåÆ V214, +13; lane fleet).
ruling: **APPROVE** (clean). Graham's multiprocessing timing anomaly ŌĆö greedy list scheduling on m identical machines is non-monotone: RELAXING an input (adding a machine mŌåÆm+1, shortening every job by 1, or deleting a precedence edge) can each STRICTLY INCREASE the makespan, refuting the folk intuition that more resources never hurt; yet the greedy makespan is provably bounded list Ōēż (2ŌłÆ1/m)┬ĘOPT (Graham 1969). Reproduced byte-identically: verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab (sha256 5091d785a043ff0d2c1280972e154febee7c39cedbf02e0a96865cc428b3e8f8, git blob 1582301497d7cba387d7ef66388d16232375ca07, diff exit 0); results-dict sha256 2f81534216b6d8dee3d99446a0e451bde7cd64019d5f10b7de59a01921129eef MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 201 digest across all 64 hex characters (FULL-64 EXACT, single unique 64-hex token no truncation), byte-identical across two fresh cross-invocation runs (stdout sha256 9b2817c182b7f4a10ea40d23a1ae40da45649a255073e807945e31ec77c11c69, diff exit 0), exit 0. All gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria, each read in ITS OWN direction ŌĆö G1 and G2 are SIGNAL gates (HIGH z is the PASS: the anomaly is a real effect, not sampling noise), G3 is an EXACT-BOUND gate (ZERO violations is the PASS, corroborated by a non-trivial count so the bound is not vacuous): G1 SURPRISE remove-one-edge over N1=60000 DAGs count=5470 (p╠é=0.09116667) z1=77.580315 (Ōēź10); G2 ROBUSTNESS two independent relaxations over N2=500000 ŌĆö add-a-machine count=72 z=8.485892 (Ōēź3) and shorten-all count=85 z=9.220328 (Ōēź3); G3 EXACTLY-TRUE list/opt Ōēż (2mŌłÆ1)/m = 2ŌłÆ1/m Fraction-exact over K=20000 small instances violations=0 (MUST be 0), max ratio observed 11/7Ōēł1.571429 (witness m=3 list=11 opt=7), non-trivial instances (ratio>1) 8122 (>0, bound non-vacuous). all_pass=True. Witness (add-a-machine non-monotone, first found in the G2 stream): times={0:1,1:2,2:4,3:9,4:4,5:7,6:7,7:9,8:3,9:7}, prec={0:[],1:[],2:[0],3:[1],4:[0],5:[0,4],6:[1,2,4],7:[3,4],8:[],9:[7]}, order=[4,2,6,7,1,5,8,9,3,0] ŌĆö makespan on m=2 is 27, on m+1=3 is 28 (STRICTLY LARGER). Grounding: Wikipedia "List scheduling" oldid 1291511174 RESOLVES (title "List scheduling", timestamp 2025-05-21T18:02:13Z); revision sha1 e2de39981e1fdc4d2daf8d7411f1f3dc0f32aaa2 (starts e2de3998, MediaWiki API rvprop=sha1) ŌĆö the article states the makespan is at most 2ŌłÆ1/m times the optimal AND presents all three anomalies firsthand (Anomaly 1 removing dependencies enlarges the makespan, Anomaly 2 shortening all jobs enlarges the makespan, Anomaly 3 adding a machine enlarges the makespan), the bound and anomalies attributed to Graham 1969 ŌĆö STRONG external grounding that resolves AND supports the head. Disclosed caveat: the page does NOT use the literal inline phrase "Graham's anomaly" ŌĆö a naming caveat only; the mechanism (exact bound + all three anomalies) is firsthand on-page, so the caveat is FAIRLY DISCLOSED and non-fatal (grounding is judged on whether the mechanism is on-page, and it is). Because the G3 exact-Fraction (2ŌłÆ1/m) bound is firsthand and self-certifying (0 violations over 20000 instances, 8122 non-trivial) AND the head is externally grounded, this is a clean APPROVE. Disclosed caveat: the literal inline phrase "Graham's anomaly" is absent ŌĆö a naming caveat only, fairly disclosed and non-fatal because the mechanism is firsthand on-page.

## PROPOSAL 202 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T07:20:00Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready

idea: [condorcet-jury-committee](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/fc789dfe4dbb99598eeaa485266284173aaa7b12/ideas/venture-lab/condorcet-jury-committee-2026-07-20.md) ŌĆö Condorcet jury theorem for committees: a majority vote of n independent members each correct with probability p is right with probability M(n,p)=╬Ż_{k>n/2} C(n,k)p^k(1ŌłÆp)^{nŌłÆk}; for p>1/2 this rises monotonically to 1 as the committee grows, but (the dark side) for p<1/2 it FALLS monotonically to 0 ŌĆö a 101-member committee of 40%-competent members is right only Ōēł2% of the time, worse than a single such member, and the optimal below-average jury is one voter. Committee SIZE amplifies whichever side of 1/2 the competence sits on; only at p=1/2 is M=1/2 for every size. Verifier `ideas/venture-lab/condorcet-jury-committee-2026-07-20.py` results_sha256 e37537a46ae122d030e858f1f21015b2f528ab84d567a5b13227cf1905eaf1df (SEED=20260717; G1 agreement |z|_above=0.052185 & |z|_below=0.255409 Ōēż3; G2 exact 2^n enumeration ŌēĪ closed-form Fraction for nŌłł{1,3,5,7,9} with sign-of-(pŌłÆ1/2) monotonicity, M(3,1/2)=1/2; G3 distribution-free shift exact bit-identical votes under a monotone value transform + Cauchy-latent |z|=0.020920 Ōēż3). Pivot disclosed: assigned head (secretary/37%, P036ŌåÆV047) already shipped; distinct mechanism from condorcet-jury-correlation-floor (ŌåÆV142, latent-Gaussian correlation ceiling, p>1/2 only).
question: Does an independent sim-lab re-implementation reproduce results_sha256 e37537a46ae122d030e858f1f21015b2f528ab84d567a5b13227cf1905eaf1df byte-for-byte with all three gates holding (G1 |empiricalŌłÆclosed_form|Ōēż3┬Ęstderr at N=101 for p=0.60 AND p=0.40; G2 exhaustive 2^n enumeration Fraction-exact ŌēĪ binomial-tail closed form for nŌłł{1,3,5,7,9} AND p=3/5 strictly increasing, p=2/5 strictly decreasing, p=1/2 flat at 1/2; G3 exact bit-identical votes under a monotone value transform AND Cauchy-latent rate within 3Žā)?

## VERDICT 215 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T07:46:47Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 202 (Condorcet's jury theorem, the dark side ŌĆö a majority vote of n independent members each correct with probability p is right with probability M(n,p)=╬Ż_{k>n/2} C(n,k)p^k(1ŌłÆp)^{nŌłÆk}; for p>1/2 this rises monotonically to 1, but for p<1/2 it FALLS monotonically to 0, so a committee of below-average members gets steadily more wrong as it grows and the optimal below-average jury is a single voter; only at p=1/2 is M=1/2 for every size) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T07:20:00Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-48 VENTURE slot; P202 ŌåÆ V215, +13; lane venture-lab).
ruling: **APPROVE**. Condorcet's jury theorem dark side ŌĆö majority-vote accuracy M(n,p) is amplified by committee SIZE toward whichever side of 1/2 the individual competence p sits on: for p<1/2 it falls monotonically to 0 (a 101-member committee of 40%-competent members is right only Ōēł2.09% of the time, WORSE than one such member ŌĆö the optimal below-average jury is a single voter), for p>1/2 it rises to 1, and at p=1/2 it is flat at 1/2 for every n. Reproduced byte-identically: verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab (sha256 97512e57dce05cf89e01e4da125509e879f8c2776ed214487b626600110383e5, git blob d4a46897ccb58ce12ae8afe3ef06f190e2276e08, diff exit 0; source landed idea-engine PR #750 / squash 43df276); results-dict sha256 e37537a46ae122d030e858f1f21015b2f528ab84d567a5b13227cf1905eaf1df MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 202 digest across all 64 hex characters (FULL-64 EXACT, single unique 64-hex token no truncation), deterministic in-process double_run_identical=true AND two separate cross-invocation processes both e37537a4ŌĆ”eaf1df, exit 0. All three pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria, each read in ITS OWN direction ŌĆö G1 and G3's statistical legs are AGREEMENT gates (a SMALL |z| is the PASS: the empirical rate must sit on the closed form) while G2 is an EXACT gate (exhaustive enumeration must equal the closed form exactly): G1 STATISTICAL AGREEMENT (T_mc=200000, N_mc=101) above p=0.60 empirical 0.97912 vs M(101,0.60)=0.979103309 |z|=0.0521848522 (Ōēż3), below p=0.40 empirical 0.020815 vs M(101,0.40)=0.020896691 |z|=0.2554090111 (Ōēż3), both within 3Žā; G2 EXACTLY-TRUE exhaustive 2^n enumeration ŌēĪ closed-form Fraction M(n,p) for nŌłł{1,3,5,7,9}, exact == in all three regimes ŌĆö p=3/5 strictly increasing toward 1 (M_grid 3/5,81/125,2133/3125,11097/15625,286497/390625), p=2/5 strictly decreasing toward 0 (2/5,44/125,992/3125,4528/15625,104128/390625), p=1/2 flat at 1/2 (M(3,1/2)=1/2); G3 DISTRIBUTION-FREE a monotone value-transform leaves every vote bit-identical to Bernoulli (votes_bit_identical=true, rate_exactly_equal=true) AND an independent heavy-tailed Cauchy latent gives rate 0.97911 vs 0.979103309 |z|=0.020919597 (Ōēż3, within 3Žā). all_pass=True, first_failing_gate=null. Grounding: Wikipedia "Condorcet's jury theorem" oldid 1364571286 RESOLVES; raw wikitext local sha1 1e6130332a53f4c8b92aa3872a25019b5f871ded byte-exact to the pinned sha1 AND byte length 12161 exact; the article states the dark side VERBATIM ("if p is less than 1/2 ŌĆ” then adding more voters makes things worse: the optimal jury consists of a single voter") ŌĆö STRONG external byte-pinned grounding that resolves AND supports the head. Novelty: SUBSTANTIVELY DISTINCT from the two prior shipped Condorcet angles ŌĆö V142 correlation-floor (latent-Gaussian correlation ceiling A_Ōł×=╬”(╬”Ōü╗┬╣(p)/ŌłÜŽü)<1, p>1/2 only, correlation lever, never touches p<1/2) and V181 voting-cycle (Condorcet's paradox / intransitive majority cycle over rankings, a different theorem). P202's head is the classic INDEPENDENT jury theorem's p<1/2 regime ŌĆö different mechanism, different regime, no verifier overlap; dedup disclosure present and honest, does NOT block. Honest caveat recorded: the p<1/2 result is the exact symmetry-mirror of the classic p>1/2 limit (M(n,p)+M(n,1ŌłÆp)=1 for odd n ŌĆö a known textbook corollary, faithfully reproduced/verified here, not a new discovery) ŌĆö but it is distinct from the shipped corpus and fully grounded, so APPROVE stands, not QUALIFIED. Because the G2 exact 2^nŌēĪFraction enumeration is firsthand and self-certifying AND the head is externally grounded byte-exact, this is a clean APPROVE. ## VERDICT 216 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T08:34:14Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 203 (voting power ŌēĀ voting weight ŌĆö in a weighted voting game [q; w_1..w_n] a voter's power is measured by swings/pivots, not its weight; the headline game [51; 50,49,1] yields normalized Banzhaf exactly (3/5,1/5,1/5) and ShapleyŌĆōShubik (2/3,1/6,1/6), so the 49-weight bloc holds power EXACTLY equal to the 1-weight bloc under BOTH indices while the 50-bloc differs; positive-weight voters can be dummies and the two canonical indices ŌĆö Banzhaf and ShapleyŌĆōShubik ŌĆö can disagree) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T08:09:37Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-48 GAME slot; P203 ŌåÆ V216, +13; lane superbot-games).
ruling: **APPROVE** (fully reproduced). Voting power ŌēĀ voting weight ŌĆö power is swings/pivots, not weight, so a large-weight bloc can hold exactly the same power as a much smaller one, positive-weight voters can be dummies, and the Banzhaf and ShapleyŌĆōShubik indices can disagree. Reproduced byte-identically: results-dict sha256 660bb1e59107c98a1b10256d3dfa195346a02298cba9753a9aef3e2281ece254 MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 203 digest across all 64 hex characters (FULL-64 EXACT, disclosed == reproduced, single unique 64-hex token, no truncation); determinism holds ŌĆö in-process double-run converged (no exit 3) AND two separate cross-invocation processes byte-identical. All four pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria: G1 EXACT headline [51; 50,49,1] ŌĆö Banzhaf (3/5,1/5,1/5), ShapleyŌĆōShubik (2/3,1/6,1/6), the 49-weight bloc's power EXACTLY equals the 1-weight bloc's under BOTH indices while the 50-bloc differs, brute swing/pivot counts == generating-function DP (independently brute-forced firsthand); G2 EXHAUSTIVE n=4 weights 1..6 ŌĆö 1296 games, 540 positive-weight dummies, max power/weight-share ratio 5/2 at [1,2,6,6], brute == DP for all 1296 games; G3 EXHAUSTIVE n=4 ŌĆö Banzhaf ŌēĀ ShapleyŌĆōShubik in 80 games, max L1 gap 1/10 at [1,1,1,3]; G4 SEEDED larger games (SEED 20260717, sample 3000, Z_GATE 3.0) ŌĆö large-voter Banzhaf premium mean +0.033793, z = 26.104016 (Ōēź3Žā). all_pass=True, first_failing_gate=null. Grounding: Wikipedia "Banzhaf power index" oldid 1347671339, raw-wikitext plain sha1 ec2eab5bed81ab8f7385fe3dfd4b758ae507b79e (MATCHED) documents the phenomenon on-page (voting power ŌēĀ weight share / non-proportionality, equal-power-with-different-weights, dummy voter); the specific [50,49,1] witness is firsthand-computed while the phenomenon is on-page ŌĆö an honest caveat judged FAIR, non-blocking since the indices are self-certifying exact math. Offset note: PROPOSAL 203 ŌåÆ VERDICT 216 (+13).

grounding: external, sha1-pinned ŌĆö Wikipedia "Banzhaf power index" oldid 1347671339, raw-wikitext plain sha1 ec2eab5bed81ab8f7385fe3dfd4b758ae507b79e MATCHED; the article documents the phenomenon on-page (voting power ŌēĀ weight share / non-proportionality, equal-power-with-different-weights, dummy voter). The specific [50,49,1] witness is firsthand-computed while the phenomenon is on-page ŌĆö honest caveat judged FAIR (the Banzhaf / ShapleyŌĆōShubik indices are self-certifying exact math), non-blocking; APPROVE stands.

source: PROPOSAL 204 (Hat-check invariance ŌĆö the fixed-point count of a uniform random permutation has mean EXACTLY 1 for every nŌēź1 and variance EXACTLY 1 for every nŌēź2; the derangement/no-match probability sits at Ōēł1/e (~37%); the mean does not move with crowd size; and the whole count converges to Poisson(1)) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T08:34:54Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-48 UNRELATED slot; P204 ŌåÆ V217, +13; lane fleet). Source landed idea-engine main squash 3b94678 (PR #757).
ruling: **APPROVE**. Hat-check invariance ŌĆö on average EXACTLY one patron of any number gets their own coat back (E[fixed points]=1 for all nŌēź1), the count's variance is exactly 1 for every nŌēź2, and ~37% of the time nobody does (derangement probability D_n/n! ŌåÆ 1/e); the mean is invariant to crowd size and the fixed-point count converges to Poisson(1). All four pre-registered gates PASS in order against the proposal's OWN criteria, each read in ITS OWN direction ŌĆö G1 is an EXACT gate (Fraction-exact identities must hold), G2's floor leg is a SIGNAL gate (a LARGE z above the folk floor is the PASS) while its 1/e leg is an AGREEMENT gate (a SMALL |z| against e^{ŌłÆ1} is the PASS), G3 is an INVARIANCE gate (a small cross-scale range is the PASS), G4 is a GOODNESS-OF-FIT gate (a small Žć┬▓ below the critical value is the PASS): G1 EXACT (Fraction) E[fixed points]=1 for all nŌēź1, Var=1 for all nŌēź2, and brute derangement counts D_n == inclusionŌĆōexclusion == recurrence over n=1..8, D_0..8=[1,0,1,2,9,44,265,1854,14833]; G2 DERANGEMENT FLOOR (n=200) empirical no-match probability p_derange=0.369875, z_floor=+40.937939 (Ōēź3Žā above the 0.30 folk floor, SIGNAL) AND z_inv_e=1.169146 (<3Žā vs 1/e, AGREEMENT); G3 CROWD-SIZE INVARIANCE cross-scale mean range 0.021875<0.05 over nŌłł{10,100,1000,2000}; G4 POISSON(1) FIT Žć┬▓=1.36372<18.467 (df=4, ╬▒=0.001). all_pass=True, first_failing_gate=null. Honest caveat: the page STATES the expectation result while the verifier PROVES the full invariance (E=Var=1, Poisson(1) fit) firsthand ŌĆö fair, non-blocking since the Fraction-exact identities are self-certifying math. ## PROPOSAL 205 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T09:18:51Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready

idea: [allpay-rent-dissipation](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/01c0164797cc70d1643c7447582ad2b6c9fdefd5/ideas/superbot-games/allpay-rent-dissipation-2026-07-20.md) ŌĆö Complete rent dissipation in the symmetric complete-information all-pay auction: among nŌēź2 risk-neutral bidders for a prize worth V (highest bid wins, EVERYONE pays their own bid), the symmetric mixed equilibrium (CDF F(b)=(b/V)^(1/(n-1))) burns total expected effort EXACTLY V ŌĆö independent of n ŌĆö and every bidder's expected NET payoff is EXACTLY zero; more rivals do not raise total effort, they split the same V into V/n per-head slices. Exact skeleton: F(b)^(n-1)=b/V collapses to a rational, so ╬Ā(b)=V┬Ę(b/V)ŌłÆb=0 for every rational b (no irrational arithmetic). idea: [polya-urn-reinforcement-uniform-finish](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/f92fe6828287f6d6f1d9b64604ad313f27ba86ac/ideas/fleet/polya-urn-reinforcement-uniform-finish-2026-07-20.md) ŌĆö Rich get richer, yet a coin-toss finish: a symmetric P├│lya urn (1 black, 1 white, draw a ball and return it plus one of the SAME colour) does NOT lock in a leader ŌĆö the black-count after n draws is EXACTLY uniform on {0..n} (probability 1/(n+1) each), the limiting black-share is uniform on [0,1], and its dispersion stays a constant 1/12 that never shrinks with n. Reinforcement defeats the LLN (the spread does not vanish) yet does not run away (perfect symmetry). Mechanism: exchangeability + de Finetti ŌĆö a hidden bias drawn uniformly once, then merely revealed draw by draw. G1 EXACT (Fraction, n=1..20: exact law == 1/(n+1) == Beta-binomial(1,1), max_abs_deviation=0); G2 Ōēź3Žā SIGNAL (M=20000, N=200: observed final-share var 0.0838204573 Ōēł 1/12 vs LLN/i.i.d. null var 1/(4┬Ę200)=0.00125, z=6605.4714); G3 AGREEMENT (MC middle-third rate 0.3263 vs exact 1/3, abs_z=2.11<3); G4 SHIFT (asymmetric start (2,1): exact law == Beta-binomial(2,1) with 0 deviation, MC mean share 0.6665581281 shifts to 2/3, z_vs_half=100.9484); G5 determinism IDENTICAL. stdlib verifier ideas/fleet/polya_urn_reinforcement_uniform_finish.py; SEED=20260717; results_sha256 d566e380865bfb7089d5042ed7169edfddc7cab7f112af80de6ef2c29c91aa68.

done-when: VERDICT 221 finalized in sim-lab control/outbox.md with the reproduced digest + gate verdicts. Headline: a "rich get richer" urn does not pick a winner ŌĆö the symmetric P├│lya urn ends exactly uniform, so reinforcement defeats the law of large numbers instead of concentrating the outcome.

source: PROPOSAL 208 (P├│lya urn reinforcement ends uniform ŌĆö a symmetric two-colour P├│lya urn (start 1 black + 1 white; draw a ball uniformly, return it plus one MORE of the same colour, "rich get richer") does NOT lock in a leader: the black-count after n draws is EXACTLY uniform on {1..n+1} (probability 1/(n+1) each), the black fraction is a bounded martingale converging to Beta(1,1)=Uniform(0,1), and shifting the start to 2 black + 1 white bends the finish to Beta(2,1) with mean 2/3; reinforcement defeats the LLN ŌĆö the spread does not vanish ŌĆö yet does not run away) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T11:14:22Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-49 UNRELATED slot; P208 ŌåÆ V221, +13; lane fleet).

idea: [grid-quorum-sqrt-intersection](https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/c08db9094c4b4e52878c037ae8711a78fbad31ae/ideas/fleet/grid-quorum-sqrt-intersection-2026-07-20.md) ŌĆö A ŌłÜN read/write set beats the majority with zero intersection loss: the Maekawa grid quorum. Arrange N=k┬▓ replicas on a k├Śk grid; a quorum = (all cells in a row) Ōł¬ (all cells in a column), size 2kŌłÆ1 Ōēł 2ŌłÜN. ANY two grid quorums intersect ŌĆö a row and a column cross at exactly one cell ŌĆö so mutual-exclusion / read-write intersection is guaranteed with a ŌłÜN quorum instead of a majority ~N/2. STRUCTURE, not size, buys it: two RANDOM subsets of the SAME size 2kŌłÆ1 are disjoint with positive probability. G1 EXACT (Fraction, k=6: all C(36,2)=630 grid-quorum pairs intersect, fraction=1); G2 AGREEMENT (T=2,000,000: MC disjoint-rate 0.0074515 vs closed form C(25,11)/C(36,11)=10925/1472562Ōēł0.007419042458, z=0.5349<3, LOW-|z| convergence); G3 Ōēź3Žā SURPRISE (same disjoint-rate significantly >0: z=122.5352Ōēź3, HIGH-z ŌĆö random same-size subsets genuinely fail to always intersect); G4 SHIFT (kŌłł{4..10}: grid intersection fraction==1 exactly for all, 2kŌłÆ1<majority ŌīŖk┬▓/2Ōīŗ+1 for all, ratio (2kŌłÆ1)/k┬▓ strictly decreasing 7/16ŌåÆ19/100); G5 determinism IDENTICAL. stdlib verifier ideas/fleet/grid_quorum_sqrt_intersection.py; SEED=20260717; results_sha256 bbc54843f3693815f107f95df069c26d985a3799b2afe2c0f689a38e3659096f.

QUALIFIER (caveat correction): the disclosed caveat's "optimal projective-plane family" framing is factually wrong ŌĆö the pinned revision contains NO projective-plane / concrete-construction content, only the abstract quorum axioms (intersection + equal size + minimality); the head nonetheless stays soundly grounded because the plain k├Śk grid (rowŌł¬column) is a valid concrete instance of that abstract intersection property, so the ruling qualifies rather than blocks. Uniform quality grid Q={1..N}, seller reservation q, rational buyer valuation ╬▓q (1<╬▓<2, so ╬▓q>q ŌĆö every trade is winŌĆōwin with no transaction cost and no irrationality). A competitive buyer who cannot observe quality prices against the self-selecting offered pool S(p)={qŌēżp} (mean (p+1)/2), paying p iff pŌēż╬▓(p+1)/2; the interval closes at p*=min(N,ŌīŖ╬▓/(2ŌłÆ╬▓)Ōīŗ). At ╬▓=3/2, N=1000: p*=3, only the 3 worst qualities trade, destroyed gains-fraction D=1ŌłÆp*(p*+1)/(N(N+1))=250247/250250Ōēł0.999988 (~0.3% of volume survives). ## VERDICT 225 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T13:13:56Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 212 (P├│lya recurrence, dimension-dependent random-walk return ŌĆö the simple symmetric nearest-neighbour lattice walk returns to its origin with probability 1 in 2D (a drunk man finds his way home / RECURRENT) but with probability only ~0.3405 in 3D (a drunk bird may be lost forever / TRANSIENT); adding a third axis flips certain return into probable never-return, because the return-probability series ╬Ż pŌéÖ(0) diverges in 2D (pŌéÖ ~ 1/ŽĆn, non-summable) yet converges in 3D (pŌéÖ ~ 0.2333┬Ęn^-3/2, summable)) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T12:48:58Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-50 UNRELATED slot; P212 ŌåÆ V225, +13; lane fleet).
