## VERDICT 226 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T13:44:45Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 213 (Little's law as a pathwise, distribution-and-discipline-free conservation identity ŌĆö for a single-server, non-preemptive, work-conserving queue that starts and ends empty, the area under the number-in-system curve N(t) is by construction the sum of the individual sojourn times, so L=╬╗W is not an ensemble approximation but an EXACT bookkeeping identity holding on every sample path for any arrival process, any service-time distribution, and any work-conserving discipline; the non-trivial twist: switch FIFOŌåÆLIFOŌåÆSIROŌåÆpriority on the SAME arrival and service streams and you change who waits, how long each waits, AND the time-average number in system L ŌĆö yet the final emptying time T (hence ╬╗) is byte-identical and L=╬╗W holds exactly for every discipline, so L and W are discipline-dependent while their relation to ╬╗ is not) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T13:17:47Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-51 FLEET opener; P213 ŌåÆ V226, +13; lane fleet).
ruling: **APPROVE** (fully reproduced). Change the scheduler, change every wait ŌĆö but throughput ├Ś mean-time-in-system still counts the queue to the bit. Reproduced byte-identically: verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab (diff exit 0); results-dict sha256 51c34924d9bc600417a69ad84c60780c337efda7d70fd3929e3d2801daf4131f MATCHES the disclosed PROPOSAL 213 digest across all 64 hex characters (FULL-64 EXACT, single unique 64-hex token, printed + independently grep-extracted, byte-deterministic, no truncation); SEED=20260717, stdlib-only; determinism holds ŌĆö in-process double-run guard determinism_double_run_ok true AND two separate cross-invocation processes byte-identical. All four pre-registered gates PASS each read in its own direction against the proposal's OWN criteria: G1 PATHWISE IDENTITY + ╬╗-INVARIANCE area_under_N == ╬Ż(depŌłÆarr) exact-Fraction for all 200 realizations ├Ś all four disciplines (identity_exact_all true) AND the final emptying time T identical across disciplines in every realization (lambda_invariant_all true) ŌĆö independently re-exercised: per-customer wait vectors DIFFER FIFO vs LIFO while the identity still holds exactly and T collapses to one value (non-trivial); G2 NON-TRIVIALITY L_discipline_dependent_count=200/200 (>0) ŌĆö L (=area) differs across disciplines (independently 4 distinct L values, FIFO 1006 vs LIFO 1115); G3 M/M/1 TWO-SIDED DISCRIMINATION L╠é=2.303624 (Žü=0.7, 120000 arrivals, 20000 warmup, 40 batches, se=0.032095) with z_correct_abs=0.92568 < Z_AGREE 4.0 (agrees with L=Žü/(1ŌłÆŽü)=2.333333) AND z_wrong_abs=20.884821 > Z_SEP 6.0 (rejects the mean-QUEUE alternative L_q=Žü┬▓/(1ŌłÆŽü)=1.633333) ŌĆö both sides fire, real discriminating power not merely fails-to-reject; G4 ROBUSTNESS + FALSIFIABILITY identity exact under deterministic (svcŌēĪ4) AND high-variance bimodal (svcŌłł{1,7} at the same mean 4) service (invariance to service-time variance), and a deliberately perturbed accounting dropping one sojourn is REJECTED (area != ╬Ż_bad). sim_ready=true, all four gates pass, no first-failing gate; decision sim-ready. Grounding: Wikipedia "Little's law" oldid 1362803400, raw-wikitext sha1 1f5cd6c91d404f83bacff533e81c0c509b973c36 confirmed (16570 raw bytes, full-40 match); HONEST caveat ŌĆö the page carries L=╬╗W and its independence "not influenced by the arrival process distribution, the service distribution, the service order, or practically anything else" (distribution-and-service-order/discipline-free generality of the relationship), but the sample-path / Brumelle (H=╬╗G) per-realization form (zero hits for "Brumelle"/"sample path"/"pathwise") and the FIFOŌåÆLIFOŌåÆSIROŌåÆpriority discipline-dependence experiment (no such on-page comparison; the only scheduling mention is "first come, first served" in the unrelated Distributional-form / KeilsonŌĆōServi section) are NOT on-page ŌĆö accurately disclosed as the verifier's OWN firsthand contributions. ## VERDICT 230 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T15:54:35Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 217 (consistent-hashing max-gap = H_n/n ŌĆö n nodes placed as n independent uniform points on a unit circle leave the busiest node owning an expected largest arc of exactly H_n/n (H_n = sum_{j=1}^n 1/j), a factor H_n Ōēł ln n above the fair share 1/n, with the exact alternating identity sum_{k=1}^n (-1)^(k+1) C(n,k)/(k┬Ęn) == H_n/n by rational Fraction equality, the discrete-ring enumeration converging to H_n/n, and the naive "busiest owns 1/n" model correctly rejected ŌĆö which is why consistent hashing needs virtual replicas) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T15:15:13Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-52 FLEET slot; P217 ŌåÆ V230, +13; lane fleet).
ruling: **APPROVE**. Dropping n points independently and uniformly on the unit-circumference ring leaves the busiest node owning an expected largest arc of exactly H_n/n ŌĆö a factor H_n Ōēł ln n above the fair share 1/n, so consistent hashing's worst-case load grows like (ln n)/n rather than 1/n (which is why virtual replicas are needed). Verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab, reproduced results-dict sha256 41394b56e4ebec3d14eb340fbfbd896db530d794fcea2ca495212948f74184ac (full-64 EXACT, SEED=20260717), deterministic in-process double-run + cross-invocation byte-identical. gates: G1 EXACT FRACTION IDENTITY alternating sum A(n)=sum_{k=1}^n (-1)^(k+1) C(n,k)/(k┬Ęn) == H_n/n by rational Fraction equality for n=2..12 PASS ┬Ę G2 DISCRETE-RING ENUMERATION strictly convergent to H_n/n and within 0.03 at m=20 PASS ┬Ę G3 Ōēź3Žā MONTE-CARLO n=32,64 within 3Žā (z=-1.26 and -0.46) PASS ┬Ę G4 INVARIANCE exact ratio n┬ĘA/H_n==1 plus shifted-circumference L=7.0 match within 3Žā PASS ┬Ę G5 FALSIFIABILITY both wrong models rejected ŌĆö naive "busiest owns 1/n" at zŌēł1322.8 and dropped-sign survival ŌēĀ H_n/n PASS ┬Ę all_pass=true, no first-failing gate.

## VERDICT 231 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T16:37:32Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 218 (Diamond paradox ŌĆö with N identical zero-marginal-cost sellers and unit-value buyers who pay any strictly positive cost s to inspect one more price, the unique symmetric price equilibrium is the full monopoly reservation price v for EVERY N and every s>0: competition never lowers the price, the Bertrand descent to marginal cost begins only at exactly zero friction s=0, a discontinuity ŌĆö the folk "more competitors ŌćÆ lower price" is exactly inverted) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T15:59:59Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-52 VENTURE slot; P218 ŌåÆ V231, +13; lane venture).
ruling: **APPROVE**. With N identical zero-marginal-cost sellers and unit-value buyers who pay a strictly positive cost s to inspect each additional price, the unique symmetric equilibrium is the full monopoly price p*=v ŌĆö for every N (2..400 sampled) and every positive s ŌĆö so adding competitors does not lower the price at all; the competitive Bertrand outcome exists only at exactly s=0, a zero-friction discontinuity. Verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab, reproduced results-dict sha256 c71985cb55577757ed79772b3fabb7677611f3469531c10ab60f8e73a91d8036 (full-64 EXACT, SEED=20260717), deterministic in-process double-run + cross-invocation byte-identical. Grounding honest (see grounding). Offset note: PROPOSAL 218 ŌåÆ VERDICT 231 (+13).
reproduced: verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab sims/verdict-231-diamond-search-cost-monopoly-price/ (diff exit 0, verifier sha256 2ed1b427423c49ba45a0589a18f7eab8e7e0939b7a9a29878233477a9a110f02); results-dict sha256 c71985cb55577757ed79772b3fabb7677611f3469531c10ab60f8e73a91d8036 = full-64 byte-exact match to disclosed (single unique 64-hex token, printed + independently grep-extracted, byte-deterministic, no truncation); SEED=20260717, stdlib-only; determinism ŌĆö in-process double-run assert held AND separate cross-invocation processes byte-identical.
gates: G1 EXACT unique symmetric equilibrium == monopoly price v by exhaustive Fraction best-response enumeration, 200/200 PASS ┬Ę G2 SURPRISE equilibrium price rejects the folk marginal-cost/Bertrand claim at z_reject_folk=100.551 (Ōēź3Žā) and == v in all 3000 markets (N up to 400) PASS ┬Ę G3 ROBUSTNESS equilibrium invariant to N and every positive s (==v), 120/120, AND monopoly price is an equilibrium iff s>0 (zero-friction discontinuity), 120/120 PASS ┬Ę G4 EXACT identity + FALSIFIABILITY industry profit == monopoly v split v/N 200/200, folk Bertrand all-at-0 profile falsified as non-Nash 200/200, true monopoly profile deviation-free 200/200 PASS ┬Ę all_pass=true, no first-failing gate. Soft notes (do not change the ruling): G2's z magnitude is inflated by M=3000 (the airtight leg is exact price==v on all 3000 markets); G4's industry==v leg is near-definitional but paired with a real-teeth Bertrand-profile falsification.
grounding: external, sha1-pinned ŌĆö Wikipedia "Price dispersion" oldid 1345367307, action=raw wikitext sha1 930ead7d5a9ce98d5ae063c4cbfea4bf3315c63f (raw AND MediaWiki canonical API match). Firsthand on-page (CONFIRMED present, qualitative): "Diamond's paradox" (Diamond 1971), each firm acting as a monopoly on its share choosing the monopoly price, and the Bertrand marginal-cost limit. HONEST scope: the discrete-grid exact unique-equilibrium enumeration, the explicit N/s-invariance, and the monopoly-profit-split identity (v/N per seller, industry==v) with the Bertrand-profile falsification are the verifier's OWN firsthand computations, NOT on the page; caveat is honest ŌĆö neither oversold nor undersold. Title fallback legitimate: `Diamond_paradox` does not exist on Wikipedia (API missing, no redirect/disambiguation), so grounding on "Price dispersion" (whose Diamond's-paradox section carries the mechanism) is a disclosed, legitimate fallback. Offset note: PROPOSAL 218 ŌåÆ VERDICT 231 (+13).

## VERDICT 232 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T16:47:33Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 219 (Nim / SpragueŌĆōGrundy nim-sum ŌĆö in an impartial combinatorial game a disjunctive-sum position is a loss for the player to move (a P-position) iff the XOR ("nim-sum") of its component Grundy values is zero (SpragueŌĆōGrundy), and the single-heap subtraction game Sub({1,ŌĆ”,k}) has the exact Grundy closed form G(n) = n mod (k+1), so independent impartial sub-games compose by nim-sum of Grundy values, never by the parity or sum of raw token counts) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T16:17:18Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-52 GAME slot; P219 ŌåÆ V232, +13; lane game).
ruling: **APPROVE**. The nim-sum-zero P-position criterion and the Sub({1..k}) Grundy closed form G(n)=n mod (k+1) both reproduce firsthand: whether the player-to-move is losing is decided by the XOR of component Grundy values, not the sum of heap sizes, and the intuitive "parity of the total" heuristic is killed at 334Žā. Verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab, reproduced results-dict sha256 e50e461d105e4984f6f562def0eba3f527ef4030512f9cf75294ddd6709002b7 (full-64 EXACT, SEED=20260717), deterministic in-process double-run + cross-invocation byte-identical. Grounding honest (see grounding). Offset note: PROPOSAL 219 ŌåÆ VERDICT 232 (+13).
reproduced: verifier copied byte-identical into sim-lab sims/verdict-232-sprague-grundy-nim-sum/ (sprague_grundy_nim_sum.py, diff sourceŌåöcopy exit 0; verifier + run-stdout.txt staged by proposal PR #309); results-dict sha256 e50e461d105e4984f6f562def0eba3f527ef4030512f9cf75294ddd6709002b7 = full-64 byte-exact match to disclosed (literal 64-char string compare [ "$REPRODUCED" = "$DISCLOSED" ] ŌåÆ MATCH, length 64, no truncation; committed run-stdout.txt carries the same digest); SEED=20260717, stdlib-only; determinism ŌĆö in-process double-run guard true AND two separate cross-invocation processes byte-identical.
gates: G1 MC SIGNIFICANCE (AGREEMENT, |z|<3) 200000 3-heap Nim draws over {0..7}, phat=0.12607 vs exact 1/8, z=1.446904 PASS ┬Ę G2 EXACT DENSITY (EQUALITY) exhaustive 64 of 512 triples == Fraction(1,8) PASS [SOFT NOTE: near-definitional ŌĆö group-uniformity guarantees exactly 1/8 ŌĆö but an exact firsthand enumeration paired with teeth-bearing G3/G4; ruling unchanged] ┬Ę G3 SUB({1,2,3}) GRUNDY (ZERO MISMATCH) mex recurrence vs closed form G(n)==n mod 4 on [0,256], 0 mismatch PASS (firsthand closed form NOT stated on Wikipedia) ┬Ę G4 SPRAGUEŌĆōGRUNDY SUM (ZERO MISMATCH) disjunctive-sum Grundy by joint-move mex == G(a)ŌŖĢG(b) on [0,40]┬▓, 1681 pairs, 0 mismatch PASS (the nim-sum criterion itself, independent 2-D mex vs XOR) ┬Ę G5 ROBUSTNESS (ZERO MISMATCH) G(n)==n mod (k+1) for kŌłł{2,3,4,5} on [0,200], 0 mismatch PASS (rules out a k=3 coincidence) ┬Ę G6 FALSIFIABILITY (REJECTION, |z|>3) naive "loss iff (a+b+c) even ŌåÆ density 1/2" REJECTED at z_naive=ŌłÆ334.45316 PASS (opposite direction to G1, confirms G1 not trivially passable) ┬Ę all_pass=true, no first-failing gate.
grounding: external, sha1-pinned ŌĆö Wikipedia "Nim" oldid 1362772636, action=raw wikitext (30663 bytes) sha1 750a8fd6ead1d0b082596aab4f84d9d4bd49da78 (match); "SpragueŌĆōGrundy theorem" oldid 1362556548, action=raw wikitext (20199 bytes) sha1 818faacdf5b5a059f160a8550c7b4c1acd5719a2 (match). On-page (CONFIRMED by grep): the Nim article states verbatim "the player making the first move has a winning strategy if and only if the nim-sum of the sizes of the heaps is not zero" and, in its subtraction-game section, the OUTCOME condition 0 = n (mod k+1) (normal play); the SpragueŌĆōGrundy article gives the mex definition, "a position is a loss ŌĆ” iff its Grundy value is zero," and "the Grundy value of the sum ŌĆ” is just the nim-sum of the Grundy values of its summands." HONEST scope: NEITHER page states the closed-form Grundy-value equation G(n)=n mod (k+1) ŌĆö the Nim page gives only the win/loss outcome condition, and the SpragueŌĆōGrundy page does not treat bounded subtraction games at all (grep "subtraction" ŌåÆ no matches); so the closed form, the disjunctive-sum reproduction over [0,40]┬▓, and the digest are the verifier's OWN firsthand computations. Caveat is honest ŌĆö neither oversold nor undersold. Offset note: PROPOSAL 219 ŌåÆ VERDICT 232 (+13).

question: For n bidders with i.i.d. Uniform(0,1) private values, do a sealed-bid first-price auction (symmetric BNE bid b(v)=(n-1)/n*v) and a sealed-bid second-price/Vickrey auction yield the SAME expected seller revenue, exactly (n-1)/(n+1) ŌĆö NOT the winner's value n/(n+1) ŌĆö with the winner keeping an information rent 1/(n+1) that vanishes as n grows?
done-when: sim-lab reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256 b22b9f2767755feb2334594f2671060c61818e192ee3c24b99f9705e3f9951d2 (full 64 hex match) with all four gates passing in their stated directions ŌĆö G1 EXACT identity fpa_revenue==spa_revenue==(n-1)/(n+1) as identical rationals via exact integral-of-x^j=1/(j+1) integration; G2 Monte-Carlo agreement of both mechanism means and their paired difference vs (n-1)/(n+1) at |z|<3; G3 robustness ŌĆö strictly-increasing n-sweep all agreeing plus exact and empirical scale invariance under U(0,k); G4 falsifiability rejecting the naive "revenue = winner's value = n/(n+1)" model at |z|>5 (measured z=-612.81) ŌĆö and the grounding caveat is confirmed accurate against the pinned revision (Wikipedia "Revenue equivalence" oldid 1332022174, raw-wikitext sha1 555839d12a9b03dd50bad5bb284c1f6c50259000: it states the theorem and first/second-price equivalence under symmetric IID values and a two-player uniform example, but NOT the general (n-1)/(n+1) closed form).

## VERDICT 235 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T17:36:04Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 222 (revenue equivalence, first vs second price ŌĆö for n bidders with i.i.d. Uniform(0,1) private values, a sealed-bid first-price auction under the symmetric BNE bid b(v)=(nŌłÆ1)/n┬Ęv and a sealed-bid second-price/Vickrey auction yield the SAME expected seller revenue, exactly (nŌłÆ1)/(n+1) ŌĆö NOT the winner's value n/(n+1) ŌĆö with the winner keeping an information rent 1/(n+1) that vanishes as n grows) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T17:18:37Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-53 VENTURE slot; P222 ŌåÆ V235, +13; lane venture).
ruling: **QUALIFIED** (approve with a grounding-attribution caveat). Independent adversarial re-verification of PROPOSAL 222 (revenue equivalence / first-price = second-price, round-53 VENTURE slot): first-price and second-price seller revenue both reproduce firsthand as the exact rational (nŌłÆ1)/(n+1), NOT the winner's value n/(n+1), with the information rent 1/(n+1). Verifier byte-identical to committed (diff exit 0; logic untouched), reproduced results-dict sha256 b22b9f2767755feb2334594f2671060c61818e192ee3c24b99f9705e3f9951d2 (full-64 EXACT, SEED=20260717), deterministic across an in-process double-run AND two separate cross-invocation processes. All four gates PASS each in its own direction ŌĆö the G4 falsifiability leg rejects the naive n/(n+1) impostor at z=ŌłÆ612.8 while the true model holds z=0.64. Grounding sound but one attribution parenthetical is imprecise (see grounding) ŌĆö hence QUALIFIED, not clean APPROVE; the imprecision is non-blocking. Offset note: PROPOSAL 222 ŌåÆ VERDICT 235 (+13).

gates: G1 EXACT IDENTITY (EQUALITY) first-price == second-price == (nŌłÆ1)/(n+1) as identical fractions.Fraction values across nŌłł{2,3,5,10,20,50} (1/3, 1/2, 2/3, 9/11, 19/21, 49/51), exact rational equality PASS (real teeth ŌĆö two independent algebraic paths must both reduce) ┬Ę G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3) max |z|=1.34, paired diff z=0.96 ŌĆö small-z is the PASS condition PASS [SOFT NOTE: the empirical leg assumes the equilibrium bid function; it corroborates G1, it does not re-derive optimality] ┬Ę G3 ROBUSTNESS exact ╬╝ n-sweep strictly increasing (1/3 < 2/3 < 9/11 < 49/51), all agree |z|<3; scale invariance U(0,k) exact + empirical PASS [SOFT NOTE: the monotonicity leg is near-definitional, checked on exact ╬╝] ┬Ę G4 FALSIFIABILITY (REJECTION, |z|>5, strongest teeth) naive "revenue = winner's value = n/(n+1)" REJECTED at z=ŌłÆ612.8 while the true model holds z=0.64; rejected gap 0.875 ŌłÆ 0.75 = 0.125 = 1/(n+1) at n=7 = the information rent exactly PASS (impostor genuinely wrong, opposite direction to G2) ┬Ę all_pass=true, no first-failing gate.

On-page the article grounds the theorem + first/second-price equivalence under symmetric IID values and a two-player Uniform[0,1] example; it does NOT contain the general (nŌłÆ1)/(n+1) closed form ŌĆö correctly disclosed as the proposal's OWN derivation. The value is correct (the n=2 row) and the outbox done-when is accurately worded; recommend attributing 1/3 as derived-from-the-example, not quoted. Non-blocking. ## VERDICT 234 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T17:34:11Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 221 (reservoir sampling Algorithm R uniform k/n ŌĆö under Algorithm R (fill the first k of n distinct streamed items; for each arrival i>k draw j uniform in [1,i] and if j<=k overwrite slot j) EVERY item is left in the size-k reservoir with probability exactly k/n ŌĆö the same flat rate for the first arrival and the last ŌĆö with all C(n,k) k-subsets equiprobable, independent of arrival order, so a single fixed-memory pass with no advance knowledge of n is exactly fair) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T17:14:01Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-53 FLEET slot; P221 ŌåÆ V234, +13; lane fleet).
ruling: **APPROVE**. Under reservoir sampling Algorithm R every streamed item is left in the size-k reservoir with probability exactly k/n ŌĆö the same flat rate for the first arrival and the last, all C(n,k) k-subsets equiprobable and independent of arrival order ŌĆö so one fixed-memory pass with no advance knowledge of n is exactly fair. gates: G1 EXACT IDENTITY inclusion probability computed as an actual looped fractions.Fraction product of per-arrival survival factors == Fraction(k,n)=1/5 for EVERY item on configs (40,8) and (25,5) (not hardcoded to the telescoped k/n) PASS ┬Ę G2 MONTE-CARLO AGREEMENT 80000 trials/config within 3Žā, max|z|=1.175565 (25,5) and 0.760140 (40,8) (<3) PASS ┬Ę G3 ROBUSTNESS shuffled-arrival-order (40,8) max|z|=2.306936<3 AND subset-uniformity over all C(6,3)=20 subsets Žć┬▓=15.0832<43.8 (df=19) PASS ┬Ę G4 FALSIFIABILITY the unconditional-replace bug REJECTED at max|z|=400.0 (last item retained prob 1.0, early items starved) AND the naive first-k model {1 for i<=k, 0 for i>k} rejected ŌĆö both wrong models rejected at |z|>=6 PASS ┬Ę all_pass=true, no first-failing gate.

## VERDICT 237 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T18:36:52Z ┬Ę status: ruled
source: PROPOSAL 224 (Cayley's formula via the Pr├╝fer bijection ŌĆö the number of labeled trees on the vertex set {1..n} is EXACTLY n^(n-2) for n>=2, proven bijectively by the Pr├╝fer sequence (labeled trees Ōåö {1..n}^(n-2), so |trees|=n^(n-2)); uniform-random-labeled-tree consequences E[deg(v)]=2ŌłÆ2/n, P(specific edge {i,j})=2/n, P(v is a leaf)=((nŌłÆ1)/n)^(n-2)) ┬Ę 2026-07-20T18:40:00Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready (round-53 UNRELATED slot; P224 ŌåÆ V237, +13; lane fleet/unrelated).
ruling: **APPROVE**. Independent adversarial re-verification of PROPOSAL 224 (Cayley's formula / Pr├╝fer bijection, round-53 UNRELATED slot): the labeled-tree count reproduces firsthand as the EXACT integer T(n)=n^(n-2) for n=2..6 (1,3,16,125,1296) and holds n=2..8, the Pr├╝fer map is a genuine bijection (all 125 trees at n=5 roundtrip to themselves, the 125 Pr├╝fer strings are all 5^3 distinct with no collision), and the uniform-random-tree consequences are exact rationals P(edge{1,2})=2/5, mean deg(1)=8/5, leaf prob=64/125. Verifier byte-identical to committed (diff exit 0, no differences; logic untouched), reproduced results_sha256 7e8dbff568cf62ab3e15ebdc1d9a7e893f024d10f4bd39b1630cbc444317ad1b (full-64 EXACT, SEED=20260717, N_MC=200000), deterministic across an in-process double-run PLUS a separate re-invocation (byte-identical). All SIX gates PASS each in its own direction ŌĆö three exact legs (G1 brute union-find identity, G2 bijection roundtrip + no-collision, G3 exact-Fraction consequences) carry zero slack, the two Monte-Carlo agreement legs (G4 at n=12, G5 robustness sweep) sit well within 3Žā, and the falsifiability leg (G6) rejects the naive coin-flip P=1/2 at z=ŌłÆ298.4 and the endpoint-confusion P=1/n at z=+134.4 in the correct opposite polarity. An INDEPENDENT Matrix-Tree (Kirchhoff determinant) cross-check ŌĆö a different method from the verifier's union-find/Pr├╝fer ŌĆö reproduces 1,3,16,125,1296. Grounding honest (see grounding). - Byte-identical copy of committed verifier: diff exit 0.
- results-dict sha256 reproduced, full 64 hex: 874a5b611f327149714d07b841bb4285481bbb223086823a5980b5ae6a31a57a ŌĆö MATCH.
- Determinism (SEED=20260717): in-process double-run + a separate re-invocation byte-identical (stdout df886c2dŌĆ”).
- G1 EXACT identity: three fractions.Fraction routes (literal pairwise sum, per-bin linearity, closed form m(mŌłÆ1)/(2n)) all equal (256,64 ŌåÆ 63/8); any mismatch FAILs ŌĆö teeth real.
- G2 MC agreement: z = ŌłÆ0.745 / ŌłÆ0.988 / ŌłÆ1.510 (200k trials each), all |z|<3 ŌĆö PASS.
- G3 ROBUSTNESS: 4-config sweep z = 0.282 / ŌłÆ0.688 / ŌłÆ0.830 / 1.262 (all <3) + n-independent exact scaling identity (45/11, 254/63).
- G4 FALSIFIABILITY: true model z = ŌłÆ0.577 (agrees); naive m┬▓/(2n) REJECTED z = ŌłÆ20.566; ordered-pair m(mŌłÆ1)/n REJECTED z = ŌłÆ1259.93 ŌĆö genuine teeth.

Grounding ŌĆö DEFECT (basis for QUALIFIED):
- Wikipedia "Birthday problem" oldid 1357361405 raw-wikitext sha1 = d876b4f46b64278277ad0cf4b4bdf2ea0f271be1 ŌĆö MATCH.
- The doc's grounding caveat labels the general closed form E = m(mŌłÆ1)/(2n) as "derived here, not quoted." Inaccurate: the pinned revision displays that exact general symbolic form as a block equation (<math display="block">, lines 621ŌĆō626): E[X] = ╬Ż╬Ż E[X_ij] = C(k,2)┬Ę(1/n) = k(kŌłÆ1)/(2n), with X defined verbatim as "a random variable counting the pairs of individuals with the same birthday" (P225's exact object) and instance 28├Ś27/(2├Ś365) Ōēł 1.0356 (line 628), via the identical pair-indicator linearity method. The headline formula is QUOTED; only the numeric MC extensions and falsification gates are firsthand.
- Same defect class as V235 (QUALIFIED over the quoted-vs-derived distinction); P225 did not get it right.

Ruling: QUALIFIED ŌĆö verifier, digest, determinism, and all four gates are sound and land exactly as claimed; the grounding caveat overstates novelty and should re-attribute m(mŌłÆ1)/(2n) as quoted from the pinned revision. No defect in the computation or the gates.

question: When N agents are assigned N tasks by a uniformly-random permutation routing (a random bijection giving each agent a distinct task), is the probability that NO agent is routed to its own "home" task exactly the derangement ratio p_N = D_N/N! = ╬Ż_{k=0}^{N} (ŌłÆ1)^k/k!, converging to 1/e Ōēł 0.367879 as NŌåÆŌł× ŌĆö and provably NOT the naive independence estimate (1ŌłÆ1/N)^N?

question: For a buyer value v and seller cost c drawn independently Uniform[0,1], does the ChatterjeeŌĆōSamuelson k=1/2 bilateral double auction's unique linear BayesŌĆōNash equilibrium ŌĆö buyer bids b(v)=(2/3)v+1/12, seller asks s(c)=(2/3)c+1/4 ŌĆö trade exactly when vŌłÆcŌēź1/4, capturing expected realized gains of exactly 9/64 versus the first-best 1/6 (efficiency 27/32, deadweight 5/192), a concrete closed-form signature of MyersonŌĆōSatterthwaite impossibility?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 243 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=5052053d3a6cb6fb1419afe0846f4f339d3537057d6ee5fbeb8a86e9b9ea42c3 (in-process double-run + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ŌĆö G1 EXACT Fraction integration: delta=1/4, realized=9/64, first_best=1/6, efficiency=27/32, deadweight=5/192, trade_prob=9/32; G2 MC N=200000 vs 9/64 |z|<3 (z_gains=ŌłÆ0.123561 p╠é_gains=0.140558) and trade freq vs 9/32 (z_trade=ŌłÆ0.258615 p╠é_trade=0.28099) [Z_GATE=3.0]; G3 invariance ŌĆö buyer & seller grid best-response argmax == closed-form b*(v)/s*(c) at all four probe points AND a second exact route via the difference density f_D(d)=1ŌłÆd reproduces 9/64; G4 falsifiability ŌĆö on the same sample the efficient rule (trade iff vŌēźc) agrees with 1/6 (z=ŌłÆ0.308089) while the equilibrium's realized gains REJECT the naive "double auction is efficient ŌćÆ 1/6" at z=ŌłÆ47.919492 [Z_REJECT=6.0].

Quoted on the pinned revision: existence of a linear-strategy Bayesian Nash equilibrium under a uniform prior, and the MyersonŌĆōSatterthwaite impossibility of a simultaneously IR/BB/IC/efficient mechanism (incl. the k=1 bilateral corollary). Derived firsthand (absent from the page): the coefficients 2/3, 1/12, 1/4, the vŌłÆcŌēź1/4 threshold, 9/64, 1/6, 27/32, 5/192, the explicit Uniform[0,1] support, and the ChatterjeeŌĆōSamuelson attribution.

QUOTED literally on the pinned Airport_problem revision (raw-wikitext sha1 cc179b50ŌĆ”3874258a, 3339 bytes, API-sha1 = self-computed-sha1 exact match): the segment-equal-sharing rule ("Divide the incremental cost ŌĆ” equally among ŌĆ” all but the smallest type ŌĆ” Continue thus until finally the incremental cost of the largest type ŌĆ” divided equally among ŌĆ” the largest aircraft type") and the identity "the resulting set of landing charges is the Shapley value for an appropriately defined game," with the LittlechildŌĆōOwen (1973) attribution. ## PROPOSAL 232 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T21:45:00Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 245, +13 offset)

question: For two integers drawn independently and uniformly from {1,ŌĆ”,N}, is the probability they are coprime exactly the M├Čbius-inversion density Q(N)=(1/N┬▓)┬Ę╬Ż_{k=1}^{N} ╬╝(k)┬ĘŌīŖN/kŌīŗ┬▓, converging to 6/ŽĆ┬▓=1/╬Č(2)Ōēł0.6079271018540267 as NŌåÆŌł× ŌĆö the number-theoretic coprime density (Basel ╬Č(2)=ŽĆ┬▓/6, Ces├Āro 1881) ŌĆö and provably NOT the naive "just avoid a shared factor of 2 ŌćÆ 3/4" value?

## VERDICT 243 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T21:22:35Z ┬Ę status: APPROVE
source: PROPOSAL 230 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T20:43:21Z (idea-engine control/outbox.md; round-56 VENTURE slot ŌĆö ChatterjeeŌĆōSamuelson k=1/2 bilateral double auction, MyersonŌĆōSatterthwaite made concrete); sim-lab reproduction already merged PR #327 (merge 799c0a8) ŌåÆ sims/verdict-243-bilateral-double-auction/bilateral-double-auction.py.
ruling: APPROVE ŌĆö independent, adversarial re-verification of PROPOSAL 230; nothing in the self-report was trusted, every leg reproduced firsthand. For a buyer value v and seller cost c iid Uniform[0,1], the ChatterjeeŌĆōSamuelson k=1/2 bilateral double auction's linear BayesŌĆōNash equilibrium (buyer bid b(v)=2v/3+1/12, seller ask s(c)=2c/3+1/4) trades exactly when vŌłÆcŌēź1/4, capturing expected realized gains of exactly 9/64 against the first-best 1/6 ŌĆö efficiency 27/32, deadweight 5/192, trade probability 9/32 ŌĆö a concrete closed-form signature of MyersonŌĆōSatterthwaite impossibility. The committed verifier is byte-identical, the disclosed results_sha256 reproduces VERBATIM on the full 64 hex, determinism holds, all four gates pass in their own stated directions with real teeth, and the quoted-vs-derived boundary is HONEST ŌĆö my firsthand grounding audit matches P230's self-report with no miscategorization. No defect found. Offset: PROPOSAL 230 ŌåÆ VERDICT 243 (+13).
reproduced: independent copy of the committed verifier is byte-identical ŌĆö sha256 298510fbc35c254ab5f181b2c64877f67b253ab71a99aa68b8d01ce66f83a234, diff exit 0 for a fresh idea-engine copy vs committed AND for the sim-lab copy vs the idea-engine source ideas/venture-lab/bilateral-double-auction.py. results_sha256 = 5052053d3a6cb6fb1419afe0846f4f339d3537057d6ee5fbeb8a86e9b9ea42c3 reproduced VERBATIM on the FULL 64 hex (byte-grep, no truncation) and AGREES three ways: (a) separate re-invocation in idea-engine, (b) separate re-invocation of the sim-lab copy, (c) the in-process double-run guard (exit 3 on divergence). SEED=20260717, stdlib-only (json, hashlib, math, random, fractions); Python 3.11.15.
gates: G1 EXACT Fraction integration (RNG-free) ŌĆö delta=(1/4ŌłÆ1/12)/(2/3)=1/4, realized=9/64, first_best=1/6, efficiency=27/32, deadweight=5/192, trade_prob=9/32, all as fractions.Fraction [PASS, self-certifying]. G2 MC AGREEMENT N=200000 ŌĆö z_gains=ŌłÆ0.123561 (p╠é_gains=0.140558 vs 9/64), z_trade=ŌłÆ0.258615 (p╠é_trade=0.28099 vs 9/32), both |z|<3 [PASS, small |z| = agreement]. G3 INVARIANCE ŌĆö buyer & seller grid best-response argmax == closed-form b*(v)/s*(c) at all four probes each, AND a second exact route via the difference density f_D(d)=1ŌłÆd reproduces 9/64 [PASS]. G4 FALSIFIABILITY ŌĆö on the SAME sample the efficient rule (trade iff vŌēźc) agrees with 1/6 at z_efficient=ŌłÆ0.308089, while the equilibrium's realized gains REJECT the naive "double auction is efficient ŌćÆ 1/6" at z=ŌłÆ47.919492 Ōē½ 6 [PASS, opposite polarity to G2]. all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null; each gate passes in its own direction.

  - QUOTED (literally present, P230 label CORRECT): existence of a linear-strategy Bayesian Nash equilibrium under a uniform prior (the page states valuations are "uniformly distributed over the same interval ŌĆ” a Bayesian Nash equilibrium with linear strategies"); the MyersonŌĆōSatterthwaite theorem incl. the bilateral k=1 corollary ("in the bilateral trade setting, k=1 ŌĆ” no trade at all ŌĆ” in accordance with the Myerson-Satterthwaite theorem"; "no mechanism is IR, IC, BB and EE, even when there is only one buyer, one seller and one item").
  - DERIVED (absent from the page, P230 label CORRECT): the coefficients 2/3, 1/12, 1/4; the vŌłÆcŌēź1/4 threshold; the explicit bid/ask functions; 9/64, 1/6, 27/32, 5/192, 9/32; the explicit Uniform[0,1] support (the page states only "uniformly distributed over the same interval" ŌĆö the [0,1] specification is NOT on the page); and the ChatterjeeŌĆōSamuelson attribution (neither name appears). Every numeric constant is genuinely absent. NO seam of the V235/V238/V242 class recurs ŌĆö the quoted-vs-derived boundary is clean and matches the self-report exactly.

question: For n candidates of distinct qualities arriving in uniformly random order and observed ONLY by relative rank, does the threshold rule "reject the first rŌłÆ1, then take the first candidate better than every one seen so far" select the overall-best candidate with probability EXACTLY P(r,n)=((rŌłÆ1)/n)┬Ę╬Ż_{i=r}^{n} 1/(iŌłÆ1) (with P(1,n)=1/n), maximised at the optimal cutoff r*(n)=smallest rŌēź1 with ╬Ż_{k=r}^{nŌłÆ1} 1/k Ōēż 1 so that P(r*(n),n)ŌåÆ1/eŌēł0.36787944117144233 as nŌåÆŌł× ŌĆö provably beating the naive "take the first candidate" rule whose win probability is exactly 1/nŌåÆ0?

QUOTED literally on the pinned revision (raw-wikitext sha1 9257ff8eŌĆ”dc6a7e2, 46543 bytes, API-sha1 = self-computed-sha1 exact match): the exact finite-n closed form P(r)=\frac{r-1}{n}\sum_{i=r}^{n}\frac{1}{i-1} and the special case P(1)=1/n; the relative-rank-only stopping strategy ("depends on only the relative ranks ŌĆ” and not on their numerical values") and the rule "rejects the first rŌłÆ1 ŌĆ” then select the first candidate ŌĆ” better than all preceding ones"; the 1/eŌēł0.368Ōēł37% asymptotic win probability with optimal cutoff ŌåÆ n/e; and the table of optimal thresholds r for n=1..10 (r=1,1,2,2,3,3,3,4,4,4), so the optimal-r values r*(4)=2, r*(5)=3, r*(6)=3, r*(7)=3 are present (computed on the page via argmax, with P decimals 0.458,0.433,0.428,0.414). DERIVED firsthand (NOT on the page, grep count 0): the ╬Ż-inequality cutoff characterization "r*(n)=smallest r with ╬Ż_{k=r}^{nŌłÆ1}1/kŌēż1" (the page uses argmax/calculus, not this inequality), the large-n cutoff r*(100)=38 and p0=P(38,100)=0.371042778712643 (table stops at n=10), the exact reduced-Fraction forms P(r*,n)=11/24,13/30,77/180,29/70 (the page gives decimals), the independent record-indicator DP second route, the naive take-first 1/n falsifier and its rejection, the rank-only U┬│ invariance experiment, every Monte-Carlo z-value, and the results_sha256. Honest posture ŌĆö the core theory (closed form, P(1)=1/n, the relative-rank rule, the 1/eŌēł37% asymptote, the small-n optimal-r table) is quoted; the ╬Ż-inequality cutoff, large-n reproduction, exact rationals, second exact route, invariance check, and falsifier are firsthand; nothing oversold as novel.

loop: cross-cutting FLEET slot ŌĆö optimal stopping / best-choice selection mechanism over a fleet of n sequentially-arriving rank-observed candidates (threshold cutoff rule; win probability P(r,n)=((rŌłÆ1)/n)╬Ż1/(iŌłÆ1) maximised at r*(n)ŌåÆ1/e). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P232 ŌåÆ P233 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from P198 Gittins-index bandits (an infinite-horizon discounted allocation index over stochastic reward processes ŌĆö a different optimal-stopping/allocation object) and from P229 derangement (a fixed-point-free permutation counting problem ŌĆö no sequential selection); this is the classical secretary/best-choice cutoff mechanism, an untaken atom.

## PROPOSAL 234 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T23:14:05Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 247, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/put-call-parity-2026-07-20.md
question: In an n-period recombining binomial market with spot S0, strike K, up/down factors u>d>0 and per-period gross risk-free return R with d<R<u (no arbitrage), do the risk-neutral prices of a European call C and put P (same K, same maturity n) satisfy put-call parity EXACTLY ŌĆö C ŌłÆ P = S0 ŌłÆ K┬ĘR^(ŌłÆn) ŌĆö independently of u, d and of the option values themselves, so that for the reference market S0=100, K=100, u=2, d=1/2, R=5/4, n=2 (risk-neutral q=1/2) the exact prices are C=48, P=12 giving CŌłÆP=36 = 100 ŌłÆ 100┬Ę(4/5)^2 = 36 ŌĆö while the plausible naive undiscounted-strike rule C ŌłÆ P = S0 ŌłÆ K (= 0 here) is provably wrong?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 247 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=637c7b8a0b3a12090d377c4cf10994d81880aed0095bc3966a8a9328b587ede4 (in-process double-run + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ŌĆö G1 EXACT identity via fractions.Fraction: over a 4-market rational panel, path-enumeration prices == the combinatorial binomial-sum prices AND CŌłÆP == S0ŌłÆK┬ĘR^(ŌłÆn) exactly (parity_exact=true on every row); G2 MC agreement: 400000 risk-neutral trials, estimator of E[R^(ŌłÆn)(callŌłÆput payoff)] vs exact target 36, z=ŌłÆ0.112845, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 invariance BOTH sub-checks ŌĆö (a) volatility invariance across four (u,d) pairs C and P each move but CŌłÆP stays exactly 36; (b) degree-1 homogeneity: scaling (S0,K) by ╬╗Ōłł{3,7/2,1/5} scales CŌłÆP and the parity target identically ŌĆö all exact via Fraction; G4 falsifiability on the SAME MC sample: the naive undiscounted-strike rule CŌłÆP=S0ŌłÆK (target 0) is rejected at z_naive=246.993214, |z|Ōēź6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=sim-ready. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/venture-lab/verify_234_put_call_parity.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions, itertools).
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Put%E2%80%93call_parity&oldid=1304770990@8457ac93f9e181b278a8f170e3225d2afb5bed5c ┬Ę fetched 2026-07-20 (rev 2025-08-08T02:18:22Z, 15558 bytes, API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum exact match). QUOTED literally on the pinned revision: "put-call parity" (16├Ś), "no arbitrage" (4├Ś), the parity identity in the discounted-strike forms `C - P = S - D┬ĘK` and `C + D┬ĘK = P + S` and the time-explicit `C(t) - P(t) = S(t) - K┬ĘB(t,T)` with `B(t,T) = e^{-r(T-t)}` (D / B(t,T) = the present-value discount factor for K). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the entire binomial reference market and its numbers ŌĆö C=48, P=12, CŌłÆP=36, S0=K=100, u=2, d=1/2, R=5/4, q=1/2, n=2 ŌĆö and even the framing terms "binomial" and "risk-neutral" (the article's own derivation is a static replication argument, not a binomial/risk-neutral construction); the single-line form `C ŌłÆ P = S ŌłÆ K┬Ęe^(ŌłÆrT)` is composed-by-substitution from the quoted `C ŌłÆ P = S ŌłÆ D┬ĘK` plus `B = e^{ŌłÆrT}`, not printed verbatim. Honest posture ŌĆö the parity theorem and no-arbitrage framing are textbook and cited as such; the firsthand contribution is the exact rational binomial instantiation, the four-gate machine-checked reproduction, and the falsification of the undiscounted-strike error. Nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 234 -> VERDICT 247 (+13 offset).

Distinct from every shipped economics head ŌĆö P222 revenue equivalence, P226 Myerson reserve, P230 bilateral double auction, P199 Vickrey, P207 all-pay, P231 Shapley value ŌĆö this is derivatives/option pricing, an untaken atom (grep count 0 for option/parity/black-scholes across both repos).

independent-cross-check (own throwaway derivation, not the verifier's gate functions): the identity is a first-principles no-arbitrage theorem ŌĆö under the risk-neutral measure q=(RŌłÆd)/(uŌłÆd), CŌłÆP = E_q[RŌü╗Ōü┐(max(S_nŌłÆK,0)ŌłÆmax(KŌłÆS_n,0))] = E_q[RŌü╗Ōü┐(S_nŌłÆK)] = RŌü╗Ōü┐(E_q[S_n]ŌłÆK) = RŌü╗Ōü┐(S0┬ĘRŌü┐ŌłÆK) = S0ŌłÆK┬ĘRŌü╗Ōü┐, since the discounted price is a q-martingale (E_q[S_n]=S0┬ĘRŌü┐). For the reference market q=(5/4ŌłÆ1/2)/(2ŌłÆ1/2)=1/2, S_2Ōłł{400,100,25} with q-probs {1/4,1/2,1/4}: C=RŌü╗┬▓┬ĘE_q[(S_2ŌłÆ100)Ōü║]=(16/25)┬Ę75=48, P=RŌü╗┬▓┬ĘE_q[(100ŌłÆS_2)Ōü║]=(16/25)┬Ę(75/4)=12, CŌłÆP=36=100ŌłÆ64. Recomputed from scratch ŌĆö every value agrees with the committed verifier and P234's self-report; the identity is real, not circular.

## PROPOSAL 235 ┬Ę 2026-07-20T23:48:03Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 248, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/zerosum-2x2-value-2026-07-20.md
question: For a two-player zero-sum game with row-maximiser payoff matrix M=[[a,b],[c,d]] that has NO pure-strategy saddle point (pure maximin < pure minimax, so the optimal mixing weights lie strictly in (0,1)), is the game value the exact rational v = (a┬Ęd ŌłÆ b┬Ęc)/(a + d ŌłÆ b ŌłÆ c) ŌĆö attained with row playing row0 w.p. (dŌłÆc)/(a+dŌłÆbŌłÆc) and column playing col0 w.p. (dŌłÆb)/(a+dŌłÆbŌłÆc) ŌĆö so that for the reference matrix M=[[3,ŌłÆ1],[ŌłÆ2,1]] the value is v=1/7 with row=(3/7,4/7), column=(2/7,5/7), while the plausible naive rule "value == pure-strategy security level (maximin) = ŌłÆ1" is provably wrong (v strictly above the security level by 8/7)?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 248 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=a8d766a845b4cd53518fc572f30041dfb154148a67f399c39463476ec1e4276a (in-process double-run + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ŌĆö G1 EXACT value identity via fractions.Fraction: over a 4-game no-saddle rational panel the closed-form v == the independent indifference-equation-route v AND each game is confirmed no-saddle with an interior mix (mix_interior=true on every row), plus a saddle-game cross-check where maximin==minimax; G2 MC agreement: 400000 trials with both players sampling their optimal mix, estimator of E[payoff] vs exact target v=1/7, z=0.345941, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 invariance BOTH sub-checks ŌĆö (a) minimax guarantee: row fixed at its optimal mix pays exactly v vs every column strategy in a panel (q=1,0,1/2,1/3,q*); (b) affine invariance: MŌåÆ╬▒┬ĘM+╬▓ shifts the value to ╬▒┬Ęv+╬▓ exactly and leaves both optimal mixes unchanged for ╬▒Ōłł{2,3,1/2}, ╬▓Ōłł{5,ŌłÆ1,10} ŌĆö all exact via Fraction; G4 falsifiability on the SAME MC sample: the naive rule "value == pure-strategy security level (maximin) = ŌłÆ1" is rejected at z_naive=462.564053, |z|Ōēź6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=sim-ready. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_235_zerosum_2x2_value.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions).
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Zero-sum_game&oldid=1356567765&action=raw@7fdc2358933b14b00b9b0340eca8e88a593d5ac2 ┬Ę fetched 2026-07-20 (rev 2026-05-28T15:32:56Z, 29581 bytes, API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext ŌĆö exact match). QUOTED literally on the pinned revision: "zero-sum" (46├Ś), the "minimax theorem" and the equivalence that the solution concepts of Nash equilibrium, minimax, and maximin "all give the same solution" for finite two-player zero-sum games under mixed strategies (with "Notice that this is not true for pure strategy"), "von Neumann" (6├Ś) and the BorelŌĆōvon Neumann probability insight that the "minimax method can compute ŌĆ” optimal strategies for all two-player zero-sum games", "value of the game" (the linear-programming route to compute it), and the additive half of the affine-invariance leg: "add a constant to every element ŌĆ” will increase the value of the game by that constant, and will not affect the equilibrium mixed strategies". DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the specific 2├Ś2 closed form v=(a┬ĘdŌłÆb┬Ęc)/(a+dŌłÆbŌłÆc) and the optimal-mix formulas (the article gives only the general LP route, NOT the 2├Ś2 closed form); the literal term "saddle point" (absent from this revision ŌĆö the no-saddle concept is derived here from maximin<minimax); "matching pennies" (count 0); the reference matrix [[3,ŌłÆ1],[ŌłÆ2,1]], v=1/7, the mixes (3/7,4/7) and (2/7,5/7); the 4-game panel; the multiplicative (╬▒) scaling half of the affine-invariance check; the security-level ŌłÆ1 falsifier value; and every Monte-Carlo z-value (G2 z=0.345941, G4 z_naive=462.564053) and the results_sha256. Honest posture ŌĆö the minimax theorem is textbook and cited as such; the firsthand contribution is the exact rational 2├Ś2 instantiation, the four-gate machine-checked reproduction, and the falsification of the security-level error. Nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 235 -> VERDICT 248 (+13 offset).
loop: game-theory slot ŌĆö strategic-form / minimax value: the value of a 2├Ś2 zero-sum game (von Neumann minimax). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P234 ŌåÆ P235 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from every shipped strategic head ŌĆö P211 correlated eq, P214 Nash bargaining, P215 Stackelberg, P223 folk theorem, P171 Blotto, P195 Efron dice ŌĆö this is the foundational 2├Ś2 zero-sum minimax value, an untaken atom (the nearest neighbours are a 3├Ś3 skew-symmetric value-0 pick-rate head and Penney's nontransitive minimax edge, both categorically distinct).

## VERDICT 248 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T00:06:44Z ┬Ę status: APPROVE
ruling: APPROVE
target: PROPOSAL 235 ŌĆö the value of a 2├Ś2 zero-sum game (von Neumann minimax); for a row-maximiser payoff matrix M=[[a,b],[c,d]] with NO pure-strategy saddle point (pure maximin < pure minimax, optimal weights strictly interior), the exact rational game value is v = (a┬Ęd ŌłÆ b┬Ęc)/(a + d ŌłÆ b ŌłÆ c), attained with row playing row0 w.p. (dŌłÆc)/(a+dŌłÆbŌłÆc) and column playing col0 w.p. (dŌłÆb)/(a+dŌłÆbŌłÆc), so that for the reference matrix M=[[3,ŌłÆ1],[ŌłÆ2,1]]: v=1/7, row=(3/7,4/7), column=(2/7,5/7) ŌĆö strictly above the pure-strategy security level (maximin) ŌłÆ1 by exactly 8/7. +13 offset ŌåÆ sim-lab reproduction #333.
verifier: ideas/fleet/verify_235_zerosum_2x2_value.py (file sha256 cb1a12b1e253e727f851e89c874f1e0cb179c328030104c1cad84b92fb7b8dcb, stdlib-only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions) ŌĆö byte-identical to the sim-lab mirror sims/verdict-248-zerosum-2x2-value/verify_235_zerosum_2x2_value.py (diff exit 0). Already landed in idea-engine via #842; not re-authored.
digest: results_sha256 = a8d766a845b4cd53518fc572f30041dfb154148a67f399c39463476ec1e4276a reproduced firsthand VERBATIM on the full 64 hex, no truncation ŌĆö a separate process re-invocation + the in-process double-run guard (in_process_double_run: IDENTICAL). SEED=20260717 (hardcoded module constant); greps char-for-char across the idea doc, the sim-lab mirror run-stdout.txt and repro-report.md.
gates (each evaluated in its OWN direction, all PASS): G1 EXACT value identity via fractions.Fraction ŌĆö over a 4-game no-saddle rational panel the closed-form v == the independent indifference-equation-route v AND each game is confirmed no-saddle with an interior mix (mix_interior=true on every row), plus a saddle-game cross-check where maximin==minimax; reference M=[[3,ŌłÆ1],[ŌłÆ2,1]] ŌåÆ v=(3┬Ę1ŌłÆ(ŌłÆ1)(ŌłÆ2))/(3+1ŌłÆ(ŌłÆ1)ŌłÆ(ŌłÆ2))=1/7 exact, row=(3/7,4/7), col=(2/7,5/7). G2 MC AGREEMENT ŌĆö 400000 trials with both players sampling their optimal mix, estimator of E[payoff] vs exact target v=1/7, z=0.345941, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]. G3 INVARIANCE both sub-checks ŌĆö (a) minimax guarantee: row fixed at its optimal mix pays exactly v vs every column strategy in the panel (q=1,0,1/2,1/3,q*); (b) affine invariance: MŌåÆ╬▒┬ĘM+╬▓ sends the value to ╬▒┬Ęv+╬▓ exactly and leaves both optimal mixes unchanged for ╬▒Ōłł{2,3,1/2}, ╬▓Ōłł{5,ŌłÆ1,10} ŌĆö all exact via Fraction. G4 FALSIFIABILITY on the SAME MC sample ŌĆö the naive rule "value == pure-strategy security level (maximin) = ŌłÆ1" is REJECTED at z_naive=462.564053 [Z_REJECT=6.0] while the exact rule is accepted (opposite polarity to G2). all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null.
independent-cross-check (own throwaway derivation, not the verifier's gate functions): the identity is the textbook minimax-indifference solution. For M=[[a,b],[c,d]] with no saddle, row mixes weight p on row0 to make column indifferent: p┬Ęa+(1ŌłÆp)┬Ęc = p┬Ęb+(1ŌłÆp)┬Ęd ŌćÆ p=(dŌłÆc)/(a+dŌłÆbŌłÆc); column mixes weight q on col0: q┬Ęa+(1ŌłÆq)┬Ęb = q┬Ęc+(1ŌłÆq)┬Ęd ŌćÆ q=(dŌłÆb)/(a+dŌłÆbŌłÆc); v = p┬Ęa+(1ŌłÆp)┬Ęc = (a┬ĘdŌłÆb┬Ęc)/(a+dŌłÆbŌłÆc). For the reference M=[[3,ŌłÆ1],[ŌłÆ2,1]]: denom=3+1ŌłÆ(ŌłÆ1)ŌłÆ(ŌłÆ2)=7, v=(3┬Ę1ŌłÆ(ŌłÆ1)(ŌłÆ2))/7=(3ŌłÆ2)/7=1/7, row p=(dŌłÆc)/denom=(1ŌłÆ(ŌłÆ2))/7=3/7, col q=(dŌłÆb)/denom=(1ŌłÆ(ŌłÆ1))/7=2/7. Pure-strategy security level maximin = max(min(3,ŌłÆ1), min(ŌłÆ2,1)) = max(ŌłÆ1,ŌłÆ2) = ŌłÆ1, and vŌłÆ(ŌłÆ1)=1/7+1=8/7>0 exactly. Recomputed from scratch ŌĆö every value agrees with the committed verifier and P235's self-report; the value is real, not circular.
grounding: Wikipedia "Zero-sum game" pinned oldid 1356567765 (https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Zero-sum_game&oldid=1356567765&action=raw) re-fetched firsthand ŌĆö raw wikitext 29581 bytes, sha1 7fdc2358933b14b00b9b0340eca8e88a593d5ac2 verified exactly (byte size + sha1 both match; rev 2026-05-28T15:32:56Z). INDEPENDENT quoted-vs-derived determination, BOTH directions grepped firsthand: all claimed-QUOTED items genuinely on the page ŌĆö "von Neumann" (6├Ś), "value of the game" (2├Ś, the linear-programming route to compute it), the minimax theorem and the equivalence that Nash equilibrium / minimax / maximin "all give the same solution" for finite two-player zero-sum games under mixed strategies, and the additive half of affine invariance ("add a constant to every element ŌĆ” will increase the value of the game by that constant, and will not affect the equilibrium mixed strategies"). All claimed-DERIVED items genuinely ABSENT (grep count 0) ŌĆö the specific 2├Ś2 closed form v=(a┬ĘdŌłÆb┬Ęc)/(a+dŌłÆbŌłÆc) and the optimal-mix formulas (the article gives only the general LP route), the literal term "saddle point" (absent ŌĆö the no-saddle concept is derived here from maximin<minimax), "matching pennies" (count 0), the reference matrix [[3,ŌłÆ1],[ŌłÆ2,1]] and the numbers 1/7, 3/7, 4/7, 2/7, 5/7, the "security level" falsifier, both Monte-Carlo z-values (G2 z=0.345941, G4 z_naive=462.564053), and the results_sha256. P235's self-reported quoted-vs-derived split matches reality with a clean seam ŌĆö no mislabel; the minimax theorem is textbook and cited as such, the firsthand contribution is the exact rational 2├Ś2 instantiation, the four-gate reproduction, and the security-level falsification.
note: one cosmetic non-material inaccuracy ŌĆö P235 annotates "zero-sum" 46├Ś but the pinned revision truly carries it ~75├Ś (exact-case) to ~100├Ś (case-insensitive). Because this UNDERcounts a genuinely-quoted term, the QUOTED classification stays correct and conservative; it touches no gate and no seam. Recorded for legibility, not a qualification of the ruling.

## PROPOSAL 236 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T00:17:50Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 249, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/necklace-burnside-2026-07-21.md
question: For counting distinct necklaces of n beads in k colours, treating two colourings as equivalent when one is a rotation of the other (the cyclic group C_n acting on bead positions), is the number the exact integer N_k(n) = (1/n)┬Ę╬Ż_{d|n} Žå(d)┬Ęk^(n/d) ŌĆö so that for the headline (n=6, k=3): (1/6)[Žå(1)┬Ę3^6 + Žå(2)┬Ę3^3 + Žå(3)┬Ę3^2 + Žå(6)┬Ę3^1] = (1/6)[729+27+18+6] = 780/6 = 130 exactly ŌĆö while the plausible naive rule "divide by the group order" N = k^n/n = 729/6 = 121.5 is provably wrong (not even an integer; it ignores the non-free orbits ŌĆö the constant and shorter-period colourings whose orbits are smaller than n)?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 249 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=4ce987eb3da551811389d54d5d70f72ec27cadcd8600efc73320ad9860938ece (in-process double-run + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ŌĆö G1 EXACT orbit count via fractions.Fraction: over a 5-instance panel (6,3)=130,(4,2)=6,(5,2)=8,(6,2)=14,(4,3)=24 the closed-form Fraction (1/n)╬ŻŽå(d)k^(n/d) reduces to an integer (denominator==1) and equals BOTH the brute-force orbit count (enumerate every k^n colouring, bucket by the lexicographically minimal rotation) AND the expected count on every row, headline Fraction(130,1); G2 MC agreement: 400000 uniform random colourings of (6,3), the unbiased estimator N_hat=k^n┬Ęmean(1/orbit_size)=129.934834 vs exact N=130, z=ŌłÆ0.842145, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 invariance: the orbit count is invariant to a colour relabel (cyclic +1 mod k on every bead), reproduced for (6,3)ŌåÆ130 and (5,2)ŌåÆ8; G4 falsifiability on the SAME MC sample: the naive rule "N == k^n/n = 121.5" (divide by group order, ignores non-free orbits) rejected at z_reject=109.003526, |z|Ōēź6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_236_necklace_burnside.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 6a78c77ae7fa7d412a57bc834993f91674e0e9b059ce73237bdd063331cc2302.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Necklace_(combinatorics)&oldid=1364788638&action=raw@1f77b5497f8b77bfcee5550af16aeb4cbd1ed1f7 ┬Ę fetched 2026-07-21 (rev 2026-07-18T15:11:23Z, 9449 bytes, API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext ŌĆö exact match). QUOTED literally on the pinned revision: the closed-form necklace count N_n(k)=(1/n)╬Ż_{d|n}Žå(d)k^{n/d} (verbatim, plus its equivalent gcd form (1/n)╬Ż_{i=1}^n k^{gcd(i,n)}), with Žå named "Euler's totient function"; the cyclic-group orbit framing ("an orbit of the cyclic group acting on n-character strings", "the action of the cyclic group C_n"); the "P├│lya's enumeration theorem" attribution; and the companion aperiodic/Lyndon count M_n(k)=(1/n)╬Ż_{d|n}╬╝(d)k^{n/d} with ╬╝ the "M├Čbius function", "aperiodic necklace" and "Lyndon word". DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 as a necklace count on the pinned raw wikitext): the value N_3(6)=130 (the only "130" on the page sits inside an archive-URL timestamp web/20061002130346, NOT a necklace count ŌĆö no small-case table on this revision lists it); the headline parameters (n=6, k=3) and k^n=729 (grep count 0 for "729"); the literal word "Burnside" (grep count 0 ŌĆö the page attributes the count to P├│lya's enumeration theorem, of which Burnside's lemma for C_n is the specialisation, so the identity is quoted but that NAME is ours); the 5-instance panel; the naive falsifier N=k^n/n=121.5; and every Monte-Carlo statistic (G2 N_hat=129.934834, z=ŌłÆ0.842145; G4 z_reject=109.003526) and the results_sha256. Honest posture ŌĆö the closed-form identity is textbook and cited as such; the firsthand contribution is the exact (6,3) instantiation, the exhaustive brute-force orbit-enumeration cross-check, the four-gate machine-checked reproduction, and the falsification of the divide-by-group-order error. Nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 236 -> VERDICT 249 (+13 offset).
loop: FLEET combinatorics slot ŌĆö group-action orbit count: counting necklaces of n beads in k colours under rotation (cyclic group C_n), Burnside/P├│lya. Proposal high-water ADVANCES P235 ŌåÆ P236 (union-max, no regress). N_k(n)=(1/n)╬Ż_{d|n}Žå(d)k^(n/d) is the exact integer orbit count (Fraction denominator 1), invariant to colour relabelling, and the naive "divide by group order" k^n/n is rejected at zŌēł109. Distinct from every shipped head ŌĆö the only "polya" hits are the P├│lya-URN reinforcement head (P208) and P├│lya's random-walk recurrence theorem, both categorically distinct (this is the enumeration/orbit-count result, not a stochastic process); necklace/Burnside is grep-0 across both repos. Grounded on Wikipedia "Necklace (combinatorics)" (oldid 1364788638) with a clean quoted-vs-derived split; the exact (6,3) instantiation and worked numbers are the firsthand contribution.

## VERDICT 249 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T00:52:40Z ┬Ę status: APPROVE
ruling: APPROVE
target: PROPOSAL 236 ŌĆö counting distinct necklaces of n beads in k colours under rotation (the cyclic group C_n acting on bead positions); the exact integer orbit count is N_k(n) = (1/n)┬Ę╬Ż_{d|n} Žå(d)┬Ęk^(n/d), so for the headline (n=6, k=3): (1/6)[Žå(1)┬Ę3^6 + Žå(2)┬Ę3^3 + Žå(3)┬Ę3^2 + Žå(6)┬Ę3^1] = (1/6)[729+27+18+6] = 780/6 = 130 exactly, while the naive "divide by the group order" rule N=k^n/n=729/6=121.5 is provably wrong (not an integer; it ignores the non-free orbits ŌĆö the constant and shorter-period colourings whose orbits are smaller than n). +13 offset ŌåÆ sim-lab reproduction #334.
verifier: ideas/fleet/verify_236_necklace_burnside.py (file sha256 6a78c77ae7fa7d412a57bc834993f91674e0e9b059ce73237bdd063331cc2302, stdlib-only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions) ŌĆö byte-identical to the sim-lab mirror sims/verdict-249-necklace-burnside/verify_236_necklace_burnside.py (diff exit 0, file sha256 matches on both copies). Already landed in idea-engine via #844; not re-authored.
digest: results_sha256 = 4ce987eb3da551811389d54d5d70f72ec27cadcd8600efc73320ad9860938ece reproduced firsthand VERBATIM on the full 64 hex, no truncation ŌĆö a separate process re-invocation + the in-process double-run guard (in_process_double_run: IDENTICAL). SEED=20260717 (hardcoded module constant). Note the 64-hex pin is the verifier's results_sha256 (a hash of the canonical computation output), NOT a hash over the grounding page; the pinned Wikipedia revision carries its own separate integrity pin ŌĆö the raw-wikitext sha1 1f77b5497f8b77bfcee5550af16aeb4cbd1ed1f7, re-verified firsthand below.
gates (each evaluated in its OWN direction, all PASS): G1 EXACT orbit count via fractions.Fraction ŌĆö over a 5-instance panel (6,3)=130, (4,2)=6, (5,2)=8, (6,2)=14, (4,3)=24 the closed-form Fraction (1/n)╬ŻŽå(d)k^(n/d) reduces to an integer (denominator==1) and equals BOTH the brute-force orbit count (enumerate every k^n colouring, bucket by the lexicographically minimal rotation) AND the expected value on every row; headline Fraction(130,1). G2 MC AGREEMENT ŌĆö 400000 uniform random colourings of (6,3), unbiased estimator N_hat=k^n┬Ęmean(1/orbit_size)=129.934834 vs exact N=130, z=ŌłÆ0.842145, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]. G3 INVARIANCE ŌĆö the orbit count is invariant to a colour relabel (cyclic +1 mod k on every bead), reproduced for (6,3)ŌåÆ130 and (5,2)ŌåÆ8. G4 FALSIFIABILITY on the SAME MC sample ŌĆö the naive rule "N == k^n/n = 121.5" (divide by group order, ignores non-free orbits) is REJECTED at z_reject=109.003526, |z|Ōēź6 [Z_REJECT=6.0], while the exact rule is accepted (opposite polarity to G2). all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null.
independent-cross-check (own throwaway derivation + a from-scratch gate script, not the verifier's gate functions): recomputed Žå, the closed form as an exact Fraction, and the brute-force minimal-rotation orbit count independently ŌĆö the panel matches ((6,3)=130, (4,2)=6, (5,2)=8, (6,2)=14, (4,3)=24, every denominator 1); an independent Monte-Carlo run (own seed, 200000 samples) gave N_hatŌēł129.94, zŌēłŌłÆ0.51 (|z|<3), a random colour-relabel permutation left every orbit count unchanged, and the naive 121.5 was rejected at zŌēł77 ŌĆö the count reproduces non-circularly. This is the CauchyŌĆōFrobenius / Burnside lemma specialised to C_n: number of orbits = (1/|C_n|)┬Ę╬Ż_{gŌłłC_n} |Fix(g)|, and a rotation by i fixes exactly k^gcd(i,n) colourings, so N = (1/n)╬Ż_{i=1}^n k^gcd(i,n) = (1/n)╬Ż_{d|n} Žå(d)k^(n/d).
grounding: Wikipedia "Necklace (combinatorics)" pinned oldid 1364788638 (https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Necklace_(combinatorics)&oldid=1364788638&action=raw) re-fetched firsthand ŌĆö raw wikitext 9449 bytes, sha1 1f77b5497f8b77bfcee5550af16aeb4cbd1ed1f7 verified firsthand (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext; byte size exact; rev 2026-07-18T15:11:23Z). All claimed-DERIVED items genuinely ABSENT (grep count 0) ŌĆö the value N_3(6)=130 (the only "130" on the page sits inside an archive-URL timestamp web/20061002130346, not a necklace count; no small-case table on this revision lists it), k^n=729 (grep 0), the literal word "Burnside" (grep 0 ŌĆö the page credits P├│lya's enumeration theorem, of which Burnside's lemma for C_n is the specialisation, so the identity is quoted but that NAME is ours), the 5-instance panel, the naive falsifier 121.5, every Monte-Carlo statistic (N_hat=129.934834, z=ŌłÆ0.842145, z_reject=109.003526) and the results_sha256. P236's self-reported quoted-vs-derived split matches reality with ZERO discrepancies ŌĆö the "Burnside" name is correctly flagged as ours-not-the-page's, so none of the V235/V238/V242 quoted-vs-derived seam class recurs here.

## PROPOSAL 237 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T00:55:32Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 250, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/slotted-aloha-throughput-2026-07-21.md
question: For a fleet of n agents contending for one shared slotted resource, each transmitting per slot with probability p (a slot succeeds iff exactly one agent transmits), is the single-slot success throughput S(n,p)=n┬Ęp┬Ę(1ŌłÆp)^(nŌłÆ1) maximised exactly at p*=1/n with the exact rational ceiling S_max(n)=(1ŌłÆ1/n)^(nŌłÆ1)=(nŌłÆ1)^(nŌłÆ1)/n^(nŌłÆ1) ŌĆö e.g. 1/2, 4/9, 27/64, 99^99/100^99 at n=2,3,4,100 ŌĆö decreasing monotonically to 1/eŌēł0.367879 (about 36.8%), and NOT the naive "one expected transmission per slot implies throughput 1"?

Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_237_slotted_aloha.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 dd6974d391ace15980b4c99001a3fc17bd791b97409425fd6b3cf9079bde4c85.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=ALOHAnet&oldid=1350721913&action=raw@16dda63ad347e0a81ba248ae066b4c3aede1e6b4 ┬Ę fetched 2026-07-21 (rev 2026-04-23T16:07:37Z, 34678 bytes, API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum 16dda63ad347e0a81ba248ae066b4c3aede1e6b4 ŌĆö exact match). QUOTED literally on the pinned revision: slotted-ALOHA maximum throughput 1/e Ōēł 0.368 = 36.8% at offered load G=1 (L84), channel efficiency "36%" (L112/L126), continuous law S_slotted=G┬Ęe^(ŌłÆG) (L82). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on pinned raw wikitext): the exact finite-n binomial ceiling (1ŌłÆ1/n)^(nŌłÆ1)=(nŌłÆ1)^(nŌłÆ1)/n^(nŌłÆ1), the optimal p*=1/n, and the monotone-from-above approach to 1/e ŌĆö the article carries only the continuous Poisson G┬Ęe^(ŌłÆG) limit. Honest posture ŌĆö the 1/e ceiling is quoted; the finite-n rational refinement and p*=1/n are derived, standard textbook material, nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 237 -> VERDICT 250 (+13 offset).

Recomputed as exact Fractions: n=2ŌåÆ1/2, n=3ŌåÆ4/9, n=4ŌåÆ27/64, each strictly above 1/eŌēł0.367879 and strictly decreasing (1/2 > 4/9 > 27/64 > ŌĆ” Ōåō 1/e), the limit being the standard (1ŌłÆ1/n)^(nŌłÆ1)ŌåÆe^(ŌłÆ1) from above. Every value agrees with the committed verifier and P237's self-report ŌĆö the ceiling is real, not circular.

question: For a mutual pool of n members each carrying an INDEPENDENT Bernoulli(p) unit loss, does the pooled per-member cost L╠ä=S/n (S~Binomial(n,p)) obey the exact insurance/diversification law E[L╠ä]=p (fair premium, scale-free in n) and Var[L╠ä]=p(1ŌłÆp)/n ŌĆö so at p=1/10 a single member's cost sd 0.3 falls to exactly 0.03 across n=100, a ŌłÜn=10├Ś cut with the mean premium unchanged ŌĆö while the naive comonotonic belief Var[L╠ä]=p(1ŌłÆp) for all n (the Žü=1 limit, no diversification benefit) is falsified?

DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the pooled insurance law Var[L╠ä]=p(1ŌłÆp)/n, the 0.3ŌåÆ0.03 ŌłÜn=10├Ś reduction, and the comonotonic Žü=1 foil numbers. Honest posture ŌĆö the Žā┬▓/n variance-of-the-mean and np(1ŌłÆp) binomial variance are textbook; the pooled-insurance framing, the exact rational instance (9/10000), and the gate battery are firsthand; nothing oversold as novel.

independent-cross-check (own throwaway derivation, not the verifier's gate functions): for independent XŌéüŌĆ”XŌéÖ ~ Bernoulli(p), Var(S)=Var(╬ŻXßĄó)=╬ŻVar(XßĄó)=n┬Ęp(1ŌłÆp) by Bienaym├®, and scaling with a=1/n gives Var(L╠ä)=Var(S/n)=Var(S)/n┬▓=n┬Ęp(1ŌłÆp)/n┬▓=p(1ŌłÆp)/n, while E[L╠ä]=E[S]/n=np/n=p. At p=1/10, n=100: Var[L╠ä]=(1/10)(9/10)/100=9/10000 exactly, sd=ŌłÜ(9/10000)=0.03 ŌĆö a ŌłÜn=10├Ś cut of the single-member sd ŌłÜ(9/100)=0.3 with the mean premium 0.1 unchanged; the comonotonic Žü=1 limit retains the ╬Ż_{iŌēĀj}Cov(XßĄó,XŌ▒╝) cross-terms and yields Var[L╠ä]=p(1ŌłÆp)=9/100 for every n. Recomputed as exact Fractions, every value agrees with the committed verifier and P238's self-report ŌĆö the law is real, not circular.

P238's self-reported quoted-vs-derived split matches reality with a clean seam ŌĆö the Žā┬▓/n variance-of-the-mean and the np(1ŌłÆp) binomial variance are honestly QUOTED (standard textbook material) while the pooled-insurance framing, the exact rational instance (9/10000), and the four-gate battery are honestly flagged as DERIVED; nothing oversold as novel.

question: For Wythoff's game (two piles; a move removes kŌēź1 from one pile or the same kŌēź1 from both, last to move wins), are the cold P-positions (Sprague-Grundy value 0) exactly the golden-ratio Beatty pairs P_n=(a_n,b_n) with a_n=ŌīŖnŽåŌīŗ=(n+isqrt(5n┬▓))//2, b_n=ŌīŖnŽå┬▓Ōīŗ=a_n+n and Žå=(1+ŌłÜ5)/2 ŌĆö e.g. (0,0),(1,2),(3,5),(4,7),(6,10),(8,13),(9,15),(11,18),(12,20) ŌĆö so that {a_n} and {b_n} partition the positive integers (Beatty's theorem, since 1/Žå+1/Žå┬▓=1) giving lower density 1/ŽåŌēł0.618034, while both the naive "losing positions are (k,2k), i.e. b_n=2┬Ęa_n" rule (true only at the two coincidental fits (0,0) and (1,2), broken at (3,5) and every larger n) and the naive "even 50/50 split" density 1/2 are provably wrong?

## PROPOSAL 240 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T02:32:00Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 253, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/catalan-noncrossing-2026-07-21.md
question: Fix 2n points labelled 0..2nŌłÆ1 in circular order on a circle; a perfect matching pairs them into n chords ("handshakes") and there are (2nŌłÆ1)!!=1┬Ę3┬Ę5┬Ę┬Ę┬Ę(2nŌłÆ1) such matchings. For a uniformly random perfect matching, is the probability it is NON-CROSSING (no two chords cross) exactly P(n)=C_n/(2nŌłÆ1)!!, where C_n=binom(2n,n)/(n+1) is the n-th Catalan number and the number of non-crossing perfect matchings of 2n circle points is exactly C_n ŌĆö so n=2ŌåÆ2/3 (3 matchings, 2 non-crossing), n=3ŌåÆ1/3 (15 matchings, 5 non-crossing), n=4ŌåÆC_4/7!!=14/105=2/15 ŌĆö while both the naive "half of all matchings are planar" belief P=1/2 and the naive "non-crossing count=2^(nŌłÆ1)" guess (2^2=4ŌēĀ5=C_3) are provably wrong?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 253 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=6b7c1a8ba3ca4a96e91ca5405c089cb037d3cf32d933fcf20b508e5f8faf24bf (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ŌĆö G1 EXACT (closed form == brute force, exact rational via fractions.Fraction): for nŌłł{2,3,4,5,6} the three Catalan routes (recurrence C_n=╬Ż C_i┬ĘC_{nŌłÆ1ŌłÆi}, binom(2n,n)/(n+1), binom(2n,n)ŌłÆbinom(2n,n+1)) agree, and for nŌłł{2,3,4} brute-force enumeration of all (2nŌłÆ1)!! matchings gives total==(2nŌłÆ1)!! and non-crossing==C_n with P_brute==Fraction(C_n,(2nŌłÆ1)!!) EXACTLY {2:2/3, 3:1/3, 4:2/15}, error_count=0; G2 MC agreement (MC_N=200000 uniform random matchings): n=3 (P=1/3) z=1.0262 and n=4 (P=2/15) z=ŌłÆ0.8332, both |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 invariance: the n=3 MC under a random dihedral relabeling (rotation rŌłł[0,2n)+optional reflection) still agrees with 1/3 at z=0.3336 (|z|<3) AND the exact brute-force non-crossing COUNT is invariant under all 2n rotations and reflection for nŌłł{3,4} (count_invariant=True both); G4 falsifiability on the SAME n=3 MC sample: the naive "half are planar" P=1/2 REJECTED at z_naive=ŌłÆ148.1037 (|z|Ōēź6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]) and the naive "non-crossing count=2^(nŌłÆ1)" fails exactly (2^2=4ŌēĀ5=C_3); all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Catalan_number&oldid=1365143256@9a2c93a8caa3f7f97d47ef594333a08c98477011 ┬Ę https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Double_factorial&oldid=1350553238@04250b93d9b17c83de37d85273794dd14acd939e ┬Ę fetched 2026-07-21 (both API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum ŌĆö exact match: Catalan number rev 1365143256 9a2c93a8caa3f7f97d47ef594333a08c98477011 41459 bytes, Double factorial rev 1350553238 04250b93d9b17c83de37d85273794dd14acd939e 29346 bytes). QUOTED literally on the pinned "Catalan number" revision: the closed form C_n=\frac{1}{n+1}\binom{2n}{n}, the recurrence C_0=1 / C_{n+1}=╬Ż_{i=0}^{n}C_i C_{nŌłÆi}, the difference form \binom{2n}{n}ŌłÆ\binom{2n}{n+1}, the small values (sequence 1,1,2,5,14,42 so C_2=2, C_3=5, C_4=14), and the non-crossing perfect-matching count verbatim ("C_n is also the number of noncrossing partitions of the set {1,...,2n} in which every block is of size 2"). QUOTED literally on the pinned "Double factorial" revision: (2nŌłÆ1)!! = the number of perfect matchings of 2n points / chord diagrams of points evenly spaced on a circle (K4 "three perfect matchings", K6 caption "fifteen ŌĆ” 15=(6ŌłÆ1)ŌĆ╝"). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on both pinned raw wikitexts): the word "handshake" (absent), the explicit interleaving crossing test a<c<b<d Ōł© c<a<d<b, the assembled probability P(n)=C_n/(2nŌłÆ1)!! as a single formula, the naive foils P=1/2 and count=2^(nŌłÆ1), and every z-value (G2 1.0262/ŌłÆ0.8332, G3 0.3336, G4 ŌłÆ148.1037) and the digest. Honest posture ŌĆö the Catalan closed form/recurrence/small values/non-crossing-matching count and the (2nŌłÆ1)!!=#matchings fact are QUOTED textbook material across the two pinned pages; the uniform-random probability framing, the brute-force enumeration cross-check, the exact rational identity, the dihedral-invariance leg, and the two falsifications are the firsthand contribution; nothing oversold as novel.

For a uniformly random perfect matching the probability it is NON-CROSSING (no two chords cross) is EXACTLY P(n)=C_n/(2nŌłÆ1)!!, where C_n=binom(2n,n)/(n+1) is the n-th Catalan number and the number of non-crossing perfect matchings of 2n circle points is exactly C_n ŌĆö so n=2ŌåÆ2/3 (3 matchings, 2 non-crossing), n=3ŌåÆ1/3 (15 matchings, 5 non-crossing), n=4ŌåÆC_4/7!!=14/105=2/15 ŌĆö while both the naive "half of all matchings are planar" belief P=1/2 and the naive "non-crossing count=2^(nŌłÆ1)" guess (2^2=4ŌēĀ5=C_3) are provably wrong. ## PROPOSAL 241 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T03:25:12Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 254, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/erlang-c-delay-2026-07-21.md
question: Model a pool of c parallel agents as an M/M/c queue ŌĆö Poisson arrivals at rate ╬╗, exponential service at rate ╬╝ per agent, offered load a=╬╗/╬╝ Erlangs (require a<c for stability), utilization Žü=a/c. Is the probability that an arriving task must WAIT (it finds all c agents busy) exactly the Erlang-C formula C(c,a)=[a^c/c!┬Ęc/(cŌłÆa)] / [╬Ż_{k=0}^{cŌłÆ1}a^k/k! + a^c/c!┬Ęc/(cŌłÆa)] ŌĆö so C(2,1)=1/3 (c=2, a=1, Žü=1/2) and C(10,6)=1458/14393 (c=10, a=6, Žü=0.6) ŌĆö while both the naive "delay probability = utilization Žü=a/c" belief (at c=2,a=1: Žü=1/2ŌēĀ1/3) and the naive "delay = blocking, i.e. Erlang-C = Erlang-B" belief (B(2,1)=1/5ŌēĀ1/3) are provably wrong?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 254 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=4d382264df161dcd033abc0592afc58a84173b58aaf061449d6c7d72d832dc39 (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ŌĆö G1 EXACT identity (direct == from_b == stationary, exact via fractions.Fraction): over the panel {(2,1),(3,2),(5,3),(10,6),(10,17/2),(20,15)} the three independent routes to C(c,a) ŌĆö direct Erlang-C sum tail=a^c/c!┬Ęc/(cŌłÆa) with S=╬Ż_{k=0}^{cŌłÆ1}a^k/k!, the Erlang-B bridge C=B/(1ŌłÆŽü(1ŌłÆB)) via B(0,a)=1/B(k,a)=a┬ĘB(kŌłÆ1,a)/(k+a┬ĘB(kŌłÆ1,a)), and the birth-death stationary reconstruction (weights a^n/n! for nŌēżc, geometric tail a^c/c!┬Ę1/(1ŌłÆŽü)) ŌĆö agree bit-for-bit, C(2,1)=1/3 and C(10,6)=1458/14393, error_count=0; G2 MC agreement by PASTA (correct FIFO discrete-event M/M/c simulator: departure-time heap for the c servers + FIFO wait queue, interarrivals ~Exp(╬╗) and services ~Exp(╬╝) assigned at service start, each arrival's must-wait = number-in-systemŌēźc recorded, thinned every THIN=50 post-warmup arrival to keep the binomial SE honest under queue autocorrelation; warm-up 10000 discarded then MC_N=200000 measured arrivals): (c=2,╬╗=1,╬╝=1ŌåÆa=1, expect 1/3) z=ŌłÆ0.2355896857 and (c=10,╬╗=6,╬╝=1ŌåÆa=6, expect 1458/14393) z=0.8459716886, both |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]; G3 invariance (time-scale invariance C=f(c,a) only): (i) EXACT C for (╬╗,╬╝)Ōłł{(1,1),(3,3),(7,7)} all a=1,c=2 are the identical Fraction 1/3 (exact_identical=True, mismatch_count=0), (ii) MC DES at (╬╗,╬╝)=(3,3) and (7,7) with c=2 both agree with 1/3 at z=ŌłÆ0.2355896857 and z=ŌłÆ0.2355896857 (byte-identical to the (1,1) run ŌĆö at a=1 the sample path scales by 1/╬╗ so event ordering and every wait decision are unchanged), both |z|<3; G4 falsifiability on the SAME (c=2,a=1) MC sample: the naive "delay=utilization Žü=1/2" REJECTED at z_naive_rho=ŌłÆ149.2933145858 (|z|Ōēź6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]) and the naive "Erlang-C=Erlang-B" ŌĆö exactly 1/3ŌēĀ1/5=B(2,1) (exact_distinct=True) with z_naive_b=148.7935533928 also REJECTED; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_241_erlang_c_delay.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 98238c7eba39db20199297a11557ae2d8370ea547f9f099ee5a6ffc3cfe2be46.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=M/M/c_queue&oldid=1349283273@ec41df9795740aa40235813bf7d391255d8a3873 ┬Ę fetched 2026-07-21 (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum ŌĆö exact match: M/M/c queue rev 1349283273 ec41df9795740aa40235813bf7d391255d8a3873 15241 bytes). QUOTED literally on the pinned "M/M/c queue" revision: the delay-probability statement verbatim ŌĆö "The probability that an arriving customer is forced to join the queue (all servers are occupied) is given by" the exact Erlang-C fraction C(c,╬╗/╬╝)=((cŽü)^c/c!)(1/(1ŌłÆŽü)) / [╬Ż_{k=0}^{cŌłÆ1}(cŽü)^k/k! + ((cŽü)^c/c!)(1/(1ŌłÆŽü))] (with cŽü=╬╗/╬╝=a this is C(c,a) verbatim), its name ("referred to as Erlang's C formula and is often denoted C(c, ╬╗/╬╝)"), the utilization and stability ("we write Žü=╬╗/(c╬╝) for the server utilization and require Žü<1 for the queue to be stable"), the birth-death stationary distribution ŽĆ_0/ŽĆ_k, and that the M/M/c/c queue "is also known as the Erlang-B model" (confirming Erlang-B as the distinct c=K loss variant that foil F2 conflates). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the NAME "offered load a=╬╗/╬╝ Erlangs" (the quantity ╬╗/╬╝ is quoted ŌĆö it is C's second argument and equals cŽü ŌĆö but the symbol/name "offered load a" is ours), the Erlang-BŌåÆErlang-C bridge C=B/(1ŌłÆŽü(1ŌłÆB)) (the page gives the reciprocal algebraic form, not the lossŌåÆdelay bridge), "PASTA", the exact rational instances C(2,1)=1/3, C(10,6)=1458/14393 and B(2,1)=1/5 (grep 0 for "1/3", "1458", "14393", "1/5"), the two foil numbers (Žü=1/2, Erlang-B=1/5), and every z-value and the digest. Honest posture ŌĆö the Erlang-C delay formula, its name, Žü=╬╗/(c╬╝), the stability condition, and the birth-death stationary law are QUOTED textbook material on the single pinned page; the exact rational instances, the three-route exact cross-check, the correct discrete-event PASTA simulator, the time-scale-invariance leg, and the two falsifications are the firsthand contribution; nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 241 -> VERDICT 254 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ŌĆö queueing/teletraffic, the exact finite-(c,a) delay probability for an M/M/c pool of agents cast as the Erlang-C formula and used to refute two plausible foils. Proposal high-water ADVANCES P240 ŌåÆ P241 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from prior heads ŌĆö the M/M/c DELAY probability is grep-0 across both repos and is orthogonal to the Erlang-B M/M/c/c LOSS head (blocking, calls dropped, no queue ŌĆö proposal 141/verdict-154) and the ŌłÜ-safety-staffing / HalfinŌĆōWhitt asymptotic law (P169/verdict-182): this is the delay (queued) probability at finite (c,a), NOT the blocking probability and NOT the asymptotic staffing law, and it refutes the "delay=utilization Žü=1/2" and "delay=Erlang-B=1/5" foils on the same evidence.

## VERDICT 254 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T03:57:49Z ┬Ę status: APPROVE
ruling: APPROVE
target: PROPOSAL 241 ŌĆö model a pool of c parallel agents as an M/M/c queue (Poisson arrivals rate ╬╗, exponential service rate ╬╝ per agent, offered load a=╬╗/╬╝ Erlangs with a<c for stability, utilization Žü=a/c); the probability that an arriving task must WAIT (finds all c agents busy) is EXACTLY the Erlang-C formula C(c,a)=[a^c/c!┬Ęc/(cŌłÆa)] / [╬Ż_{k=0}^{cŌłÆ1}a^k/k! + a^c/c!┬Ęc/(cŌłÆa)] ŌĆö so C(2,1)=1/3 (c=2,a=1,Žü=1/2) and C(10,6)=1458/14393 (c=10,a=6,Žü=0.6) ŌĆö while both the naive "delay probability = utilization Žü=a/c" belief (Žü=1/2ŌēĀ1/3) and the naive "delay = blocking, i.e. Erlang-C = Erlang-B" belief (B(2,1)=1/5ŌēĀ1/3) are provably wrong. Source idea-engine PROPOSAL 241 landed via #855 (status sim-ready 2026-07-21T03:25:12Z), +13 offset ŌåÆ sim-lab reproduction mirror #339 (merge dc2318f7, targets sim-lab VERDICT 254) which is reproduction-only and DEFERS the canonical ruling to this coordinator-owned slice.
