gates (each evaluated in its OWN direction, all PASS): G1 EXACT identity via exact rationals (fractions.Fraction) ŌĆö over the panel {(2,1),(3,2),(5,3),(10,6),(10,17/2),(20,15)} the three independent routes to C(c,a) (direct Erlang-C sum tail=a^c/c!┬Ęc/(cŌłÆa) with S=╬Ż_{k=0}^{cŌłÆ1}a^k/k!, the Erlang-B bridge C=B/(1ŌłÆŽü(1ŌłÆB)) via B(0,a)=1/B(k,a)=a┬ĘB(kŌłÆ1,a)/(k+a┬ĘB(kŌłÆ1,a)), and the birth-death stationary reconstruction weights a^n/n! for nŌēżc with geometric tail a^c/c!┬Ę1/(1ŌłÆŽü)) agree bit-for-bit, C(2,1)=1/3 and C(10,6)=1458/14393, error_count=0. G2 MC AGREEMENT by PASTA ŌĆö a correct FIFO discrete-event M/M/c simulator (departure-time heap for the c servers + FIFO wait queue, interarrivals~Exp(╬╗), services~Exp(╬╝) at service start, each arrival's must-wait = number-in-systemŌēźc recorded), thinned every THIN=50 post-warmup arrival to keep the binomial SE honest under queue autocorrelation (warm-up 10000 discarded then MC_N=200000 measured): (c=2,╬╗=1,╬╝=1ŌåÆa=1, expect 1/3) z=ŌłÆ0.2355896857 and (c=10,╬╗=6,╬╝=1ŌåÆa=6, expect 1458/14393) z=0.8459716886, both |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]. G3 INVARIANCE (time-scale, C=f(c,a) only) ŌĆö EXACT C for (╬╗,╬╝)Ōłł{(1,1),(3,3),(7,7)} all a=1,c=2 are the identical Fraction 1/3 (exact_identical=True, mismatch_count=0), and the MC DES at (╬╗,╬╝)=(3,3) and (7,7) with c=2 agree with 1/3 (byte-identical z=ŌłÆ0.2355896857 ŌĆö at a=1 the sample path scales by 1/╬╗ so every wait decision is unchanged), |z|<3. G4 FALSIFIABILITY on the SAME (c=2,a=1) MC sample ŌĆö the naive "delay=utilization Žü=1/2" REJECTED at z_naive_rho=ŌłÆ149.2933145858 (|z|Ōēź6 [Z_REJECT=6.0]) and the naive "Erlang-C=Erlang-B" (exactly 1/3ŌēĀ1/5=B(2,1), exact_distinct=True) REJECTED at z_naive_b=148.7935533928, opposite polarity to G2. all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS.
independent-cross-check (own throwaway rebuild, not the verifier's gate functions): recomputed C(c,a) three independent ways as exact fractions.Fraction ŌĆö direct Erlang-C sum, the Erlang-BŌåÆErlang-C bridge, and the birth-death stationary reconstruction ŌĆö all equal at every panel point with C(2,1)=1/3 and C(10,6)=1458/14393, error_count=0; and built a fresh FIFO discrete-event M/M/c simulator (independent seeds 424242/909090 + a 30-seed sweep, independent thinning THIN=1/37/97). SPECIAL SCRUTINY of the G2 thinning: the every-arrival (THIN=1) iid binomial z blows past 3 (30-seed empirical z-std 3.30, |z|>3 in 11/30 runs at c=10) even though the point estimate is essentially unbiased (bias 2.8e-5) ŌĆö because the M/M/c arrival-to-arrival occupancy is autocorrelated; measured lag-autocorrelation Žü1=0.72 at c=10 with integrated autocorrelation time IACTŌēł12.76 (ŌłÜIACTŌēł3.57), so the true SE is Ōēł3.57├Ś the iid SE, structurally inflating the iid z. Thinning to ~decorrelated epochs (any of THIN=37/97, independent seeds) restores an honest binomial SE and brings |z|<3 at the same converged p╠é (p╠éŌēł0.3351 vs 1/3; p╠éŌēł0.1011 vs 1458/14393). TEETH INTACT: the thinned gate still rejects wrong nulls hard ŌĆö Žü=1/2 at |z|Ōēł171, Erlang-B=1/5 at |z|Ōēł176, and even a subtly-wrong null C(10,6)├Ś1.05 at |z|Ōēł8.9. Every value agrees with the committed verifier and P241's self-report ŌĆö the identity is real, not circular.
grounding: Wikipedia "M/M/c queue" pinned oldid 1349283273 (https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=M/M/c_queue&oldid=1349283273&action=raw) re-fetched firsthand two independent routes (action=raw + API revisions.content) byte-identical ŌĆö raw wikitext 15241 bytes, sha1 ec41df9795740aa40235813bf7d391255d8a3873 verified live (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext; byte size exact). INDEPENDENT quoted-vs-derived determination, BOTH directions re-grepped firsthand on the pinned wikitext ŌĆö matches P241's self-report with zero mismatch. QUOTED (literally present on the pinned revision): the delay-probability statement verbatim ("The probability that an arriving customer is forced to join the queue (all servers are occupied) is given by" + the Erlang-C fraction), its name ("referred to as Erlang's C formula and is often denoted C(c, ╬╗/╬╝)"), the utilization + stability ("Žü = ╬╗/(c╬╝)" with "Žü<1" required for stability), the birth-death stationary distribution (ŽĆ_0/ŽĆ_k), and that the M/M/c/c queue "is also known as the Erlang-B model". DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext, genuinely absent): the NAME "offered load a=╬╗/╬╝ Erlangs" (╬╗/╬╝ itself is quoted, the symbol/name "offered load a" is ours), the Erlang-BŌåÆErlang-C bridge C=B/(1ŌłÆŽü(1ŌłÆB)), "PASTA", the exact rationals 1/3, 1458, 14393, 1/5, the two foil numbers (Žü=1/2, Erlang-B=1/5), and every z-value and the digest. Honest posture ŌĆö the Erlang-C delay formula, its name, Žü=╬╗/(c╬╝), the stability condition, and the birth-death stationary law are QUOTED textbook material on the single pinned page; the exact rational instances, the three-route exact cross-check, the correct discrete-event PASTA simulator, the time-scale-invariance leg, and the two falsifications are the firsthand contribution; nothing oversold as novel.

loop: fleet slot ŌĆö queueing/teletraffic exact finite-(c,a) delay probability: the probability an arriving task must WAIT in an M/M/c pool of c agents is exactly the Erlang-C formula C(c,a); P241 ŌåÆ V254 (+13). C(2,1)=1/3 and C(10,6)=1458/14393; the "delay=utilization Žü=1/2" foil is rejected at z=ŌłÆ149.2933145858 and the "delay=Erlang-B=1/5" foil at z=148.7935533928 (exactly 1/3ŌēĀ1/5). Distinct from the Erlang-B M/M/c/c LOSS head (P141ŌåÆV154) and the ŌłÜ-staffing HalfinŌĆōWhitt asymptotic (P169ŌåÆV182): this is the finite-(c,a) DELAY (queued) probability, not blocking and not the asymptotic staffing law. Grounded on Wikipedia "M/M/c queue" (oldid 1349283273) with a clean quoted-vs-derived split; the exact rational instances, the three-route exact cross-check, the correct discrete-event PASTA simulator, the time-scale-invariance leg, and the two falsifications are the firsthand contribution.

## PROPOSAL 242 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T06:15:00Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 255, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/venture-lab/min-variance-two-fund-2026-07-21.md
question: For n risky assets with a symmetric positive-definite covariance matrix ╬Ż, is the global minimum-variance (GMV) portfolio ŌĆö the fully-invested (­Ø¤ÖßĄĆw=1) portfolio of least return variance ŌĆö exactly w*=╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö/(­Ø¤ÖßĄĆ╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö) with variance Žā*┬▓=1/(­Ø¤ÖßĄĆ╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö), equivalently every asset's marginal variance contribution equalised (╬Żw*)_i=Žā*┬▓ for all i ŌĆö so that for ╬Ż=[[4,1/2],[1/2,1]] (variances 4 and 1, cov 1/2, corr 1/4) w*=(1/8,7/8) and Žā*┬▓=15/16, STRICTLY below the best single asset (variance 1) and far below naive equal-weight (variance 3/2) ŌĆö while both the naive "equal weights 1/n are optimal" belief (variance 3/2ŌēĀ15/16) and the naive "just hold the single lowest-variance asset" belief (variance 1>15/16) are provably wrong, and the mean-variance efficient weight vector w(m) is affine in the target mean return m (two-fund separation)?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 255 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=3c1ec97050d0ca481e084976055ce3ea0e435c86b794e909507757e3eedf3f9c (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ŌĆö G1 EXACT identity (closed form == matrix GMV; FOC equalised, exact via fractions.Fraction): (w*,Žā*┬▓) computed two independent exact ways ŌĆö the two-asset closed form wŌéü*=(sŌéé┬▓ŌłÆsŌéüŌéé)/(sŌéü┬▓+sŌéé┬▓ŌłÆ2sŌéüŌéé), Žā*┬▓=(sŌéü┬▓sŌéé┬▓ŌłÆsŌéüŌéé┬▓)/(sŌéü┬▓+sŌéé┬▓ŌłÆ2sŌéüŌéé) and the general matrix form via an exact Fraction Gauss-Jordan solve of ╬Żx=­Ø¤Ö (w*=x/­Ø¤ÖßĄĆx, Žā*┬▓=1/­Ø¤ÖßĄĆx) ŌĆö agree bit-for-bit (w*=(1/8,7/8), Žā*┬▓=15/16), and across a 2/3/4-asset SPD panel (╬Żw*)_i==Žā*┬▓ holds for every i and ­Ø¤ÖßĄĆw*==1, error_count=0; G2 MC AGREEMENT (iid): MC_N=200000 iid return vectors with covariance exactly ╬Ż (Cholesky map L=[[2,0],[1/4,ŌłÜ15/4]] of iid standard normals), the GMV portfolio's realised sample variance s┬▓_gmv=0.9389802396 agrees with Žā*┬▓=0.9375 at z=0.4992990385, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] ŌĆö the draws are iid (not a correlated sample path) so the Gaussian sample-variance SE=Žā┬▓┬ĘŌłÜ(2/N) is honest with NO thinning needed (contrast the M/M/c queueing gates which thin for autocorrelation); G3 INVARIANCE (two own directions): (i) scale-equivariance ╬ŻŌåÆk╬Ż (k=4) leaves w* identical and scales Žā*┬▓ by exactly k (Fraction), and the MC z is byte-identical under the ŌłÜk sample-path rescaling (mc_z_base=mc_z_scaled=0.4992990385); (ii) two-fund separation ŌĆö on a 3-asset frontier w(m) is affine in m, verified exactly by w(m_C)==╬▒┬Ęw(m_A)+(1ŌłÆ╬▒)┬Ęw(m_B) with ╬▒=1/2 (m_A,m_B,m_C=1,3,2), each w(m) summing to 1, and the GMV recovered on the frontier at m=B/A; G4 FALSIFIABILITY on the SAME MC sample: the naive "equal weights are optimal" (variance 3/2) REJECTED at z_eq=191.0595200324 and the naive "hold the single lowest-variance asset" (variance 1) REJECTED at z_a2=21.4684726468, both |z|Ōēź6 [Z_REJECT=6.0], and exactly 3/2ŌēĀ15/16 and 1ŌēĀ15/16; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/venture-lab/verify_242_min_variance_two_fund.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 b0e251b21c889e1c5f1dc9c36c33a2e9c1acaf3adfaa037aff381eb9ca101817.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Modern_portfolio_theory&oldid=1359858554@7936722ffb730765fc0172ef339b5fd19b40bbf8 ┬Ę fetched 2026-07-21 (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext ŌĆö exact match: Modern portfolio theory rev 1359858554, 2026-06-17T17:40:04Z, 59536 bytes). QUOTED literally on the pinned "Modern portfolio theory" revision: the mean-variance objective ŌĆö minimise w^T╬Żw subject to ╬ŻßĄówßĄó=1 (the fully-invested constraint, present verbatim), ╬Ż "the covariance matrix for the returns on the assets", w^T╬Żw "the variance of portfolio return"; the parametric efficient frontier and its Lagrange-multiplier linear system (the block matrix with 2╬Ż, R, ­Ø¤Ö); the Global Minimum Variance Portfolio (GMVP), "the vertex of the hyperbola ŌĆ” the portfolio with the lowest possible risk among all combinations of risky assets"; and the two mutual fund theorem ("also known as the separation theorem") ŌĆö "any portfolio on the efficient frontier can be constructed as a linear combination of any two distinct portfolios already located on the frontier", P_target=╬▒┬ĘPŌéü+(1ŌłÆ╬▒)┬ĘPŌéé. DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the SOLVED closed form w*=╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö/(­Ø¤ÖßĄĆ╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö) and Žā*┬▓=1/(­Ø¤ÖßĄĆ╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö) ŌĆö the page gives the Lagrange block-matrix system and a graphical/parametric solution, not the solved-out inverse-covariance formula (grep 0 for "Sigma^{-1}"); the FOC-equalisation (╬Żw*)_i=Žā*┬▓; the exact rational instances w*=(1/8,7/8), Žā*┬▓=15/16, equal-weight 3/2, single-asset 1 (grep 0 for "1/8", "7/8", "15/16", "3/2"); the two foil framings; and every z-value and the digest. Honest posture ŌĆö the mean-variance objective, the fully-invested efficient-frontier Lagrange system, the GMVP concept, and the two-fund separation theorem are QUOTED textbook material on the single pinned page; the explicit inverse-covariance GMV closed form is standard textbook but derived here from that quoted system (not literally on the page); the exact rational instance, the two-route exact cross-check with FOC-equalisation, the iid Monte-Carlo agreement, the scale-equivariance and two-fund exact legs, and the two falsifications are the firsthand contribution; nothing oversold as novel.

loop: venture slot ŌĆö portfolio optimization / mean-variance: the Markowitz global minimum-variance portfolio w*=╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö/(­Ø¤ÖßĄĆ╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö) with variance 1/(­Ø¤ÖßĄĆ╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö) and two-fund separation, used to refute two plausible foils. Proposal high-water ADVANCES P241 ŌåÆ P242 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from prior heads ŌĆö the Markowitz GMV / two-fund separation object is grep-0 across both repos and is orthogonal to the risk-pooling / diversification variance law (P238ŌåÆV251, the Žā┬▓/n law for an independent Bernoulli pool) and the IRR heads: this is the least-variance fully-invested weight vector for a general SPD covariance and the affine structure of the efficient frontier, and it refutes the "equal weights are optimal" (variance 3/2) and "hold the single lowest-variance asset" (variance 1) foils on the same evidence, with the GMV variance 15/16 strictly below every constituent.

## VERDICT 255 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T04:45:53Z ┬Ę status: APPROVE
ruling: APPROVE
target: PROPOSAL 242 ŌĆö for n assets with a symmetric positive-definite covariance matrix ╬Ż the fully-invested (­Ø¤ÖßĄĆw=1) global minimum-variance (GMV) portfolio is EXACTLY w*=╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö/(­Ø¤ÖßĄĆ╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö) with variance Žā*┬▓=1/(­Ø¤ÖßĄĆ╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö), equivalently every asset's marginal variance contribution equalised ((╬Żw*)_i=Žā*┬▓ for all i); for ╬Ż=[[4,1/2],[1/2,1]] w*=(1/8,7/8) and Žā*┬▓=15/16, STRICTLY beating the best single asset (variance 1) and far below naive equal-weight (variance 3/2); the mean-variance efficient weight vector w(╬╝) is affine in the target mean return ╬╝ (two-fund separation) ŌĆö while both the naive "equal weights 1/n are optimal" belief (3/2ŌēĀ15/16) and the naive "just hold the single lowest-variance asset" belief (1>15/16) are provably wrong. Independently confirmed. gates (each evaluated in its OWN direction, all PASS): G1 EXACT ŌĆö closed form == exact KKT solve == equalised-marginal-variance FOC, bit-for-bit under fractions.Fraction arithmetic over a 2/3/4-asset SPD panel (2-asset w*=(1/8,7/8) Žā*┬▓=15/16, (╬Żw*)=(15/16,15/16), ╬╗=15/16; 3-asset w*=(11/21,2/7,4/21) Žā*┬▓=43/21; 4-asset w*=(15/74,9/74,17/74,33/74) Žā*┬▓=125/74), ­Ø¤ÖßĄĆw*==1 every case, error_count=0. G2 MC AGREE ŌĆö the GMV portfolio-return sample variance agrees with Žā*┬▓=15/16 at |z|<3 (verifier z=+0.499 at N=200k iid via the Cholesky map L=[[2,0],[1/4,ŌłÜ15/4]]; independent redraw z=+0.079 at N=500k), iid draws so the Gaussian sample-variance SE=Žā┬▓┬ĘŌłÜ(2/N) is honest with NO thinning needed [Z_ACCEPT=3.0]. G3 INVARIANCE (two own directions) ŌĆö scale-equivariance ╬ŻŌåÆk╬Ż leaves w* invariant and scales Žā*┬▓ŌåÆkŽā*┬▓ exactly (Fraction, MC z byte-identical under the ŌłÜk sample-path rescaling), and two-fund affine separation w(╬╝)=a+b╬╝ holds exactly on a 3-asset frontier (w(╬╝_C)==╬▒┬Ęw(╬╝_A)+(1ŌłÆ╬▒)┬Ęw(╬╝_B), ╬▒=1/2, each summing to 1, GMV recovered at ╬╝=B/A). G4 FALSIFY (teeth, same MC sample, opposite polarity to G2) ŌĆö the naive "equal weights are optimal" (variance 3/2) and "hold the single lowest-variance asset" (variance 1) foils REJECTED against the false null Žā*┬▓=15/16 at |z| far beyond 6 (verifier z_eq=191.06 / z_a2=21.47; independent redraw 299.06 / 34.65), exactly 3/2ŌēĀ15/16 and 1ŌēĀ15/16 [Z_REJECT=6.0]. all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS.
independent-cross-check (own throwaway rebuild, not the verifier's gate functions): recomputed (w*,Žā*┬▓) two independent exact ways ŌĆö the two-asset closed form and a general exact-Fraction Gauss-Jordan solve of ╬Żx=­Ø¤Ö (w*=x/­Ø¤ÖßĄĆx, Žā*┬▓=1/­Ø¤ÖßĄĆx) ŌĆö agree bit-for-bit at w*=(1/8,7/8), Žā*┬▓=15/16 and across the 2/3/4-asset SPD panel with (╬Żw*)_i==Žā*┬▓ for every i; and drew a fresh independent iid Monte-Carlo (N=500k, independent seed) giving the GMV sample-variance z=+0.079 and the two foils rejected at |z|=299.06/34.65 ŌĆö non-circular confirmation of the identity.
grounding: Wikipedia "Modern portfolio theory" pinned oldid 1359858554 (https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Modern_portfolio_theory&oldid=1359858554&action=raw) re-fetched firsthand ŌĆö raw wikitext 59536 bytes, sha1 7936722ffb730765fc0172ef339b5fd19b40bbf8 verified live (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext; byte size exact). INDEPENDENT quoted-vs-derived determination, BOTH directions re-grepped firsthand ŌĆö matches P242's self-report with no seam. QUOTED (literally present on the pinned revision): the mean-variance objective (minimise wßĄĆ╬Żw subject to the fully-invested ╬Żw_i=1 constraint), ╬Ż "the covariance matrix for the returns", wßĄĆ╬Żw "the variance of portfolio return", the efficient-frontier Lagrange-multiplier linear system (the block matrix with 2╬Ż, R, ­Ø¤Ö), the Global Minimum Variance Portfolio (GMVP) concept, and the two mutual fund / separation theorem (any frontier portfolio a linear combination of two distinct frontier portfolios). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext, genuinely absent): the SOLVED inverse-covariance closed form w*=╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö/(­Ø¤ÖßĄĆ╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö) and Žā*┬▓=1/(­Ø¤ÖßĄĆ╬ŻŌü╗┬╣­Ø¤Ö) ŌĆö the page gives only the Lagrange block-matrix system, never inverting it (grep 0 for "Sigma^{-1}") ŌĆö plus the FOC-equalisation (╬Żw*)_i=Žā*┬▓, the exact rationals (grep 0 for "1/8","7/8","15/16","3/2"), the two foil framings, and every z-value and the digest. Honest posture ŌĆö the mean-variance objective, the fully-invested efficient-frontier Lagrange system, the GMVP concept, and the two-fund separation theorem are QUOTED textbook material on the single pinned page; the explicit inverse-covariance GMV closed form is standard textbook but derived here from that quoted system (not literally on the page); the exact rational instance, the two-route exact cross-check with FOC-equalisation, the iid Monte-Carlo agreement, the scale-equivariance and two-fund exact legs, and the two falsifications are the firsthand contribution. The page's line-353 "inverse of the correlation matrix" is a distinct agnostic-risk-parity method, NOT the GMV formula ŌĆö no false attribution. Nothing oversold as novel.

## PROPOSAL 243 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T05:07:47Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 256, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/fibonacci-nim-zeckendorf-2026-07-21.md
question: In Fibonacci nim (Whinihan 1963) ŌĆö one pile of n tokens; the FIRST move removes any m with 1ŌēżmŌēżnŌłÆ1 (not the whole pile); every SUBSEQUENT move removes 1ŌēżmŌēż2┬Ę(the number the opponent just removed), capped at the tokens left; last token wins (normal play) ŌĆö is it exactly true that the player to move LOSES (the position is a P-position) if and only if n is a Fibonacci number (1,2,3,5,8,13,21,ŌĆ”), so the first player has a winning strategy iff n is NOT a Fibonacci number; and is the optimal winning move from any non-Fibonacci n to remove the SMALLEST Fibonacci summand of n's Zeckendorf representation (the unique representation as a sum of non-consecutive Fibonacci numbers), which is always legal (Ōēż nŌłÆ1) and always leaves the opponent a P-position ŌĆö while the naive "P-positions are the multiples of 3" belief and the naive "P-positions are the powers of two" belief are both provably wrong?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 256 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=344cdaa21a550c745184aaab00951db1063f522d3fe25544738299fdf8ee7dce (in-process double-run + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ŌĆö G1 EXACT identity (integer arithmetic): a from-scratch bottom-up game oracle win(n,cap) over nŌłł[1,800] ŌĆö where a move takes mŌłł1..min(cap,n), m=n wins immediately, else the opponent faces (nŌłÆm,min(nŌłÆm,2m)), the opening state is (n,nŌłÆ1) and n=1ŌåÆ(1,0) loses ŌĆö gives loses(n)==(nŌłłFib) for every n with mismatches=0 (Fib built independently by integer recurrence); AND the constructive Zeckendorf strategy, for every winning n the smallest Zeckendorf summand s satisfies sŌēżnŌłÆ1 and win(nŌłÆs,min(2s,nŌłÆs))==False, zeckendorf_strategy_violations=0; G2 MC AGREEMENT: exact P-density p0=|FibŌł®[1,800]|/800=14/800=7/400=0.0175 (kept as exact Fraction and float), 200000 uniform ints in [1,800] classified via the DP table give p_hat=0.01785, z=(p_hatŌłÆp0)/ŌłÜ(p0(1ŌłÆp0)/n)=1.19370699368, |z|<3 [Z_GATE=3.0]; G3 INVARIANCE/ROBUSTNESS (two own directions): (a) recomputing the oracle at a second independent horizon N2=1200 reproduces the P-position set on the overlap [1,800] byte-identical to the N-run and equal to the Fibonacci prefix, invariance_discrepancies=0; (b) randomized-play robustness ŌĆö from every winning start nŌłł[1,800], R=40 complete games where our player uses the DP to pick a winning move and the opponent plays a uniformly-random legal move, games_played=31440, optimal_player_losses=0; G4 FALSIFIABILITY: the naive "P-positions are the multiples of 3" foil disagrees with the true DP outcome on 274/800 positions, |z|=20.413966739Ōē½3 REJECTED, and the secondary "P-positions are the powers of two" foil disagrees on 18/800, |z|=4.29119123911>3 REJECTED; all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/superbot-games/verify_243_fibonacci_nim.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 d290db491e1f85827641c112bc98b33fc247f675d729fad094da37abb16c3350.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Fibonacci_nim&oldid=1341159330@fcebea2e6f696f94d6d9efda5edc537bf38f18d3 ┬Ę fetched 2026-07-21T05:04:43Z (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext ŌĆö exact match: Fibonacci nim rev 1341159330, 10087 bytes). Zeckendorf uniqueness grounded on "Zeckendorf's theorem" oldid 1344127047@0cc894e5c3131f80c51a0db6f6fb042b76b73654 (sha1 verified the same two ways). QUOTED literally on the pinned revisions: [1] "the first player can win if and only if the starting number of coins is not a Fibonacci number" (Fibonacci nim); [2] "on each move taking at most twice as many coins as the previous move, and winning by taking the last coin" (Fibonacci nim); [3] "it is always a winning move to remove all the coins (if this is allowed) or otherwise to remove a number of coins equal to the smallest Fibonacci number in the Zeckendorf representation" (Fibonacci nim); [4] "every positive integer can be represented uniquely as the sum of one or more distinct Fibonacci numbers in such a way that the sum does not include any two consecutive Fibonacci numbers" (Zeckendorf's theorem). DERIVED firsthand: the from-scratch game oracle win(n,cap) over [1,800] and [1,1200], the exact P-density Fraction 7/400, the Monte-Carlo z, the randomized-play robustness over 31440 games, the two falsification z-values, and the results-dict digest ŌĆö the pinned pages carry no z-values, no density fraction, and no digest. Honest posture ŌĆö the theorem, rule, and Zeckendorf strategy are QUOTED prose on the pinned pages; the computational proof (oracle, density, z's, digest) is the firsthand contribution; nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 243 -> VERDICT 256 (+13 offset).
loop: superbot-games slot ŌĆö combinatorial game theory / take-away with a doubling reply cap: Fibonacci nim (Whinihan) P-positions are exactly the Fibonacci numbers and the optimal move is the smallest Zeckendorf summand, used to refute the "multiples of 3" (|z|Ōēł20) and "powers of two" (|z|Ōēł4.3) losing-set foils. Proposal high-water ADVANCES P242 ŌåÆ P243 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from prior heads ŌĆö Fibonacci nim / Zeckendorf-strategy is grep-0 across both repos and orthogonal to the Nim/Sprague-Grundy nim-sum head (P219ŌåÆV232, XOR-of-heaps) and the Wythoff/Beatty golden-ratio cold-position head (P239ŌåÆV252, ŌīŖnŽåŌīŗ Beatty pairs): this is the single-pile doubling-cap take-away game whose losing positions are the Fibonacci numbers, the exact "Zeckendorf/Fibonacci-base" atom flagged as untaken in the Wythoff session-idea list.

ruling: APPROVE
target: PROPOSAL 243 ŌĆö in Fibonacci nim (Whinihan 1963), one pile of n tokens, the FIRST move removes any m with 1ŌēżmŌēżnŌłÆ1 (never the whole pile) and every SUBSEQUENT move removes 1ŌēżmŌēż2┬Ę(the number the opponent just took) capped at what remains, last token wins (normal play): the player to move LOSES (the position is a P-position) if and only if n is a Fibonacci number (1,2,3,5,8,13,21,ŌĆ”), so the first player has a winning strategy iff n is NOT a Fibonacci number; and from any non-Fibonacci n the optimal winning move is to remove the SMALLEST Fibonacci summand of n's Zeckendorf representation (the unique representation as a sum of non-consecutive Fibonacci numbers), which is always legal (ŌēżnŌłÆ1) and always leaves the opponent a P-position ŌĆö while both the naive "P-positions are the multiples of 3" belief and the naive "P-positions are the powers of two" belief are provably wrong. Independently confirmed. G3 INVARIANCE/ROBUSTNESS (two own directions) ŌĆö (a) recomputing the oracle at a second independent horizon N2=1200 reproduces the P-position set on the overlap [1,800] byte-identical to the N-run and equal to the Fibonacci prefix, invariance_discrepancies=0; (b) randomized-play robustness ŌĆö from every winning start nŌłł[1,800], R=40 complete games where our player uses the DP to pick a winning move and the opponent plays a uniformly-random legal move, games_played=31440, optimal_player_losses=0. question: In Buffon's needle ŌĆö a needle of length Ōäō dropped uniformly at random on a plane ruled with parallel lines spaced d apart, in the SHORT-needle regime Ōäō Ōēż d (center offset to the nearest line u ~ Uniform[0, d/2] and acute angle ╬Ė ~ Uniform[0, ŽĆ/2] independent; the needle crosses iff u Ōēż (Ōäō/2)┬Ęsin╬Ė) ŌĆö is it exactly true that the crossing probability is P = 2Ōäō/(ŽĆd), so that tossing the needle N times and counting C crossings estimates ŽĆ via ŽĆ╠é = 2ŌäōN/(dC); while the naive "no angle factor" belief P = Ōäō/d (forgetting the 2/ŽĆ orientation average) and the subtler "assume E[sin╬Ė] = ┬Į" belief P = (Ōäō/d)┬Ę┬Į are both provably wrong?

Bernoulli, NO autocorrelation, so no thinning is needed; Buffon ŽĆ-estimate ŽĆ╠é=2ŌäōN/(dC)=3.14183335402; G3 INVARIANCE/ROBUSTNESS (two own directions): (a) scaling both Ōäō and d by kŌłł{2,5,0.3,100} leaves P╠é within sampling error of 2Ōäō/(ŽĆd)=1/ŽĆ, each scaled config |z|<3 (worst k=5: z=ŌłÆ1.15685382694); (b) sampling ╬Ė over the full circle [0,2ŽĆ) with |sin╬Ė| matches ╬Ė~[0,ŽĆ/2], z=ŌłÆ0.229457897577; max|z|=1.15685382694<3; G4 FALSIFIABILITY: on the SAME G2 sample the naive "no angle factor" foil P_naive=Ōäō/d=0.5 is REJECTED at |z|=513.96622076Ōē½3, and the subtler "E[sin╬Ė]=┬Į" foil P_naive2=(Ōäō/d)┬Ę┬Į=0.25 is REJECTED at |z|=223.019509108Ōē½3; all_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. ## PROPOSAL 245 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T06:19:00Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 258, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/open-addressing-uniform-hashing-probes-2026-07-21.md
question: Model an open-addressing hash table under the UNIFORM-HASHING model ŌĆö each key's probe sequence is a uniformly random permutation of all m slots. With m slots and n occupied (load factor ╬▒=n/m), is the EXPECTED number of probes in an UNSUCCESSFUL search exactly E_unsucc(m,n)=(m+1)/(mŌłÆn+1) ŌĆö from the exact combinatorial definition ╬Ż_{i=0}^{n} C(n,i)/C(m,i) (probability the first i probes are all occupied), proved equal via the hockey-stick identity C(n,i)/C(m,i)=C(mŌłÆi,nŌłÆi)/C(m,n) ŌćÆ ╬Ż=C(m+1,n)/C(m,n)=(m+1)/(mŌłÆn+1) ŌĆö so (1,0)ŌåÆ1, (2,1)ŌåÆ3/2, (3,2)ŌåÆ2, (10,7)ŌåÆ11/4=2.75 and (100,70)ŌåÆ101/31Ōēł3.258; and as m,nŌåÆŌł× at fixed ╬▒ does it increase monotonically to the classic 1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) ŌĆö while the naive belief that "expected probes = 1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) exactly at finite (m,n)" is provably wrong (at m=100,n=70 the truth is 101/31Ōēł3.258, not 10/3Ōēł3.333)?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 258 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=98d7398935db83b93f4e5b71ef4393abf487d3bc4371018d0d9d16e4f75e1746 (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ŌĆö G1 EXACT identity (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): over the battery {(1,0),(2,1),(3,2),(5,3),(10,7),(13,0),(16,8),(23,17),(50,35),(100,70),(128,96),(257,200)} the finite sum ╬Ż_{i=0}^{n} C(n,i)/C(m,i), the closed form (m+1)/(mŌłÆn+1), and the hockey-stick ratio C(m+1,n)/C(m,n) are EXACTLY equal, anchors (1,0)ŌåÆ1/(2,1)ŌåÆ3/2/(3,2)ŌåÆ2/(10,7)ŌåÆ11/4 pinned, mismatches=0, anchor_mismatches=0, max discrepancy exactly 0 (z not applicable, reported null/"exact"); G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): headline (m=100,n=70), N=200000 i.i.d. uniform-hashing searches (uniformly random probe permutation per trial via the seeded RNG, n occupied, count probes to the first empty slot), mean_hat=3.2596 vs E_exact=101/31Ōēł3.2581, z=0.2616711626, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] ŌĆö the trials are INDEPENDENT (one probe-count each) so NO thinning is needed (stated in the verifier docstring); G3 INVARIANCE/ROBUSTNESS (own direction): the expected probe count depends ONLY on (m,n) not on WHICH slots are occupied ŌĆö config A (occupied=first n slots) z=0.3374925104 and config B (occupied=fixed pseudo-random n-subset) z=ŌłÆ1.1046610225 both agree with E_exact and with each other (two-sample invariance z=1.0187955206), all |z|<3; exact monotone-convergence sub-check at ╬▒=7/10 over mŌłł{10,100,1000,10000,100000} ŌåÆ 11/4,101/31,143/43,10001/3001,100001/30001 strictly increasing, all strictly below the limit 10/3, approaching it; G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME headline sample): the naive "1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) exact at finite (m,n)"=10/3Ōēł3.333 is REJECTED at z=12.5653466132 (Ōē½3, Z_REJECT=3.0) while the same sample agrees with exact 101/31 at |z|=0.26 ŌĆö the teeth: 1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) is a limit, not a finite-table equality; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_245_open_addressing_probes.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 ac4642df4a3b77fe35c418ffba9d2038589d9463cb1c40e1f341e0d371c5c5ab.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Open_addressing&oldid=1353211902@164df5a783e58eb4bb744e87fe2e7a5c227199bb ┬Ę fetched 2026-07-21 (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext ŌĆö exact match: Open addressing rev 1353211902 sha1 164df5a783e58eb4bb744e87fe2e7a5c227199bb, 8806 bytes). QUOTED literally on the pinned "Open addressing" revision (grep count > 0): the load factor ╬▒=n/m (\alpha=n/m, grep 1); "load factor" (grep 7) as "the proportion of the slots in the array that are used"; "the number of probes in an unsuccessful search" U_n (grep 1); the open-addressing search mechanism verbatim ("searching through alternative locations in the array (the probe sequence) until either the target record is found, or an unused array slot is found, which indicates that there is no such key in the table"); and the subexpression 1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) (\frac{1}{1-\alpha}, grep 1) ŌĆö BUT embedded in the LINEAR-PROBING unsuccessful formula EU_n=┬Į(1+1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒))+╬ś(1/m), a different probe model from uniform hashing. DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the exact finite identity (m+1)/(mŌłÆn+1) (grep 0 for m-n+1/m+1); the UNIFORM-HASHING model itself (uniformly random permutation probe sequence) and its standalone unsuccessful-search asymptotic 1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) ŌĆö the page's 1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) is the linear-probing sub-term ┬Į(1+1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒)), whereas the uniform-hashing standalone limit (CLRS Theorem 11.6) is not on this revision as such; the sum-of-products definition ╬Ż C(n,i)/C(m,i); the hockey-stick proof; all exact rational instances (1, 3/2, 2, 11/4, 101/31); the Monte-Carlo agreement, the invariance leg, the falsification, and every z-value and the digest. Honest posture ŌĆö the load factor ╬▒=n/m, the "number of probes in an unsuccessful search" object, and the subexpression 1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) are QUOTED on the pinned page (the last inside the linear-probing formula, a distinct model); the exact finite identity (m+1)/(mŌłÆn+1), the uniform-hashing model, all exact instances, the hockey-stick proof, the MC agreement, the invariance leg, and the falsification of "1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) exact at finite (m,n)" are the firsthand contribution ŌĆö a falsification that directly clarifies that the page's asymptotic is a limit, not a finite-table equality. Nothing oversold as novel.
depends: PROPOSAL 245 -> VERDICT 258 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ŌĆö hashing / load-balancing: the exact finite-(m,n) expected probe count of an UNSUCCESSFUL search in an open-addressing hash table under uniform hashing is (m+1)/(mŌłÆn+1), rising monotonically to the classic 1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) as m,nŌåÆŌł× at fixed ╬▒, used to refute the "1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) exact at finite (m,n)" naive belief (at m=100,n=70 truth 101/31Ōēł3.258 vs naive 10/3Ōēł3.333, rejected at |z|=12.57). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P244 ŌåÆ P245 (union-max, no regress). Distinct from prior heads ŌĆö open-addressing uniform-hashing unsuccessful-search probes is grep-0 across both repos and orthogonal to the named prior hashing/allocation heads: birthday-collision (P132, coincidence probability), balls-into-bins (P225, max-load), consistent-hashing-max-gap (gap distribution), and Maekawa grid-quorum (P209, quorum size) ŌĆö this is the exact expected probe count of an unsuccessful open-addressing search, not a collision/allocation/quorum object.

## VERDICT 258 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T06:46:02Z ┬Ę status: APPROVE
source: PROPOSAL 245 (idea-engine, sim-ready) ŌåÆ VERDICT 258 (+13 offset); idea-engine merge fb46010 (PR #864); reproduction mirror sim-lab sims/verdict-258-open-addressing-probes/ (PR #343, merge 4cd6599, proposal-authored, no competing open PR).
claim: under the UNIFORM-HASHING model (each key's probe sequence a uniformly random permutation of all m slots), the EXPECTED probes in an UNSUCCESSFUL search is EXACTLY E_unsucc(m,n)=(m+1)/(mŌłÆn+1) ŌĆö from ╬Ż_{i=0}^{n} C(n,i)/C(m,i) via the hockey-stick identity ŌćÆ C(m+1,n)/C(m,n)=(m+1)/(mŌłÆn+1) ŌĆö increasing monotonically to the classic 1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) as m,nŌåÆŌł× at fixed ╬▒=n/m, while the naive "1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) exact at finite (m,n)" is provably wrong (m=100,n=70: truth 101/31Ōēł3.258, not 10/3Ōēł3.333).
ruling: APPROVE. Verifier ideas/fleet/verify_245_open_addressing_probes.py (sha256 ac4642df4a3b77fe35c418ffba9d2038589d9463cb1c40e1f341e0d371c5c5ab) copied byte-identical (diff exit 0) and re-run firsthand: results_sha256 98d7398935db83b93f4e5b71ef4393abf487d3bc4371018d0d9d16e4f75e1746 reproduces byte-for-byte across THREE routes (default run, --selfcheck in-process double-run guard, separate re-invocation). All four gates PASS each in its own direction.
G1 EXACT identity (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): mismatches=0, anchor_mismatches=0, max_discrepancy exactly 0 (z n/a ŌĆö exact). Reproduced INDEPENDENTLY via a distinct route ŌĆö the full distribution of the first-empty-slot position P(X=k)=[n!/(nŌłÆk+1)!]┬Ę(mŌłÆn)┬Ę(mŌłÆk)!/m! in exact Fraction, each distribution normalized to 1 ŌĆö agreeing with the closed form and the tail-sum over ALL 780 pairs 1Ōēżn<mŌēż40 plus the full battery, mismatches=0. Hockey-stick ╬Ż C(n,i)/C(m,i)=C(m+1,n)/C(m,n)=(m+1)/(mŌłÆn+1) confirmed.
G2 MC agreement: headline (m=100,n=70), N=200000 independent uniform-hashing searches, mean_hat=3.2596 vs E_exact=101/31Ōēł3.2581, |z|=0.2617 (<3, Z_ACCEPT=3.0). Independent trials, no thinning.
G3 invariance: occupancy-set independence ŌĆö config A (first-n) z=0.3375, config B (pseudo-random n-subset) z=ŌłÆ1.1047, two-sample invariance z=1.0188 (all |z|<3); exact monotone convergence at ╬▒=7/10 over mŌłł{10,100,1000,10000,100000} ŌåÆ 11/4,101/31,143/43,10001/3001,100001/30001 strictly increasing, all strictly below 10/3.
G4 falsifiability (same headline sample): the naive 1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒)=10/3 is REJECTED at |z|=12.5653 (Ōē½3, Z_REJECT=3.0) while the same sample agrees with exact 101/31 at |z|=0.26 ŌĆö the teeth hold: 1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) is a limit, not a finite-table equality.
grounding: pin Wikipedia "Open addressing" oldid 1353211902 @ raw-wikitext sha1 164df5a783e58eb4bb744e87fe2e7a5c227199bb (8806 bytes) ŌĆö fetched firsthand, sha1 matches exactly. INDEPENDENT re-grep of the raw wikitext confirms P245's quoted-vs-derived split is ACCURATE: the page's 1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) occurs ONLY as the linear-probing sub-term EU_n=┬Į(1+1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒))+╬ś(1/m), a DISTINCT probe model, NOT as a standalone uniform-hashing unsuccessful-search result; (m+1)/(mŌłÆn+1) and the standalone uniform-hashing 1/(1ŌłÆ╬▒) limit are genuinely grep-0; the QUOTED items are literally present ŌĆö ╬▒=n/m (grep 1), "load factor" (grep 7), the U_n "number of probes in an unsuccessful search" object (grep 1), and the probe-sequence search mechanism verbatim. The standalone uniform-hashing limit (CLRS Theorem 11.6) and the exact finite identity are correctly labeled DERIVED, not quoted. No overclaim; the seam is honestly disclosed ŌĆö this matched P245's self-report exactly.
reconciliation: sim-lab mirror verifier is byte-identical (diff exit 0, same sha256), its committed run-stdout digest matches, repro-report.md defers the canonical ruling to this slice and advances no high-water ŌĆö zero discrepancy. Ruling built on top; no competing sim-lab PR (open PRs empty).
decision: APPROVE. all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null. Verdict high-water ADVANCES V257 ŌåÆ V258 (union-max, no regress).
­¤ōŖ Model: Claude Opus family ┬Ę high effort ┬Ę idea-verdict adjudication.

## PROPOSAL 246 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T06:54:53Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 259, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/gordon-growth-dividend-discount-2026-07-21.md
question: Model a growing perpetuity (the constant-growth dividend-discount / Gordon growth model) that pays first cashflow D1 at t=1 and grows at a constant rate g per period thereafter, discounted at rate r with r>g>ŌłÆ1. Is its present value EXACTLY PV = D1/(rŌłÆg) ŌĆö the closed sum of the infinite geometric series ╬Ż_{tŌēź1} D1(1+g)^{tŌłÆ1}/(1+r)^t = (D1/(1+r))┬Ę1/(1ŌłÆŽü) with Žü=(1+g)/(1+r)<1, equivalently the fixed point of the recurrence PV = D1/(1+r) + PV┬Ę(1+g)/(1+r) ŌćÆ PV┬Ę(rŌłÆg)=D1 ŌĆö so (D1=1,r=1/10,g=0)ŌåÆ10, (1,1/10,4/100)ŌåÆ50/3Ōēł16.667, (6,1/10,2/100)ŌåÆ75, (3,1/5,1/20)ŌåÆ20 and (2,12/100,5/100)ŌåÆ200/7Ōēł28.571; and is the naive level-perpetuity P=D1/r (which ignores growth) provably wrong for gŌēĀ0 ŌĆö at (D1=1,r=1/10,g=4/100) the truth is 50/3Ōēł16.667, not 10, undervaluing the growing stream by the exact factor r/(rŌłÆg)?
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 259 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=3cb527ff7b7873d2bacb56dcb4eea6f490ef7817b67766f45696ab6d706230e4 (in-process double-run + --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ŌĆö G1 EXACT identity (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): over the 10-triple battery {(1,1/10,0),(1,1/10,4/100),(6,1/10,2/100),(3,1/5,1/20),(2,12/100,5/100),(10,8/100,3/100),(5,7/100,ŌłÆ2/100),(100,15/100,6/100),(7/2,9/100,1/100),(1,1/4,1/10)} the closed form D1/(rŌłÆg), the reconstruction partial_sum(T=60)+exact_tail(T=60), the fixed-point cf┬Ę(rŌłÆg)==D1, and the recurrence cf==D1/(1+r)+cf┬Ę(1+g)/(1+r) are EXACTLY equal, anchors (1,1/10,0)ŌåÆ10/(1,1/10,4/100)ŌåÆ50/3/(6,1/10,2/100)ŌåÆ75/(3,1/5,1/20)ŌåÆ20/(2,12/100,5/100)ŌåÆ200/7 pinned, mismatches=0, anchor_mismatches=0, max discrepancy exactly 0 (z not applicable, reported null/"exact"); G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): headline (D1=1,r=1/10,g=4/100), N=100000 i.i.d. stochastic-dividend DDM paths (per-period mean-1 multiplicative shocks ╬┤=0.3 via the seeded RNG, T=160, one PV draw per path), mean_hat=16.6681667254 vs the exact finite-horizon target PV┬Ę(1ŌłÆŽü^160)=16.6645563219 (infinite-horizon E=50/3Ōēł16.6666666667), z=0.0408270129, |z|<3 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] ŌĆö the paths are INDEPENDENT (one PV each) so NO thinning is needed (stated in the verifier docstring); G3 INVARIANCE/ROBUSTNESS (own direction): value invariant to the noise structure ŌĆö config A (╬┤=0.3) zA=0.0408270129 and config B (╬┤=0.5, SEED+1) meanB=16.662406346 zB=ŌłÆ0.0034001459 both agree with the exact target and with each other (two-sample z=0.0090221259), all |z|<3; exact common-shift-invariance sub-check (Fraction): D1/(rŌłÆg) is unchanged under (r,g)ŌåÆ(r+c,g+c) for cŌłł{1/100,3/100,1/2,ŌłÆ1/50} across 3 triples ŌåÆ shift_invariance_mismatches=0 over the 12-case battery (the value depends only on the spread rŌłÆg, not the levels); G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME headline sample): the naive level-perpetuity D1/r=10 (ignoring growth) is REJECTED at z_naive=75.4046816262 (Ōē½3, Z_REJECT=3.0) while the same sample agrees with exact 50/3 at |z|=0.04 ŌĆö the teeth: ignoring positive growth undervalues a growing stream by the exact factor r/(rŌłÆg); all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/fleet/verify_246_gordon_growth_ddm.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions), file sha256 afdd5047a87a048bf86f8a4b1c13e0307da2826c914e616bedfe3b7e8b89da9f.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Dividend_discount_model&oldid=1345461329@4e3d4d4d29f5cc6486b0fa92fe5cca52c547f45b ┬Ę fetched 2026-07-21 (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum of the raw wikitext ŌĆö exact match: Dividend discount model rev 1345461329 sha1 4e3d4d4d29f5cc6486b0fa92fe5cca52c547f45b, 9106 bytes). QUOTED literally on the pinned "Dividend discount model" revision (grep count > 0): the exact closed form P=\frac{D_1}{r-g} (grep frac{D_1}=6; also P_0=\frac{D_0(1+g)}{r-g}=\frac{D_1}{r-g}); the name "Gordon growth model"/"Gordon" (grep 6); the infinite geometric series P_0=\sum_{t=1}^{\infty} D_0 (1+g)^t/(1+r)^t verbatim (a D_0-indexed writing of the D_1(1+g)^{tŌłÆ1} series ŌĆö equivalent since D_1=D_0(1+g)); the variable glossary "P is the current stock price ŌĆ” g is the constant growth rate in perpetuity ŌĆ” r is the constant cost of equity capital ŌĆ” D_1 is the value of dividends at the end of the first period"; "perpetuity" (grep 2); "growth rate" (grep 5); "cost of equity" (grep 5); the fixed-point rearrangement \frac{D_1}{r-g}=P_0 ŌćÆ \frac{D_1}{P_0}+g=r; r-g literal (grep 4) with "r-g cannot be negative"; and ŌĆö critically for G4 ŌĆö the zero-growth case P_0=\frac{D_1}{r} presented as the g=0 "dividend is capitalized" special case (grep 1). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): all exact rational instances 10, 50/3, 75, 20, 200/7 (grep 0 for 50/3, 200/7, 101/31); the finite-T residual identity partial_sum(T)+PV┬ĘŽü^T=PV and the finite-horizon MC target PV┬Ę(1ŌłÆŽü^T); the stochastic-dividend Monte-Carlo agreement; the mean-1-shock noise-structure invariance (configs A/B); the exact common-shift invariance (r,g)ŌåÆ(r+c,g+c); the falsification of the level-perpetuity D1/r foil for gŌēĀ0; and every z-value plus the digest. Honest posture / relabeling disclosed ŌĆö the page uses P/P_0 (price) for PV (present value, same object), r="cost of equity capital" for the discount rate (the "Yield-plus-growth" variant also writes it k, \frac{D_1}{k-g}, grep k-g=1), and indexes the series as D_0(1+g)^t where I index D_1(1+g)^{tŌłÆ1} (equivalent); "required rate of return" is grep 0 (the article says "required total return" and cites a paper titled "Required Rate of Profit") ŌĆö not claimed. The quoted formula sits specifically in the constant-growth DDM / Gordon growth model context. The naive D1/r that G4 rejects is not a page error: the page presents D1/r correctly as the g=0 case, and the firsthand contribution is the falsification that it undervalues a stream with g>0 (by the exact factor r/(rŌłÆg)). Nothing oversold as novel ŌĆö the closed form and the series are textbook (Gordon & Shapiro 1956; Williams 1938) and cited as such; the exact instances, the finite-T identity, the stochastic-DDM MC agreement, the two invariance legs, the falsification, and every z-value + the digest are the firsthand contribution.
depends: PROPOSAL 246 -> VERDICT 259 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ŌĆö venture / valuation: the present value of a growing perpetuity (constant-growth dividend-discount / Gordon growth model) with first cashflow D1 at t=1, constant growth g and discount rate r (r>g>ŌłÆ1) is EXACTLY PV = D1/(rŌłÆg), the closed sum of the geometric series ╬Ż_{tŌēź1} D1(1+g)^{tŌłÆ1}/(1+r)^t, used to refute the naive level-perpetuity D1/r that ignores growth (at D1=1,r=1/10,g=4/100 truth 50/3Ōēł16.667 vs naive 10, rejected at |z|=75.40). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P245 ŌåÆ P246 (union-max, no regress). Explicit distinctness: gordon-growth / dividend-discount is grep-0 across both repos and orthogonal to the named prior venture heads (Markowitz GMV P242, Kelly P100, put-call P234, newsvendor critical-fractile, St. Petersburg cap-collapse, Cournot/Stackelberg, Vickrey/revenue-equivalence) ŌĆö this is the exact closed-form valuation of a growing perpetuity, not a portfolio/auction/stopping object.

## VERDICT 259 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T07:36:19Z ┬Ę status: APPROVE
source: PROPOSAL 246 (idea-engine, sim-ready) ŌåÆ VERDICT 259 (+13 offset); idea-engine proposal+verifier landed sim-ready @ d0bf551 (PR #866); reproduction mirror sim-lab sims/verdict-259-gordon-growth-ddm/ (PR #344, head 3ea29d2, reproduction-only, defers the canonical ruling ŌĆö open/unmerged at ruling time, no competing PR opened).
claim: the present value of a growing perpetuity (constant-growth dividend-discount / Gordon growth model) paying first cashflow D1 at t=1 and growing at constant rate g per period thereafter, discounted at rate r with r>g>ŌłÆ1, is EXACTLY PV = D1/(rŌłÆg) ŌĆö the closed sum of the geometric series ╬Ż_{tŌēź1} D1(1+g)^{tŌłÆ1}/(1+r)^t = (D1/(1+r))┬Ę1/(1ŌłÆŽü) with Žü=(1+g)/(1+r)<1, equivalently the fixed point PV┬Ę(rŌłÆg)=D1 ŌĆö so (D1=1,r=1/10,g=0)ŌåÆ10, (1,1/10,4/100)ŌåÆ50/3, (6,1/10,2/100)ŌåÆ75, (3,1/5,1/20)ŌåÆ20, (2,12/100,5/100)ŌåÆ200/7; while the naive level-perpetuity P=D1/r (which ignores growth) is provably wrong for gŌēĀ0 (at r=1/10,g=4/100 the truth is 50/3Ōēł16.667, not 10, undervaluing by the exact factor r/(rŌłÆg)).
ruling: APPROVE. Verifier ideas/fleet/verify_246_gordon_growth_ddm.py (sha256 afdd5047a87a048bf86f8a4b1c13e0307da2826c914e616bedfe3b7e8b89da9f) copied byte-identical (diff exit 0) and re-run firsthand: results_sha256 3cb527ff7b7873d2bacb56dcb4eea6f490ef7817b67766f45696ab6d706230e4 reproduces byte-for-byte across THREE routes (default run, --selfcheck in-process double-run guard, separate re-invocation of the byte-identical copy). All four gates PASS each in its own direction; independently re-adjudicated with my own fractions.Fraction kernel and distinct RNG seeds (424242 / 999983) ŌĆö same PASS directions.
G1 EXACT identity (fractions.Fraction, zero tolerance): over the 10-triple battery the closed form D1/(rŌłÆg), the truncated-sum+exact-tail reconstruction, the fixed-point cf┬Ę(rŌłÆg)==D1 and the recurrence cf==D1/(1+r)+cf┬Ę(1+g)/(1+r) are EXACTLY equal ŌĆö mismatches=0, anchor_mismatches=0, max_discrepancy exactly 0 (z n/a ŌĆö exact). Anchors (1,1/10,0)ŌåÆ10; (1,1/10,4/100)ŌåÆ50/3; (6,1/10,2/100)ŌåÆ75; (3,1/5,1/20)ŌåÆ20; (2,12/100,5/100)ŌåÆ200/7 pinned. Independently reconfirmed via a from-scratch geometric-sum closed form (D1/(1+r))┬Ę1/(1ŌłÆŽü) and an independent T=80 truncation ŌĆö 0 mismatches.
G2 MC agreement (|z|<3): headline D1=1, r=1/10, g=4/100, N=100000 i.i.d. stochastic-dividend DDM paths (mean-1 multiplicative shocks ╬┤=0.3, T=160, one PV per path), mean_hat=16.6681667254 vs exact finite-horizon target PV┬Ę(1ŌłÆŽü^160)=16.6645563219 (E_infinite=50/3Ōēł16.6667), committed z=0.0408270129 (<3, Z_ACCEPT=3.0); independent distinct-seed re-run z=ŌłÆ1.07 (<3). Independent paths, no thinning.
G3 invariance/robustness (own direction): value invariant to noise structure ŌĆö config A (╬┤=0.3) zA=0.0408270129 and config B (╬┤=0.5, SEED+1) zB=ŌłÆ0.0034001459 both agree with the exact target and with each other (two_sample_z=0.0090221259); exact common-shift invariance (r,g)ŌåÆ(r+c,g+c) over the 12-case battery ŌåÆ shift_invariance_mismatches=0 (PV depends only on the spread rŌłÆg). Independent distinct-seed leg: zB=ŌłÆ1.10, two_sample_z=0.74, 0 shift mismatches ŌĆö all |z|<3.
G4 falsifiability (own direction, SAME headline sample): the naive level-perpetuity P=D1/r=10.0 (ignoring growth) is REJECTED at z_naive=75.4046816262 (Ōē½3, Z_REJECT=3.0) while the same sample agrees with the exact 50/3 at |z|=0.04 ŌĆö the teeth: ignoring positive growth undervalues the stream by the exact factor r/(rŌłÆg). Independent distinct-seed re-run rejects the same foil at |z|=80.48.
grounding: pin Wikipedia "Dividend discount model" oldid 1345461329 @ raw-wikitext sha1 4e3d4d4d29f5cc6486b0fa92fe5cca52c547f45b (9106 bytes) ŌĆö fetched firsthand, sha1 recomputed and matches exactly. INDEPENDENT quoted-vs-derived re-grep, BOTH directions, confirms P246's split is ACCURATE. QUOTED (literally present, grep>0): the closed form \frac{D_1}{r-g} (present in 3 spacings; frac{D_1} total 7); the "Gordon growth model" name (Gordon grep 10); the g=0 "dividend is capitalized" special case P_0=\frac{D_1}{r} (grep 1) ŌĆö critical for G4; "perpetuity" (grep 3); "growth rate" (grep 8); "cost of equity" (grep 5); "r-g" (grep 5) with "cannot be negative"; the fixed-point rearrangement \frac{D_1}{P_0} (grep 2); the infinite geometric series sum_{t=1} (grep 2); and the Yield-plus-growth variant \frac{D_1}{k - g} (present, literal with spaces). DERIVED (grep count 0, genuinely absent): all exact rational instances 50/3, 200/7, 101/31; the decimal 16.66ŌĆ”; the four gate z-values (75.40ŌĆ”); the results digest 3cb527ffŌĆ”; "Monte", z_naive, shift, SEED, 20260717; the finite-T target identity and the stochastic-DDM MC. Honest posture ŌĆö P/P_0 (price) relabels PV, r ("cost of equity") the discount rate, the series indexed D_0(1+g)^t vs my D_1(1+g)^{tŌłÆ1} (equivalent); "required rate of return" is grep 0 (page says "required total return") ŌĆö not claimed. The naive D1/r that G4 rejects is the page's correct g=0 case; the firsthand contribution is the falsification that it undervalues a g>0 stream. No overclaim; matched P246's self-report ŌĆö no seam. (Only cosmetic count nuances: P246's "frac{D_1}=6"/"k-g=1" normalize spacing variants the raw page writes as "r -g" and "k - g"; the quoted/derived PARTITION holds exactly both directions.)

## PROPOSAL 247 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T07:51:40Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 260, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/superbot-games/green-hackenbush-nim-2026-07-21.md
question: In Green Hackenbush (every edge green, either player may cut an edge, after which all edges disconnected from the ground fall; normal play) the SpragueŌĆōGrundy value of any rooted forest equals the colon-principle value sub(v,parent)=XOR over children c of (1+sub(c,v)), forest value = XOR over ground-neighbours r of (1+sub(r,0)); hence a bamboo forest of stalk-lengths (aŌéü,ŌĆ”,aŌé¢) has value aŌéüŌŖĢŌĆ”ŌŖĢaŌé¢ and is a first-player win iff the nim-sum is nonzero ŌĆö Green Hackenbush IS Nim. Anchors: (3,5,7)ŌåÆ3ŌŖĢ5ŌŖĢ7=1; Y-tree {0-1,1-2,1-3}ŌåÆ1; the naive "P iff arithmetic sum is even" foil is false.
done-when: sim-lab VERDICT 260 reproduces SEED=20260717 results_sha256=f1967eac6a0158fb4b1546facd309355577f251033067167296f6b921903c0b9 (in-process double-run via --selfcheck + separate re-invocation byte-identical), all four gates PASS each in its own direction ŌĆö G1 exact colon_value==grundy over 800 positions (exhaustive rooted trees Ōēż7 edges + random battery), 0 mismatches; G2 Monte-Carlo P-density z=0.731 (p╠é=0.08248 vs exact p0=21/256, N=200000 iid); G3 invariance 0 relabel discrepancies + 360 optimal-vs-random games 0 losses; G4 falsifiability rejects sum-parity foil |z|=373.4 and sum-mod-3 foil |z|=237.0; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. Verifier ships firsthand in idea-engine: ideas/superbot-games/verify_247_green_hackenbush_nim.py (stdlib only: json, hashlib, math, random, fractions, itertools), file sha256 da7f11191025addd748d29a49d22eb9173bd05ab8775bf8458a62e65b31b639b.
grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Hackenbush&oldid=1360289482@1a8d4f063a7890b389c959e4b675791d2bc15754 ┬Ę fetched 2026-07-21 (API revisions.sha1 == self-computed sha1sum, 9955 bytes). QUOTED literally (grep>0): "colon principle" ├Ś4, "Green Hackenbush" ├Ś4, "bamboo" ├Ś1, "nim sum" ├Ś2, "Sprague"/"Grundy" ├Ś2, and the colon-principle wording "replace the branches by a non-branching stalk of length equal to their nim sum". DERIVED firsthand (grep 0): "XOR"/"exclusive or", literal "equivalent to Nim", hyphenated "nim-sum", the heap-size-n and XOR-of-lengths numeric formulas, the mex-DP engine, all z-values, p0=21/256, and the digest. Honest posture: the article develops the colon principle under an "impartial version" heading while naming the green variant separately ŌĆö joining them is our inference; the ground-fall cutting rule is standard, not restated.
depends: PROPOSAL 247 -> VERDICT 260 (+13 offset).

## PROPOSAL 249 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T09:16:00Z ┬Ę status: sim-ready
target: sim-lab (VERDICT 262, +13 offset)
idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/pagerank-random-surfer-stationary-2026-07-21.md
question: Model the PageRank random-surfer process on the FIXED directed graph of N=4 nodes with out-links 0ŌåÆ{1,2}, 1ŌåÆ{2,3}, 2ŌåÆ{0}, 3ŌåÆ{} (node 3 DANGLING ŌĆö no out-links), damping d=1/2, uniform teleport 1/N. Build the row-stochastic Google matrix G ŌĆö non-dangling row i: G[i][j]=d┬Ę(A[i][j]/outdeg(i))+(1ŌłÆd)/N; dangling row uniform G[i][j]=d┬Ę(1/N)+(1ŌłÆd)/N=1/N ŌĆö and solve its stationary left-eigenvector. Is the EXACT rational stationary distribution ŽĆ=(52/179, 40/179, 50/179, 37/179) (the unique probability vector with ŽĆG=ŽĆ, ╬ŻŽĆ=1) Ōēł (0.2905, 0.2235, 0.2793, 0.2067) ŌĆö with node 2 (in-degree 2) sitting BELOW node 0 (in-degree 1) because a link's weight is set by the linking node's own PageRank and by the damping/teleport structure (including the dangling node's uniformly redistributed mass) ŌĆö while the naive belief that "the stationary attention share is proportional to raw in-degree" = (1/5,1/5,2/5,1/5) is provably wrong (rejected on the random-surfer sample at max|z|Ōēł646.5)?

assert ŽĆG=ŽĆ with max residual EXACTLY 0 on every entry and ╬ŻŽĆ=1 exactly; independent adjugate/cofactor null-vector of (GßĄĆŌłÆI) equals ŽĆ exactly, adjugate_mismatches=0; exact power iteration from uniform converges to ŽĆ within 1e-18 in 32 iters (corroboration ŌĆö ╬╗ŌééŌēĀ0 so an exact stochastic matrix never reaches ŽĆ in finitely many exact steps), z=null/"exact"; batch means (batches Ōē½ mixing time) give an honest SE (stated in the verifier docstring); G3 EQUIVARIANCE/INVARIANCE (own direction): apply the fixed non-trivial node relabel Žā=[2,0,3,1], rebuild G and re-solve ŽĆ on the permuted graph EXACTLY, assert it equals Žā(ŽĆ_original) exactly ŌĆö ŽĆ_permuted=(40/179,37/179,52/179,50/179)=Žā(ŽĆ), relabel_mismatches=0 (a verifier that hard-codes node indices breaks this); QUOTED literally on the pinned "PageRank" revision (grep count > 0): the exact PageRank formula PR(p_i)=\frac{1-d}{N}+d\sum_{p_jŌłłM(p_i)}\frac{PR(p_j)}{L(p_j)} (grep 1) with the teleport term \frac{1-d}{N} (grep 5), the damping factor d, and the out-degree normalization L(p_j) (grep 4); "random surfer" (grep 2, "a random surfer will land on that page by clicking on a link"); "damping factor" (grep 7) with the article's illustrative value 0.85 (grep 2); "Google matrix" (grep 2); "Markov chain" (grep 3) and the "probability distribution" over pages (grep 7). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on the pinned raw wikitext): the specific fixed 4-node graph 0ŌåÆ{1,2},1ŌåÆ{2,3},2ŌåÆ{0},3ŌåÆ{} and its adjacency; the chosen damping d=1/2 for THIS graph (the page's illustrative value is 0.85, not 1/2); the exact rational stationary vector ŽĆ=(52/179,40/179,50/179,37/179) (grep 0 for 179, 52/179); the matrix form ŽĆG=ŽĆ solved as an exact rational left-eigenvector plus the independent adjugate null-vector cross-check; the DANGLING-node uniform-row handling (the words "dangling" and "teleport" are grep 0 on this revision ŌĆö the uniform redistribution of a dead-end's mass is the standard Google-matrix construction, applied firsthand here, not literally on the page); the naive in-degree foil; the batch-means Monte-Carlo random-surfer chain; every z-value; SEED 20260717; and the results digest. Honest posture ŌĆö the random-surfer model, the damping factor, the exact PageRank recurrence (1ŌłÆd)/N+d┬Ę╬Ż PR(p_j)/L(p_j), the Google matrix, and the Markov-chain / stationary-distribution framing are QUOTED textbook material on the pinned page; the specific fixed graph, the chosen d=1/2, the exact rational ŽĆ, the two exact solve routes, the dangling-uniform handling, the batch-means MC agreement, the equivariance leg, the in-degree falsification, and every z-value + the digest are the firsthand contribution. Nothing oversold as novel: PageRank and the Google matrix are textbook (Brin & Page 1998; Langville & Meyer), cited as such.
depends: PROPOSAL 249 -> VERDICT 262 (+13 offset).
loop: fleet slot ŌĆö PageRank / random-surfer / Markov-stationary: the exact stationary distribution of the random-surfer Google matrix on the fixed 4-node directed graph 0ŌåÆ{1,2},1ŌåÆ{2,3},2ŌåÆ{0},3ŌåÆ{} (dangling) with damping d=1/2 and uniform teleport 1/N is the closed-form rational left-eigenvector ŽĆ=(52/179,40/179,50/179,37/179), used to refute the naive "stationary attention ŌłØ raw in-degree" belief (node 2 has in-degree 2 but ranks below node 0 with in-degree 1; the in-degree share (1/5,1/5,2/5,1/5) is rejected at max|z|=646.5). Proposal high-water ADVANCES P248 ŌåÆ P249 (union-max, no regress). Explicit distinctness: pagerank / random-surfer / Google-matrix stationary distribution is grep-0 across both repos and orthogonal to the named prior Markov/queueing stationary heads ŌĆö Parrondo's paradox (capital-parity chain stationary ŽĆ), Jackson product-form independence, M/M/c Erlang-C delay (P241, birth-death stationary law), and Palm / M/G/Ōł× insensitivity ŌĆö this is the exact closed-form left-eigenvector of the Google matrix on a fixed directed graph with a dangling node, not a queueing / game-theoretic stationary object.

## VERDICT 262 ┬Ę 2026-07-21T09:40:46Z ┬Ę status: ruled APPROVE
source: PROPOSAL 249 (idea-engine control/outbox.md, sim-ready 2026-07-21T09:16:00Z; VERDICT 262, +13 offset)
decision: APPROVE ŌĆö the exact rational stationary distribution of the random-surfer Google matrix on the fixed 4-node directed graph 0->{1,2}, 1->{2,3}, 2->{0}, 3->{} (node 3 dangling), damping d=1/2, uniform teleport 1/N is pi=(52/179, 40/179, 50/179, 37/179), the unique probability vector with piG=pi. Upheld by fully independent re-derivation, not a rubber-stamp of the verifier self-report.
adjudication:
- Verifier byte-identical across both repos (ideas/fleet/verify_249_pagerank_random_surfer.py, file sha256 3e1e7647fb485f0739ebce9f4d3f288f09ea5c97b15115e7166cf91324f812c1; diff exit 0 vs sim-lab sims/verdict-262-pagerank-random-surfer/).
- results_sha256 reproduced firsthand THREE ways (default run, in-process --selfcheck double-run, separate re-invocation), byte-grepped with no truncation: 18ba2ac7a7bfe36dc476214f79d55c26c3e212d8073d581ae226da55d4154355.
- G1 EXACT (own direction): independent Gaussian elimination over fractions.Fraction on (G^T - I) with the sum(pi)=1 constraint yields pi=(52/179,40/179,50/179,37/179); piG - pi residual EXACTLY 0 on every entry; an independent adjugate/cofactor null-vector of (G^T - I) equals pi exactly (two distinct exact routes agree). G rows [1/8,3/8,3/8,1/8], [1/8,1/8,3/8,3/8], [5/8,1/8,1/8,1/8], [1/4,1/4,1/4,1/4] (dangling row uniform).
- G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3, batch means): committed run max|z|=1.7965 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] PASS; an independent re-run at a fresh seed (100 batches x 30000 = 3,000,000 post-burn-in steps) accepts pi at max|z|=1.40. Batch-means over the iid binomial SE assessed INDEPENDENTLY as a SOUND autocorrelation correction, not gate-weakening: the sample-path occupancy of a Markov chain is genuinely autocorrelated so the iid SE is dishonestly small; batch means with batches >> mixing time restore an honest CLT-based SE while KEEPING teeth ŌĆö on the same independent sample a 2/179 perturbation (50/179,40/179,50/179,39/179) is rejected at max|z|=52 and the in-degree foil at max|z|=541.
- G3 EQUIVARIANCE (own direction): relabel sigma=[2,0,3,1] re-solved independently in Fraction gives pi_permuted=(40/179,37/179,52/179,50/179)=sigma(pi), mismatches=0.
- G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, same MC sample): the naive "pi proportional to raw in-degree" = (1/5,1/5,2/5,1/5) is rejected at max|z|=646.5432 [Z_REJECT=6.0] while the true pi agrees at max|z|=1.80 ŌĆö node 2 (in-degree 2) ranks below node 0 (in-degree 1) because a link's weight is set by the linking node's own PageRank and the damping/teleport structure.
grounding: Wikipedia "PageRank" oldid 1364775010, raw-wikitext sha1 faa1d38442418ee08add5a47d8950ae58d0bbedf (74463 bytes) ŌĆö self-verified firsthand (sha1sum and byte count both match). Quoted-vs-derived determined INDEPENDENTLY by grepping the raw wikitext both directions. QUOTED (grep count > 0): "random surfer" (2), "damping factor" (7), "Google matrix" (2), "Markov chain" (1), the recurrence pieces L(p (5) / PR(p (7) / {1-d} (5), and the article's illustrative damping 0.85 (5). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0): "dangling" (0) and "teleport" (0) genuinely absent, "179"/"52/179" (0), the fixed 4-node graph, the chosen d=1/2 (the lone "1/2" hit is inside a citation URL, not a damping value ŌĆö the page uses 0.85), the exact rational pi, the two exact solve routes, the dangling-uniform Google-matrix construction, the in-degree foil, batch-means MC, every z-value, SEED 20260717, and the digest. This matches P249's self-report exactly; nothing oversold as novel (PageRank and the Google matrix cited as textbook, Brin & Page 1998 / Langville & Meyer).
reconciliation: sim-lab reproduction mirror PR #347 (merge 14a64e7, sims/verdict-262-pagerank-random-surfer/) is reproduction-only ŌĆö it carries the byte-identical verifier + digest + run-stdout + repro-report and explicitly defers the canonical ruling to this slice. This VERDICT is built on top of that mirror; no competing sim-lab PR opened.

idea: https://github.com/menno420/idea-engine/blob/main/ideas/fleet/vcg-clarke-pivot-2026-07-21.md
question: Model the VickreyŌĆōClarkeŌĆōGroves (Clarke-pivot) mechanism in its MULTI-UNIT SINGLE-DEMAND instance: k identical units auctioned to n bidders who each want AT MOST ONE unit, valuations vŌéüŌēźvŌééŌēźŌĆ”ŌēźvŌéÖ. Is it exactly true that (1) the efficient allocation gives the k units to the k highest-value bidders; (2) each winner i's Clarke-pivot payment = the externality it imposes on others = WŌéŗßĄó(efficient allocation WITHOUT agent i) ŌłÆ WŌéŗßĄó(efficient allocation WITH agent i) collapses, for EVERY winner, to the SAME uniform price = the (k+1)-th highest valuation (the highest LOSING bid); (3) truthful reporting is a dominant strategy (a winner's payment is independent of its own report as long as it stays a winner); and (4) expected revenue = k┬ĘE[VŌéŹŌé¢ŌéŖŌéüŌéÄ:ŌéÖ] ŌĆö with the headline instance n=5, k=2, v=(10,8,6,4,2) giving winners {10,8}, uniform price = 3rd-highest = 6, revenue = k┬Ęprice = 12, pivot for the 10-bidder = 14ŌłÆ8=6 and for the 8-bidder = 16ŌłÆ10=6, and at k=1 recovering single-item second-price (price = 2nd-highest = 8)? Pre-registered naive foil (the classic multi-unit confusion): "the uniform price is the lowest WINNING bid" (the k-th highest = 2nd-highest for k=2), i.e. E[revenue] = k┬ĘE[VŌéŹŌé¢ŌéÄ:ŌéÖ] = 5831/648 Ōēł 8.998457 ŌĆö REJECTED on the same random-profile sample of TRUE revenue at |z_foil| = 378.9138 Ōē½ Z_REJECT=6.0 (charge the highest LOSING bid, not the lowest WINNING bid).

headline winners {10,8}, uniform price 6, revenue 12, pivots 14ŌłÆ8=6 / 16ŌłÆ10=6; k=1 recovers second-price (price 8); k=3 price 4, revenue 12; z=null/"exact"; G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): N=200000 iid profiles (n=5 iid Discrete-Uniform{1..6}, k=2), revenue = 2┬Ę(3rd highest), exact target E[revenue]=k┬ĘE[VŌéŹŌéāŌéÄ:Ōéģ]=7 by FULL 6ŌüĄ=7776-profile enumeration with Fraction, sample mean 700641/100000 Ōēł 7.006410, z=1.2193 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] ŌĆö iid profiles are NOT autocorrelated so the plain iid SE is honest (no batch means/thinning; stated in the verifier docstring); the whole-sample sum and sum-of-squares are accumulated in EXACT integer arithmetic so only the final sqrt/division touch float; G3 INVARIANCE (own direction, exact, 0 mismatches): (a) DSIC ŌĆö a winner's payment is INVARIANT to its own report across the whole winning range (vary the index-1 winner's bid over every integer in {7..30} keeping it a winner; the only distinct payment is 6 = the highest losing bid), (b) EQUIVARIANCE ŌĆö relabel Žā=[2,0,3,1,4] maps the winner set {0,1}ŌåÆ{0,2} and permutes the payment vector, mismatches=0; G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME MC sample): the "uniform price = lowest WINNING bid (k-th highest)" foil E[revenue]=k┬ĘE[VŌéŹŌééŌéÄ:Ōéģ]=5831/648Ōēł8.998457 is REJECTED at z_foil=ŌłÆ378.9138, |z_foil|=378.9138 Ōē½ Z_REJECT=6.0, while the same sample AGREES with the true k┬ĘE[VŌéŹŌéāŌéÄ:Ōéģ] at |z|=1.2193; all_gates_pass=true, first_failing_gate=null, decision=PASS. grounding: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=VickreyŌĆōClarkeŌĆōGroves_auction&oldid=1311466659@1d816a80feb3bcc457f4cab2b999ddfd15f75681 ┬Ę https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Vickrey_auction&oldid=1338833083@398d2e4148cc7ee83f1343f720f6e243926bb895 ┬Ę fetched 2026-07-21 (MediaWiki API revisions.sha1 == self-computed hashlib.sha1 of the raw wikitext ŌĆö exact match on both: VCG rev 1311466659 sha1 1d816a80feb3bcc457f4cab2b999ddfd15f75681, 14374 bytes; Vickrey rev 1338833083 sha1 398d2e4148cc7ee83f1343f720f6e243926bb895, 12916 bytes). QUOTED (grep count > 0): the externality-payment definition ŌĆö VCG page "charges each individual the harm they cause to other bidders" (harm grep 4) plus the words "(sum of bids ŌĆ” excluding the participant under consideration) ŌłÆ (what other winning bidders have bid ŌĆ”)" and the math V^{M}_{NŌł¢{bßĄó}} ŌłÆ V^{MŌł¢{tŌ▒╝}}_{NŌł¢{bßĄó}} = "the social cost of their winning ŌĆ” incurred by the rest of the agents"; Vickrey page "each bidder pays the 'opportunity cost' that their presence introduces ŌĆ” the total bids of all the other bidders that would have won if the first bidder had not bid, minus the total bids of all the other actual winning bidders"; the uniform-price / highest-losing-bid identity + the unit-demand condition ŌĆö Vickrey page "all winning bidders pay the amount of the highest non-winning bid ŌĆ” known as a uniform price auction ŌĆ” does not ŌĆ” result in bidders bidding their true valuations ŌĆ” unless each bidder has demand for only a single unit"; dominant-strategy truthfulness ŌĆö VCG "making truthful reporting a weakly-dominant strategy" (dominant strategy grep 1, truthful grep 6, incentive compatib grep 2); the names Clarke (grep 6), Groves (grep 6), Vickrey (grep 8); the multi-unit generalization framing ("generalization of a Vickrey auction for multiple items"; "multiunit auction"; "second-price"). DERIVED firsthand (grep count 0 on both pinned raw wikitexts): the instance n=5,k=2,v=(10,8,6,4,2) (grep 0 for 10,8,6,4,2); the exact integer pivot values, uniform price 6, revenue 12; the (k+1)-th-order-statistic characterization of the uniform price and revenue=k┬ĘE[VŌéŹŌé¢ŌéŖŌéüŌéÄ:ŌéÖ] (words "order statistic" and "(k+1)" grep 0 ŌĆö the pages say "highest non-winning bid" in words); the tie/k=1/k=3 companions; the DSIC own-report invariance sweep and the relabel equivariance leg; the Monte-Carlo profile sample; every z-value; the lowest-winning-bid foil; SEED 20260717; and the results digest. Honest posture ŌĆö DISCLOSED SEAMS: the word "pivot" is grep 0 on BOTH revisions and "externality" is grep 0 on the VCG page (the concept appears as "harm"/"marginal harm"/"social cost"/"opportunity cost"; "Clarke pivot" is my label for the standard construction); the VCG page's externality formula + worked "apples" example are stated for the GENERAL combinatorial case (items tŌ▒╝, item-sets M, a multi-demand example), so the unit-demand collapse to ONE uniform (k+1)-th-highest price is quoted-supported on the Vickrey-auction page ("highest non-winning bid ŌĆ” truthful ŌĆ” unless each bidder has demand for only a single unit") and worked firsthand here into the exact rational instance + order-statistic revenue law. Nothing oversold as novel: VCG / Clarke-pivot and the uniform-price auction are textbook (Vickrey 1961; Clarke 1971; Groves 1973), cited as such; the exact instance, the two-route pivot=externality check, the DSIC + equivariance legs, the MC agreement, the lowest-winning-bid falsification, and every z-value + the digest are the firsthand contribution.

Proposal high-water ADVANCES P249 ŌåÆ P250 (union-max, no regress). Explicit distinctness: vcg-clarke-pivot is grep-0 (VCG/clarke/groves/pivot mechanism) across both repos and orthogonal to single-item Vickrey second-price V212 (this is the multi-unit generalization ŌĆö the pivot price is the (k+1)-th, not 2nd, order statistic, and the content is the externality-payment identity; k=1 recovers V212), revenue-equivalence P235, and the power-index / airport-Shapley heads (P203/P231) ŌĆö this is the exact externality-payment / uniform-clearing-price identity of a k-identical-unit unit-demand auction, not a cooperative-game value or a revenue-equivalence object.

Headline k=2, v=(10,8,6,4,2): winners {10,8}, price 6/1, revenue 12/1, pivots 14ŌłÆ8=6 and 16ŌłÆ10=6; k=1 leg recovers single-item second-price (price 8/1).
- G2 MC AGREEMENT (|z|<3): sample mean revenue vs the exact target E[revenue]=k┬ĘE[VŌéŹŌé¢ŌéŖŌéüŌéÄ:ŌéÖ] (full-enumeration Fraction) agree at |z|=1.2193 [Z_ACCEPT=3.0] PASS ŌĆö iid profiles carry an honest iid SE (no autocorrelation, no batch means needed).
- G3 DSIC INVARIANT (own direction, exact, 0 mismatches): a winner's payment is INVARIANT to its own bid ŌĆö the only distinct payment across the swept own-bid range is 6/1 (the highest losing bid) ŌĆö plus a label-equivariance relabel that permutes winner set and payment vector with mismatches=0.
- G4 FALSIFIABILITY (own direction, SAME MC sample): the pre-registered foil "uniform price = lowest WINNING bid (k-th highest)" is REJECTED at |z_foil|=378.9138 [Z_REJECT=6.0] Ōē½ 6, while the same sample AGREES with the true k┬ĘE[VŌéŹŌé¢ŌéŖŌéüŌéÄ:ŌéÖ] at |z|=1.2193.

DERIVED firsthand (grep 0): the specific instance tuple, the (k+1)-th order-statistic characterization, the "pivot" / "externality" labels, and the z-values / SEED / digest. Nothing oversold as novel: VCG / Clarke-pivot and the uniform-price auction are textbook (Vickrey 1961; Clarke 1971; Groves 1973), cited as such.
